section_header: Investments in Local and Sustainable Food
policy_statement: Implement and support investments in local and sustainable food initiatives through the Salt Lake City Food Policy Task Force programs, including Culinary Incubator Kitchen, Dining with Discretion, Excess SLC Food Recovery, Green City Growers, and Salt Lake City FruitShare.
verbatim_text:
They work with the City to identify opportunities to increase access to fresh and nutritious food for all City residents:

Culinary Incubator Kitchen gives food businesses an advantage in the local marketplace by providing an affordable kitchen space for food prep as well as business marketing tools and resources.

Excess SLC Food Recovery is a partnership between community organizations that diverts consumable food waste from the landfill to food assistance programs.

Green City Growers was created to expand community garden access for residents by making Cityowned/managed land available for development.
extraction_rationale: A named investment/program area with multiple distinct initiatives listed beneath it; appropriate as a parent policy capturing the grouped interventions.
mechanism_description: Recovering and redirecting edible surplus food away from landfill disposal reduces organic waste decomposition in landfills and avoids methane emissions.

policy_statement: Run Dining with Discretion to provide resources that empower residents to make low carbon food choices.
parent_statement: Implement and support investments in local and sustainable food initiatives through the Salt Lake City Food Policy Task Force programs, including Culinary Incubator Kitchen, Dining with Discretion, Excess SLC Food Recovery, Green City Growers, and Salt Lake City FruitShare.
verbatim_text:
Dining with Discretion

provides resources to empower residents to make low carbon food choices.
extraction_rationale: Specific named program with a defined intervention (providing resources) under the food investments grouping.
mechanism_description: Provide education/resources that shift residents toward lower-carbon food choices, reducing lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions from food consumption.

policy_statement: Support Excess SLC Food Recovery to divert consumable food waste from landfill to food assistance programs.
parent_statement: Implement and support investments in local and sustainable food initiatives through the Salt Lake City Food Policy Task Force programs, including Culinary Incubator Kitchen, Dining with Discretion, Excess SLC Food Recovery, Green City Growers, and Salt Lake City FruitShare.
verbatim_text:
Excess SLC Food Recovery

is a partnership between community organizations that diverts consumable food waste from the landfill to food assistance programs.
extraction_rationale: Specific named partnership intervention to divert food waste; listed under the food investments program.
mechanism_description: Recovering edible surplus food for redistribution reduces organic waste sent to landfills, which in turn avoids landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Implement Green City Growers to expand community garden access by making City-owned/managed land available for development.
parent_statement: Implement and support investments in local and sustainable food initiatives through the Salt Lake City Food Policy Task Force programs, including Culinary Incubator Kitchen, Dining with Discretion, Excess SLC Food Recovery, Green City Growers, and Salt Lake City FruitShare.
verbatim_text:
Green City Growers

was created to expand community garden access for residents by making Cityowned/managed land available for development.
extraction_rationale: Specific named program with a concrete mechanism (making city land available).
mechanism_description: Making city land available for community gardens increases urban green space and local food production, improving heat/stormwater resilience and delivering small carbon/ecosystem co-benefits.

policy_statement: Implement Salt Lake City FruitShare to divert surplus fruit from local trees to individuals with low access to healthy foods.
parent_statement: Implement and support investments in local and sustainable food initiatives through the Salt Lake City Food Policy Task Force programs, including Culinary Incubator Kitchen, Dining with Discretion, Excess SLC Food Recovery, Green City Growers, and Salt Lake City FruitShare.
verbatim_text:
Salt Lake City FruitShare

diverts surplus fruit from local trees to individuals with low access to healthy foods.
extraction_rationale: Specific named diversion program listed under the food investments program.
mechanism_description: Redistributing surplus fruit prevents it from becoming landfill-bound organic waste, reducing methane emissions from anaerobic decomposition.

policy_statement: Adopt and implement Salt Lake City's Zero Waste Resolution, including goals of achieving zero waste by 2040 and diverting/recycling/composting 70% of the waste stream by 2025.
verbatim_text: Salt Lake City's Zero Waste Resolution values what we typically throw away as resources that can be returned to the local economy. Through a joint resolution signed in 2011, the City set a goal of Zero Waste by 2040 and a goal of diverting, recycling or composting 70% of the waste stream by 2025.
extraction_rationale: Named resolution with quantified targets and multiple subsequent strategies/ordinances, functioning as an umbrella policy.
mechanism_description: Implementing a zero-waste program increases recycling/composting and other diversion, reducing landfill disposal and avoiding methane emissions.

policy_statement: Establish fee structures for residential waste and recycling services that encourage waste reduction.
parent_statement: Adopt and implement Salt Lake City's Zero Waste Resolution, including goals of achieving zero waste by 2040 and diverting/recycling/composting 70% of the waste stream by 2025.
verbatim_text:
## Waste Reduction

Establish fee structures for residential waste and recycling services that encourage the reduction of waste.
extraction_rationale: Concrete policy instrument (fee structures) listed as a key strategy under the Zero Waste pathway.
mechanism_description: Variable residential waste and recycling fees incentivize waste reduction (and diversion), decreasing landfill disposal and avoiding methane emissions.

policy_statement: Expand composting capability in the city to enable food waste diversion.
parent_statement: Adopt and implement Salt Lake City's Zero Waste Resolution, including goals of achieving zero waste by 2040 and diverting/recycling/composting 70% of the waste stream by 2025.
verbatim_text:
## Increased Recycling

Expand composting capability in the city to enable food waste diversion.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure/service expansion action listed under Zero Waste strategies.
mechanism_description: Increase composting of food/organic waste so it is diverted from landfill, reducing methane emissions and enabling nutrient recovery.

policy_statement: Work with local and state partners to develop a long-term plan for regional materials management and explore recycling market development opportunities.
parent_statement: Adopt and implement Salt Lake City's Zero Waste Resolution, including goals of achieving zero waste by 2040 and diverting/recycling/composting 70% of the waste stream by 2025.
verbatim_text:
## Highest and Best Use

Work with local and state partners to develop a long-term plan for regional materials management and explore recycling market development opportunities.
extraction_rationale: Specified planning and partnership action listed as a key Zero Waste strategy.
mechanism_description: Developing regional materials management plans and recycling markets increases diversion of waste from landfills, reducing methane emissions from landfilled waste.

policy_statement: Require recycling of debris for major construction and renovation projects permitted by the City through the Construction and Demolition Ordinance.
parent_statement: Adopt and implement Salt Lake City's Zero Waste Resolution, including goals of achieving zero waste by 2040 and diverting/recycling/composting 70% of the waste stream by 2025.
verbatim_text: The Construction and Demolition Ordinance requires recycling of debris for major construction and renovation projects permitted by the City.
extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism (ordinance requirement) described as part of the city's zero waste actions.
mechanism_description: A construction and demolition debris recycling requirement diverts material from landfills, reducing methane emissions associated with landfilled waste.

policy_statement: Require multi-family properties, businesses, and special events to implement recycling programs beginning in 2017 through the Business Recycling Ordinance.
parent_statement: Adopt and implement Salt Lake City's Zero Waste Resolution, including goals of achieving zero waste by 2040 and diverting/recycling/composting 70% of the waste stream by 2025.
verbatim_text: The Business Recycling Ordinance requires multi-family properties, businesses and special events to implement recycling programs beginning in 2017.
extraction_rationale: Binding ordinance with a clear implementation start date (beginning in 2017).
mechanism_description: A recycling ordinance increases diversion of waste from landfills, avoiding landfill methane emissions and reducing overall waste-sector GHGs.

policy_statement: Embed Climate Positive solutions in city government operations through a set of internal operational policies including comprehensive energy management, net zero construction, renewable energy development, and a clean vehicle fleet.
verbatim_text: Salt Lake City leads by example by embedding Climate Positive solutions in its own internal government operations. The following are a few key examples:
extraction_rationale: Introduces a grouped set of specific internal operational commitments listed as examples; suitable umbrella/parent.
mechanism_description: Implementing internal government energy, building, renewable procurement, and fleet policies reduces municipal operational energy use and fossil-fuel consumption, lowering GHG emissions.

policy_statement: Implement a Comprehensive Energy Management Executive Order requiring measures such as energy benchmarking, efficiency investments, facility operator training, and employee engagement to reduce energy waste and spur clean energy development.
parent_statement: Embed Climate Positive solutions in city government operations through a set of internal operational policies including comprehensive energy management, net zero construction, renewable energy development, and a clean vehicle fleet.
verbatim_text: The City adopted an Executive Order to enhance interdepartmental coordination, encourage energy waste reduction and spur the development of clean energy. This comprehensive Order requires measures such as energy benchmarking, efficiency investments, facility operator training and employee engagement.
extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism (Executive Order) with specified required measures.
mechanism_description: An executive-order energy management program (benchmarking plus operational improvements and efficiency actions) reduces energy waste in buildings, lowering overall energy use and associated emissions.

policy_statement: Require new Salt Lake City government facilities above 10,000 square feet (with certain exceptions) to be constructed to Net Zero Energy standards and include onsite and/or offsite renewables to offset anticipated annual carbon emissions.
parent_statement: Embed Climate Positive solutions in city government operations through a set of internal operational policies including comprehensive energy management, net zero construction, renewable energy development, and a clean vehicle fleet.
verbatim_text: New Salt Lake City government facilities above 10,000 square feet, with the exception of certain use types, must be constructed to Net Zero Energy standards. These projects must include onsite and/or offsite renewable energy projects that offset the anticipated annual carbon emissions of the facility.
extraction_rationale: Mandatory requirement (“must”) with a clear applicability threshold (>10,000 sq ft) and specified compliance mechanism (renewables to offset emissions).
mechanism_description: Mandating net-zero standards for new public facilities (including renewable energy to offset operations) reduces operational energy demand and associated greenhouse gas emissions from building energy use.

policy_statement: Install renewable energy generation on at least 16 separate government properties by end of 2017, obtain the equivalent of three megawatts of solar via Rocky Mountain Power Subscriber Solar, and achieve 50% renewable electricity for government operations by 2020.
parent_statement: Embed Climate Positive solutions in city government operations through a set of internal operational policies including comprehensive energy management, net zero construction, renewable energy development, and a clean vehicle fleet.
verbatim_text: By the end of 2017, Salt Lake City will have at least 16 separate government properties that include renewable energy generation. The City has also enrolled in the Rocky Mountain Power 'Subscriber Solar' program and will receive the equivalent of three megawatts of solar power through this initiative. Salt Lake City is committed to achieving 50% renewable electricity for its government operations by 2020.
extraction_rationale: Multiple quantified targets with deadlines and a specified procurement mechanism (Subscriber Solar 3 MW equivalent).
mechanism_description: Deploying and procuring solar electricity for government facilities increases renewable power use and displaces fossil-fueled electricity generation, reducing emissions.

policy_statement: Develop and implement Tailpipe Emissions Reduction Plans to guide fleet investments and operations, and expand/maintain a city fleet that included over 130 hybrid-electric or all-electric vehicles (as of end of 2016).
parent_statement: Embed Climate Positive solutions in city government operations through a set of internal operational policies including comprehensive energy management, net zero construction, renewable energy development, and a clean vehicle fleet.
verbatim_text: Numerous departments and divisions at the City have developed Tailpipe Emissions Reduction Plans to inform their investments and operations. Over 130 hybrid-electric or all-electric vehicles were included in the City fleet at the end of 2016.
extraction_rationale: Concrete operational policy approach (formal plans guiding investments) with evidence of implementation scale (over 130 hybrid/all-electric vehicles).
mechanism_description: Electrifying (and hybridizing) the city fleet through guided investments and operations reduces gasoline/diesel use and lowers transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Ensure renewable energy provides 100% of Salt Lake City community electricity needs by 2032.
verbatim_text: Salt Lake City is working to ensure that renewable energy provides 100% of community electricity needs by 2032.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline (100% renewable electricity by 2032).
mechanism_description: Setting a renewable electricity target drives higher renewable generation on the grid, displacing fossil generation and reducing power-sector emissions.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure
policy_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text:
## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure

- ... Priority Action E.1 Implement Green Building Standards and Incentivize Innovative Green Building Design
- ... Priority Action E.2 Improve Low-Income Housing Energy Efficiency
- ... Priority Action E.3 Enhance and Expand Energy Outreach and Education Programs
- ... Priority Action E.4 Retrofit Outdoor Streetlights to LED
- ... Priority Action E.5 Expand Financing Options for Residential Energy Efficiency Retrofits
extraction_rationale: Named action area with multiple specified priority actions listed beneath it; functions as an umbrella program.
mechanism_description: Implementing building standards, retrofit support, and efficiency upgrades (including LED streetlighting) reduces energy demand, which in turn lowers associated greenhouse gas emissions from energy supply.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure
policy_statement: Implement green building standards by 2022 with 100% compliance by 2025, including requiring new municipal buildings and major renovations to meet LEED Silver or Net Zero Energy standards and adopting improved green building/energy codes.
parent_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text:
E.1 Implement Green Building Standards and Incentivize Innovative Green Building Design | Codify and implement green building standards by 2022, with 100% compliance by 2025

Require that all new municipal buildings and major renovations for the City and County meet LEED Silver or Net Zero Energy standards.
extraction_rationale: Contains clear deadlines and compliance target plus specific regulatory/standards actions; listed as Priority Action E.1 under the action area.
mechanism_description: Adopting and enforcing higher-performance green building and energy code standards for new construction and major renovations reduces building energy consumption and thus lowers operational greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure
policy_statement: Expand weatherization/retrofit programs for low-income housing to increase the number of low-income residences served annually by 500% over 10 years and target a 30% reduction in household energy use.
parent_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text: E.2 Improve Low-Income Housing Energy Efficiency | Increase the number of low- income residences served by weatherization and retrofit programs annually by 500% over 10 years, with a 30% reduction in energy usage target per household
extraction_rationale: Quantified scale-up and performance target; listed as Priority Action E.2.
mechanism_description: Expanding residential weatherization and retrofit upgrades reduces household energy demand, lowering associated emissions from building energy use.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure
policy_statement: Enhance and expand energy outreach and education programs to achieve a 10% or greater reduction in average electricity consumption in the residential and commercial sectors.
parent_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.3: Enhance and Expand Energy Outreach and Education Programs

Objective: Attain a 10% or greater reduction in average electricity consumption for residential and commercial sectors
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction target; explicitly framed as a priority action under the action area.
mechanism_description: Expand energy outreach and education to influence user behavior and operational practices, lowering residential and commercial electricity consumption and thereby reducing associated emissions.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure
policy_statement: Replace all existing non-LED streetlights and leased outdoor lighting with LED bulbs by 2030 or sooner, beginning in 2025.
parent_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.4: Retrofit Outdoor Streetlights to LED

Objective: Replace all existing non-LED streetlights and leased outdoor lighting with LED bulbs by 2030 or sooner, starting in 2025
extraction_rationale: Clear infrastructure retrofit commitment with timeline; listed as Priority Action E.4.
mechanism_description: Replacing non-LED outdoor/street lighting with LEDs reduces electricity demand for lighting, cutting emissions from electricity generation.

section_header: ## Priority Action E.5: Expand Financing Options for Residential Energy Efficiency Retrofits
policy_statement: Expand financing options for residential energy efficiency retrofits so that by 2025, 15% of all residences have energy-efficient space heating/cooling equipment and major appliances (including tariffed on-bill financing and advocacy for residential PACE and rebates).
parent_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.5: Expand Financing Options for Residential Energy Efficiency Retrofits

## OBJECTIVE

By 2025, 15% of all residences will have energy-efficient space heating and cooling equipment and major appliances

- Work with MLGW and other partners to develop a tariffed on-bill financing program for residential energy retrofits.
- Advocate for state legislation that allows the implementation of a local Residential Property Assessed Clean Energy (PACE) program.
extraction_rationale: Quantified adoption target with specific policy/program mechanisms; explicitly a priority action.
mechanism_description: Expanding financing options increases adoption of residential efficiency retrofits and equipment upgrades, which lowers building energy use and related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: ## Action Area 2: Transform Our Energy Supply
policy_statement: Transform the community energy supply through priority actions, including decarbonizing the electric grid with renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Action Area 2: Transform Our Energy Supply

- ... Priority Action E.6 Decarbonize the Electric Grid with Renewable Energy
extraction_rationale: Named action area introducing a priority action; serves as an umbrella heading in the plan’s structure.
mechanism_description: Increase renewable electricity supply on the grid to reduce grid emissions intensity and cut power-sector greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action E.6: Decarbonize the Electric Grid with Renewable Energy
policy_statement: Increase the percentage of carbon-free energy in the electricity supply to 80% by 2035 and 100% by 2050, focusing on renewable sources such as solar and wind, including implementing at least one community shared solar project with low-income subsidies by 2022.
parent_statement: Transform the community energy supply through priority actions, including decarbonizing the electric grid with renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.6: Decarbonize the Electric Grid with Renewable Energy

Objective: Increase the percentage of carbon-free energy in our electricity supply to 80% by 2035 and 100% by 2050, focusing on renewable sources such as solar and wind

Implement at least one local community shared solar project that includes subsidies for low-income household participation in the Memphis area by 2022
extraction_rationale: Contains explicit quantified targets and a dated implementation commitment; listed as Priority Action E.6.
mechanism_description: Set and pursue higher renewable/carbon-free electricity supply targets (including shared solar) to increase renewable generation on the grid and thereby reduce fossil-based electricity generation and associated emissions.

section_header: ## Action  Area  3:  Increase  Green  Infrastructure  and Community Resilience
policy_statement: Increase green infrastructure and community resilience through priority actions, including expanding the urban tree canopy and aligning mitigation and adaptation efforts.
verbatim_text:
## Action  Area  3:  Increase  Green  Infrastructure  and Community Resilience

- ... Priority Action E.7 Nurture and Expand the Urban Tree Canopy
- ... Priority Action E.8 Ensure Alignment of Climate Change Mitigation and Adaptation Efforts
extraction_rationale: Named action area with multiple priority actions beneath it; umbrella program.
mechanism_description: Expand urban tree canopy to increase shade and cooling while storing carbon in biomass, improving heat resilience and contributing to emissions mitigation.

section_header: ## Priority Action E.7: Nurture and Expand the Urban Tree Canopy
policy_statement: Increase urban tree canopy coverage countywide to 60% by 2050 through tree planting/replanting and related program and code measures.
parent_statement: Increase green infrastructure and community resilience through priority actions, including expanding the urban tree canopy and aligning mitigation and adaptation efforts.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.7: Nurture and Expand the Urban Tree Canopy

Objective: Plant or replant trees with the intention of increasing urban tree canopy to 60% coverage countywide by 2050
extraction_rationale: Quantified canopy target with deadline; explicitly a priority action under the action area.
mechanism_description: Expand urban tree canopy through planting and related measures to increase carbon sequestration and reduce urban heat impacts.

section_header: ## Priority Action E.8: Align Climate Mitigation and Climate Adaptation Efforts
policy_statement: Identify and prioritize cross-cutting strategies that address climate change mitigation and adaptation, including implementing Mid-South Regional Resilience Plan projects upon completion and adoption and developing neighborhood-scale demonstration projects.
parent_statement: Increase green infrastructure and community resilience through priority actions, including expanding the urban tree canopy and aligning mitigation and adaptation efforts.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.8: Align Climate Mitigation and Climate Adaptation Efforts

Objective: Identify and prioritize cross-cutting strategies for climate change mitigation and adaptation

Implement projects and practices recommended in the Mid-South Regional Resilience Plan upon completion and adoption.
extraction_rationale: Priority action with concrete implementation commitments (implement resilience plan; develop demonstration projects).
mechanism_description: Developing and implementing an integrated resilience planning framework improves coordination and decision-making, increasing institutional capacity to mitigate emissions while adapting to climate risks.

section_header: ## Action  Area  1:  Shift  to  Low-Carbon  Transportation Modes and Reduce Reliance on Motor Vehicles
policy_statement: Shift to low-carbon transportation modes and reduce reliance on motor vehicles through a package of priority actions (land use, complete streets, public transit, and demand management).
verbatim_text:
## Action  Area  1:  Shift  to  Low-Carbon  Transportation Modes and Reduce Reliance on Motor Vehicles

- ... Priority Action T.1 Implement Land Use Patterns that Support Active Transportation and Transit
- ... Priority Action T.2 Create Complete Streets that Prioritize Walking and Bicycling
- ... Priority Action T.3 Enhance Public Transit
- ... Priority Action T.4 Reduce Commute Trips through Transportation Demand Management Strategies
extraction_rationale: Named action area with multiple priority actions listed; umbrella program.
mechanism_description: Implementing land use, street design, transit service, and demand-management measures shifts travel away from private car use toward low-carbon modes, reducing transportation emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action T.1: Implement Land Use Patterns that Support Active Transportation and Transit
policy_statement: Implement land use patterns that support active transportation and transit by implementing the Memphis 3.0 growth strategy and updating zoning/development codes to support walkable infill, mixed-use development and transit-oriented development.
parent_statement: Shift to low-carbon transportation modes and reduce reliance on motor vehicles through a package of priority actions (land use, complete streets, public transit, and demand management).
verbatim_text:
T.1 Implement Land Use Patterns that Support Active Transportation and Transit

Implement the Memphis 3.0 vision and growth strategy focused on building density and mixed uses around community and citywide anchor areas.

Update zoning and development codes to reflect the Memphis 3.0 vision and growth strategy, particularly focusing on regulations that support walkable, infill, mixed-use development and the implementation of Transit Oriented Development (TOD).
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (code updates and implementation of a named comprehensive plan); presented as Priority Action T.1.
mechanism_description: Implementing zoning and land-use reforms that enable walkable mixed-use infill and transit-oriented development reduces car dependence and vehicle miles traveled, lowering transportation emissions and supporting more efficient land use patterns.

section_header: ## Priority Action T.2: Create Complete Streets that Prioritize Walking and Bicycling
policy_statement: Create Complete Streets to convert 10% of vehicle trips to walking/bicycling by 2030 (starting in 2020) and increase to 40% of vehicle trips by 2050, including establishing a dedicated annual funding source of $20 million for pedestrian and bicycle safety improvements.
parent_statement: Shift to low-carbon transportation modes and reduce reliance on motor vehicles through a package of priority actions (land use, complete streets, public transit, and demand management).
verbatim_text:
T.2 Create Complete Streets that Prioritize Walking and Bicycling | Starting in 2020, convert 10% of vehicle trips to bicycle and pedestrian modes by 2030 and then increase this percentage linearly to 40% of vehicle trips by 2050.

Establish a dedicated annual funding source of $20 million for pedestrian and bicycle safety improvements
extraction_rationale: Contains quantified mode-shift targets with dates and a specific funding commitment; listed as Priority Action T.2.
mechanism_description: Investing in Complete Streets and active-transport safety infrastructure shifts trips from car travel to walking and bicycling, reducing vehicle use and transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action T.3: Enhance Public Transit
policy_statement: Enhance public transit by fully implementing the Memphis 3.0 Transit Vision by 2022, increasing ridership/frequency to meet long-range objectives, and converting MATA’s entire fleet to electric by 2050, supported by increasing immediate annual funding by $30 million.
parent_statement: Shift to low-carbon transportation modes and reduce reliance on motor vehicles through a package of priority actions (land use, complete streets, public transit, and demand management).
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action T.3: Enhance Public Transit

Objective: Fully implement the Memphis 3.0 Transit Vision by 2022. Increase ridership and improve frequency to meet long- range (2040/2050) ridership objectives. Convert MATA's entire fleet to electric by 2050.

Provide dedicated annual funding to MATA and increase immediate annual funding by $30 million to implement the Memphis 3.0 Transit Vision.
extraction_rationale: Multiple explicit deadlines/targets plus funding amount; presented as Priority Action T.3.
mechanism_description: Increase and improve public transit service and convert the transit fleet to electric vehicles to reduce transportation fuel combustion and associated greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action T.4: Reduce Commute Trips through Transportation Demand Management
policy_statement: Reduce drive-alone commute trips by 10% by 2022 and 40% by 2050 by expanding Transportation Demand Management programs at major employers and implementing smart parking and related measures.
parent_statement: Shift to low-carbon transportation modes and reduce reliance on motor vehicles through a package of priority actions (land use, complete streets, public transit, and demand management).
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action T.4: Reduce Commute Trips through Transportation Demand Management

## Objective: Reduce drive-alone commute trips 10% by 2022 and 40% by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction targets with dates; clearly a priority action.
mechanism_description: Expand employer-based TDM and parking management to reduce single-occupancy vehicle commuting, lowering driving activity and transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: ## Action Area 2: Set the Stage for Vehicle Electrification
policy_statement: Set the stage for vehicle electrification through a priority action to encourage electric vehicle adoption and develop charging infrastructure.
verbatim_text:
## Action Area 2: Set the Stage for Vehicle Electrification

- ... Priority Action T.5 Encourage Electric Vehicle Adoption and the Development of Charging Infrastructure
extraction_rationale: Named action area introducing a priority action; used as parent for hierarchy.
mechanism_description: Deploying EV charging infrastructure enables greater electric vehicle adoption, supporting a shift away from internal combustion vehicles and reducing transportation-related emissions.

section_header: ## PRIORITY ACTION  T.5 Encourage Electric Vehicle Adoption and the Development of Charging Infrastructure
policy_statement: Increase passenger EV travel to 5% by 2025, 30% by 2035, and 50% by 2050; and increase freight EV travel to 3% by 2025, 20% by 2035, and 50% by 2050, including developing a comprehensive community-wide EV strategy and committing to transition local government fleets to electric with charging infrastructure.
parent_statement: Set the stage for vehicle electrification through a priority action to encourage electric vehicle adoption and develop charging infrastructure.
verbatim_text:
## PRIORITY ACTION

T.5 Encourage Electric Vehicle Adoption and the Development of Charging Infrastructure

## OBJECTIVE

Increase passenger vehicle travel using electric vehicles to 5% by 2025; 30% by 2035; and 50% by 2050.

Increase freight vehicle travel using electric vehicles to 3% by 2025; 20% by 2035; and 50% by 2050.

To lead by example, Memphis and Shelby County government should commit to transitioning their fleets to electric and begin building necessary charging infrastructure.
extraction_rationale: Contains explicit quantified adoption targets with timelines and specific implementation actions; presented as Priority Action T.5.
mechanism_description: Increase adoption and use of electric passenger and freight vehicles (including electrifying public fleets and enabling charging) to displace gasoline/diesel vehicle travel and reduce transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Reduce Waste and Move Toward a ZeroWaste Future
policy_statement: Reduce waste and move toward a zero-waste future through priority actions focused on organic waste diversion, overall waste reduction, and inorganic materials diversion.
verbatim_text:
## Action Area 1: Reduce Waste and Move Toward a ZeroWaste Future

- ... Priority Action W.1 Divert Greater Amounts of Organic Waste from Landfills
- ... Priority Action W.2 Reduce the Overall Amount of Waste Generated
- ... Priority Action W.3 Divert Greater Amounts of Inorganic Materials from Landfills
extraction_rationale: Named action area with multiple priority actions listed; umbrella program.
mechanism_description: Reduce total waste and divert organic and inorganic materials from landfill to avoid landfill methane emissions and associated climate impacts.

section_header: ## Priority Action W.1 Divert Greater Amounts of Organic Waste from Landfills
policy_statement: By 2035, cut the portion of yard and wood waste in municipal solid waste destined for landfills in half relative to 2016, including implementing municipal curbside composting for yard waste and advocating for updated state landfill regulations on organic waste.
parent_statement: Reduce waste and move toward a zero-waste future through priority actions focused on organic waste diversion, overall waste reduction, and inorganic materials diversion.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action W.1 Divert Greater Amounts of Organic Waste from Landfills

Objective: By 2035, cut the portion of yard and wood waste in municipal solid waste destined for landfills  in half relative to 2016.

Implement a municipal curbside composting program for yard waste that includes fee changes to incentivize composting.

Advocate for updated state-level landfill regulations that discourage and/or phase out disposal of organic waste in landfills.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline and specific program/policy actions; listed as Priority Action W.1.
mechanism_description: Implementing and incentivizing curbside composting (and discouraging landfill disposal of organics) diverts organic waste from landfills, avoiding methane generation and enabling nutrient recovery through compost use.

section_header: ## Priority Action W.2 Reduce the Overall Amount of Waste Generated
policy_statement: By 2030, reduce landfill disposal from commercial/institutional/industrial sectors by achieving a 20% reduction in the share of paper/cardboard and a 10% reduction in the share of food waste in the municipal solid waste stream, including considering regulations requiring private waste haulers to offer recycling and food-waste composting services.
parent_statement: Reduce waste and move toward a zero-waste future through priority actions focused on organic waste diversion, overall waste reduction, and inorganic materials diversion.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action W.2 Reduce the Overall Amount of Waste Generated

Objective: By 2030, achieve a 20% reduction in the share of paper and cardboard in the municipal solid waste stream from commercial, institutional, and industrial (cii) sectors sent to landfills. By 2030, achieve a 10% reduction in the share of food waste in the municipal solid waste stream sent to landfills from cii sectors.

Consider new regulations that require private waste hauling companies to offer recycling and food waste composting services.
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified waste reduction targets with date and a regulatory measure; listed as Priority Action W.2.
mechanism_description: Require/provide recycling and food-waste composting services to divert material from landfill, thereby avoiding landfill methane emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action W.3 Divert Greater Amounts of Inorganic Materials from Landfills
policy_statement: Increase diversion of inorganic materials (construction and demolition waste and plastics) from municipal solid waste landfills, including revising the building code so permitting requires reduction, reuse, and recycling of materials and advocating for state policies on single-use plastics and recycling/reuse incentives.
parent_statement: Reduce waste and move toward a zero-waste future through priority actions focused on organic waste diversion, overall waste reduction, and inorganic materials diversion.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action W.3 Divert Greater Amounts of Inorganic Materials from Landfills

Objective: Increase the diversion rate of inorganic materials, namely construction and demolition waste as well as plastics, from disposal in municipal solid waste landfills

Reduce construction and demolition waste by revising the building code to require the reduction, reuse, and recycling of materials.
extraction_rationale: Specific regulatory change (building code revision) and defined objective; presented as Priority Action W.3.
mechanism_description: Require and incentivize reduction, reuse, and recycling of inorganic materials so less waste is landfilled, thereby avoiding landfill-related methane emissions.

extraction_rationale: Named action area with multiple priority actions listed; umbrella program.
mechanism_description: Improve tire collection and waste education to shift behavior toward proper disposal/diversion, reducing waste sent to landfill and avoiding associated methane emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action W.4 Improve Tire Management and Collection Practices
policy_statement: Increase the use of recycled tire materials in building projects and reduce improper tire disposal through targeted programs, including reworking pickup practices, incentivizing recycling, and considering more stringent tracking requirements for tire handling/disposal companies.
parent_statement: Promote a cultural shift in the community’s approach to waste through priority actions on tire management/collection and waste education/outreach.

section_header: Priority Action W.6 Enhance Methane Recovery and Landfill Gas Destruction
policy_statement: Priority Action W.6 will enhance methane recovery and landfill gas destruction by extending biogas controls to Class III/IV landfills, improving Class I landfill biogas capture from 75% to 85% by 2020, and installing methane recovery systems in all Shelby County wastewater treatment plants by 2035 while promoting methane recovery over flaring.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action W.6 Enhance Methane Recovery and Landfill Gas Destruction

Objective: Extend the use of biogas controls to Class III and Class IV landfills. Improve the biogas capture rate at Class I landfills from 75% to 85% by 2020, and promote greater methane recovery over biogas flaring.
extraction_rationale: This is a named priority action with explicit targets (85% capture by 2020; controls/installation timelines) and multiple implementation steps listed below it, supporting classification as a parent policy.
mechanism_description: Expand and improve biogas capture and methane recovery/destruction at landfills and wastewater treatment plants to prevent methane from escaping to the atmosphere, reducing greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Starting in 2020, DPD staff will assess Class I landfill biogas capture performance versus the 75% national average and, if feasible, launch an implementation and monitoring plan to achieve an 85% landfill biogas capture rate by 2020.
parent_statement: Priority Action W.6 will enhance methane recovery and landfill gas destruction by extending biogas controls to Class III/IV landfills, improving Class I landfill biogas capture from 75% to 85% by 2020, and installing methane recovery systems in all Shelby County wastewater treatment plants by 2035 while promoting methane recovery over flaring.
verbatim_text: Starting in 2020, Division of Planning and Development (DPD) staff aim to inquire with Class I landfill operator(s) how their biogas capture rate compares with the national average rate of 75%. Provided that incremental capture rates are feasible, DPD staff will coordinate the conversations to launch an implementation and monitoring plan to achieve a landfill biogas capture rate of 85% by 2020.
extraction_rationale: Concrete implementation commitment with a start date (2020) and a quantified target (85% capture rate by 2020) tied to the W.6 action.
mechanism_description: Improve landfill biogas capture through an implementation and monitoring plan, thereby reducing fugitive methane emissions from landfill operations.

policy_statement: Starting in 2020, City of Memphis Public Works and DPD will initiate discussions to install biogas controls at Class III and IV landfills, targeting completion by 2035.
parent_statement: Priority Action W.6 will enhance methane recovery and landfill gas destruction by extending biogas controls to Class III/IV landfills, improving Class I landfill biogas capture from 75% to 85% by 2020, and installing methane recovery systems in all Shelby County wastewater treatment plants by 2035 while promoting methane recovery over flaring.
verbatim_text: Starting in 2020, City of Memphis Public Works and DPD staff will initiate the conversations surrounding installation of controls at Class III and IV landfills with targeted completion by 2035.
extraction_rationale: Specific action (installation of controls) with responsible parties and a target completion year (2035), presented as an implementation step under W.6.
mechanism_description: Install landfill gas/biogas controls to capture or manage methane from landfills, reducing fugitive methane emissions.

policy_statement: The City/partners will implement and support new legislation to lower the threshold for mandatory installation of biogas controls.
parent_statement: Priority Action W.6 will enhance methane recovery and landfill gas destruction by extending biogas controls to Class III/IV landfills, improving Class I landfill biogas capture from 75% to 85% by 2020, and installing methane recovery systems in all Shelby County wastewater treatment plants by 2035 while promoting methane recovery over flaring.
verbatim_text: Implement and support new legislation that lowers the threshold for mandatory installation of biogas controls, which can be used as a complementary implementation mechanism.
extraction_rationale: Explicit legislative/regulatory mechanism is a binding policy instrument and is listed as an implementation step under W.6.
mechanism_description: Lowering the mandate threshold increases installation of biogas control systems, which capture and manage biogas (methane) that would otherwise be released, thereby reducing methane emissions.

policy_statement: WWTP operators will plan to install methane recovery systems in all Shelby County facilities by the end of 2035, with feasibility evaluation beginning in 2020 and an implementation and monitoring plan launched if feasible.
parent_statement: Priority Action W.6 will enhance methane recovery and landfill gas destruction by extending biogas controls to Class III/IV landfills, improving Class I landfill biogas capture from 75% to 85% by 2020, and installing methane recovery systems in all Shelby County wastewater treatment plants by 2035 while promoting methane recovery over flaring.
verbatim_text: Wastewater treatment plant (WWTP) operators can plan to install methane recovery systems in all facilities within Shelby County by the end of 2035 with support from DPD and City government personnel. Starting in 2020, staff can evaluate whether methane recovery or biogas flaring is feasible at uncontrolled WWTPs. Provided that installation of controls is feasible, launch an implementation and monitoring plan to achieve this goal.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure deployment commitment with a deadline (end of 2035) and near-term action (starting in 2020 feasibility evaluation), framed as implementation under W.6.
mechanism_description: Install methane recovery/biogas control systems at wastewater treatment plants to capture or destroy methane (and potentially use it for energy), reducing fugitive methane emissions and displacing fossil energy use.

policy_statement: Adopt local government operations (City of Memphis and Shelby County) GHG reduction targets of 20% (buildings) and 15% (fleet) by 2020; 55% (buildings) and 45% (fleet) by 2035; and 80% for both buildings and fleet by 2050.
verbatim_text:

| 2020 | 20% | 15% |
| 2035 | 55% | 45% |
| 2050 | 80% | 80% |

mechanism_description: Adopting formal GHG reduction targets for government buildings and fleet commits the jurisdiction to a declining emissions trajectory that drives subsequent actions to cut operational energy and fuel-related emissions.

mechanism_description: Convert residual waste and wastewater organics into usable energy (via waste-to-energy and cogeneration) and improve recycling to reduce landfilled waste, thereby avoiding landfill methane and displacing fossil energy with recovered energy.

policy_statement: Generate 48 GWh/year of electricity from cogeneration at wastewater plants by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy through cogeneration, waste-to-energy conversion, and improved recycling/contamination performance.
verbatim_text: - 48 GWh/year of electricity from cogeneration at wastewater plants by 2030
extraction_rationale: Quantified energy generation target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Install cogeneration at wastewater plants to use captured biogas for electricity, reducing methane emissions and offsetting fossil electricity generation.

policy_statement: Convert 50% of non-recycled garbage to energy by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy through cogeneration, waste-to-energy conversion, and improved recycling/contamination performance.
verbatim_text: - 50% of non-recycled garbage converted to energy by 2030
extraction_rationale: Quantified waste-to-energy conversion target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Convert non-recycled solid waste into energy instead of landfilling, reducing methane emissions and generating energy that can offset fossil fuel use.

policy_statement: Increase recycling rates by 10% and cut non-recyclables contamination in half.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy through cogeneration, waste-to-energy conversion, and improved recycling/contamination performance.
verbatim_text: - 10% increase in recycling rates and cut non-recyclables 'contamination' in half
extraction_rationale: Quantified recycling/contamination targets under Approach 6.
mechanism_description: Improving recycling performance and reducing contamination increases diversion of waste from landfill, thereby avoiding methane emissions from landfilled waste.

policy_statement: Build a new, more efficient electricity-from-waste facility with onsite solar designed to charge electric vehicles in the County fleet.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy through cogeneration, waste-to-energy conversion, and improved recycling/contamination performance.
verbatim_text: -   Build a new, more efficient electricity-from-waste facility with onsite solar, designed to charge electric vehicles in the County fleet
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure investment commitment stated as an action.
mechanism_description: Convert non-recycled waste into usable electricity (augmented by onsite solar for EV fleet charging), reducing landfill methane emissions and recovering energy that can displace fossil-based generation.

policy_statement: Upgrade cogeneration at wastewater treatment plants to produce 9.6 MW of electricity from biogas.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy through cogeneration, waste-to-energy conversion, and improved recycling/contamination performance.
verbatim_text: -   Upgrade cogeneration at wastewater treatment plants to produce 9.6 megawatts of electricity from biogas
extraction_rationale: Concrete technology upgrade action with quantified capacity.
mechanism_description: Upgrading wastewater-plant cogeneration uses biogas to produce electricity, capturing methane and displacing fossil-based power generation.

policy_statement: Upgrade landfill gas collection at the South Dade Landfill.
parent_statement: Approach 6: Convert waste to energy through cogeneration, waste-to-energy conversion, and improved recycling/contamination performance.
verbatim_text: -   Upgrade landfill gas collection at the South Dade Landfill
extraction_rationale: Specific operational/infrastructure upgrade action under Approach 6.
mechanism_description: Upgrading landfill gas collection increases methane capture from decomposing waste, reducing fugitive landfill methane emissions.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use to cut emissions through per-capita landfill and water consumption reductions.
verbatim_text:
## APPROACH 7:

## REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE

- Reduce landfill waste per person 50% by 2030
- Reduce water consumption per person 30% by 2030
extraction_rationale: Named approach with multiple quantified per-capita reduction targets listed beneath.
mechanism_description: Per-capita targets to reduce landfill waste and water consumption lower landfill methane generation and reduce energy-related emissions from water pumping, treatment, and distribution.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Reduce landfill waste per person by 50% by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use to cut emissions through per-capita landfill and water consumption reductions.
verbatim_text: - Reduce landfill waste per person 50% by 2030
extraction_rationale: Quantified per-capita landfill waste reduction target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Reducing the amount of waste landfilled per person increases diversion from landfill, which avoids methane emissions generated by decomposing waste in landfills.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Reduce water consumption per person by 30% by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use to cut emissions through per-capita landfill and water consumption reductions.
verbatim_text: - Reduce water consumption per person 30% by 2030
extraction_rationale: Quantified per-capita water use reduction target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Setting and pursuing per-person water reduction targets drives water efficiency actions that lower per-capita water consumption.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Create a community-wide food rescue plan in collaboration with community-based organizations, businesses, and farmers.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use to cut emissions through per-capita landfill and water consumption reductions.
verbatim_text: -   Create a community-wide food rescue plan in collaboration with community-based organizations, businesses, and farmers
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (food rescue plan) stated as a county action.
mechanism_description: Implementing a food rescue program diverts edible food from disposal, reducing landfill-bound organic waste and avoiding methane emissions.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Require waste reduction plans for all large events.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use to cut emissions through per-capita landfill and water consumption reductions.
verbatim_text: -   Require waste reduction plans for all large events
extraction_rationale: Explicit requirement (policy measure) to reduce waste at large events.
mechanism_description: Mandating waste reduction planning for large events reduces and diverts waste from landfills, which lowers methane emissions from landfill decomposition.

section_header: MIAMI-DADE COUNTY EMISSIONS, ELECTRICITY, AND FUEL REDUCTION GOALS IN THE COMPREHENSIVE MASTER DEVELOPMENT PLAN (CDMP)
policy_statement: Meet Comprehensive Development Master Plan (CDMP) goal to reduce electricity usage for County facilities by 20% from the 2009 baseline by 2025.
verbatim_text: Reduce electricity usage for County facilities by 20% from the baseline year of 2009 by 2025.
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction target with baseline year and deadline, stated as a CDMP goal.
mechanism_description: Set and pursue an energy-use reduction target for public facilities, resulting in lower building electricity consumption and associated emissions.

section_header: MIAMI-DADE COUNTY EMISSIONS, ELECTRICITY, AND FUEL REDUCTION GOALS IN THE COMPREHENSIVE MASTER DEVELOPMENT PLAN (CDMP)
policy_statement: Meet CDMP goal to reduce gasoline consumption in County operations by 30% and diesel consumption by 70% from the 2016 baseline by 2028, moving toward conversion of the County fleet to electric vehicles.
verbatim_text: Reduce the consumption of gasoline in County operations by 30% and the consumption of diesel fuel in County operations by 70% from the baseline year of 2016 by 2028 and further move toward conversion of the County's fleet to electric vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Quantified fuel reduction targets with baseline year and deadline; includes fleet electrification direction.
mechanism_description: Electrifying the county vehicle fleet reduces gasoline and diesel combustion, lowering transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: MIAMI-DADE COUNTY EMISSIONS, ELECTRICITY, AND FUEL REDUCTION GOALS IN THE COMPREHENSIVE MASTER DEVELOPMENT PLAN (CDMP)
policy_statement: Meet CDMP goal to achieve an 80% community-wide emissions reduction by 2050 from the 2008 baseline.
verbatim_text: Achieve 80% community-wide emissions reduction by 2050 from baseline year of 2008.
extraction_rationale: Explicit long-term emissions reduction target with baseline year and deadline.
mechanism_description: Sets a community-wide GHG reduction target that drives planning and actions across multiple sectors to reduce overall emissions.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing

## Components of the Strategy

- Integrate energy requirements and resources into affordable housing deals...
- Integrate energy performance into underwriting...
- Expand access to financing...
- Expand energy savings performance contracting for public sector buildings...
- Create a revolving loan fund...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple specific bullet actions beneath it, suitable as a parent policy.
mechanism_description: Provide low-cost public/private financing and performance-contracting tools to enable building energy upgrades, resulting in reduced building energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: Require that by no later than 2023, all new construction projects receiving HCR Tax Credit funding be high-performance and all-electric buildings per HCR Sustainability Guidelines for New Construction, and streamline access to incentives through housing agencies for regulated affordable housing decarbonization.
parent_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Integrate energy requirements and resources into affordable housing deals: ... For example, by no later than 2023, all new construction projects that receive Tax Credit funding through HCR will be required to be high-performance and all-electric buildings, as required in HCR's Sustainability Guidelines for New Construction. The State should also continue to streamline access to all incentives and resources for regulated affordable housing building decarbonization to go through the housing agencies...
extraction_rationale: Quantified deadline (no later than 2023) and explicit requirement; clearly a bullet under B5.
mechanism_description: Requiring funded new affordable housing to be built as high-performance all-electric buildings (and easing access to incentives) reduces on-site fossil fuel use and overall building energy demand, cutting operational greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: Provide support for lenders to underwrite to energy performance standards; consider updating HCR underwriting standards to account for reduced operational costs from high-performance building standards informed by data from existing projects.
parent_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Integrate energy performance into underwriting: The State should provide support for lenders to underwrite to energy performance standards and applicable regulatory requirements. HCR should consider updating its underwriting standards... to take into account potential reduced operational costs stemming from the new high performance buildings standards.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (underwriting support/updates) under B5.
mechanism_description: Updating and supporting lender underwriting to account for energy performance lowers financing barriers for high-performance buildings, increasing their adoption and reducing building energy consumption and associated emissions.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: Provide greater access to low-cost financing products for upgrades (including for low-income homeowners and buildings in Disadvantaged Communities), including financing for non-energy improvements needed for code compliance and electrification; explore low-interest financing coupled with credit enhancement/insurance; prioritize CDFIs and credit unions.
parent_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Expand access to financing: The State should provide greater access to low-cost financing products for upgrades, including for low-income homeowners and buildings located in Disadvantaged Communities. This financing also should be available for non-energy improvements needed for code compliance and to enable electrification... The State should also explore new mechanisms... coupled with credit enhancement or insurance. The State should prioritize support for financing products made available by community development financial institutions and credit unions...
extraction_rationale: Concrete expansion of financing access with target groups and mechanisms; listed under B5.
mechanism_description: Providing low-cost financing and credit support makes it easier for households and building owners to undertake upgrades and electrification, increasing retrofit adoption and reducing building energy use and related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: Enact enabling legislation to expand energy savings performance contracting for State and municipal buildings, schools, and other public facilities, including expanding eligible measures and payback terms and allowing retention of some cost savings.
parent_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Expand energy savings performance contracting for public sector buildings: The State should enact enabling legislation to expand the use of energy savings performance contracting to support implementation of emissions reduction upgrades in State and municipal buildings, P-12 schools, and other public facilities... Changes to existing statute should expand the eligible list of measures... expand the allowable payback term... allow... deferred maintenance needs, and allow a State agency or authority to request to keep a portion of cost savings...
extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism via proposed legislation; detailed statutory changes.
mechanism_description: Enabling legislation expands performance contracting so public facilities can finance and implement efficiency/emissions-reduction upgrades, lowering building energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: Create a revolving loan fund for building decarbonization and reuse of buildings and building materials, implemented through a bond-issuing government authority.
parent_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Create a revolving loan fund: The State should create a revolving loan fund for building decarbonization and the reuse of buildings and building materials... This would be implemented through a bond-issuing government authority.
extraction_rationale: Specific financial mechanism and implementation approach; bullet under B5.
mechanism_description: A bond-backed revolving loan fund lowers financing barriers for building decarbonization and reuse of buildings/materials, increasing project uptake and reducing both operational energy emissions and embodied construction emissions.

section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
policy_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks

## Components of the Strategy

- Workforce training for gas sector workers...
- Develop appropriate regulations and permit fees...
- Streamline access to public and utility rights of way...
- Support public-private partnerships...
- Prioritize public sector support...
- Explore thermal energy networks as part of the gas system transition...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple listed component actions; appropriate parent.
mechanism_description: Enable and scale district thermal energy networks to replace or reduce fossil-gas heating in buildings, lowering heating-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
policy_statement: Provide workforce training and transition support for gas sector workers to install/operate thermal energy networks, coordinating PSC/DPS, DOL, Office of Just Transition, utilities and unions.
parent_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Workforce training for gas sector workers to operate thermal energy networks: ... The PSC and DPS, DOL, the Office of Just Transition, and other State agencies should work together with utilities and unions... to help connect trained gas workers with new thermal energy network projects, on training and skills development...
extraction_rationale: Specific training/transition action under B6.
mechanism_description: Training and transition support grows the skilled labor pipeline for thermal energy networks, enabling their buildout and operation and thereby facilitating lower-emissions building heating.

section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
policy_statement: Develop geothermal well regulations and revise statutory financial security and permit fee provisions to reduce burden for geothermal wells >500 feet, including allowing DEC to set requirements/fees in regulation and developing geothermal/stratigraphic well regulations.
parent_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
- Develop appropriate regulations and permit fees for geothermal wells greater than 500 feet deep: ... The following actions should be taken... 
- DEC should develop regulations for geothermal and stratigraphic wells...
- Remove the dollar amounts from ECL §§ 23-0305... and allow DEC to establish financial security requirements in regulation...
- Remove the fixed dollar amount in ECL § 23-1903... and allow DEC to develop an appropriate permit fee schedule in regulation.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory/statutory change actions are explicitly enumerated.
mechanism_description: Streamlining and updating permitting, fees, and financial assurance rules for deep geothermal wells lowers barriers to geothermal projects, enabling geothermal energy/heat deployment that can substitute for fossil energy and reduce emissions.

section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
policy_statement: Develop clear processes/rules for thermal energy networks to access public and utility rights-of-way and establish default compensation levels for affected property owners; provide transparent access to heat sources/sinks.
parent_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Streamline access to public and utility rights of way as well as heat sources/sinks: The State, in partnership with local governments, should develop clear processes and rules for thermal energy network projects to access already established public and utility rights of way. In addition, the State should establish appropriate default compensation levels intended to compensate property owners in these rights of way... State and local governments also should provide simple and transparent access to heat sources and sinks...
extraction_rationale: Specific administrative rules/compensation commitments under B6.
mechanism_description: Streamlined rights-of-way access and transparent rules enable thermal energy network buildout and use of accessible heat sources/sinks, displacing fossil-based heating and reducing building emissions.

section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
policy_statement: Emphasize public sector support for thermal energy networks serving LMI/affordable/public housing and Disadvantaged Communities, including requiring at least one pilot per utility territory be located in a Disadvantaged Community per the Utility Thermal Energy Networks and Jobs Act.
parent_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Prioritize public sector support for thermal energy networks that serve LMI housing and buildings in Disadvantaged Communities: ... The Utility Thermal Energy Networks and Jobs Act... requir[es] that at least one thermal energy network pilot project per utility service territory must be located in a Disadvantaged Community.
extraction_rationale: Contains an explicit requirement (at least one pilot per territory in a DAC) tied to statute.
mechanism_description: Supporting and requiring pilot deployment of district thermal energy networks shifts building heating/cooling to shared, more efficient and potentially low-carbon thermal supply, reducing fossil fuel use and associated emissions.

policy_statement: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## B8. Invest in Workforce Development

## Components of the Strategy

- Expand training...
- Curricula and career services...
- Prioritize Disadvantaged Communities and other priority populations...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component bullets; extract as parent.
mechanism_description: Investing in buildings-related workforce training increases skilled labor availability, enabling more building efficiency and electrification projects that reduce energy use and emissions.

policy_statement: Increase training investments for incumbent and new clean energy workers via training infrastructure, career pathways, apprenticeships, on-the-job training, and industry partnerships in specified priority areas.
parent_statement: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Expand training: Training for incumbent and new clean energy workers and adjacent industries needs to be increased dramatically, through investments in training infrastructure/delivery, career pathways, direct-entry Pre-apprenticeship and Registered Apprenticeship programs, on-the-job-training, and industry partnerships.
extraction_rationale: Specific workforce intervention and delivery mechanisms listed; sub-bullet under B8.
mechanism_description: Expanded training and apprenticeship pathways grow the skilled clean-energy workforce, enabling faster deployment of clean energy projects and associated emissions reductions.

policy_statement: Encourage building decarbonization curricula and career services in State-funded and private education, including P-12, technical schools, apprenticeships, and university programs.
parent_statement: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Curricula and career services: The State should encourage building decarbonization curricula and career services in State-funded and private education including P-12, technical schools, apprenticeships, and engineering and architecture programs at universities.
extraction_rationale: Explicit education-system action under B8.
mechanism_description: Expand building-decarbonization education and career pathways to grow the skilled workforce needed to implement low-carbon building upgrades, thereby enabling building emissions reductions.

policy_statement: Prioritize Disadvantaged Communities and low-income residents for training and job placement via community-to-employment pipelines, wrap-around services, and labor standards mechanisms (pre-apprenticeships, apprenticeships, CBAs, OJT funding); increase MWBE/SDVOB/cooperative participation via funding and certification assistance.
parent_statement: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Prioritize Disadvantaged Communities and other priority populations: The State should prioritize Disadvantaged Communities and low-income residents for training and job placement by creating community-to-employment pipelines... pared with wrap-around services... The State should increase ranks of MWBEs... SDVOBs, and worker cooperatives through increased funding for workforce training, business development support, and certification assistance...
extraction_rationale: Targeting plus specific mechanisms and funding commitments; sub-bullet under B8.
mechanism_description: Create prioritized training-to-employment pipelines and business support for disadvantaged groups to expand and diversify the workforce and supplier base needed to implement climate actions.

section_header: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction
policy_statement: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction

## Components of the Strategy

- Lead by example in State projects...
- Make embodied carbon transparent...
- Follow lower-carbon specifications...
- Set reduction targets for projects...
- Incorporate embodied carbon budgets into permitting...
- Encourage building reuse...
- Support RD&D...
- Expand in-state manufacturing for products...
- Incorporate embodied carbon specifications into incentive programs...
- Leverage federal efforts and policy development...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with extensive bullet list; parent appropriate.
mechanism_description: Require/enable measurement, standards, and procurement/permitting practices that favor lower-carbon materials and reuse, thereby reducing embodied emissions from building construction.

section_header: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction
policy_statement: Require Environmental Product Declarations for structural materials (where available) and require embodied carbon modeling/tools and standardized methodologies/database adoption via GreenNY Council.
parent_statement: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Make embodied carbon transparent: In design specifications, the State should require Environmental Product Declarations for structural building materials where available and require the use of available modeling software and design tools for calculation of the project's embodied carbon budget. The State, through the GreenNY Council, should adopt methodologies for calculation of carbon reduction...
extraction_rationale: Concrete procurement/design requirements; sub-bullet under B11.
mechanism_description: Require EPDs and standardized embodied-carbon modeling to make material impacts visible and drive procurement/design toward lower-embodied-carbon structural materials, reducing construction-related emissions.

section_header: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction
policy_statement: Set embodied carbon reduction targets for projects below the established mean embodied carbon budget after establishing baselines.
parent_statement: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Set reduction targets for projects: Subsequently, the State should set a target embodied carbon reduction level for projects that is below the established mean embodied carbon budget, as illustrated over the previous years.
extraction_rationale: Explicit target-setting action under B11.
mechanism_description: Setting embodied-carbon reduction targets for projects drives lower-carbon material and design choices, reducing construction-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction
policy_statement: Require embodied carbon budgets be submitted with permits for commercial/institutional new construction: immediately for State entities and no later than 2025 for local government entities; provide funding for training/resources for designers and permitting entities.
parent_statement: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Incorporate embodied carbon budgets into permitting: The State should require an embodied carbon budget to be submitted as part of the permit process for all commercial and institutional new construction... immediately for State entities and no later than 2025 for local government entities. The State should provide funding for training and resources...
extraction_rationale: Contains a deadline (no later than 2025) and specific permitting requirement plus funding.
mechanism_description: Requiring embodied carbon budgets at permitting drives lower-carbon material and design choices in new construction, reducing embodied (upstream) greenhouse gas emissions from building construction.

section_header: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use
policy_statement: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use

## Components of the Strategy

- Provide education and training...
- Update regulations, codes, and standards...
- Support the HFC phase-out in food stores...
- Phase out high-GWP HFCs...
- Research health effects and environmental impacts...
- Support RD&D...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple distinct policy components; parent appropriate. (Not duplicating earlier general HFC transition policy; these are additional specific actions.)
mechanism_description: Phase out and better manage high-GWP HFC refrigerants (through codes, training, and support) to reduce refrigerant leakage and other fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use
policy_statement: Promulgate regulations for proper disposal of HFCs in existing equipment and support those regulations with training on handling, maintenance, and disposal protocols.
parent_statement: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use (strategy framework).
