verbatim_text: - Provide education and training: ... DEC should promulgate regulations regarding proper disposal of HFCs already in use in existing equipment and such regulations should be supported by training installers and contractors on handling, equipment maintenance, and disposal protocols.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory action plus supporting training; concrete and enforceable.
mechanism_description: Mandating and training for proper handling and end-of-life disposal of HFC refrigerants reduces refrigerant leakage/venting, cutting fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use
policy_statement: Update relevant codes (including mechanical code) to allow low-GWP alternatives and promulgate DEC regulations requiring refrigerant reclamation/destruction at end-of-life with verification/reporting and leak detection for certain commercial refrigeration; provide economic support for transition (e.g., incentives for equipment or compensation for reclamation).
parent_statement: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Update regulations, codes, and standards: As soon as possible, the State should update relevant codes, including the mechanical code, to allow the use of low-GWP alternatives... DEC should promulgate regulations requiring reclamation or destruction of refrigerants from appliances at end-of-life, with verification and reporting, and require leak detection for certain commercial refrigeration... the State should provide economic support (such as... incentives... or compensation...)
extraction_rationale: Multiple concrete code/regulatory requirements plus support mechanisms.
mechanism_description: Allowing low-GWP refrigerant alternatives and requiring leak detection and end-of-life refrigerant reclamation/destruction reduces fugitive high-GWP refrigerant emissions from refrigeration equipment.

section_header: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use
policy_statement: Develop incentives (utility rebates and grant programs) to support adoption of natural refrigerants in food stores, prioritizing substantial funding for equipment installation in Disadvantaged Communities and for independent/small chains.
parent_statement: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Support the HFC phase-out in food stores: ... NYSERDA, DPS, and DEC should coordinate to develop incentives such as utility rebates and grant programs to support the adoption of natural refrigerants in food stores. Incentives are particularly needed to fund a substantial portion of the installation of new equipment in existing stores in Disadvantaged Communities or stores operated by independent companies or small chains...
extraction_rationale: Specific incentive instruments and targeting; actionable coordination directive.
mechanism_description: Provide rebates and grants for food stores to install equipment using natural refrigerants, which lowers high‑GWP refrigerant leakage and thus reduces fugitive GHG emissions.

section_header: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use
policy_statement: Expand 6 NYCRR Part 494 by establishing a GWP threshold that decreases over time and addressing leakage in existing equipment; align state policy with anticipated EPA measures and other U.S. Climate Alliance states.
parent_statement: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Phase out high-GWP HFCs: DEC should expand the scope of 6 NYCRR Part 494... including through the establishment of a GWP threshold that decreases over time... and addressing leakage in existing equipment... DEC should align New York policy with anticipated federal (EPA) policy measures... as well as with other U.S. Climate Alliance states...
extraction_rationale: Explicit regulatory expansion direction with defined mechanism (declining GWP threshold).
mechanism_description: Tightening HFC rules and reducing refrigerant leaks lowers releases of high‑GWP gases from equipment, cutting fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities
policy_statement: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities

## Components of the Strategy

- Assessment and determination of emissions reduction targets...
- Promulgation of emissions regulations...
- Regular and transparent resource planning...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component bullets; parent appropriate.
mechanism_description: Retiring or repurposing fossil fuel–fired power facilities reduces fossil electricity generation, thereby cutting power-sector greenhouse gas emissions through cleaner replacement supply.

section_header: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities
policy_statement: Determine potential GHG and co-pollutant reductions from fossil fuel generation by 2030 and set interim emissions reduction targets/timeline, with stakeholder input and biennial evaluation every two years starting in 2024.
parent_statement: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Assessment and determination of emissions reduction targets: The PSC, DEC, NYSERDA, and the New York State Energy Planning Board should work in coordination to determine the potential for GHG emission and co-pollutant reductions from fossil fuel generation by 2030 and set a corresponding timeline for interim emissions reduction targets... The effectiveness... shall be evaluated every two years starting in 2024 and adjusted accordingly...
extraction_rationale: Time-bound target-setting process and periodic evaluation requirement; explicit agencies.
mechanism_description: Set and periodically update interim emissions-reduction targets for the power sector to drive reduced fossil electricity generation and corresponding GHG and co-pollutant emissions.

section_header: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities
policy_statement: Assess and promulgate emissions regulations/options to reduce emissions from fossil fuel-fired generating units to the maximum extent practicable while maintaining reliability, with three-year effectiveness evaluations.
parent_statement: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
- Promulgation of emissions regulations: DEC should assess regulatory options to reduce emissions from fossil fuel-fired generating units to the maximum extent practicable... Once completed, DEC should follow SAPA in promulgating the identified regulation(s).

- Consistent with SAPA, the effectiveness of the regulations should be evaluated every three years.
extraction_rationale: Clear regulatory commitment plus review cycle.
mechanism_description: Implementing and periodically evaluating emissions regulations for fossil fuel-fired generating units reduces power plant pollution, lowering overall electricity-sector greenhouse gas emissions while maintaining reliability.

section_header: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities
policy_statement: Conduct biannual PSC reviews starting in 2024 of the renewable energy program and resource mix to support emissions targets and evaluate retirement/repurposing of fossil generation; complete a 2023 NYC fossil facility retirement/redevelopment blueprint including specified community and workforce measures.
parent_statement: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Regular and transparent resource planning: The PSC will conduct biannual reviews of the renewable energy program and electric system resource mix starting in 2024... As part of this review process, the PSC should evaluate options to retire and/or repurpose existing fossil fuel electric generation facilities. In 2022, the State initiated the development of a blueprint... by 2030... will be completed in 2023... The blueprint will also include detailed analysis and planning to address the impacts on communities and workers.
extraction_rationale: Contains explicit deadlines (starting 2024; completed in 2023) and concrete planning actions.
mechanism_description: Regular regulatory resource planning and review supports retiring or repurposing fossil power plants and shifting the electricity mix toward renewables, reducing grid emissions.

section_header: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities
policy_statement: Consider and implement market mechanisms to remove fossil generation, including a Clean Dispatch Credit program requiring load-serving entities to purchase increasing amounts annually, with protections/prioritization for Disadvantaged Communities.
parent_statement: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Investigate and implement options to develop market mechanisms to assist in the removal of fossil fuel-fired generating facilities from the system... Specifically, the State should consider a clean dispatch program that creates Clean Dispatch Credits... Load-serving entities would be required to purchase increasing amounts of Clean Dispatch Credits annually... New York must ensure Disadvantaged Communities are not disproportionately burdened and prioritize reductions...
extraction_rationale: Specific market mechanism with requirement on LSEs; explicit equity constraint.
mechanism_description: Requiring load-serving entities to buy increasing clean dispatch credits creates a market signal that reduces fossil generator dispatch and accelerates replacement with cleaner generation, lowering power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: E6. Deploy Existing Storage Technologies (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## E6. Deploy Existing Storage Technologies

## Components of the Strategy

- Provide increased funding for energy storage deployment...
- Incorporate energy storage into energy delivery and transmission planning...
- Work with the NYISO...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component bullets.
mechanism_description: Deploying energy storage increases grid flexibility, which enables higher renewable energy penetration and reduces reliance on fossil generation.

policy_statement: Create a dedicated funding mechanism and binding targets for energy storage similar to CES as soon as practicable and no later than end of 2023, prioritizing funding to projects benefiting frontline communities.
parent_statement: E6. Deploy Existing Storage Technologies (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Provide increased funding for energy storage deployment: The PSC should consider methods... to initiate a new docket that sets new binding targets and creates a dedicated funding mechanism that is similar to the CES for storage as soon as practicable and no later than the end of 2023. Funding should be prioritized to projects that benefit frontline communities...
extraction_rationale: Deadline (end of 2023) and binding targets/funding mechanism; clear action.
mechanism_description: Create binding targets and dedicated funding to deploy energy storage, increasing grid flexibility so more renewable electricity can be integrated and fossil generation can be displaced.

section_header: E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades
policy_statement: E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades

## Components of Strategy

- Transmission and distribution expansion...
- Strategic investment...
- SF6 emissions...
- Hosting capacity...
- Renewable Energy Zones...
- Upgrades for offshore wind...
- Multi-port infrastructure...
- Education...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple actionable bullets; parent appropriate.
mechanism_description: Upgrading and expanding transmission and distribution infrastructure increases grid capacity and hosting capability, enabling more renewable generation to connect and operate reliably, which supports reductions in fossil electricity generation and associated emissions.

section_header: E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades
policy_statement: Adopt DEC regulations to reduce SF6 emissions and establish a timeline to phase out new SF6 equipment, coordinating with other U.S. Climate Alliance states.
parent_statement: E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - SF6 emissions: DEC should adopt regulations to reduce SF6 emissions and establish a timeline for phasing out new SF6 equipment. New York should also collaborate with other U.S. Climate Alliance states to align policies...
extraction_rationale: Clear regulatory action plus phase-out timeline requirement.
mechanism_description: Regulate and phase out SF6-containing equipment to prevent SF6 leakage, reducing high-global-warming-potential fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades
policy_statement: Create a Renewable Energy Zones database and establish a process to designate zones, set renewable quantities per zone, and plan transmission needed for deliverability.
parent_statement: E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Renewable Energy Zones: The State should create a database to identify Renewable Energy Zones and track renewable energy development and availability capacity in these zones. The database should inform recommendations for a process to establish Renewable Energy Zones... determine quantity... within each zone, and develop a plan... to build sufficient transmission...
extraction_rationale: Specific database and planning process creation; actionable under E7.
mechanism_description: Create and use a renewable energy zone database and designation process to guide transmission planning and upgrades, enabling more renewable generation to connect and displace fossil electricity.

section_header: I2. Incentivize Procurement of Low-Carbon Products
policy_statement: I2. Incentivize Procurement of Low-Carbon Products (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## I2. Incentivize Procurement of Low-Carbon Products

## Components of the Strategy

- Identify carbon intense materials...
- Develop standards...
- Provide policy support...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple listed component actions; parent appropriate.
mechanism_description: Incentives and standards shift purchasing toward lower-carbon products and materials, reducing lifecycle/embodied greenhouse gas emissions from producing those goods.

section_header: I2. Incentivize Procurement of Low-Carbon Products
policy_statement: Develop a list of the most carbon-intense building materials/products eligible for incentives or preferential procurement treatment via the interagency GreenNY Council.
parent_statement: I2. Incentivize Procurement of Low-Carbon Products (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Identify carbon intense materials: First, the interagency GreenNY Council should develop a list of the most carbon-intense building materials and products eligible for incentives or preferential treatment in procurement.
extraction_rationale: Concrete first-step deliverable under I2.
mechanism_description: By targeting procurement and incentives away from carbon-intensive building materials and toward lower-carbon substitutes, the policy reduces embodied greenhouse gas emissions from construction materials production.

section_header: I2. Incentivize Procurement of Low-Carbon Products
policy_statement: Set standards for determining the GWP of eligible building materials in coordination with manufacturers, researchers, and other states/federal agencies.
parent_statement: I2. Incentivize Procurement of Low-Carbon Products (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Develop standards: After identification of eligible products, the interagency group should work with manufacturers, trade associations, researchers, and other like-minded states or federal agencies to set standards for determining the GWP of each building material.
extraction_rationale: Specific standard-setting commitment under I2.
mechanism_description: Create common GWP calculation standards for building materials to enable embodied-carbon measurement and drive selection of lower-emission materials, reducing construction supply-chain emissions.

section_header: I2. Incentivize Procurement of Low-Carbon Products
policy_statement: Implement procurement policy mechanisms that advantage bids/projects using materials that meet or exceed GWP targets.
parent_statement: I2. Incentivize Procurement of Low-Carbon Products (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Provide policy support: The State should implement policy mechanisms that provide advantages to projects or procurement bids utilizing products that meet or exceed GWP targets.
extraction_rationale: Explicit procurement mechanism direction; actionable.
mechanism_description: Preferential procurement for low-GWP materials shifts purchasing toward lower-embodied-carbon products, reducing lifecycle emissions from construction materials production and use.

section_header: AF2. Prevent Forest Pests, Diseases, and Invasive Species and Restore Degraded Forests
policy_statement: AF2. Prevent Forest Pests, Diseases, and Invasive Species and Restore Degraded Forests (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## AF2. Prevent Forest Pests, Diseases, and Invasive Species and Restore Degraded Forests

## Components of the Strategy

- Increase prevention of invasive species...
- Expand statutory authority...
- Support programs that combat invasive species...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component actions.
mechanism_description: Preventing and controlling pests/invasives and restoring degraded forests maintains and rebuilds forest biomass, avoiding carbon loss and supporting long-term carbon storage and ecosystem resilience.

section_header: AF2. Prevent Forest Pests, Diseases, and Invasive Species and Restore Degraded Forests
policy_statement: Enact legislation to expand ECL § 9-1709 authority to allow DEC to more rapidly list invasive species for regulation by strengthening/amending 6 NYCRR Parts 575 and 576.
parent_statement: AF2. Prevent Forest Pests, Diseases, and Invasive Species and Restore Degraded Forests (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Expand statutory authority: The State should enact legislation to expand the scope of ECL § 9-1709 to provide DEC with more statutory authority to strengthen and amend 6 NYCRR Parts 575 and 576 to allow more rapid listing of invasive species for regulation.
extraction_rationale: Explicit legislative change enabling regulatory speed; concrete.
mechanism_description: Expanding agency authority to rapidly list and regulate invasive species enables quicker control actions, protecting ecosystems and their resilience (including carbon storage and related climate benefits).

policy_statement: AF8. Advance Alternative Manure Management (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## AF8. Advance Alternative Manure Management

## Components of the Strategy

- Expand funding for current programs...
- Expand private sector and NGO partnerships...
- Expand farmer access...
- Strengthen program policies...
- Expand SWCD capacity...
- Expand technical assistance...
- Expand training...
- Develop new funding opportunities...
- Expand purchasing opportunities...
- Advance energy production...
- Minimize fugitive methane...
- Align market opportunities...
- Make market connections...
- Support research and outreach...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple detailed component actions.
mechanism_description: Increase adoption of improved manure management practices and systems to reduce fugitive methane from manure while capturing biogas for energy that can displace fossil fuels.

policy_statement: Significantly expand Climate Resilient Farming (CRF) program funding to help farmers implement alternative manure management systems that reduce methane emissions.
parent_statement: AF8. Advance Alternative Manure Management (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Expand funding for current programs: AGM and SWCC should continue to significantly expand CRF program funding to assist farmers in implementing alternative manure management practice systems that reduce methane emissions.
extraction_rationale: Direct program funding expansion commitment with clear purpose.
mechanism_description: Expanded program funding enables farmers to adopt alternative manure management systems, which reduces methane generated from manure storage and handling.

policy_statement: Develop a State-funded loan guarantee program to stimulate investment in alternative manure management systems.
parent_statement: AF8. Advance Alternative Manure Management (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Develop new funding opportunities: AGM should develop a State-funded loan guarantee program to stimulate investment in alternative manure management systems.
extraction_rationale: Creation of a specific financial instrument (loan guarantee) is a clear policy action.
mechanism_description: A state loan guarantee program lowers financing barriers so farms invest in alternative manure management systems that reduce methane and nitrous oxide emissions from manure handling.

policy_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
verbatim_text: Farms across New York will continue to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution to waterways while achieving desired yield and quality through continued and expanded nutrient management planning and implementation on crop fields, hay fields, pastures, orchards, vineyards, and other agricultural lands receiving nutrients.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy (AF10) with a defined objective and a bulleted 'Components of the Strategy' list; qualifies as an umbrella program/strategy.
mechanism_description: Implementing nutrient management planning and practices optimizes fertilizer and manure use on farms, which reduces soil N2O emissions and associated nitrogen pollution.

policy_statement: Increase funding and technical assistance for nutrient management planning and implementation through the AEM Framework and associated programs (including CRF and AgNPS), including funding to quantify climate and co-benefits for farm-level GHG accounting and reductions.
parent_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
verbatim_text: - Expand funding and technical assistance: AGM and SWCC should increase support for planning, technical assistance, and soil health or nutrient management practice implementation through the AEM Framework and associated programs, including the CRF and Agricultural Nonpoint Source Abatement and Control (AgNPS) water quality program. This should include funding to quantify climate and co-benefits related to farm-level GHG emission accounting and reduction strategies.
extraction_rationale: Specific implementation action under AF10 with responsible agencies and resource/funding expansion.
mechanism_description: Provide funding and technical assistance for farm nutrient/soil management planning and practice implementation to improve soil health and nutrient efficiency, which reduces farm GHG emissions and increases soil carbon storage.

policy_statement: Engage and expand nutrient management program participation by seeking feedback from historically unrepresented and beginning farmers and removing participation obstacles (e.g., focus groups, surveys, farm-to-farm education).
parent_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
verbatim_text: - Engage with and expand program participants: AGM, SWCC, and SWCDs should seek feedback from historically unrepresented and beginning farmers who are not currently engaged in practices and programs to remove obstacles for participation (such as holding focus groups, surveys, and farm-to-farm education).
extraction_rationale: Concrete engagement actions and target groups stated as 'should' under AF10.
mechanism_description: Increase farmer participation in nutrient management so fertilizer use is better optimized, reducing soil nitrous oxide emissions and nutrient losses.

policy_statement: Expand cost-share eligibility for equipment needed to implement advanced soil health and nutrient management practices, emphasizing access for historically unrepresented and beginning farmers.
parent_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
verbatim_text: - Expand cost-share eligibility for historically unrepresented farmers and capital-intensive equipment: AGM and SWCC should expand cost-share eligibility for equipment needed by farms to implement more advanced soil health and nutrient management practices. Emphasis should be on improved access to technical and financial support for historically unrepresented and beginning farmers.
extraction_rationale: Explicit expansion of cost-share eligibility and equity emphasis under AF10.
mechanism_description: Cost-share support enables farms to adopt advanced soil health and nutrient management practices, improving soil carbon storage and reducing agricultural emissions impacts.

policy_statement: Continue and enhance workforce training for technical assistance providers, planners, farmers, and farm workers to support nutrient management implementation.
parent_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
verbatim_text: - Enhance workforce training: AGM and SWCC should continue and enhance training for technical assistance providers, agricultural planners, farmers, and farm workers.
extraction_rationale: Specific training expansion commitment listed as a component under AF10.
mechanism_description: Training technical assistance providers and farm workers increases adoption and quality of nutrient management, lowering fertilizer-related emissions and pollution.

policy_statement: Support expanding capacity of custom farming service providers to aid on-farm implementation of nutrient management practices.
parent_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
verbatim_text: - Support implementation services: AGM and SWCC should support expanding capacity of custom farming service providers to aid on-farm implementation of nutrient management practices.
extraction_rationale: Clear support action for service provider capacity under AF10.
mechanism_description: Expand technical/implementation support for on-farm nutrient management, leading to more efficient fertilizer use and reduced nitrous oxide and nutrient-loss emissions.

policy_statement: Increase the use of improved monitoring methods for nutrient management (crop yield measurement, nitrogen use efficiency, and Whole Farm Nutrient Mass Balances) and document/share benefits with stakeholders.
parent_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
verbatim_text: - Increase use of existing tools: AGM should work with partners such as Cornell CALS, CCE, and others to increase the use of improved methods of monitoring performance via crop yield measurement, nitrogen use efficiency, and Whole Farm Nutrient Mass Balances (for farm-wide nitrogen management). These benefits of nutrient management should be documented and shared with farmers, policymakers, and the public.
extraction_rationale: Specifies tools/metrics to increase and an action to document/share outcomes.
mechanism_description: Implement improved farm nutrient monitoring and performance reporting to enable more efficient fertilizer use, which reduces surplus nitrogen losses and associated nitrous oxide emissions.

policy_statement: Collaborate with industry-led nutrient management initiatives (e.g., 4R Nutrient Stewardship) and initiate/expand nitrogen efficiency and yield crop contests.
parent_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
verbatim_text: - Collaborate with industry led initiatives: AGM and partners should collaborate with industry led nutrient management initiatives and services, such as the 4R Nutrient Stewardship Program . They should also initiate and expand nitrogen efficiency and yield crop contests for peer-to-peer competition and informational opportunities.
extraction_rationale: Specific collaboration mechanism and contest expansion stated under AF10.
mechanism_description: Industry collaboration and peer learning contests encourage more efficient fertilizer use, lowering nitrogen losses and nitrous oxide emissions from cropland.

policy_statement: Implement long-term funding support for nutrient management applied research and outreach.
parent_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
verbatim_text: - Increase funding for applied research: AGM should implement long-term funding support for nutrient management applied research and outreach (such as management approaches and technology).
extraction_rationale: Resource allocation (long-term funding support) explicitly stated under AF10.
mechanism_description: Long-term funding for nutrient management applied research and outreach promotes improved on-farm nutrient practices that reduce nitrous oxide emissions from agricultural soils.

policy_statement: Increase outreach to farmers on New York research/technical standards and increase consumer outreach on nutrient management benefits such as water quality improvements.
parent_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
verbatim_text: - Increase outreach: The State should increase outreach to all farmers with the research and technical standards used in New York. The State should also increase outreach to consumers on the benefits of nutrient management, such as water quality improvements.
extraction_rationale: Two explicit outreach commitments under AF10.
mechanism_description: Provide education and outreach on nutrient management to increase adoption of best practices, thereby reducing fertilizer/nutrient runoff and improving water quality.

section_header: AF11. Adopt Soil Health Practice Systems
policy_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: This strategy focuses on reducing net GHG emissions as well as increasing carbon sequestration and other environmental benefits through adoption of soil health management practice systems (such as combinations of cover and double crops, conservation crop rotations, perennial crops, prescribed grazing, and nutrient management), also referred to as regenerative agricultural practices.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a defined focus and an enumerated 'Components of Strategy' list.
mechanism_description: Support and adoption of regenerative soil health practices increases soil carbon storage and improves ecosystem function, lowering net agricultural greenhouse gas emissions over time.

policy_statement: Increase financial support for current soil health programs (including expanded CRF funding, AEM Base, AgNPS, payment rates, access/equity measures), develop a soil health standard, and establish an annual goal for common practices (including urban soils awareness/support).
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Expand funding for current programs: AGM and SWCC should increase financial support for currently available and implemented practices. This includes expanded funding for CRF, AEM Base, AgNPS, increased payment rates, increased access, building equity into programs, reducing barriers that limit access in applications, increased technical assistance, encouraging adoption of a system of practices, developing a soil health standard, and establishing an annual goal for common practices. This should also include increasing awareness and support for urban soils and agriculture.
extraction_rationale: Detailed package of funding, standards, and goal-setting actions under AF11.
mechanism_description: Provide financial/technical support and standards/goals to increase adoption of soil health practices, resulting in higher soil carbon sequestration and improved ecosystem condition.

policy_statement: Seek feedback from under-engaged groups/communities (including urban soils/operations) via engagement activities to improve access to soil health practices and programs.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Engage with and expand program participants: AGM and SWCC should seek feedback from groups/communities not currently engaged in practices and programs (such as holding focus groups, surveys, farm-to-farm educational events and addressing urban soils and urban agricultural operations). Improving access reflects the need to ensure that all farmers can take part in these practices and programs.
extraction_rationale: Specific engagement activities and objective under AF11.
mechanism_description: Conduct outreach and feedback-driven engagement to improve access and participation in soil health programs, enabling broader adoption of soil health practices that increase soil carbon and restore ecosystem function.

policy_statement: Increase adoption of soil health practices on rented/leased land by engaging, educating, and incentivizing landowners.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Increase adoption of soil health practices on rented lands: AGM should increase practice adoption on rented and leased land. AGM should seek feedback regarding support needed for farmers not currently engaged in practices and programs and engage, educate, and incentivize landowners to increase adoption of practices on land they rent to farmers.
extraction_rationale: Concrete actions aimed at rented land adoption under AF11.
mechanism_description: Engage, educate, and incentivize landowners to implement soil health practices on farmland, increasing soil carbon sequestration and improving ecosystem condition, which reduces net climate impacts.

policy_statement: Support converting annual cropland to perennial hay/pasture and forests where appropriate and continue research/trials on perennial grains efficacy in New York State.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Increase perennial plant systems: AGM and SWCC should support converting annual cropland to perennial hay land/pasture and forests where appropriate (such as steep slopes and highly erodible lands). This is a current effort supported through the State's AgNPS and CRF programs. Research and trials should continue to determine the efficacy of perennial grains systems in New York State.
extraction_rationale: Specific land conversion support plus ongoing research commitment under AF11.
mechanism_description: Convert annual cropland to perennial pasture/hay and forested systems to build soil/biomass carbon and improve ecosystem resilience, reducing net greenhouse gas impacts.

policy_statement: Conduct comprehensive on-farm planning to include carbon sequestration goals along with GHG emissions, nutrient management, water quality, and soil health.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Increase integrated planning efforts: AGM and SWCDs should conduct comprehensive on-farm planning to include carbon sequestration goals, GHG emission, nutrient management, water quality, and soil health.
extraction_rationale: Specific planning intervention under AF11.
mechanism_description: Implementing comprehensive farm planning that embeds carbon sequestration goals leads to adoption of regenerative land management practices that increase soil carbon storage and improve ecosystem health, reducing net emissions.

policy_statement: Support continued development and implementation of precision/digital agricultural tools and sustainable intensification to increase yields on current cropland and reduce stress on marginal cropland.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Increase use of precision and digital agricultural tools: AGM should support continued development and implementation of precision/digital agricultural tools and sustainable intensification, which is the sustainable increase in yields on current cropland to reduce stress on marginal cropland to support this mitigation strategy.
extraction_rationale: Clear technology support action under AF11.
mechanism_description: Deploying precision/digital farming and sustainable intensification increases yields on existing cropland, reducing pressure to expand into marginal lands and avoiding emissions from land conversion.

policy_statement: Develop tools to verify benefits and invest in remote sensing to quantify adoption of practices and environmental benefits.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Develop tools for quantification of benefits: AGM, SWCC, and partners such as Cornell CALS should develop tools for verification of benefits and invest in remote sensing to quantify adoption of practices and environmental benefits.
extraction_rationale: Verification/remote sensing investment commitment under AF11.
mechanism_description: Build monitoring and verification tools (including remote sensing) to measure practice adoption and environmental outcomes, improving data-driven climate planning and program effectiveness.

policy_statement: Establish and maintain a comprehensive RD&D strategy for monitoring and verification of soil health addressing additionality and permanence to support State climate goals and enable federal/private funding.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Support research for monitoring and verification: AGM, SWCC, and partners such as Cornell CALS should establish and maintain a comprehensive RD&D strategy for monitoring and verification of soil health that address additionality and permanence to support State climate goals and enable federal and private funding of GHG emissions mitigation practices.
extraction_rationale: Explicit RD&D strategy commitment under AF11.
mechanism_description: Developing and maintaining soil-health monitoring and verification R&D improves credible measurement of additional, durable benefits, which enables funding and broader deployment of soil-based mitigation practices.

policy_statement: Establish and maintain a comprehensive research strategy in soil health to develop innovative practices (e.g., enhanced rock weathering, biochar) that increase sequestration and scale adoption.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Support research for new innovative practices: AGM and partners such as Cornell CALS should establish and maintain a comprehensive research strategy in soil health to bring new practices and approaches (such as enhanced rock weathering and biochar) that increase sequestration rates, productivity, other environmental benefits, and scale for adoption.
extraction_rationale: R&D commitment including examples under AF11.
mechanism_description: Fund and coordinate soil health research to develop and scale practices like biochar and enhanced rock weathering, leading to higher rates of carbon sequestration in soils.

policy_statement: Identify soil health practice systems that can generate revenue/added value and identify diverse public and private funding sources.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Develop a business case for practices: The State should identify practice systems that can generate revenue and/or added value to the farm, as well as identify a variety of public and private funding sources.
extraction_rationale: Economic development/funding identification action under AF11.
mechanism_description: Developing revenue and funding pathways for soil health practices increases adoption of regenerative agriculture, which builds soil carbon and restores ecosystem function, reducing net greenhouse gas impacts.

policy_statement: Conduct consumer outreach campaigns and marketing efforts to make regenerative practice efforts visible (including expanding regenerative practices in programs like New York Grown & Certified).
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Educate consumers: AGM should make efforts visible to the public through outreach campaigns making information more available, expand regenerative agricultural practices in marketing programs (such as New York Grown & Certified), and improve information provided to the public to help customers understand the practices involved in products they purchase.
extraction_rationale: Specific consumer education/marketing actions under AF11.
mechanism_description: Outreach and labeling/marketing improve consumer awareness and demand for regenerative products, encouraging producers to adopt regenerative practices that enhance soil carbon and ecosystem health.

policy_statement: Expand education/outreach to farmers and farmworkers to support adoption and encourage coupling practices into systems, including peer-to-peer networking.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Educate farmers and farmworkers: AGM, SWCC, and SWCDs should expand education and outreach to include all farmers and farmworkers and to support practice adoption and encourage coupling of practices into systems for maximum benefit. They should support farm-to-farm and peer-to-peer networking to elevate long-term adoption of soil health management practice systems (such as through local farmer discussion groups).
extraction_rationale: Detailed education/networking interventions under AF11.
mechanism_description: Education, outreach, and peer-to-peer support increase adoption of soil health management practices, which builds soil carbon and restores ecosystem function.

policy_statement: Support agricultural and soil health instruction in schools and connect students with farms and ecological benefits of healthy soils.
parent_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
verbatim_text: - Educate students: AGM, SWCC, SWCDs, and partners such as Cornell CALS and CCE should support agricultural and soil health instruction in schools and connect students with farms and farmers and the knowledge of the ecological benefit of healthy soils.
extraction_rationale: Education program support under AF11 with responsible parties.
mechanism_description: Provide agricultural and soil health education that builds awareness and capacity, leading over time to wider use of soil-health practices that increase soil carbon and improve ecosystem resilience.

policy_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: Adding trees into areas of agricultural production increases carbon sequestration and other environmental benefits. Current programs, technical services, and support should be leveraged to increase agroforestry adoption while new programs, increased investment in technical support, and capacity will be necessary.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a 'Components of Strategy' list; constitutes an umbrella policy.
mechanism_description: Provide programs, investment, and technical assistance to increase adoption of agroforestry, leading to more trees on agricultural land and higher carbon sequestration.

policy_statement: Expand SWCD and partner capacity to support on-farm implementation of GHG reduction and sequestration practices.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Increase SWCD capacity: The State should expand capacity of SWCDs and partners to aid onfarm implementation of GHG emissions reduction and sequestration management practices.
extraction_rationale: Capacity expansion commitment listed under AF12.
mechanism_description: Expand technical/support capacity so more farms adopt GHG-reducing and carbon-sequestering practices, increasing soil carbon storage and lowering land-based emissions.

policy_statement: Expand the CRF program to incentivize agroforestry and set acreage targets for priority practices.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Increase adoption of practices: AGM and SWCC should expand the CRF program to incentivize agroforestry and set acreage targets for priority practices.
extraction_rationale: Explicit program expansion plus quantified element (acreage targets) under AF12.
mechanism_description: Expand incentives for agroforestry so more land adopts tree-integrated farming systems, increasing biomass and soil carbon storage while improving ecosystem resilience.

policy_statement: Emphasize and expand forested riparian buffers through AgNPS, Source Water Buffer Program, Trees for Tributaries, and USDA CRP/CREP.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Increase riparian buffers: AGM and SWCC should continue to emphasize forested buffers through the State's AgNPS and Source Water Buffer Program, DEC's Trees for Tributaries Program, and the USDA Conservation Reserve Program/Conservation Reserve Enhancement Program.
extraction_rationale: Specific continuation/emphasis across named programs under AF12.
mechanism_description: Expanding forested riparian buffers increases vegetation and soil carbon storage while protecting and restoring streamside ecosystems, contributing to climate mitigation and resilience.

policy_statement: Expand programs to plan, design, and implement silvopasture systems with appropriate site/species selection.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Increase silvopasturing: AGM, SWCC, DEC, SWCDs, and CCE should expand programs that plan, design, and implement silvopasture systems. Silvopasture systems integrate tress, livestock, and forage in intensively managed rotational grazing systems with a focus on proper site and species selection for adding trees to ensure appropriate and responsible implementation of silvopasture systems.
extraction_rationale: Concrete program expansion with implementation criteria under AF12.
mechanism_description: Expanding silvopasture adds trees to grazing lands, increasing carbon stored in vegetation and soils while enhancing ecosystem resilience.

policy_statement: Expand DEC funding for Trees for Tributaries, the Non-Agricultural NPS Program, and Division of Fish and Wildlife Programs.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Expand funding for existing programs: DEC should expand the Trees for Tributaries Program, the Non-Agricultural NPS Program, and Division of Fish and Wildlife Programs.
extraction_rationale: Direct funding expansion commitment under AF12.
mechanism_description: Expand funding for riparian/tree and nonpoint-source watershed programs to restore habitat and reduce runoff impacts, improving water quality and ecosystem resilience to climate stressors.

policy_statement: Conduct field trials/pilot projects and expand education and technical assistance for alley cropping.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Advance alley cropping practices: AGM, SWCC, SWCDs, and partners such as Cornell CALS, SUNY ESF, and CCE should conduct field trials and pilot projects and expand landowner and farmworker education and technical assistance for alley cropping.
extraction_rationale: Specific R&D pilots and outreach actions under AF12.
mechanism_description: Provide pilots, education, and technical assistance to increase alley cropping adoption, which adds trees to farmland and boosts soil and biomass carbon storage.

policy_statement: Expand agroforestry education and technical assistance for beginning farmers and those planning generational transfer.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Expand education and technical assistance: AGM, along with partners such as Cornell CALS, CCE, SWCDs, should expand education and technical assistance for agroforestry practices for beginning farmers and farmers experiencing or planning for generational transfer.
extraction_rationale: Targeted TA expansion and audience specified under AF12.
mechanism_description: Provide agroforestry education and technical assistance so more farmers adopt agroforestry practices, increasing carbon stored in soils and biomass and improving ecosystem resilience.

policy_statement: Assist historically unrepresented and beginning farmers in securing long-term leases and farm transfers to enable perennial systems.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Support long-term agricultural land transfers: AGM, American Farmland Trust, and land trusts should assist farmers in securing long-term leasing and farm transfer to historically unrepresented and beginning farmers; long-term leases are required for long-term perennial systems.
extraction_rationale: Explicit assistance for land tenure/transfer linked to agroforestry adoption under AF12.
mechanism_description: Supporting long-term farmland leases and transfers enables farmers to invest in perennial farming systems, which can increase soil and biomass carbon storage and improve ecosystem resilience.

policy_statement: Assist farmers with business planning/modeling and supply chain development for new agroforestry products.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Support business planning and market development: AGM and SWCC should assist farmers with business planning and modeling for value-added practices in agroforestry. They should assist to expand supply chain development for new products in agroforestry.
extraction_rationale: Business planning and supply chain development actions under AF12.
mechanism_description: Provide business planning and supply-chain support for agroforestry products to increase agroforestry adoption, leading to greater on-farm carbon sequestration and ecosystem resilience.

policy_statement: Conduct outreach to financial lenders and insurance providers on environmental and economic benefits of agroforestry.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Conduct outreach to financial lenders: AGM should work with partners such as CCE and Cornell CALS to conduct outreach on the environmental and potential economic benefits of agroforestry systems to financial lenders and insurance providers.
extraction_rationale: Clear outreach activity with target stakeholders under AF12.
mechanism_description: Outreach to lenders and insurers is intended to improve access to financing and risk coverage for agroforestry, enabling broader adoption that enhances carbon sequestration and ecosystem resilience.

policy_statement: Collaborate with federal partners to better align federal and State policy priorities for agroforestry.
parent_statement: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
verbatim_text: - Collaborate with federal partners: The State should collaborate with federal partners to better align federal and State policy priorities.
extraction_rationale: Explicit coordination action included as a component under AF12.
mechanism_description: Aligning state and federal priorities to support agroforestry increases tree integration on farms, which enhances soil and biomass carbon storage and associated ecosystem benefits.

section_header: AF14. Monitor and Benchmark Agricultural Greenhouse Gas Emissions
policy_statement: AF14 establishes annual monitoring and benchmarking of agricultural GHG mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation performance, including farm-level benchmarking and aggregated public reporting.
verbatim_text: Annual monitoring and benchmarking of GHG emissions mitigation, carbon sequestration, and adaptation performance across applicable areas of management on farms in New York is critical to determining success in meeting targets and providing further insight into what strategies lead to the greatest achievements.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with detailed components including funding, methods, outreach, and data delivery.
mechanism_description: Establishing farm-level monitoring, benchmarking, and public reporting provides data and accountability signals that enable improved agricultural management decisions for mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation.

policy_statement: Establish funding for an agricultural benchmarking and monitoring program for GHG emissions via a partnership including Cornell CALS, CCE, SWCDs, farmer groups, and industry groups.
parent_statement: AF14 establishes annual monitoring and benchmarking of agricultural GHG mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation performance, including farm-level benchmarking and aggregated public reporting.
verbatim_text: - Establish funding: AGM and DEC should establish funding for an agricultural benchmarking and monitoring program for GHG emissions. This should be a partnership effort among groups such as Cornell CALS, CCE, SWCDs, famer groups, and agricultural industry groups.
extraction_rationale: Direct funding commitment under AF14.
mechanism_description: Funding agricultural GHG benchmarking and monitoring improves emissions data and accountability, enabling better targeting and planning of mitigation/adaptation actions.

policy_statement: Develop methods for monitoring and benchmarking agricultural emissions, including staffing and an advisory committee, with benchmarking at farm, county, and State levels using anonymous comparisons.
parent_statement: AF14 establishes annual monitoring and benchmarking of agricultural GHG mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation performance, including farm-level benchmarking and aggregated public reporting.
verbatim_text: - Monitoring and benchmarking: AGM should develop methods for monitoring and benchmarking (including program staff and advisory committee). Benchmarking at the farm level for farmer use should be based on comparisons with the farm's historical performance as well as the performance of similar types of farms (each kept anonymous). Benchmarking should also occur at the county and State level with aggregated, anonymous data based on comparisons with our historical performance.
extraction_rationale: Specific methodological design commitments under AF14.
mechanism_description: Create anonymized agricultural emissions monitoring and benchmarking so farms and governments can compare performance over time and against peers, enabling targeted emissions-reduction actions.

policy_statement: Introduce the monitoring and benchmarking program to farmers and farm advisors via outreach.
parent_statement: AF14 establishes annual monitoring and benchmarking of agricultural GHG mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation performance, including farm-level benchmarking and aggregated public reporting.
verbatim_text: - Outreach: AGM and SWCC should introduce monitoring and benchmarking program with farmers and farm advisors.
extraction_rationale: Outreach action explicitly listed under AF14.
mechanism_description: Outreach increases farmer and advisor uptake of monitoring and benchmarking, enabling better farm management decisions that can reduce agricultural emissions over time.

policy_statement: Deliver confidential farm-scale data summaries and aggregated public summaries on BMP implementation and estimated GHG mitigation/sequestration, and develop additional performance metrics via an advisory committee/expert panel.
parent_statement: AF14 establishes annual monitoring and benchmarking of agricultural GHG mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation performance, including farm-level benchmarking and aggregated public reporting.
verbatim_text: - Make data available: AGM should deliver data summaries of BMP implementation and associated estimates of GHG mitigation or sequestration levels for confidential farm-scale use and aggregated summaries for public use. Additional key performance metrics would be developed by the advisory committee/expert panel.
extraction_rationale: Concrete data delivery and metric development commitments under AF14.
mechanism_description: Collecting and publishing aggregated BMP and GHG-impact data (with confidential farm-level reporting) creates an environmental monitoring and metrics system that supports decision-making, oversight, and improved climate planning.

section_header: AF15. Establish a Payment for Ecosystem Services Program
policy_statement: AF15 establishes a Payment for Ecosystem Services program for agricultural producers and forest landowners to incentivize practices that reduce GHG emissions and sequester carbon.
verbatim_text: Establishing a payment for ecosystem services mechanism to provide a new structure for establishing and maintaining practice systems that reduce GHG emissions and sequester carbon in addition to providing other environmental benefits would provide additional incentives to farmers and forest landowners.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components including funding, design, outreach, and piloting; qualifies as a parent policy.
mechanism_description: Provide payments to farmers and forest landowners to adopt land-management practices that cut on-site emissions and increase carbon stored in soils and biomass.

policy_statement: Establish funding for a payment for ecosystem services program for agricultural producers and forest landowners.
parent_statement: AF15 establishes a Payment for Ecosystem Services program for agricultural producers and forest landowners to incentivize practices that reduce GHG emissions and sequester carbon.
verbatim_text: - Dedicate funding: AGM and DEC should establish funding for a payment for ecosystem services program to be developed for agricultural producers and forest landowners.
extraction_rationale: Explicit funding dedication under AF15.
mechanism_description: Provide financial incentives to farmers and forest landowners to deliver ecosystem services, which supports conservation practices that preserve or increase ecosystem carbon storage and resilience.

policy_statement: Research and design a payment for ecosystem services approach providing fair compensation and sustaining natural capital (water quality, flood resilience, climate stability).
parent_statement: AF15 establishes a Payment for Ecosystem Services program for agricultural producers and forest landowners to incentivize practices that reduce GHG emissions and sequester carbon.
verbatim_text: - Develop and design program: AGM, SWCC, and DEC should research and design a payment for ecosystem services approach that will sustain our natural capital; provide ecosystem services of water quality, flood resilience, and climate stability; and include fair compensation for program participants.
extraction_rationale: Program design commitment with specified service areas under AF15.
mechanism_description: Create financial incentives that pay land stewards to maintain or improve ecosystems, resulting in preserved natural capital that supports climate stability, water quality, and flood resilience.

policy_statement: Conduct education and outreach about the payment for ecosystem services program and its benefits.
parent_statement: AF15 establishes a Payment for Ecosystem Services program for agricultural producers and forest landowners to incentivize practices that reduce GHG emissions and sequester carbon.
verbatim_text: - Conduct outreach: AGM, SWCC, and DEC should conduct education and outreach regarding payment for ecosystem services program and benefits to local communities.
extraction_rationale: Outreach commitment under AF15.
mechanism_description: Education and outreach increases participation in payment-for-ecosystem-services programs, which incentivizes conservation/restoration and helps preserve ecosystem carbon stocks and climate resilience.

policy_statement: Pilot a payment for ecosystem services program to ensure continued implementation of practices/actions that increase ecosystem services.
parent_statement: AF15 establishes a Payment for Ecosystem Services program for agricultural producers and forest landowners to incentivize practices that reduce GHG emissions and sequester carbon.
verbatim_text: - Pilot payment for ecosystem services program: AGM, SWCC, and DEC should pilot a payment for ecosystem services program for agricultural producers and forest landowners to ensure continued implementation of practices or actions that lead to increased ecosystem services.
extraction_rationale: Pilot program action specified under AF15.
mechanism_description: Provide payments to landowners to sustain ecosystem-enhancing land management practices, leading to preserved/restored ecosystems and increased carbon storage.

policy_statement: AF16 bolsters local agricultural economies by expanding programs and markets that encourage farm viability and local food production/consumption to support emissions reductions.
verbatim_text: This strategy supports emission reductions by enhancing existing programs, and promoting the expansion of those programs, that encourage farm viability and resilient communities through the production and consumption of local food.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a components list specifying expansions and support actions.
mechanism_description: Expanding programs and markets for locally produced and consumed food shortens supply chains, reducing transportation-related emissions while supporting viable local agriculture.

policy_statement: Expand State procurement programs supporting local procurement of New York agricultural products (e.g., Fresh Connect Checks, Farmers Market Nutrition Program, Farm-to-School, Nourish NY).
parent_statement: AF16 bolsters local agricultural economies by expanding programs and markets that encourage farm viability and local food production/consumption to support emissions reductions.
verbatim_text: - Expand procurement programs for New York products: AGM, OGS, and ESD should expand existing programs in the State that support local procurement of New York agricultural products (such as the Fresh Connect Checks Program, Farmers Market Nutrition Program, Farmto-School, and Nourish NY).
extraction_rationale: Concrete expansion of specified procurement programs under AF16.
mechanism_description: Expanding public procurement of local agricultural products reduces long-distance food transport and associated supply-chain emissions by shifting demand to nearer producers.

policy_statement: Engage communities and producers to advertise opportunities and increase participation in State programs supporting local agriculture.
parent_statement: AF16 bolsters local agricultural economies by expanding programs and markets that encourage farm viability and local food production/consumption to support emissions reductions.
verbatim_text: - Increase engagement and participation in State programs: AGM, New York State Council on Hunger and Food Policy, and ESD should engage with communities and producers to advertise these opportunities.
extraction_rationale: Specific engagement/advertising commitment under AF16.
mechanism_description: Conducting outreach to communities and producers increases awareness and enrollment in state agriculture support programs, strengthening implementation capacity for climate-relevant agricultural actions.

policy_statement: Expand education and technical assistance for beginning farmers and generational transfer, including business planning and modeling support.
parent_statement: AF16 bolsters local agricultural economies by expanding programs and markets that encourage farm viability and local food production/consumption to support emissions reductions.
verbatim_text: - Expand education and outreach for new farmers: AGM should work with partners such as CCE to expand education and technical assistance for beginning farmers and generational transfer. They should assist farmers with business planning and modeling.
extraction_rationale: TA and business planning support under AF16.
mechanism_description: Providing education and technical assistance to new and transitioning farmers strengthens agricultural planning and continuity, enabling adoption of more resilient and climate-smart farming practices over time.

policy_statement: Support expansion of supply chain development for new New York agricultural products, including agroforestry and perennial plant system products.
parent_statement: AF16 bolsters local agricultural economies by expanding programs and markets that encourage farm viability and local food production/consumption to support emissions reductions.
verbatim_text: - Support new agricultural products: The State, led by AGM, should help expand supply chain development for new agricultural products in New York such as agroforestry and perennial plant system products.
extraction_rationale: Explicit supply chain development support under AF16.
mechanism_description: Supporting agroforestry and perennial agricultural product supply chains incentivizes adoption of perennial/regenerative land practices, increasing carbon sequestration in vegetation and soils and improving ecosystem function.

section_header: AF17. Develop Forestry Training Programs to Support Expanding Workforce and Climate Knowledge
policy_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: There needs to be information on forest carbon management and climate resilience incorporated into existing forestry trainings and education programs to help encourage such an expansion.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components listing specific training, policy, and outreach actions.
mechanism_description: Expand and update forestry education and training to grow a skilled workforce that implements forest carbon management and climate-resilient practices, improving forest carbon outcomes and resilience.

policy_statement: Develop and support workforce development and training programs for the forest sector, including incorporating forest carbon management into high school and college curricula and supporting apprenticeships.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Support workforce development and training: DEC should work with partners such as the WPDC to develop and support workforce development and training programs for the forest sector, including incorporating forest carbon management into curriculums at the high school (such as the Boards of Cooperative Education Services) and college level and supporting existing training apprenticeship programs for careers in forestry and the forest product supply chain.
extraction_rationale: Concrete training program development action with examples under AF17.
mechanism_description: Training and education programs build a skilled forestry workforce capable of implementing forest carbon management practices, improving the capacity to sequester and retain carbon in forest landscapes.

policy_statement: Integrate and support forest carbon and climate resilience content within existing forestry training and continuing education programs.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Integrate forest carbon into education and outreach: DEC should integrate and support forest carbon, forest carbon management, climate change, and climate resilience into existing forestry training and continuing education programs.
extraction_rationale: Explicit integration action under AF17.
mechanism_description: Integrating forest carbon and climate resilience into forestry training builds practitioner capacity, leading to improved forest management that increases carbon storage and climate resilience.

policy_statement: Add a continuing education requirement in forest carbon/forest carbon management to the Cooperating Consulting Forest Policy (CP-36) and approve program rigor/accountability.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Enhance cooperating consulting forester policy: DEC should add a requirement for continuing education in forest carbon or forest carbon management to Cooperating Consulting Forest Policy, CP-36, and approve rigor and accountability of the program.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy change (continuing education requirement) under AF17.
mechanism_description: Require continuing education in forest carbon management for consulting foresters to improve forest management practices, leading to increased carbon sequestration and reduced forest-related emissions.

policy_statement: Integrate forest carbon and forest carbon management into urban forestry and utility forestry training programs.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Integrate forest carbon management: DEC should integrate forest carbon and forest carbon management into urban forestry and utility forestry training programs.
extraction_rationale: Training integration action under AF17.
mechanism_description: Integrating forest carbon management into forestry training builds practitioner capacity to manage urban and utility forests in ways that increase or protect carbon storage over time.

policy_statement: Bolster support for the Trained Logger Certification Program and implement new training modules including forest carbon BMPs and increasing carbon sequestration.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Bolster logger training: DEC and New York Logger Training should bolster support for the Trained Logger Certification Program and implement new training modules including forest carbon BMPs into the Trained Logger Certification program, including increasing carbon sequestration.
extraction_rationale: Specific training program expansion and module implementation under AF17.
mechanism_description: Provide logger training and certification on forest carbon best management practices to improve forest management outcomes and increase carbon sequestration in forests.

policy_statement: Provide outreach and education to construction industry and the public on mass timber and harvested wood products, including workforce training for use/installation of wood in buildings and infrastructure.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Provide outreach and education: SUNY ESF and Cornell CALS should provide outreach and education to the construction industry, architects, engineers, and the public on mass timber construction and use of harvested wood products (fire safety, high-rise applications). Education and outreach should include workforce training on the use and installation of wood in buildings and infrastructure.
extraction_rationale: Explicit outreach and workforce training actions under AF17 with target audiences.
mechanism_description: Provide outreach and workforce training to increase mass timber and harvested wood product use in construction, which shifts materials toward lower-embodied-carbon options and reduces construction emissions.

section_header: AF18. Expand Markets for Sustainably Harvested Wood Products
policy_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
verbatim_text: Use of durable, long-lasting wood products, especially as a substitute for fossil fuel-intensive products like steel, concrete, brick, or vinyl, can reduce overall emissions for construction projects.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component actions; serves as umbrella policy.
mechanism_description: Promoting durable, sustainably harvested wood as a substitute for emissions-intensive construction materials reduces embodied (upfront) emissions from construction projects.

policy_statement: Promote long-term durable wood products that store carbon and substitute for fossil-fuel-based materials, and incentivize longer-lived products while avoiding disincentives for residue/low-grade markets.
parent_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
