section_header: W5. Refrigerant Leak Reduction and Destruction
policy_statement: W5 reduces refrigerant leaks and ensures destruction at end-of-life through DEC regulations, EPR legislation, sales bans for virgin high-GWP servicing refrigerants, reporting registries, and research.
verbatim_text: Policies incentivizing the destruction of refrigerants at end-of-life would encourage the retirement of old, energy-inefficient equipment so that these materials are not released to the atmosphere.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components specifying regulations, legislation, registries, and research.
mechanism_description: Strengthened refrigerant controls and end-of-life recovery/destruction prevent refrigerant leakage and venting, cutting high-GWP fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Promulgate regulations requiring reclamation or destruction of refrigerants from appliances at end-of-life with verification and reporting; enact EPR legislation for refrigerant-containing appliances.
parent_statement: W5 reduces refrigerant leaks and ensures destruction at end-of-life through DEC regulations, EPR legislation, sales bans for virgin high-GWP servicing refrigerants, reporting registries, and research.
verbatim_text: - End of Life: DEC should promulgate regulations to address leaks at end-of-life by requiring reclamation or destruction of refrigerants from appliances and institute requirements for verification and reporting. The State should enact EPR legislation for refrigerant-containing appliances.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory requirement plus EPR legislation under W5.
mechanism_description: Require verified end-of-life refrigerant reclamation or destruction (including via producer responsibility), preventing refrigerant leaks and thereby reducing high-GWP fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Promulgate regulations banning sale of virgin high-GWP refrigerants for servicing, with an exception for reclaimed refrigerants.
parent_statement: W5 reduces refrigerant leaks and ensures destruction at end-of-life through DEC regulations, EPR legislation, sales bans for virgin high-GWP servicing refrigerants, reporting registries, and research.
verbatim_text: - Appliance Servicing: DEC should promulgate regulations banning the sale of virgin high GWP refrigerants for servicing with an exception for reclaimed refrigerants.
extraction_rationale: Specific sales ban regulation under W5.
mechanism_description: Restricting sales of virgin high-GWP refrigerants for servicing shifts the market toward reclaimed refrigerants and reduces the circulation of high-GWP gases, decreasing fugitive refrigerant emissions.

policy_statement: Create a registry and reporting requirements to track sales, stockpiles, and leaks for large refrigeration/HVAC systems and refrigerant wholesalers/distributors.
parent_statement: W5 reduces refrigerant leaks and ensures destruction at end-of-life through DEC regulations, EPR legislation, sales bans for virgin high-GWP servicing refrigerants, reporting registries, and research.
verbatim_text: - Reporting: DEC should create a registry and reporting requirements (to track sales, stockpiles, and leaks) for large refrigeration and heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems and refrigerant wholesalers and distributors.
extraction_rationale: Registry/reporting system is a concrete policy tool under W5.
mechanism_description: Create refrigerant tracking and reporting systems that enable identification and reduction of refrigerant leaks, lowering high-global-warming-potential fugitive emissions.

policy_statement: Research end-of-life management approaches for various refrigerants and alternatives.
parent_statement: W5 reduces refrigerant leaks and ensures destruction at end-of-life through DEC regulations, EPR legislation, sales bans for virgin high-GWP servicing refrigerants, reporting registries, and research.
verbatim_text: - Research: DEC should research end-of-life management for various refrigerants and their alternatives.
extraction_rationale: Research commitment under W5.
mechanism_description: Research on refrigerant end-of-life practices supports improved refrigerant recovery and destruction, reducing fugitive emissions of high-global-warming-potential gases.

section_header: W6. Reduce Fugitive Emissions of Methane and Co-Pollutants from Solid Waste Management Facilities
policy_statement: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
verbatim_text: Identifying and reducing fugitive emissions of methane from landfills and anaerobic digesters through baseline measurement, increased monitoring, and engineering and regulatory programs can reduce leaks and provide facilities with comprehensive data on their operations.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a components list including regulatory changes, monitoring mandates, and funding.
mechanism_description: Enhanced monitoring and regulation of landfills and digesters identifies leaks and drives mitigation actions that reduce fugitive methane emissions.

policy_statement: Enhance landfill regulations before 2025 to reduce methane leakage (earlier gas collection installation, specialty collectors, improved dewatering).
parent_statement: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
verbatim_text: - Landfill gas capture for active landfills: DEC should enhance existing regulations, before 2025, for landfills that will lead to less methane leakage, such as a requirement to install gas collection systems sooner after waste placement, install specialty landfill gas collectors for difficult to access areas, and enhance gas dewatering systems to increase collection efficiency.
extraction_rationale: Specific regulatory enhancement with timeline (before 2025) under W6.
mechanism_description: Tightened landfill rules require earlier and more effective gas collection upgrades, increasing methane capture and reducing methane leakage from active landfills.

policy_statement: Provide additional funding for the municipal landfill closure and landfill gas management grant program to control emissions from closed landfills.
parent_statement: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
verbatim_text: - Control emissions from closed landfills: The State should provide additional funding for the municipal landfill closure and landfill gas management grant program.
extraction_rationale: Funding expansion commitment under W6.
mechanism_description: Funding landfill closure and landfill gas control increases capture/management of landfill gas, reducing methane emissions from closed landfills.

policy_statement: Develop regulations for enhanced landfill covers to increase methane oxidation.
parent_statement: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
verbatim_text: - Enhanced landfill cover systems: DEC should develop regulations for enhanced landfill covers to increase oxidation of methane.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory development action under W6.
mechanism_description: Regulating enhanced landfill covers increases in-situ oxidation of methane, thereby lowering landfill methane emissions released to the atmosphere.

policy_statement: Require improved emissions monitoring at applicable solid waste facilities using new technologies (e.g., drones) and require emissions reductions at these facilities.
parent_statement: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
verbatim_text: - Emission monitoring and emission reduction: DEC should require improved emissions monitoring programs at all applicable solid waste management facilities, utilizing new technologies (such as drones). Also, DEC should require reductions in emissions at these facilities.
extraction_rationale: Monitoring mandate plus reduction requirement under W6.
mechanism_description: Require advanced monitoring at solid waste facilities to detect emissions and enforce mitigation actions, resulting in reduced methane and other facility air emissions.

policy_statement: Provide funding for proper closure of municipal landfills to reduce emissions.
parent_statement: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
verbatim_text: - Funding to aid municipal landfills:  he State should provide funding for the proper closure of municipal landfills and reduce emissions.
extraction_rationale: Funding commitment under W6.
mechanism_description: Provide funding to properly close municipal landfills to prevent methane and other landfill gas emissions, reducing overall GHG emissions from waste sites.

policy_statement: Research landfill and anaerobic digestion emissions to evaluate monitoring techniques, quantify fugitive emissions, and evaluate best gas uses during electrification transition; research regulatory frameworks/best practices to minimize methane and co-pollutant emissions from AD/RNG.
parent_statement: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
verbatim_text: - Research: DEC should research comprehensive landfill GHG emissions to evaluate monitoring techniques, quantify fugitive GHG emissions, and evaluate most appropriate uses for the gas during the transition to statewide electrification. DEC should research methods to improve estimates of methane emissions from anaerobic digestion and strategies to reduce or eliminate these emissions, including consideration of a regulatory framework to ensure best practices to minimize methane and co-pollutant emissions from anerobic digestion and RNG production.
extraction_rationale: Explicit research program including regulatory framework consideration under W6.
mechanism_description: Research and improved monitoring/regulatory best practices for landfill and anaerobic digestion/RNG systems enable identification and mitigation of fugitive methane, reducing overall greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Use regulations, monitoring, and enforcement to reduce co-pollutant impacts from solid waste management facilities and enact legislation to reduce toxics in consumer products and support EPR to reduce co-pollutants.
parent_statement: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
verbatim_text: - Co-pollutant control: DEC should use regulations, monitoring, and enforcement to reduce the impact from co-pollutants associated with solid waste management facilities. The State should enact legislation to support reduction of toxic substances in consumer products, as well as EPR (see Strategy W2 and Strategy W3), since these efforts will also reduce the potential for copollutants present at solid waste management facilities.
extraction_rationale: Specific control mechanisms plus legislative action referenced under W6.
mechanism_description: Tightening regulation, monitoring, enforcement, and extended producer responsibility reduces toxic substances in products and controls releases at solid waste facilities, lowering co-pollutant emissions and exposures.

section_header: W7. Reduce Fugitive Emissions of Methane and Co-Pollutants from Water Resource Recovery Facilities
policy_statement: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
verbatim_text: Municipalities will need to measure and reduce GHG emissions from WRRFs, septic, and sewer systems, which can be difficult without the proper equipment and training.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a component list including regulatory, funding, training, and research actions.
mechanism_description: Monitor and upgrade wastewater/septic/sewer operations to capture biogas and reduce leaks, thereby cutting fugitive methane (and associated co-pollutant) emissions.

policy_statement: Repair and consistently operate WRRF biogas equipment and identify strategic beneficial uses of captured biogas before flaring excess capacity.
parent_statement: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
verbatim_text: - Capture and beneficially reuse fugitive biogas: DEC, EFC, and NYSERDA should work with local utilities and municipalities to repair and consistently operate WRRF flares, boilers, engines, or other equipment on-site and identify strategic beneficial uses of captured biogas before flaring excess capacity (see Strategy W9).
extraction_rationale: Operational repair and beneficial use planning under W7.
mechanism_description: Improve capture and beneficial use of WRRF biogas so methane is controlled and used for energy rather than escaping or being wasted, thereby cutting methane emissions and displacing fossil generation.

policy_statement: Require emissions monitoring and leak reduction at WRRFs.
parent_statement: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
verbatim_text: - Monitoring: DEC should require emissions monitoring and leak reduction at WRRFs.
extraction_rationale: Clear regulatory requirement under W7.
mechanism_description: Requiring emissions monitoring and leak repairs at wastewater treatment facilities reduces fugitive greenhouse gas releases, lowering overall emissions.

policy_statement: Enact legislation establishing a municipal funding mechanism for routine septic system maintenance via contractual services.
parent_statement: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
verbatim_text: - Ensure proper maintenance of septic systems at the municipal level: The State should enact legislation to establish a municipal funding mechanism to allow contractual services for routine maintenance on septic systems.
extraction_rationale: Legislative funding mechanism under W7.
mechanism_description: Establishing funded, routine septic maintenance services reduces system failures and contamination, improving wastewater sanitation and protecting local water quality.

policy_statement: Provide financial assistance for sewer hookups to defray high upfront sewering costs.
parent_statement: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
verbatim_text: - Encouraging transition to sewer: The State should provide financial assistance for sewer hookups to defray high upfront costs of sewering.
extraction_rationale: Financial assistance commitment under W7.
mechanism_description: Providing subsidies for sewer hookups increases connections to sewer networks, moving wastewater to regulated treatment and improving sanitation outcomes.

policy_statement: Provide financial support and job training to wastewater system operators.
parent_statement: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
verbatim_text: - Operator training: The State should provide financial support and job training to wastewater system operators.
extraction_rationale: Workforce training and funding under W7.
mechanism_description: Provide funding and training to wastewater operators to improve treatment plant operations and maintenance, leading to better wastewater system performance and resilience.

policy_statement: Support research on WRRF emissions profiles and how emissions can be controlled.
parent_statement: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
verbatim_text: - Research: DEC should support research on the emission profile of WRRFs and how they can be controlled.
extraction_rationale: Explicit research support under W7.
mechanism_description: Funding and conducting research on WRRF emissions profiles improves understanding of mitigation options, enabling implementation of emissions controls that can reduce facility-related GHGs.

policy_statement: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
verbatim_text: Implementing additional funding resources for municipalities and implementing sound post-consumer content requirements across different sectors can make secondary material processing cost competitive with virgin materials...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components including legislation, procurement, tax credits, and studies.
mechanism_description: Support for recycling facilities, recycled-content rules, procurement, and incentives increases recycling and secondary material processing, diverting waste from landfills and avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Support domestic recycling facilities/markets and enact recycled content requirements for certain products and packaging; incentivize public-private partnerships for recycling facility development.
parent_statement: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
verbatim_text: - Market development for recovered resources: The State should support domestic recycling facilities and markets for recovered resources (including compost, digestate, and recycled aggregate/building deconstruction materials) and incentivize public-private partnerships for recycling facility development. The State should also enact legislation to require a minimum level of recycled content in certain products and packaging.
extraction_rationale: Explicit market support plus legislative recycled-content requirement under W8.
mechanism_description: Supporting recycling facilities/markets and mandating recycled content increases recycling and use of recovered materials, reducing landfill disposal and avoiding emissions from both landfill methane and virgin material production.

policy_statement: Provide financial assistance to research increased capture and reuse of building deconstruction materials and recovered aggregate, including potential government procurement/bid requirements.
parent_statement: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
verbatim_text: - Reuse of building materials: The State should provide financial assistance to research the increase of capture and reuse of building deconstruction materials and recovered aggregate. This may include government requirements (such as procurement standards and bid specifications) to include recycled or reused deconstruction materials.
extraction_rationale: Financial assistance for research and potential procurement requirements under W8.
mechanism_description: Funding and procurement standards increase reuse of deconstruction materials, displacing virgin material production and reducing embodied greenhouse gas emissions from construction.

policy_statement: Enhance and implement green procurement programs requiring use of recyclables (e.g., compost, construction aggregate) by State/local entities and contractors.
parent_statement: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
verbatim_text: - Recyclables in green procurement: DEC and OGS should enhance and implement new green procurement programs to require the use of recyclables (such as compost and construction aggregate) by State and local entities and those contracting with the government.
extraction_rationale: Procurement requirement under W8.
mechanism_description: Mandating recycled-content purchasing by public entities and contractors increases end-market demand for recyclables, leading to more materials recovered/used and fewer emissions from producing and extracting virgin materials.

policy_statement: Enact a production tax credit for companies that turn recycled materials into intermediate products if they locate facilities in New York.
parent_statement: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
verbatim_text: - Production tax credit for recycled products: The State should enact a production tax credit, similar to the credit for clean energy systems, that is applied to companies that turn recycled materials into intermediate products if they locate their facilities in New York.
extraction_rationale: Explicit tax credit legislation under W8.
mechanism_description: A production tax credit incentivizes recycled-material processing/manufacturing, expanding end-markets for recyclables and displacing more carbon-intensive virgin material production, reducing lifecycle emissions.

policy_statement: Conduct an organics market study (organics roadmap) on quantities/characteristics and end uses of organics statewide.
parent_statement: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
verbatim_text: - Organics roadmap: DEC should conduct a market study of the quantity and characteristics of organics (food waste, biosolids, other high strength waste) produced statewide, including possible end uses of such products (such as agriculture, mine reclamation, roadside soil amendments, recycled bioproducts such as bioplastics, and erosion control).
extraction_rationale: Specific statewide study commitment under W8.
mechanism_description: Conducting a statewide organics market study identifies quantities and viable end uses for organic wastes, enabling future diversion/beneficial use programs that reduce landfill disposal and associated methane emissions.

policy_statement: Continue financial support to NY colleges/universities for research on recycling market challenges (plastics, low-grade paper, glass innovations).
parent_statement: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
verbatim_text: - Research on markets: The State should continue financial support of colleges and universities within New York in researching recycling market challenges, plastics recycling, low-grade paper recycling, and glass processing innovations for New York.
extraction_rationale: Research funding support under W8.
mechanism_description: Provide financial support for university research to solve recycling market and processing challenges, enabling higher recycling rates and reduced disposal-related emissions.

policy_statement: Increase State purchasing of products with recycled content and recycled products (e.g., compost).
parent_statement: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
verbatim_text: - State purchasing: State agencies, authorities, and local governments should support greater purchasing of products with recycled content as well as the purchase of recycled products (compost, etc.).
extraction_rationale: Procurement behavior commitment under W8.
mechanism_description: Government procurement of recycled-content products and compost increases end-market demand for recovered materials and organics, supporting diversion from landfill and reducing landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: W9 supports strategic biogas use during the transition by evaluating local uses with air quality/health/GHG safeguards, developing pricing models and funding for organics infrastructure, and conducting comprehensive emissions research.
verbatim_text: The limited supply of biogas or RNG should be targeted to strategic uses such as locations where it can provide electric system capacity for buildings and transportation electrification by alleviating system constraints.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components specifying evaluation, pricing models, funding mechanisms, and research studies.
mechanism_description: Directing biogas/RNG to constrained parts of the energy system provides dispatchable capacity that eases grid limits, enabling electrification of buildings and transport and reducing fossil-fuel combustion emissions.

policy_statement: Evaluate strategic and local uses of biogas-derived fuels/electricity/energy stressing fuel cells, waste transport fuels, co-location/cogeneration, hard-to-electrify uses, and requiring demonstration of air quality/health/GHG benefits and avoiding localized pollution in Disadvantaged Communities.
parent_statement: W9 supports strategic biogas use during the transition by evaluating local uses with air quality/health/GHG safeguards, developing pricing models and funding for organics infrastructure, and conducting comprehensive emissions research.
verbatim_text: - Strategic use of biogas: NYSERDA and New York State Department of Public Service (DPS), along with utilities, should evaluate strategic and local uses of generated fuels, electricity, or other energy produced from biogas for essential needs during the transition to electrification and other low-emissions energy sources. This evaluation should stress the use of fuel cells for electricity in lieu of generators or pipeline use... This evaluation should demonstrate air quality, health, and GHG benefits before implementation, including requirements to avoid localized pollution in Disadvantaged Communities.
extraction_rationale: Detailed evaluation requirement and safeguards under W9.
mechanism_description: Promoting targeted, local biogas-to-energy uses (e.g., fuel cells and cogeneration) displaces fossil energy while capturing/using biogas that could otherwise contribute methane emissions, contingent on demonstrating net air-quality and GHG benefits.

policy_statement: Identify energy pricing models and conduct a market-based study for waste-generated biogas; require AD incentive programs to use maximum methane mitigation system designs/retrofits; provide a funding mechanism to support organics recycling infrastructure.
parent_statement: W9 supports strategic biogas use during the transition by evaluating local uses with air quality/health/GHG safeguards, developing pricing models and funding for organics infrastructure, and conducting comprehensive emissions research.
verbatim_text: - Funding for organics recycling infrastructure: NYSERDA and DPS, along with utilities, should identify energy pricing models and conduct a market-based study for waste-generated biogas. Programs that incentivize anerobic digestion should require systems be built (or retrofit) for maximum methane mitigation... The State should also provide a funding mechanism to support an organics recycling infrastructure.
extraction_rationale: Pricing model study plus conditional incentive design and infrastructure funding mechanism under W9.
mechanism_description: Support and incentivize organics recycling/anaerobic digestion with strong methane controls to divert organic waste from landfills, reducing methane emissions while recovering useful nutrients/energy.

policy_statement: Complete a comprehensive landfill gas and WRRF emissions research study to evaluate monitoring techniques, quantify fugitive emissions, evaluate appropriate gas uses, and improve AD emission estimates and mitigation methods.
parent_statement: W9 supports strategic biogas use during the transition by evaluating local uses with air quality/health/GHG safeguards, developing pricing models and funding for organics infrastructure, and conducting comprehensive emissions research.
verbatim_text: - Research: DEC should complete a comprehensive landfill gas and WRRF emissions research study. The study should evaluate emissions monitoring techniques, quantify fugitive emissions, and evaluate the most appropriate uses for the gas during the transition to statewide electrification.
extraction_rationale: Research study commitment under W9.
mechanism_description: Conducting a comprehensive emissions research and monitoring study improves measurement and understanding of landfill and WRRF fugitive emissions, enabling better mitigation strategies and reducing methane leakage.

section_header: LU1. Mitigate Carbon Emissions by Protection of Forest Lands
policy_statement: LU1 protects forest lands to mitigate carbon emissions via legislation, enhanced land acquisition/easement programs, increased funding, and a statewide target to acquire 500,000 forested acres by 2050.
verbatim_text: The State should implement the following tactics that keep forests as forests and maintain New York's forest carbon sequestration and storage levels and prevent emissions from development.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a components list including legislation and quantified acquisition goal by 2050.
mechanism_description: Protecting forest lands from conversion maintains existing carbon stocks and continued sequestration, avoiding emissions that would result from development.

policy_statement: Immediately enact a 'Keep Forests as Forests' law requiring developers to purchase and set aside forested land when forest carbon is lost during development, following avoid/minimize/mitigate principles.
parent_statement: LU1 protects forest lands to mitigate carbon emissions via legislation, enhanced land acquisition/easement programs, increased funding, and a statewide target to acquire 500,000 forested acres by 2050.
verbatim_text: - Enact 'Keep Forests as Forests' law: The State should immediately enact legislation to 'keep forest as forests,' requiring developers to purchase and set aside forested land when forest carbon is lost during development following the principles of avoid, minimize, and mitigate.
extraction_rationale: Explicit immediate legislation with a defined developer requirement under LU1.
mechanism_description: A conservation offset requirement protects forested land to compensate for development-related forest carbon loss, maintaining ecosystem integrity and stored carbon.

policy_statement: Enhance support for local land acquisition and conservation easements through programs such as the Community Preservation Act, Conservation Partnership Program, Forest Conservation Easements for Land Trusts, and Community Forest programs.
parent_statement: LU1 protects forest lands to mitigate carbon emissions via legislation, enhanced land acquisition/easement programs, increased funding, and a statewide target to acquire 500,000 forested acres by 2050.
verbatim_text: - Establish programs to support local land acquisition: DEC should considerably enhance support for local land acquisition and conservation easements by municipalities and land trusts through mechanisms such as the Community Preservation Act, Conservation Partnership Program, Forest Conservation Easements for Land Trusts, and Community Forest programs.
extraction_rationale: Program support expansion under LU1 with named mechanisms.
mechanism_description: Provide programs that enable land acquisition and conservation easements to prevent land conversion and preserve ecosystems and stored carbon.

policy_statement: Maintain and significantly increase land acquisition funding (fee and easements) by State agencies, municipalities, and land trusts.
parent_statement: LU1 protects forest lands to mitigate carbon emissions via legislation, enhanced land acquisition/easement programs, increased funding, and a statewide target to acquire 500,000 forested acres by 2050.
verbatim_text: - Maintain and increase State land acquisition: DEC should continue to maintain and significantly increase land acquisition (fee and conservation easement) funding by State agencies, municipalities, and land trusts.
extraction_rationale: Funding increase commitment under LU1.
mechanism_description: Increase funding for land acquisition and conservation easements to protect natural lands, avoiding land conversion and preserving ecosystems and stored carbon.

policy_statement: Collaboratively aim to acquire at least 500,000 forested acres by 2050, prioritizing highest carbon/climate/conservation benefit forests.
parent_statement: LU1 protects forest lands to mitigate carbon emissions via legislation, enhanced land acquisition/easement programs, increased funding, and a statewide target to acquire 500,000 forested acres by 2050.
verbatim_text: - Increase collaborative efforts for statewide forest acquisition: Statewide agencies and organizations, land trusts, programs, and local municipalities should collaboratively aim to expand upon historical land acquisition rates to acquire at least 500,000 forested acres by 2050, prioritizing forests with the highest quality carbon, climate, and other conservation benefits.
extraction_rationale: Quantified acreage target with deadline (2050) under LU1.
mechanism_description: Acquire and conserve forested land to prevent conversion and degradation, preserving stored carbon and ecosystem functions and thereby avoiding emissions.

policy_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: In order to ensure that reforestation and afforestation are effective GHG emissions mitigation strategies by 2030 and 2050, these efforts need to be started as soon as possible to allow time for seedling establishment.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with extensive components including quantified acreage/tree targets and program creation.
mechanism_description: Expand tree planting and reforestation to increase vegetative cover, improving ecosystem resilience and storing more carbon over time.

policy_statement: Develop a Statewide Reforestation and Afforestation Plan with goals for 2030/2040/2050 and encourage/support programs to establish and maintain 1.7 million acres (~680 million trees) by 2040.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: Develop a Statewide Reforestation and Afforestation Plan: DEC should work with other agencies and partners to develop a plan that sets ambitious and collaborative goals... for reforestation and afforestation by 2030, 2040, and 2050. State agencies, authorities, partners, and organizations should broadly encourage and support statewide tree planting, tree regeneration, and tree maintenance programs to establish and maintain 1.7 million acres (approximately 680 million trees) by 2040.
extraction_rationale: Explicit plan creation with deadlines and quantified acreage/tree target under LU2.
mechanism_description: Plan-and-program support for afforestation and reforestation expands and maintains tree cover, increasing biomass carbon sequestration and improving ecosystem resilience.

policy_statement: Identify and prioritize locations where afforestation/reforestation are most likely to succeed using data and expert input.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Prioritize locations for afforestation and reforestation: DEC should identify areas where afforestation and reforestation are the most likely to succeed using data provided by the Reforestation Hub, experts, and other authorities.
extraction_rationale: Specific siting prioritization action under LU2.
mechanism_description: Using data-driven site prioritization improves the success of afforestation/reforestation projects, increasing tree cover that sequesters carbon and restores ecosystems.

policy_statement: Develop partnerships and programs to reforest State rights-of-way (roadsides, pipelines, railroads, utility corridors) using compatible species and 'right tree, right place' outreach.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Reforest rights-of-way: DOT should work with public and private partners on reforestation efforts in right-of-way areas of the State... and develop programs for afforestation and reforestation in these locations. Public outreach for right tree, right place is needed.
extraction_rationale: Concrete partnership/program development under LU2.
mechanism_description: Reforesting linear rights-of-way increases vegetative cover, which sequesters carbon in biomass/soils and enhances ecosystem function over time.

policy_statement: Fund staffing and infrastructure expansions at the Colonel William F. Fox Memorial Saratoga Tree Nursery to support large-scale afforestation/reforestation and improve seed collection/storage and production practices.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Invest in and update the Colonel William F. Fox Memorial Saratoga Tree Nursery: The State should provide funding to increase the State tree nursery's staff and infrastructure capacity to support large-scale afforestation and reforestation efforts... and implementing upgrades to enhance seed collection, seed storage, seedling production, workforce development, and pre- and postplanting practices.
extraction_rationale: Explicit funding and capacity expansion at a named facility under LU2.
mechanism_description: Increase nursery capacity and planting support to enable more afforestation/reforestation, leading to greater carbon sequestration and improved ecosystem condition.

policy_statement: Create a statewide program/network to increase tree seed collection and efficiently deliver seeds to the State nursery for processing, storage, and use.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Increase and accelerate seed collection: DEC should create a program to increase statewide tree seed collection across the state. This program should establish a network of seed collectors and an efficient method to deliver seeds to the Colonel William F. Fox Memorial Saratoga Tree Nursery for processing, storage, and use.
extraction_rationale: Program creation with operational details under LU2.
mechanism_description: A statewide seed-collection and delivery network increases the supply of viable tree seed for planting, enabling more forest establishment/restoration that sequesters carbon and strengthens ecosystems.

policy_statement: Increase funding for Urban and Community Forestry Grants and other tree programs (Regenerate NY/ReLeaf Community Canopy) to support planning, planting, and maintenance including for private trees.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Increase grant program funding: The State should increase funding for the Urban and Community Forestry Grants... for planning, planting, and maintenance of trees. The State should also increase funding opportunities such as Regenerate NY or the ReLeaf program's Community Canopy program for private individuals to establish and maintain privately owned trees.
extraction_rationale: Funding expansion and named programs under LU2.
mechanism_description: Increasing grant funding enables more tree planning, planting, and maintenance, expanding urban tree canopy that sequesters carbon and reduces urban heat.

policy_statement: Develop an urban tree planting opportunity assessment to focus establishment/maintenance where greatest climate/societal/health benefits occur.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Prioritize locations for urban forest tree planting: ... DEC should develop an opportunity assessment to focus tree establishment and maintenance efforts within urban areas and communities where the most climate, societal, and public health benefits are likely to be achieved.
extraction_rationale: Specific assessment development action under LU2.
mechanism_description: Prioritizing and directing urban tree planting and maintenance increases urban canopy, which reduces heat impacts and sequesters carbon over time.

policy_statement: Develop guidance and provide support/funding to local communities for planning and implementing tree planting and maintenance projects (including shared resources).
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Provide guidance and support: DEC should work with partners to develop guidance and provide support and funding to local communities for planning and implementing planting and maintenance projects that help communities adapt to climate change. This may include sharing resources (like equipment, staff, and bulk ordering).
extraction_rationale: Guidance/support/funding actions under LU2.
mechanism_description: Support and funding enable communities to plant and maintain more trees, increasing canopy cover that reduces urban heat and sequesters carbon over time.

policy_statement: Expand funding for cost-share programs (e.g., Regenerate NY) to help landowners implement reforestation/afforestation while larger initiatives are established.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Fund cost-share programs: The State should continue to expand the funding for cost share programs, such as Regenerate NY, to assist forest landowners in widespread implementation of reforestation and afforestation efforts.
extraction_rationale: Explicit cost-share funding expansion under LU2.
mechanism_description: Provide cost-share funding to landowners to increase reforestation/afforestation, which expands forest cover and increases carbon sequestration and ecosystem resilience.

policy_statement: Develop a tree planting equipment loan program to provide access to specialized equipment for small- and large-scale planting projects.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Develop equipment loan program: The SWCC should develop a tree planting equipment loan program to give landowners and operators access to specialized equipment for small- and largescale tree planting projects.
extraction_rationale: Equipment loan program creation under LU2.
mechanism_description: Providing access to specialized tree-planting equipment enables more tree planting, which increases vegetative cover and carbon storage while improving land resilience (e.g., reducing erosion and land degradation).

policy_statement: Expand or create free tree seedling programs (e.g., Trees for Tributaries, Buffer in a Bag) and explore partnerships to scale programs.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Provide free tree seedlings: DEC should expand or create new, free tree seedling programs such as Trees for Tributaries and Buffer in a Bag programs to assist landowners with planting projects. DEC should also explore partnerships with local governments and regional organizations to scale up programs.
extraction_rationale: Program expansion/creation under LU2.
mechanism_description: Providing free tree seedlings and scaling planting partnerships increases tree cover, which sequesters carbon and helps mitigate heat and related climate impacts.

policy_statement: Establish a NY Tree or Climate Corps to provide direct tree establishment and maintenance services with regionally based teams and equipment.
parent_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
verbatim_text: - Establish NY Tree or Climate Corps: DEC should establish NY Tree or Climate Corp to provide direct tree establishment and maintenance services to public and private landowners. Staff for a NY Tree or Climate Corps would be regionally based...
extraction_rationale: Creation of a service corps is a specific institutional program action under LU2.
mechanism_description: A dedicated tree corps increases tree planting and long-term maintenance, expanding canopy and biomass that sequester carbon and reduce urban heat.

section_header: LG1.  Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard
policy_statement: Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard (LG1): develop a statewide dashboard of community GHG emissions inventories to support local planning, equity monitoring, progress measurement, and data consistency.
verbatim_text:
## LG1.  Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard

One strategy for supporting local climate action is to develop a statewide dashboard of community GHG emissions inventories to promote local climate action planning, monitor equity considerations, measure progress, and ensure data consistency at the county and municipal levels.
extraction_rationale: Creates a specific statewide instrument (dashboard) with defined purpose for tracking/monitoring emissions and equity.
mechanism_description: A standardized community GHG emissions dashboard improves data transparency and consistency, enabling better climate planning, equity monitoring, and progress measurement.

policy_statement: Establish a Community GHG Working Group led by NYSERDA and DEC to develop/standardize local community GHG emissions inventories and secure New York-specific data.
parent_statement: Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard (LG1): develop a statewide dashboard of community GHG emissions inventories to support local planning, equity monitoring, progress measurement, and data consistency.
verbatim_text:
- Form a community GHG working group: NYSERDA and DEC should establish a Community GHG Working Group consisting, as appropriate, of Metropolitan Planning Organizations (MPOs), utilities, State agencies, academic institutions, consultants, labor representation, community benefit organizations, and regional and municipal officials. The group may consider several activities:

- Review existing guidance including the International Council for Local Environmental Initiative's U.S. Community Protocol for Accounting and Reporting of Greenhouse Gas Emissions to identify methods
- Identify and secure New York-specific data needed to complete the emission inventories
- Develop standards for local community GHG emissions inventories
extraction_rationale: Concrete institutional action (establish working group) with defined tasks.
mechanism_description: Establishing a working group to standardize community GHG inventories and data improves emissions measurement and information quality, enabling more effective climate planning and management actions.

policy_statement: Incentivize development of software/tools to modernize GHG emissions accounting for local climate action, consistent with Climate Act methodology.
parent_statement: Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard (LG1): develop a statewide dashboard of community GHG emissions inventories to support local planning, equity monitoring, progress measurement, and data consistency.
verbatim_text: - Support modernization of GHG emission accounting to facilitate data accessibility: NYSERDA should incentivize software developers to create new software and other tools that allow for clear and transparent accounting of GHG emissions to inform and support local climate action. These tools should include considerations for the emissions accounting methodology required by the Climate Act.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (incentivize software developers) to create tools supporting accounting.
mechanism_description: Incentivizing modern GHG accounting tools improves the quality and accessibility of emissions data, which supports local climate planning and implementation decisions.

policy_statement: Launch and maintain the community GHG dashboard with accessible, secure, privacy-protecting inventory information.
parent_statement: Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard (LG1): develop a statewide dashboard of community GHG emissions inventories to support local planning, equity monitoring, progress measurement, and data consistency.
verbatim_text: - Launch the community dashboard: NYSERDA, working with DEC and community stakeholders, should launch and maintain the dashboard, ensuring that GHG emissions inventory information is accessible to all communities in a manner that ensures data security and protects privacy.
extraction_rationale: Action commitment to launch and maintain a statewide dashboard with specified safeguards.
mechanism_description: Publishing and maintaining a secure, privacy-protecting community GHG inventory dashboard improves emissions data availability, enabling better planning, tracking, and policy decisions to support climate action.

section_header: LG3. Provide Clean Energy Siting Support for Local Governments
policy_statement: Provide Clean Energy Siting Support for Local Governments (LG3) through model local laws and streamlined permitting for renewable energy and storage.
verbatim_text:
## LG3. Provide Clean Energy Siting Support for Local Governments

This strategy facilitates clean energy siting through development and promotion of model local laws and streamlined permitting.
extraction_rationale: Strategy states a concrete intervention (model laws, streamlined permitting) and has components.
mechanism_description: Streamlined local siting rules and permitting make it easier and faster to build renewable energy and storage projects, enabling more clean energy deployment and associated emissions reductions.

policy_statement: Develop and promote model local laws and development regulations for clean energy siting via Clean Energy Communities and Climate Smart Communities, minimizing impacts on forests/agricultural lands and considering co-location.
parent_statement: Provide Clean Energy Siting Support for Local Governments (LG3) through model local laws and streamlined permitting for renewable energy and storage.
verbatim_text: - Create model local laws and regulations: NYSERDA, DEC, and DOS should work with community stakeholders and the clean energy industry to develop and promote model local laws and development regulations through the Clean Energy Communities and Climate Smart Communities programs. These model local laws and development regulations should minimize the impact of clean energy siting on forests and agricultural lands, as well as consider colocational opportunities for agriculture and renewable energy generation.
extraction_rationale: Specific deliverable (model laws/regulations) with implementation channel and constraints.
mechanism_description: Developing and promoting model local siting laws and regulations facilitates renewable energy project approval and placement while minimizing impacts to forests and farmland and supporting co-location, enabling cleaner energy deployment with lower land-use disruption.

policy_statement: Within one year, promote local adoption of the New York State Solar Permit and other actions to streamline permitting for clean energy technologies including energy storage.
parent_statement: Provide Clean Energy Siting Support for Local Governments (LG3) through model local laws and streamlined permitting for renewable energy and storage.
verbatim_text: - Promote New York State Solar Permit Adoption: Within one year, NYSERDA and DOS should work with code enforcement officers to promote local adoption of the New York State Solar Permit and other local actions to streamline the permitting process for clean energy technologies, including energy storage, at a variety of scales.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound action ('Within one year') with specific instrument (NY State Solar Permit).
mechanism_description: Streamlining and standardizing permitting reduces administrative barriers and costs, enabling increased deployment of solar and energy storage that supports cleaner electricity supply.

section_header: LG5.  Provide State Support and Guidance
policy_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text:
## LG5.  Provide State Support and Guidance

This strategy discusses continuing and expanding program opportunities, incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and centralized procurement services to motivate local governments... This includes LED lighting, energy efficiency upgrades, heat pump projects, methane recovery for energy production from wastewater treatment and landfills, solar on municipal premises, and municipal and school district fleet electrification.
extraction_rationale: Umbrella strategy with multiple specific action components listed.
mechanism_description: State incentives, technical assistance, financing, and procurement support enable local governments to deploy a portfolio of clean energy projects that collectively reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Encourage local governments to benchmark and report municipal building/facility energy use.
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Encourage energy benchmarking: NYSERDA should encourage local governments to track and report the energy use of municipal buildings and facilities (benchmarking).
extraction_rationale: Specific action (encourage benchmarking) with implementing agency.
mechanism_description: Encouraging municipalities to benchmark and report building energy use increases transparency and accountability, creating a market/management signal that motivates energy-efficiency improvements.

policy_statement: Provide technical support to reduce municipal/public-entity building and operations energy use and GHG emissions through aggressive energy efficiency measures (including weatherization).
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Encourage energy efficiency: NYSERDA should work with community stakeholders to provide technical support to help local governments and related public entities reduce energy use and GHG emissions of buildings and operations through aggressive energy efficiency measures, including, but not limited to, weatherization.
extraction_rationale: Concrete support action (technical support) with defined objective (reduce energy/GHG).
mechanism_description: Technical assistance enables energy-efficiency retrofits such as weatherization in public buildings and operations, reducing energy use and the emissions tied to that energy consumption.

policy_statement: Provide technical support to local governments/public entities to develop and implement clean energy projects.
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Provide technical support for clean energy projects: NYSERDA should work with community stakeholders to provide technical support to help local governments and related public entities develop and implement clean energy projects.
extraction_rationale: Direct technical assistance commitment for project development/implementation.
mechanism_description: Provide technical assistance to public entities so they can plan and deliver clean energy projects, enabling greater clean energy deployment that can displace fossil energy use.

policy_statement: Evaluate options to reduce grid interconnection costs for municipally owned priority sites.
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Reduce grid interconnection costs: NYSERDA, in collaboration with the clean energy industry, should evaluate options to reduce interconnection costs for municipally owned priority sites.
extraction_rationale: Specific evaluative action aimed at lowering interconnection costs.
mechanism_description: Reducing the cost of grid interconnection lowers project barriers at municipal sites, enabling more renewable generation to connect to the grid and supporting eventual displacement of fossil-based electricity.

policy_statement: Prioritize funding for methane recovery projects at wastewater treatment and landfills for on-site energy production.
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Prioritize methane recovery: NYSERDA, working with community stakeholders, should prioritize funding for projects that recover methane from wastewater treatment and landfills for on-site energy production in a manner consistent with the strategies in Chapter 16. Waste (Strategy W4 and Strategy W9).
extraction_rationale: Resource allocation prioritization (funding) for a defined project type.
mechanism_description: Fund methane recovery and on-site energy projects at landfills and wastewater facilities to capture methane and generate energy, reducing fugitive methane emissions and displacing fossil-based generation.

policy_statement: Develop tools/resources to help municipalities procure energy and enable direct wholesale market energy purchases.
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Support direct energy purchasing: NYSERDA and DPS should develop tools and resources to help municipalities procure energy and enable direct purchases of energy by municipalities from the wholesale market.
extraction_rationale: Specific deliverable (tools/resources) enabling direct purchasing.
mechanism_description: Providing procurement tools enables municipalities to buy electricity directly in wholesale markets, facilitating cleaner power sourcing and associated emissions reductions from electricity use.

policy_statement: Support electrification of municipal and school district fleets while increasing fleet-wide fuel economy.
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Support fleet electrification: NYSERDA and DEC should support electrification of municipal and school district fleets while increasing fleet-wide fuel economy.
extraction_rationale: Concrete support action for fleet electrification.
mechanism_description: Electrifying public-sector vehicle fleets (and improving their efficiency) reduces fossil fuel use from driving, lowering transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Provide policy guidance and financial support to municipalities adopting building electrification and energy efficiency policies informed by city decarbonization plans (e.g., Ithaca).
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Support building electrification: NYSERDA should provide policy guidance and financial support to municipalities that adopt building electrification and energy efficiency policies that incorporate insights from cities like Ithaca that have made ambitious commitments and developed innovative decarbonization plans.
extraction_rationale: Commits financial support and guidance tied to adoption of local policies.
mechanism_description: Provide guidance and funding to enable municipal building electrification policies, leading to replacement of fossil-fueled building equipment with electric alternatives and reduced building greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Increase waste reduction and recycling rates in municipal operations and communities.
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Increase recycling and reduce waste: DEC should seek to increase waste reduction and recycling rates in municipal operations and in the community (see also Chapter 16. Waste Strategy W1, Strategy W2, and Strategy W8).
extraction_rationale: Agency action direction with clear outcome (increase reduction/recycling rates).
mechanism_description: Increasing waste reduction and recycling diverts material from landfills, reducing landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Encourage intermunicipal coordination and planning on cross-boundary issues and ease access to funding without penalizing Disadvantaged Communities lacking partnerships.
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Support intermunicipal planning and coordination on cross-boundary issues: NYSERDA, DEC, and DOS should encourage intermunicipal coordination and planning on cross-boundary issues while easing access to funding opportunities without penalizing Disadvantaged Communities that may not have similar partnerships in place.
extraction_rationale: Specific governance/support commitment with equity condition (do not penalize DACs).
mechanism_description: Promoting cross-jurisdiction planning and removing funding access barriers for disadvantaged communities increases institutional capacity and coordination to implement climate and resilience projects.

policy_statement: Provide educational materials and training to local governments/public entities so they understand available resources and are prepared to receive funding.
parent_statement: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
verbatim_text: - Support capacity-building for local governments and related public entities: The State should provide educational materials and training to local governments and related public entities, so that they understand what resources are available to them and are prepared to receive funding.
extraction_rationale: Capacity-building commitment via training/materials.
mechanism_description: Providing training and educational resources increases local governments’ ability to secure and deploy climate funding, enabling implementation of downstream mitigation and adaptation measures.

section_header: AR1.  Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan
policy_statement: Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan (AR1).
verbatim_text: ## AR1.  Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple implementation components listed below.
mechanism_description: Creating and updating an equitable adaptation and resilience plan improves coordination, prioritization, and preparedness, increasing the state’s capacity to manage and reduce climate risks.

policy_statement: Appoint a Chief State Resilience Officer (CSRO) to convene an adaptation and resilience sub-cabinet, oversee all State adaptation/resilience activities including bond expenditures, and ensure just transition principles are incorporated.
parent_statement: Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan (AR1).
verbatim_text: - Provide executive-level coordination of adaptation and resilience activities: The Governor should appoint a Chief State Resilience Officer (CSRO) who would convene an adaptation and resilience sub-cabinet. Importantly, the CSRO's portfolio must include all State adaptation and resilience activities, including oversight of relevant bond fund expenditures, and not be limited to disaster response. The CSRO must ensure incorporation of just transition principles into all adaptation and resilience activities and should report directly to the Director of State Operations.
extraction_rationale: Specific executive action (appoint CSRO) and defined governance/oversight requirements.
mechanism_description: Appointing a centralized resilience officer to coordinate agencies and oversee adaptation funding improves governance and implementation capacity, strengthening overall adaptive capacity and resilience outcomes.

policy_statement: Develop a comprehensive State climate change adaptation and resilience plan overseen by the CSRO and sub-cabinet, ensuring equitable application and consultation with Indigenous Nations.
parent_statement: Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan (AR1).
verbatim_text: - Develop an adaptation and resilience plan: The CSRO and adaptation and resilience subcabinet should oversee development of a comprehensive State climate change adaptation and resilience plan. This plan and the CSRO should ensure not only that programs are applied equitably, but that, as feasible, adaptation and resilience activities serve to ameliorate environmental, health, social, and economic inequities in historically marginalized communities and in Disadvantaged Communities. Planning should also include consultation with Indigenous Nations.
extraction_rationale: Direct commitment to produce a statewide plan with equity and consultation requirements.
mechanism_description: Establishing an overseen, equity-informed adaptation and resilience plan (including Indigenous consultation) strengthens institutions’ ability to plan, coordinate, and implement climate risk reduction actions.

policy_statement: Complete preliminary agency vulnerability assessments and adaptation plans and prioritize State adaptation/resilience projects (DEC with OGS support).
parent_statement: Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan (AR1).
verbatim_text: - Complete vulnerability assessments and adaptation plans: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (DEC), with support from New York State Office of General Services (OGS), should complete preliminary agency vulnerability assessments and adaptation plans and identify and prioritize State adaptation and resilience projects.
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning deliverables (assessments/plans) and prioritization directive.
mechanism_description: Conducting vulnerability assessments and developing adaptation plans enables agencies to identify risks and prioritize resilience investments, increasing institutional capacity to adapt to climate impacts.

policy_statement: Continue the ongoing update to the climate change assessment and initiate/fund additional research (NYSERDA/DEC or other agencies).
parent_statement: Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan (AR1).
verbatim_text: - Continue assessments and research: The New York State Energy Research and Development Authority (NYSERDA) should continue its ongoing update to the climate change assessment, and DEC or other agencies should initiate or fund additional research.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to ongoing assessment updates and additional research funding.
mechanism_description: Updating climate assessments and funding additional research improves climate data and analysis, which supports better-informed climate planning and implementation.

section_header: AR2.  Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies
policy_statement: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
verbatim_text: ## AR2.  Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple actionable components listed.
mechanism_description: Integrating equitable adaptation and risk-reduction requirements into state funding and regulatory programs changes how projects and policies are designed and selected, strengthening institutional capacity to reduce climate impacts and improve resilience outcomes.

policy_statement: Release DEC guidance on projected climatic changes and inequitable risk distribution to support decision-making.
parent_statement: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
verbatim_text: - Provide guidance on use of climate change projections: DEC should release guidance describing projected climatic changes, including the inequitable distribution of risks, impacts and vulnerabilities, to support relevant decision-making.
extraction_rationale: Specific deliverable (guidance) with equity content.
mechanism_description: Publish guidance on climate projections and inequitable risk distribution to improve how agencies and stakeholders incorporate climate risks into planning and decisions.

policy_statement: Adopt a process to integrate federal/State/local infrastructure investments for efficient and equitable land/resource use and consideration of adaptation/resilience (CSRO recommendations to Executive Chamber).
parent_statement: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
verbatim_text: - Coordinate infrastructure investments: The CSRO should provide recommendations to the Executive Chamber to adopt a process to ensure integration of federal, State, and local infrastructure investments to ensure efficient and equitable use of land and other resources, addressing historic and present climate and environmental injustices in infrastructure design, siting, and land use decisions, and consideration of adaptation and resilience.
extraction_rationale: Process change commitment aimed at integrated and equitable infrastructure planning.
mechanism_description: Coordinating multi-level infrastructure investment decisions through a formal process embeds equity and resilience criteria into planning and siting, improving overall adaptation readiness and institutional capacity.

policy_statement: Establish goals/processes/criteria and a formal policy to evaluate equity and justice impacts of State adaptation and resilience decisions, and provide guidance to prioritize action in Disadvantaged Communities.
parent_statement: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
verbatim_text: - Evaluate equity and justice: The State should establish a comprehensive set of goals, processes, and selection criteria for identifying and implementing adaptation and resilience projects that involve meaningful community consultation and empowerment, especially in historically marginalized communities and Disadvantaged Communities; develop a formal policy on evaluation of equity and justice impacts of State adaptation and resilience decisions, including consideration of benefit flows and evaluation of outcomes; and provide guidance on use of such evaluation to prioritize action in Disadvantaged Communities.
extraction_rationale: Explicit establishment of policy framework and guidance with DAC prioritization.
mechanism_description: Establishing an equity and justice impact assessment policy for adaptation decisions changes how projects are selected and prioritized, leading to more equitable and effective resilience investments in disadvantaged communities.

policy_statement: Convene a work group to adopt climate resilient design guidelines for State-funded projects (OGS and DEC).
parent_statement: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
verbatim_text: - Adopt resilient design guidelines: OGS and DEC should convene a work group to adopt climate resilient design guidelines for State-funded projects.
extraction_rationale: Concrete action to create statewide design guidelines via work group.
mechanism_description: Adopting climate-resilient design guidelines for state-funded projects leads to more climate-proof infrastructure and reduced vulnerability to climate hazards.

policy_statement: Amend the Smart Growth Public Infrastructure Policy Act and similar statutes to require consideration of climate hazards and climate equity and develop agency guidance.
parent_statement: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
verbatim_text: - Amend the Smart Growth Public Infrastructure Policy Act: The State should amend the Smart Growth Public Infrastructure Policy Act and similar statutes to require consideration of climate hazards and climate equity and development of guidance by relevant agencies.
extraction_rationale: Legislative amendment requirement with specified considerations (hazards, equity) and guidance.
mechanism_description: Require climate hazard and equity consideration in public infrastructure policy and agency guidance, improving planning and decision-making to support more resilient and equitable investments.

policy_statement: Establish policies/procedures requiring design professionals and contractors on State-funded projects to consider future climate conditions (OGS work group).
parent_statement: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
verbatim_text: - Enhance design capacity: OGS should convene a work group to establish policies and procedures to require design professionals and contractors on State-funded projects to consider future climate conditions.
extraction_rationale: Procedural requirement for State-funded projects with concrete implementation step.
mechanism_description: Require climate-informed design practices for state-funded projects so infrastructure is planned and built to withstand future climate conditions, reducing climate-related failure and disruption risk.
