parent_statement: Má-2 objective: Improve energy efficiency of transport infrastructure and support and develop environmentally friendly transport modes.
verbatim_text:
## Facilitation of the use of electric driven or low emission motor vehicles

M10

...The Municipality of Budapest can provide support... by offering free parking... and by establishing charging stations...

Target year: 2030 ... Target year value: 30% (225,000 pcs)
extraction_rationale: Specific e-mobility support measure with quantified target share and actions (parking incentives, charging stations).
mechanism_description: Providing public EV charging infrastructure (with supportive incentives) enables greater adoption of electric/low-emission vehicles, which supports downstream transport emissions reductions.

policy_statement: M11: Promote car-sharing and car-pooling, including establishing rules for an integrated community car rental/sharing system and encouraging providers to replace conventional vehicles with electric vehicles; also create car-pooling meeting points.
parent_statement: Má-2 objective: Improve energy efficiency of transport infrastructure and support and develop environmentally friendly transport modes.
verbatim_text:
## Promotion of the use of car-sharing and car-pooling systems

M11

...establish the rules of a uniform... integrated community system of car rental or car sharing...

Service providers need to be encouraged to gradually replace the vehicles using traditional fuels by electric vehicles.

...promote the use of car-pooling systems... by... establishing car-pooling meeting points...
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention package describing concrete institutional/regulatory and infrastructure actions.
mechanism_description: Promoting car-sharing and car-pooling (including shared-system rules and meeting points) increases shared trips and reduces single-occupancy driving, cutting fuel use and transport greenhouse-gas emissions.

policy_statement: M12: Implement traffic control measures including low-emission/climate protection zones and introduce a congestion charge for passenger traffic in 2021–2025 per the Budapest Mobility Plan, alongside P+R expansion (target 22,000 P+R spaces by 2030) and related parking reforms.
parent_statement: Má-2 objective: Improve energy efficiency of transport infrastructure and support and develop environmentally friendly transport modes.
verbatim_text:
## Traffic control to reduce emission, designation of low-emission zones and construction of the related infrastructure

M12

...The designation of climate protection zones...

As prognosticated in the BMP, the congestion charge would be introduced for passenger traffic in the period of 2021-2025.

...Target year: 2030 ... Target year value: 22,000 pcs
extraction_rationale: Regulatory/economic instruments (zones, congestion charging) with stated introduction period and quantified P+R target.
mechanism_description: Introduce low-emission zones and congestion charging (with supporting P+R/parking reforms) to reduce car travel and shift trips to lower-emission options, cutting transport greenhouse-gas emissions.

policy_statement: Aá-1 objective: Develop Budapest’s green-blue infrastructure to support adaptation (and co-benefits for mitigation).
verbatim_text: Objective Aá -1         Development of the green - blue infrastructure
extraction_rationale: Named umbrella objective followed by multiple letter/numbered measures (A1–A7) in the Adaptation Action Plan.
mechanism_description: Expanding and enhancing connected green and water-based urban infrastructure reduces heat stress and improves stormwater/flood management, strengthening climate resilience.

policy_statement: A1: Increase and improve green areas and water surfaces, including creating/expanding support instruments (e.g., Environmental Protection Fund) for green community areas, green roofs, roof gardens, and green walls, with a target green area intensity value increase (52.5% to 57%) by 2030.
parent_statement: Aá-1 objective: Develop Budapest’s green-blue infrastructure to support adaptation (and co-benefits for mitigation).
verbatim_text:
## Increasing the ratio of green areas and water surfaces

A1

...ratio of green areas needs to be increased... alternative green area elements, e.g. green roofs and green façades can be established...

...recommend to widen the scope and enhance the volume of tender funds which support the establishing of green community areas, green roofs, roof gardens, green walls (e.g. Environmental Protection Fund).

Base year value: 52.5% ... Target year value: 57%
extraction_rationale: Specific green-blue infrastructure measure with concrete instruments and quantified indicator/target.
mechanism_description: Funding and promoting more parks/green spaces, green roofs/walls, and water surfaces increases urban vegetation and blue-green cover, which cools the city and adds carbon sequestration benefits.

policy_statement: A2: Create new public-purpose green areas (parks, gardens, promenades) and improve quality; increase per-capita green area supply to 7 m²/person by 2030 and 9 m²/person by 2050.
parent_statement: Aá-1 objective: Develop Budapest’s green-blue infrastructure to support adaptation (and co-benefits for mitigation).
verbatim_text:
## Improving the ratio of green area and forest area

A2

...new green areas of public purposes... need to be created...

Base year value: 6m2/person

Target year: 2030 ... 7m2/person

Target year: 2050 ... Target year value: 9m2/person
extraction_rationale: Quantified per-capita green space targets with explicit milestone years.
mechanism_description: Expand and improve public green spaces to increase per-capita green area, which reduces urban heat impacts and improves stormwater absorption and climate resilience.

policy_statement: A3: Implement small watercourse revitalization programs (blue infrastructure), with a pilot on Rákos creek including an ecotourism corridor project to be implemented until 2024 and a target of 10 km revitalized sections by 2026.
parent_statement: Aá-1 objective: Develop Budapest’s green-blue infrastructure to support adaptation (and co-benefits for mitigation).
verbatim_text:
## Blue infrastructure development (revitalization of small watercourses)

A3

...Rákos creek can be a suitable pilot project...

...the Rákos creek ecotourism corridor project will be implemented until 2024...

Target year value: 10km
extraction_rationale: Specific adaptation infrastructure program with named pilot, timeframe, and a quantified target.
mechanism_description: Revitalizing small watercourses restores natural hydrology and aquatic ecosystems, improving flood/stormwater resilience and local climate adaptation capacity.

policy_statement: A4: Elaborate a 10-year rolling plan for planting trees in public spaces with annual timetables, technical preparation, and a communication protocol for tree felling/replacement; target managed public-space trees increasing from 107,000 (2020) to 130,000 by 2030.
parent_statement: Aá-1 objective: Develop Budapest’s green-blue infrastructure to support adaptation (and co-benefits for mitigation).
verbatim_text:
## Elaboration of a plan for planting trees in public spaces

A4

It is necessary to elaborate a 10 years' plan for planting trees in public spaces, then to continue rolling planning...

...a communication protocol needs to be elaborated for the felling of trees...

Base year value: 107,000 pcs; ... Target year value: 130,000 pcs;
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning and implementation measure with quantified tree target by 2030.
mechanism_description: A rolling public-space tree planting and replacement plan increases urban tree canopy, which sequesters carbon and reduces urban heat impacts.

policy_statement: A5: Initiate amendments to higher-level and municipal legal rules to strengthen tree protection, including expert involvement in felling decisions and options for compensation payments to the Environmental Fund; update Budapest’s Regulation No. 10/2005 and require relevant standards in public procurement.
parent_statement: Aá-1 objective: Develop Budapest’s green-blue infrastructure to support adaptation (and co-benefits for mitigation).
verbatim_text:
## Amendment of legal rules to protect trees

A5

It is necessary to initiate the amendment of higher-level legal rules to ensure the more effective protection of trees...

...it is also allowed to make payment into the Environmental Fund instead of replacing a tree...

The relevant regulation of the Municipality of Budapest (Regulation No. 10/2005. (III. 8.)...) requires some update...

In public procurement projects, the application of the standard 'MSZ 12172'... and 'MSZ 12042'... shall be required.

Timeframe: 2022
extraction_rationale: Explicit legal/regulatory change initiative with a timeframe and named instruments/standards.
mechanism_description: Stronger tree-protection rules reduce tree removal and loss of canopy, preserving carbon storage/sequestration and providing urban cooling benefits.

policy_statement: A6: Establish and operate a uniform, up-to-date green space cadastre for Budapest by 2024, expanding the FATÁR system to include district-managed green areas and ensuring methodology, data obligations, access, publicity, and integration.
parent_statement: Aá-1 objective: Develop Budapest’s green-blue infrastructure to support adaptation (and co-benefits for mitigation).
verbatim_text:
## Establishing and operation of a uniform, up-to-date green space cadastre

A6

It is necessary to create an up-to-date green space cadastre encompassing the entire public administrative area of Budapest... It is advisable to further develop the FATÁR application...

Timeframe: 2024
extraction_rationale: Specific institutional/data-system measure with deadline year and clear deliverable.
mechanism_description: Creating a comprehensive green space inventory and data platform improves visibility and management of urban green infrastructure, enabling better adaptation planning and implementation.

policy_statement: A7: Promote revitalization of brownfield areas by updating and publishing a brownfield cadastre and modifying urban planning instruments to prioritize green-area use and redirect development to brownfields rather than designating new buildable greenfield areas; support temporary greening on municipally managed properties.
parent_statement: Aá-1 objective: Develop Budapest’s green-blue infrastructure to support adaptation (and co-benefits for mitigation).
verbatim_text:
## Promotion of the revitalisation of brown-field areas

A7

By the regular updating and publishing of the cadastre of brown-field areas...

...urban planning instruments should be modified... giving priority to the use of land as green areas...

...developments should be diverted to brown-field areas instead of making greenfield investments... it is advisable not to designate any new areas to be built up in the Settlement Structure Plan...
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning/instrument commitments (cadastre updates; planning instrument modifications) aimed at brownfield-first development and greening.
mechanism_description: Redirecting development to brownfields and limiting new greenfield buildout preserves natural/green areas, maintaining ecosystem carbon storage and avoiding emissions from land conversion.

policy_statement: Aá-2 objective: Mitigate the urban heat island effect in the built environment.
verbatim_text: Objective Aá -2         Mitigation of the heat island effect in the built environment
extraction_rationale: Umbrella adaptation objective with sub-measures A8 and A9 described in the Adaptation Action Plan.
mechanism_description: Implement measures in the built environment to reduce the urban heat island effect, thereby lowering ambient temperatures and reducing heat exposure.

policy_statement: A8: Protect ventilation corridors through Budapest’s Settlement Structure Plan and Rules of Urban Planning by not supporting new buildable area designation or increased building heights in these corridors; compile a manual of good district examples to support climate-protection-related planning instrument review (continuous 2021–2030).
parent_statement: Aá-2 objective: Mitigate the urban heat island effect in the built environment.
verbatim_text:
## Protection of ventilation corridors, ensuring sufficient airspace ratio in streets

A8

...provide for the ventilation corridors... In these areas, the designation of new areas to be built up or the raising of the permitted building height may not be supported...

The goal is to compile a manual...

Timeframe: continuous (2021-2030)
extraction_rationale: Explicit regulatory/planning commitment plus a concrete deliverable (manual) with stated continuous timeframe.
mechanism_description: Restricting new buildable area designations and building height increases in ventilation corridors preserves urban airflow, helping reduce heat stress and stagnant-air pollution impacts.

policy_statement: A9: Prioritize climate protection in municipal public procurement and support schemes by promoting climate-friendly construction materials and technologies (e.g., permeable/high-albedo pavements, green roofs/façades) and consider adopting an environmental building rating scheme for municipal investments.
parent_statement: Aá-2 objective: Mitigate the urban heat island effect in the built environment.
verbatim_text:
## Promotion of climate friendly construction materials and technologies

A9

Climate protection has to be a priority criterion in the public procurement processes and the tender-based support schemes of the Municipality of Budapest...

Instead of traditional asphalt, 'cooler' and, if possible, permeable surfaces of higher albedo should be applied.

...it is advisable to introduce one of the environmentally friendly building rating schemes or an own scheme.
extraction_rationale: Concrete procurement/support policy direction specifying technologies and instruments (procurement criteria, rating schemes).
mechanism_description: Using municipal procurement criteria to favor cool/permeable surfaces and green roofs/façades changes built materials and designs, which reduces urban heat and improves stormwater management resilience.

policy_statement: Aá-3 objective: Develop the flood control system.
verbatim_text: Objective Aá -3         Development of the flood control system
extraction_rationale: Umbrella objective with sub-measure A10 in the Adaptation Action Plan.
mechanism_description: Developing flood control systems (e.g., drainage, levees, retention) reduces flooding frequency and impacts, improving climate resilience.

policy_statement: A10: Develop and further construct flood control structures to increase the length of flood protection line meeting DFE + 1.3m safety level from 33.73 km (2019) to 69 km by 2030.
parent_statement: Aá-3 objective: Develop the flood control system.
verbatim_text:
## Construction and development of flood control structures

A10

...the flood protection line needs to be developed urgently...

Indicator... Base year value: 33.73 km

Target year: 2030 ... Target year value: 69 km
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure development measure with quantified indicator and 2030 target.
mechanism_description: Build and extend flood control structures so more areas meet higher flood-protection standards, reducing flooding impacts and exposure.

policy_statement: Aá-4 objective: Implement adaptive rainwater management.
verbatim_text: Objective Aá -4         Adaptive rainwater management
extraction_rationale: Umbrella objective with sub-measures A11 and A12 in the Adaptation Action Plan.
mechanism_description: Implementing adaptive rainwater (stormwater) management reduces flooding and harmful runoff impacts by better capturing, storing, infiltrating, or routing rainwater during extreme precipitation.

policy_statement: A11: Develop a hybrid rainwater drainage management system for Budapest via review of sewage plans, inventory and modelling of drainage structures, identification of critical points, and creation of a mid- and long-term rainwater drainage concept; establish smart digital monitoring and control elements (continuous).
parent_statement: Aá-4 objective: Implement adaptive rainwater management.
verbatim_text:
## Surface runoff regulation and the system required for its safety

A11

In the case of Budapest, we could aim at the creation of a hybrid system...

...necessary to review general sewage plans... lay the foundations for modelling the universal rainwater drainage...

The complete midand long-term rainwater drainage concept for Budapest is to be created...

A smart, digital system needs to be established...
extraction_rationale: Concrete system-planning and infrastructure/ICT measure describing specific actions (review, modelling, concept, smart system).
mechanism_description: Improving and digitally monitoring/controlling stormwater drainage infrastructure increases system performance during heavy rain, reducing runoff impacts and urban flooding risk.

policy_statement: A12: Support on-site rainwater retention and utilization (and greywater use) through integrating such systems into public investments and creating regulatory instruments and support schemes for private investments; require/encourage delayed runoff, retention or reuse for major emitters (e.g., industry, malls, office blocks) (continuous).
parent_statement: Aá-4 objective: Implement adaptive rainwater management.
verbatim_text:
## Supporting the utilization and retention of rainwater

A12

...for private investments, the appropriate regulatory instruments... and the related support system should be elaborated...

In the case of major emitters (industry, shopping malls, office blocks), users should be made interested in applying or compelled to apply the delayed runoff, retention or secondary use of rainwater.
extraction_rationale: Policy includes regulatory/support mechanisms and explicitly notes compulsion/requirements for major emitters.
mechanism_description: Mandating and incentivizing on-site rainwater/greywater retention and reuse reduces stormwater runoff and peak flows, improving flood resilience and local water management.

policy_statement: Aá-5 objective: Prepare for extreme weather conditions and health impacts of climate change.
verbatim_text: Objective Aá -5         Preparation for extreme weather conditions and the health impacts of climate change
extraction_rationale: Umbrella objective with measures A13–A16.
mechanism_description: Developing climate and health preparedness planning increases institutional capacity to anticipate and respond to extreme weather and climate-related health risks.

policy_statement: A13: Adapt the transport system to extreme weather by incorporating climate considerations into design standards, materials and procurement; consider permeable pavements, tree planting/grassing of tracks, and develop emergency scenarios for service disruptions (continuous).
parent_statement: Aá-5 objective: Prepare for extreme weather conditions and health impacts of climate change.
verbatim_text:
## Preparation of the transport system for extreme weather phenomena

A13

Transport systems and public utility networks should be designed with consideration to the prognostications...

The aspects described above need to be enforced in the planning process, in public procurement process...

It is recommended to elaborate an emergency scenario for the resolution of operating disruptions caused by extreme weather conditions...
extraction_rationale: Specific adaptation actions affecting infrastructure design/procurement and emergency preparedness.
mechanism_description: Incorporating climate risk into transport design/procurement and preparing emergency response scenarios increases system resilience, reducing damage and service disruptions during extreme weather events.

policy_statement: A14: Ensure hazard control and emergency planning accounts for climate-change-related extreme events (drought, flood, flash flood, critical infrastructure damage) and review citizen information publications accordingly (continuous).
parent_statement: Aá-5 objective: Prepare for extreme weather conditions and health impacts of climate change.
verbatim_text:
## Managing hazards caused by extreme weather phenomena

A14

It is necessary to examine and ensure that emergency plans manage extreme events which may occur due to climate change (drought, flood, flash flood, damage to critical infrastructure, etc.), and to review the publication prepared to inform the citizens.

Timeframe: continuous
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to update emergency plans and public guidance for climate-related hazards.
mechanism_description: Integrating climate-driven extreme events into emergency plans and citizen information improves preparedness and response, reducing damages and health/safety impacts from climate hazards.

policy_statement: A15: Examine climate vulnerability of drinking water bases and supply and implement climate-adaptive development/operation, including capacity enhancements and reconstruction of water production wells; Budapest Waterworks has started development of 60 water production wells (timeframe 2022).
parent_statement: Aá-5 objective: Prepare for extreme weather conditions and health impacts of climate change.
verbatim_text:
## Examination of the climate vulnerability of water bases and drinking water supply and their climate-adaptive development and operation

A15

...there is a justified demand for elaborating operational changes for periods hit by extreme drought...

Budapest Waterworks has started the development of 60 water production wells as part of a project.

Timeframe: 2022
extraction_rationale: Concrete adaptation measure for water supply with specified actions and timeline.
mechanism_description: Upgrading and expanding drinking-water production well infrastructure and operations increases system capacity and robustness, improving resilience of water supply under climate stresses such as drought.

policy_statement: A16: Incorporate preparation for windstorms and flood/flash flood/inundation risks into preservation and renovation of municipal buildings, including attention to sizing of structures, basements, windows, sun shades, and waterproofing (by 2030).
parent_statement: Aá-5 objective: Prepare for extreme weather conditions and health impacts of climate change.
verbatim_text:
## Preparation for extreme weather conditions during the preservation and renovation of buildings

A16

...the Municipality has to pay priority attention concerning its own building stock to the proper sizing of support structures, basements, windows, sun shades and waterproofing sheets.

Timeframe: 2030
extraction_rationale: Defined adaptation action for municipal building asset management with a target year.
mechanism_description: Upgrade and design municipal building components during renovation to withstand windstorms and flooding, thereby reducing damage and service disruption risks from extreme weather.

policy_statement: Aá-6 objective: Mitigate vulnerability of natural and landscape values.
verbatim_text: Objective Aá -6         Mitigation of the vulnerability of natural and landscape values
extraction_rationale: Umbrella objective with measures A17–A19.
mechanism_description: Protecting and managing natural and landscape areas reduces their vulnerability to climate impacts and helps preserve ecosystem services and stored carbon.

policy_statement: A17: Prepare a detailed climate change risk and vulnerability assessment for natural values by 2023, to support nature conservation and invasive species control.
parent_statement: Aá-6 objective: Mitigate vulnerability of natural and landscape values.
verbatim_text:
## Preparation of the detailed climate change risk and vulnerability assessment for natural values

A17

...requires... the assessment and monitoring of risks and vulnerabilities.

Timeframe: 2023
extraction_rationale: Specific assessment deliverable with timeline, supporting adaptation/nature management.
mechanism_description: Conducting climate risk and vulnerability assessment and monitoring for natural ecosystems provides information that improves adaptation planning and prioritization for conservation and invasive species management.

policy_statement: A18: Expand protected natural sites of local importance, examining extensions continuously, targeting growth from 861 ha (2019) to 1,200 ha by 2026 (and referencing further planned additions).
parent_statement: Aá-6 objective: Mitigate vulnerability of natural and landscape values.
verbatim_text:
## Continuing the expansion and extension of protected natural sites of local importance

A18

...new areas are declared to be protected sites on an estimated area of 800 ha...

Base year value: 861 ha

Target year: 2026 ... Target year value: 1200 ha
extraction_rationale: Land protection expansion measure with quantified baseline and target area.
mechanism_description: Expanding protected natural areas prevents land-use change and habitat degradation, preserving carbon stored in ecosystems and maintaining ecological resilience.

policy_statement: A19: Implement environmental/nature conservation management including confinement of invasive plant and animal species in protected natural sites, supported by continuous monitoring and data collection; target is to reduce the highest coverage ratio of invasive plant species by 2030.
parent_statement: Aá-6 objective: Mitigate vulnerability of natural and landscape values.
verbatim_text:
## Environmental protection management, confinement of invasive plant and animal species

A19

One of the most important tasks of this management is to confine invasive species...

Indicator... Base year value: 18%

Target year: 2030 ... Target year value: it should be reduced
extraction_rationale: Ongoing management measure with an explicit performance indicator direction and target year.
mechanism_description: Managing and monitoring protected natural areas to confine invasive species helps maintain healthy ecosystems, supporting biodiversity and preserving ecosystem functions that contribute to climate resilience and carbon storage.

policy_statement: SZ-1 objective: Implement climate-conscious city management, with cooperative leadership by the Municipality taking the lead in climate protection.
verbatim_text: Objective SZ-1          Climate conscious city management: cooperative city management taking the lead in climate protection
extraction_rationale: Named umbrella awareness/city management objective with measures SZ1–SZ4 described subsequently.
mechanism_description: Municipal leadership and coordinated climate-conscious management strengthen institutional capacity to plan and implement climate protection measures, enabling broad emissions reductions over time.

policy_statement: SZ1: Promote climate-conscious operation within the Mayor’s Office and its companies through supported initiatives and training, targeting 1,500 persons participating in training by 2030.
parent_statement: SZ-1 objective: Implement climate-conscious city management, with cooperative leadership by the Municipality taking the lead in climate protection.
verbatim_text:
## Climate conscious workplace, climate conscious colleagues

SZ1

The goal of the measure is to support and encourage initiatives promoting the climateconscious operation of the Mayor's Office and its companies and to introduce climate protection measures.

Target year value: 1,500 persons
extraction_rationale: Specific organizational measure with quantified participation target and timeframe.
mechanism_description: Provide climate-focused training and support initiatives to improve organizational capacity and practices, enabling implementation of emissions-reducing measures across operations.

policy_statement: SZ2: Integrate horizontal climate protection principles into urban development and sectoral strategies, climate due diligence for major investments, and into decrees and tender criteria; ongoing continuous implementation.
parent_statement: SZ-1 objective: Implement climate-conscious city management, with cooperative leadership by the Municipality taking the lead in climate protection.
verbatim_text:
## Enforcement of horizontal climate protection principles...

SZ2

This measure is intended to integrate climate protection measures and/or horizontal climate protection principles during the preparation and review of urban development and thematic sectoral strategies...

It is also necessary to enforce climate protection principles... when the criteria for the tenders announced by the capital city are determined.

Timeframe: continuous
extraction_rationale: Cross-cutting governance commitment describing specific integration points (strategies, investments, decrees, tenders).
mechanism_description: Integrating climate criteria into strategies, major investment due diligence, and procurement rules shifts public decisions and spending toward lower-emission alternatives, enabling economy-wide emissions reductions over time.

policy_statement: SZ3: Operate the Budapest Climate Change Platform and an online platform to share best practices and build partnerships, targeting 20 sessions and 30,000 visitors by 2030.
parent_statement: SZ-1 objective: Implement climate-conscious city management, with cooperative leadership by the Municipality taking the lead in climate protection.
verbatim_text:
## Operation of the Budapest Climate Change Program and a dedicated online platform...

SZ3

...The platform facilitates... sharing of best practices... comprehensive follow-up... and the creation of a database...

Target year: 2030 ... Target year value: 20 sessions, 30,000 visitors
extraction_rationale: Specific institutional platform measure with quantified output indicators and target year.
mechanism_description: Operating climate change platforms improves coordination, information sharing, and partnerships, which strengthens institutional capacity to plan and implement mitigation and adaptation actions.

policy_statement: SZ4: Establish cooperation with the corporate sector to support Budapest’s climate objectives, including involving companies in financing/implementing mitigation, adaptation and awareness measures and organizing climate-friendly alliances, voluntary commitments, awards and competitions (continuous to 2030).
parent_statement: SZ-1 objective: Implement climate-conscious city management, with cooperative leadership by the Municipality taking the lead in climate protection.
verbatim_text:
## Establishing cooperation with the corporate sector...

SZ4

The purpose of the measure is to establish cooperation between the Municipality of Budapest and companies and to involve companies in the implementation and financing of the mitigation, adaptation and awareness-raising measure of Budapest...

The Municipality of Budapest also wishes to promote climate-conscious city management by organising and coordinating climate-friendly alliances with companies, voluntary commitments, awards and competitions.
extraction_rationale: Defined cooperation/engagement measure with clear mechanisms (alliances, commitments, awards) and implementation intent.
mechanism_description: The city partners with companies through alliances and voluntary commitments to leverage private funding and implementation, enabling broader mitigation and adaptation actions.

section_header: 4.1.   REDUCTION OF THE EMISSION OF GREENHOUSE GASES (MITIGATION)
policy_statement: Budapest has set a target to reduce its greenhouse gas emissions by at least 40% by 2030 compared to the 2015 base year.
verbatim_text:
Accordingly, the Municipality of Budapest has set the target to reduce its emissions by 40% by 2030 compared to the base year of 2015.

Chart 2: The decarbonisation target of Budapest

2015: 6.11 million t CO2; 2030: -40%
extraction_rationale: Quantified mitigation target with deadline and stated baseline year.
mechanism_description: Setting a citywide GHG reduction target drives planning and implementation across sectors to achieve an overall emissions reduction trajectory.

section_header: 4.1.   REDUCTION OF THE EMISSION OF GREENHOUSE GASES (MITIGATION)
policy_statement: By joining the Under 2 Coalition, Budapest committed to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by at least 80% by 2050 compared to 1990 levels and/or reduce annual per-capita emissions below 2 metric tons.
verbatim_text: By joining the Coalition, the parties undertake to reduce their emission of greenhouse gases by 80% as a minimum by 2050 compared to the values in 1990 and/or reduce their annual per capita emission below 2 metric tons.
extraction_rationale: Long-term quantified commitment (percentage reduction and/or per-capita threshold) with a deadline year.
mechanism_description: Setting an economy-wide greenhouse-gas reduction target establishes a jurisdiction-wide pathway that drives policies and investments to reduce overall emissions.

section_header: SZ-2 Climate conscious citizens: strengthening the environmental awareness and responsibility among the population and economic actors
policy_statement: Carry out thematic awareness-raising campaigns and informational activities via the Mayor’s Office and Budapest business organisations, focusing on reducing household energy consumption, with annual financing of HUF 100 million and 2030 energy/CO2 targets.
verbatim_text:
Thematic  awareness-raising  campaigns  and  informational  activities  through  the Mayor's  Office  and  the business  organisations  of  Budapest,  with  special  regard  to reducing the volume of household energy consumption

| Necessary financing | HUF 100 million per annum |
| ... |
| Target year: 2030 | Target year value: 432,496 MWh; 85,744 tCO 2 /annum |
extraction_rationale: This is a defined municipal measure (SZ5) with specified activities plus committed annual financing and quantified 2030 mitigation targets (MWh saved and tCO2/yr).
mechanism_description: Public awareness-raising and informational activities encourage households to adopt energy-saving behaviors, leading to lower residential energy consumption and associated emissions.

section_header: Developing the adaptation knowledge and ability of the population, particularly in respect of heat waves, rainwater management, property protection and green infrastructure
policy_statement: Develop the adaptation knowledge and ability of the population (heat waves, rainwater management, property protection, green infrastructure) through ongoing information/awareness actions, targeting 100,000 people reached by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Developing  the  adaptation  knowledge  and  ability  of  the  population,  particularly  in respect of heat waves, rainwater management, property protection and green infrastructure

| Indicator | Number of persons reached actively and passively |
| Base year: 2020 | Base year value: n.a. |
| Target year: 2030 | Target year value: 100 000 |
extraction_rationale: Concrete adaptation measure (SZ6) with an explicit quantified target (people reached by 2030) and described intervention focus areas.
mechanism_description: Public information and awareness actions increase residents’ knowledge and preparedness, leading to improved adoption of heat, stormwater, and property-protection measures that strengthen climate resilience.

section_header: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas
policy_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
verbatim_text:
This section summarises a large number of measures, with councils, committees and executive boards designated to assume responsibility for helping to achieve the climate goals by 2030.
The following tables list approximately 150 measures divided into the five transition areas. ... New measures will be gradually generated within the five transition areas.
extraction_rationale: Introduces a named, structured set of measures with responsible entities and ongoing development; serves as an umbrella for many specific sub-measures.
mechanism_description: Assigning accountable bodies to implement and continuously develop a portfolio of climate measures strengthens execution capacity and drives progress toward economy-wide emissions reductions.

section_header: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas
policy_statement: Develop a City-wide forum to coordinate applications for external financial support (2024–2030).
parent_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
verbatim_text: 0.2 Develop a City-wide forum for the coordination of applications for external financial support.
extraction_rationale: Specific institutional intervention with implementation period in the measures table.
mechanism_description: Establishing a city-wide coordination forum streamlines applications for external funding, increasing institutional capacity and resources to implement climate mitigation and adaptation projects.

section_header: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas
policy_statement: Develop work with digital tools in the climate transition including IoT and AI, as well as data management, structuring and visualisation (2024–2030).
parent_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
verbatim_text: 0.3 Develop work with digital tools in the climate transition including IoT and AI, as well as data management, structuring and visualisation.
extraction_rationale: Concrete enabling measure specifying technologies and timeframe.
mechanism_description: Deploying digital monitoring and data management tools improves climate-related information and analytics, supporting better planning and implementation of mitigation and adaptation actions.

section_header: Transition area 1: Work to achieve a just, inclusive climate transition
policy_statement: Develop labour market and education initiatives to meet the climate transition’s skills supply needs (2024–2030).
parent_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
verbatim_text: 1.3 Develop labour market and education initiatives with a focus on meeting the skills supply needs required by the climate transition.
extraction_rationale: Specific programmatic initiative (skills supply) with duration indicated in the measures table.
mechanism_description: Workforce and education programs increase the supply of relevant skills, enabling faster and broader deployment of climate mitigation and adaptation actions.

section_header: Transition area 1: Work to achieve a just, inclusive climate transition
policy_statement: Develop local arenas for collaboration where municipality, business and civil society collaborate to improve health and reduce climate impact (2024–2030).
parent_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
verbatim_text: 1.6 Develop local arenas for collaboration, where the municipality, business and civil society collaborate to improve health and reduce climate impact.
extraction_rationale: Specified intervention (local collaboration arenas) with timeframe.
mechanism_description: Establishing local collaboration arenas aligns municipal, business, and civil-society efforts, enabling coordinated implementation of measures that reduce emissions and improve health.

section_header: Transition area 1: Work to achieve a just, inclusive climate transition
policy_statement: Create extended opportunities for Stockholmers to rent, repair, exchange, share, borrow or give instead of buying new (2024–2026).
parent_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
verbatim_text: 1.9 Create extended opportunities for Stockholmers to rent, repair, exchange, share, borrow or give instead of buying new.
extraction_rationale: Concrete consumption-reduction intervention (sharing/repair) with defined implementation period.
mechanism_description: Expand reuse/repair/sharing options so fewer new products are manufactured, reducing materials production and the embodied greenhouse-gas emissions associated with goods.

section_header: Transition area 1: Work to achieve a just, inclusive climate transition
policy_statement: Collaborate with civil society and business to reduce plastics use and increase reuse and recycling of plastics (2024–2030).
parent_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
verbatim_text: 1.13 Collaborate with civil society and business to reduce the use of plastics and increase the reuse and recycling of plastics.
extraction_rationale: Specific collaboration action targeting plastics with timeframe.
mechanism_description: Reduce plastic use and increase reuse/recycling so less waste is landfilled, avoiding landfill-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Transition area 2 measures: Electricity grid and electricity generation
policy_statement: Strive to strengthen the electricity grid in Stockholm where expansion is needed and coordinate planning between grid companies and the City (2024–2030).
parent_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
verbatim_text: 2.1 Strive to strengthen the electricity grid in Stockholm where there is a need for expansion, and coordinate planning between electricity grid companies and the City.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure/planning action with stated timeframe.
mechanism_description: Upgrading and coordinating electricity grid expansion increases grid capacity and reliability, enabling greater electrification and renewable integration that supports emissions reductions.

section_header: Transition area 2 measures: Electricity grid and electricity generation
policy_statement: Install solar panels on existing and new City-managed buildings based on a cost-efficiency principle (2024–2030).
parent_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
verbatim_text: 2.2 Installation of solar panels based on a cost- efficiency principle on existing and new buildings managed by the City of Stockholm.
extraction_rationale: Concrete renewable generation deployment measure with duration.
mechanism_description: Deploying solar PV on municipal buildings generates renewable electricity that replaces fossil electricity generation, reducing power-sector emissions.

section_header: Transition area 2 measures: District heating and waste management
policy_statement: Commission the RUS (Resource Extraction Stockholm) facility in Högdalen for sorting food waste, plastic and metal (2024).
parent_statement: Tables presenting measures in the transition areas: implement and manage approximately 150 assigned measures (with designated responsible bodies) to achieve the climate goals by 2030, with new measures generated over time.
verbatim_text: 2.6 Commissioning of RUS facility (RUS stands for Resource Extraction Stockholm) for the sorting of food waste, plastic and metal in Högdalen.
extraction_rationale: Specific facility commissioning with location and year.
mechanism_description: Commissioning a waste-sorting facility increases diversion of organics and recyclables from landfill, which avoids methane emissions and supports material recovery.

section_header: Transition area 2 measures: Carbon capture and storage

policy_statement: Limit emissions within Stockholm’s geographical area to a maximum of 0.6 tonnes CO2e per resident by 2030 and ensure negative emissions are greater than remaining emissions, as part of being climate-positive by 2030 and fossil-free by 2040.
verbatim_text:
Milestone 2.1 A Stockholm that is climate-positive by 2030and fossil-freeby2040
area shall not exceed 0.6 tonnes carbon dioxide equivalents (CO 2 e) per resident. Negative emissions must be greater than the remaining emissions.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline and defined condition on negative emissions.
mechanism_description: Setting a jurisdiction-wide per-capita emissions cap and net-negative requirement drives planning and policy actions across sectors to reduce and offset emissions toward an overall emissions-reduction pathway.

policy_statement: Reduce transport-sector emissions within Stockholm’s geographical area by 80% by 2030 compared with 2010.
verbatim_text:
Milestone 2.2 Reduced climate impact from the transport sector
area shall be reduced by 80% by 2030 (compared with 2010).
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction target with baseline year and deadline.
mechanism_description: Setting a transport-sector emissions reduction target drives planning and measures that collectively reduce transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Climate impact from consumption a different perspective
policy_statement: Halve consumption-based greenhouse gas emissions in Stockholm by 2030 compared with 2019 (from 10.7 to 5.4 tonnes CO2e per resident).
verbatim_text:
Milestone 2.4 Ahalving of emissions from consumption
Consumption-based greenhouse gas emissions in Stockholm shall be halved by 2030 compared with 2019.

According to the City's milestone 2.4, A halving of emissions from consumption, these emissions must be halved to 5.4 tonnes per resident by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with baseline and per-capita numeric endpoint.
mechanism_description: Setting a consumption-based, economy-wide emissions reduction target is intended to drive policies and actions that reduce overall greenhouse gas emissions associated with goods and services consumed.

section_header: Emission budget sets the pace in reducing emissions
policy_statement: Establish a local emissions budget for Stockholm for 2024–2040 with a maximum of 9 million tonnes CO2e emitted over the period to complement Milestone 2.1.
verbatim_text:
This action plan defines a local emissions budget for Stockholm as a complement to milestone 2.1 A Stockholm that is climate-positive by 2030 and fossil-free by 2040 .
Stockholm's emissions budget 2024-2040 states that a maximum of 9 million tonnes CO2e may be emitted for the period 2024-2040.
extraction_rationale: Quantified carbon budget over a defined period; explicit policy instrument.
mechanism_description: Setting a jurisdiction-wide cumulative emissions budget creates a binding target that drives planning and actions across sectors to reduce greenhouse gas emissions over time.

policy_statement: Reduce car traffic by 30% by 2030 from the 2017 level.
verbatim_text: By 2030, the City aims to reduce car traffic by 30% from the 2017 level, which requires strong measures.
extraction_rationale: Clear quantified target with baseline year and deadline.
mechanism_description: Setting a target to reduce car traffic lowers car travel activity, which reduces fuel use and associated transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Install and commission a bio-CCS (BECCS) plant in Stockholm to enable capture and permanent storage of 800,000 tonnes of carbon dioxide annually (commissioning planned for 2027).
verbatim_text:
Building and commissioning a bio-CCS plant in Stockholm will enable 800,000 tonnes of carbon dioxide to be captured and stored annually.

Negative emissions in the emissions budget assume that a bioCCS/BECCS plant in connection with the biofuel-fired CHP KVV8 at the Värtaverket plant will be built and taken into operation in 2027.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure intervention with quantified annual capture and an operational start-year assumption.
mechanism_description: Deploying BECCS captures CO2 from bioenergy combustion and permanently stores it, producing net-negative emissions by removing CO2 from the atmosphere.

policy_statement: Cease all firing using fossil oil by 2025.
verbatim_text: All firing using fossil oil must have ceased by 2025.
extraction_rationale: Clear phase-out requirement with a deadline.
mechanism_description: Phasing out fossil oil combustion forces a switch to non-oil energy sources, reducing greenhouse gas emissions from burning oil.

section_header: PART 3-IMPLEMENTATION OF THE PARIS AGREEMENT — Implementation of the Paris Agreement
policy_statement: Minister must take all reasonable steps to prepare, communicate, maintain and update successive NDCs, pursue domestic mitigation measures, and communicate an NDC every 5 years with progression and highest possible ambition, including specified accounting and reporting information.
extraction_rationale: Creates a binding umbrella obligation with multiple lettered sub-requirements under section 7(2).
mechanism_description: Mandating successive, progressively more ambitious NDCs and associated domestic mitigation measures creates an economy-wide policy pathway intended to drive greenhouse gas emissions reductions over time.

section_header: PART 9-CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION — Minister has the power to introduce and implement regulations, measures and actions
policy_statement: Minister may introduce and implement economy-wide mitigation regulations/measures including carbon pricing/ETS, emissions fees, fiscal incentives/levies, sink protection measures, sustainable building regulations, and awareness programmes.
verbatim_text:
The Minister has the power to introduce and implement regulations, measures and actions with the purpose of limiting or reducing Fiji's greenhouse gas emissions across the economy...

Without limiting subsection (1), the Minister may- (a) introduce and implement a carbon pricing mechanism, including an emissions trading scheme; ...
extraction_rationale: Umbrella enabling provision with a lettered list of specific instruments, requiring parent/sub extraction.
mechanism_description: Empowers implementation of economy-wide regulations and carbon pricing instruments that shift incentives and requirements across sectors to drive greenhouse gas emissions reductions.

section_header: PART 2-DECLARATION OF CLIMATE EMERGENCY — Declaration of climate emergency
policy_statement: Fiji declares a climate emergency and commits to contribute to a net zero emissions global economy by achieving net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050.
verbatim_text:
This Act recognises and declares that Fiji and the Earth are facing a climate emergency.

A rapid and ambitious transformation to a net zero emissions global economy is necessary to address and mitigate the climate emergency, and Fiji will contribute to this transformation by achieving net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Binding legal declaration and an explicit, time-bound mitigation target (net zero by 2050).
mechanism_description: Establishes an economy-wide net-zero GHG target to drive coordinated policies and actions that reduce emissions across all sectors.

section_header: PART 9-CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION — Long term emissions reduction target
policy_statement: Long-term emissions reduction target is net zero GHG emissions by 2050; Minister must promote achievement via carbon budgets and publish a 5-yearly emissions/progress statement with recommendations.
verbatim_text:
The long term emissions reduction target for Fiji is net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050.

Every 5 years the Minister must... publish a statement of- (a) Fiji's greenhouse gas emissions over the 5-year period... (b) an assessment of the progress...; and (c) recommendations on measures needed...
extraction_rationale: Quantified national target plus recurring mandatory reporting and recommendations.
mechanism_description: An economy-wide net-zero target, operationalized through carbon budgets and periodic progress reporting, drives coordinated measures that reduce greenhouse gas emissions over time.

## Buildings
