section_header: SINGAPORE'S COMMITMENTS TO ADDRESS CLIMATE CHANGE
policy_statement: Singapore pledged to reduce emissions intensity by 36% from 2005 levels by 2030 and stabilise emissions with the aim of peaking around 2030.
verbatim_text: In 2015, building on our earlier commitment, Singapore pledged to reduce our Emissions Intensity (EI) by 36 per cent from 2005 levels by 2030, and stabilise emissions with the aim of peaking around 2030.
extraction_rationale: Formal pledge with numeric EI target and timeframe; reiterated national commitment.
mechanism_description: Setting an economy-wide emissions-intensity reduction and peaking target creates an overarching constraint that drives policies and investments to reduce greenhouse gas emissions across sectors.

section_header: SINGAPORE'S COMMITMENTS TO ADDRESS CLIMATE CHANGE
policy_statement: Singapore pledged to reduce emissions by 16% from business-as-usual levels by 2020.
verbatim_text: In 2009, Singapore pledged to reduce emissions by 16 per cent from business-as-usual (BAU) levels by 2020.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with a deadline (2020).
mechanism_description: Setting an economy-wide emissions reduction target creates an overall pathway that drives policies and actions to cut greenhouse gas emissions across sectors.

section_header: Increasing Energy and Carbon Efficiency in the Waste Management Process
policy_statement: NEA aims to increase Singapore’s overall recycling rate from 61% (2015) to 70% in 2030.
verbatim_text: NEA aims to increase the overall recycling rate from 61 per cent in 2015 to 70 per cent in 2030, by increasing awareness and implementing other policy initiatives (see feature story).
extraction_rationale: Quantified national target with baseline and deadline.
mechanism_description: Increasing recycling diverts waste away from landfills, reducing methane generation and associated greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: INCREASING RENEWABLE ENERGY: SOLAR PHOTOVOLTAIC (PV) SYSTEMS
policy_statement: Singapore plans to raise solar power adoption to 350 MWp by 2020 (about 5% of peak electricity demand).
verbatim_text: Singapore plans to raise the adoption of solar power in our system to 350MWp by 2020, representing about 5 per cent of peak electricity demand.
extraction_rationale: Quantified renewable energy deployment target with a deadline (2020).
mechanism_description: Increase solar PV deployment so that solar electricity replaces fossil electricity generation, lowering grid emissions.

policy_statement: Land Transport Master Plan: Singapore will expand rail network to 360 km by 2030 and add more than 120 trains, increasing total fleet size by more than 40%.
verbatim_text: By 2030, the rail network will be 360km, and will be comparable in rail density with developed cities like London, New York and Tokyo. More than 120 trains will also be added to existing train lines, increasing the total fleet size by more than 40 per cent.
extraction_rationale: Quantified infrastructure expansion commitments with timeframe (2030) and fleet increase.
mechanism_description: Expanding rail network coverage and train fleet capacity increases public transit service, shifting trips away from higher-emitting private vehicles and lowering transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Singapore will quadruple sheltered walkways from 56 km to 200 km by 2018; expand cycling paths to more than 700 km by 2030; and require private developers (from July 2016) to consider pedestrians’ and cyclists’ needs in development plans.
verbatim_text:
To facilitate walking and cycling, our sheltered walkway network will be quadrupled from the existing 56km to 200km by 2018. Cycling paths will double from 355km in 2015 to more than 700km by 2030.

From July 2016, LTA and the Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA) will require private developers to consider the safety, convenience, and accessibility needs of pedestrians and cyclists in their development plans.
extraction_rationale: Multiple quantified infrastructure targets plus a dated regulatory requirement (from July 2016).
mechanism_description: Expand and require pedestrian/cycling-supportive infrastructure and design so more trips shift to walking and cycling, lowering vehicle travel and transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Seven new integrated transport hubs will be built by 2023.
verbatim_text: Seven new integrated transport hubs will be built by 2023 to make it convenient for people to access different transport modes and to enjoy shopping and lifestyle services at the same location.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure commitment with deadline (2023).
mechanism_description: Developing integrated transport hubs makes transfers and access to different modes easier, promoting a shift to lower-emission travel options and reducing transport-related emissions.

policy_statement: These measures aim to increase public transport mode share during morning peak hours to 75% by 2030 and 85% by 2050.
verbatim_text: These measures collectively aim to increase the use of public transport as a share of overall transport during morning peak hours from 66 per cent in 2015 to 75 per cent by 2030. By 2050, the aim is to further increase this share to 85 per cent.
extraction_rationale: Quantified mode share targets with time horizons (2030, 2050).
mechanism_description: Increasing public transport mode share shifts trips from private vehicles to lower-emitting transit, reducing overall transport emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, every HDB town will have a dedicated cycling network.
verbatim_text: By 2030, every HDB town will have a dedicated cycling network.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound infrastructure commitment.
mechanism_description: Deploying dedicated cycling networks enables mode shift from car trips to cycling, lowering vehicle-kilometres traveled and transport emissions.

section_header: Chapter 6: Adapting to the effects of climate change (Adaptation) > 2. Direction and structure of measures
policy_statement: Hiroshima City will implement an Adaptation Plan with two pillars: improving acknowledgement/understanding of climate change and improving regional comprehensive ability to deal with climate risks, and will comprehensively develop three measures under these pillars.
verbatim_text:
Adaptation measures will have 2 pillars as the directions of measures; under those pillars, there are 3 measures that will be comprehensively developed in line with the plan.

<<First Pillar>> Improvement of acknowledgement and understanding of climate change and its effect

<<Second Pillar>> Improvement of regions' comprehensive ability to deal with climate change risks
extraction_rationale: Defines the structured adaptation policy framework that subsequent concrete measures fall under.
mechanism_description: Implementing an adaptation planning framework increases awareness and coordinated regional capability, improving institutional capacity to anticipate, prepare for, and respond to climate-related risks.

section_header: Chapter 6: Adapting to the effects of climate change (Adaptation) > 3. Concrete measures > 1) Creating an environment to promote understanding of climate change and its effect
policy_statement: Hiroshima City will promote creation of an environment for more accurate understanding of climate change and its effects through publicity/education via city media, school sub-readings, and delivery lectures.
parent_statement: Hiroshima City will implement an Adaptation Plan with two pillars: improving acknowledgement/understanding of climate change and improving regional comprehensive ability to deal with climate risks, and will comprehensively develop three measures under these pillars.
verbatim_text:
## 1) Creating an environment to promote understanding of climate change and its effect

Promote the creation of an environment for more accurate understanding of climate change and its effect.

- -New Publicity and education using the city's PR magazine... and the city website,
- -New Preparation of sub-readings available for school education,
- -New Delivery lecture by the city and Hiroshima City Global Warming Countermeasure Regional Council, etc.
extraction_rationale: Concrete adaptation measure with specific delivery channels and tools; linked as a sub-measure under the adaptation framework.
mechanism_description: Publicity, school materials, and lectures increase climate literacy and awareness, which enables broader mitigation and adaptation actions by residents and institutions.

section_header: Chapter 6: Adapting to the effects of climate change (Adaptation) > 3. Concrete measures > 2) Building resilience against climate change
policy_statement: Hiroshima City will advance adaptation measures focusing particularly on natural disaster/coast area, health, and people's life/city life, incorporating an additional 'adaptation' viewpoint into existing disaster prevention and healthcare measures, and steadily promoting selected effective measures from its FY2015 comprehensive strategy.
parent_statement: Hiroshima City will implement an Adaptation Plan with two pillars: improving acknowledgement/understanding of climate change and improving regional comprehensive ability to deal with climate risks, and will comprehensively develop three measures under these pillars.
verbatim_text:
The city will advance measures in 7 areas...; particularly, the city will focus on the items of 'natural disaster/coast area', 'health' and 'people's life/city life' whose damage by climate change has become obvious in the city.

There are many measures that have already been implemented...; however, they will be advanced with the additional 'adaptation' viewpoint.

...the city will conduct the steady promotion.
extraction_rationale: Explicit prioritization and commitment to advance measures with an adaptation lens.
mechanism_description: Integrating climate adaptation into existing disaster risk reduction and public health measures strengthens institutional capacity and implementation to reduce climate-related impacts on coastal hazards, health, and daily life.

section_header: Chapter 6: Adapting to the effects of climate change (Adaptation) > 3. Concrete measures > 2) Building resilience against climate change
policy_statement: As adaptation measures for increased short-time heavy rain, Hiroshima City will implement city renovation for disaster prevention, construct a disaster information sharing system, and enhance disaster information delivery systems.
parent_statement: Hiroshima City will implement an Adaptation Plan with two pillars: improving acknowledgement/understanding of climate change and improving regional comprehensive ability to deal with climate risks, and will comprehensively develop three measures under these pillars.
verbatim_text:
【 Measures against increase of heavy rain in short time 】

- -City renovation for disaster prevention, construction of disaster information sharing system, enhancement of disaster information delivery system
extraction_rationale: Specific adaptation interventions for extreme rainfall and disasters.
mechanism_description: Enhancing disaster information sharing and delivery systems improves early warning and coordination during heavy rainfall, reducing damages and health/safety impacts from flooding events.

section_header: Chapter 6: Adapting to the effects of climate change (Adaptation) > 3. Concrete measures > 2) Building resilience against climate change
policy_statement: As adaptation measures for health impacts from temperature rise, Hiroshima City will publicize heat disorder risks and provide information for heat disorder prevention.
parent_statement: Hiroshima City will implement an Adaptation Plan with two pillars: improving acknowledgement/understanding of climate change and improving regional comprehensive ability to deal with climate risks, and will comprehensively develop three measures under these pillars.
verbatim_text:
【 Measures against the effect of climate change on health due to the temperature rises, etc. 】

- -Publicity on heat disorder, information provision of heat disorder prevention
extraction_rationale: Concrete public-health adaptation actions.
mechanism_description: Publicizing heat illness risks and prevention information increases awareness and protective actions, reducing heat exposure and heat-related health harms.

section_header: Chapter 6: Adapting to the effects of climate change (Adaptation) > 3. Concrete measures > 2) Building resilience against climate change
policy_statement: As heat/heat-island adaptation measures, Hiroshima City will create a water and green network through conservation/creation of public open spaces and promote roof and wall greening of buildings (including via the green promotion system).
parent_statement: Hiroshima City will implement an Adaptation Plan with two pillars: improving acknowledgement/understanding of climate change and improving regional comprehensive ability to deal with climate risks, and will comprehensively develop three measures under these pillars.
verbatim_text:
【 Countermeasures against heat (heat island phenomenon, etc.) 】

- -Creation of water and green network by the conservation and creation of public open space such as rivers and roads through which the wind passes; promotion of greenery increase on roof and walls of buildings (including those by the green promotion system).
extraction_rationale: Specific urban cooling/heat-island interventions with clear actions.
mechanism_description: Creating connected green/blue open spaces and increasing building rooftop/wall vegetation cools the urban environment and reduces heat-island and heat exposure impacts.

section_header: Chapter 6: Adapting to the effects of climate change (Adaptation) > 3. Concrete measures > 3) Building of a system to learn and evaluate the effects of climate change
policy_statement: Hiroshima City will cooperate with the state and universities to consider a system to learn and evaluate climate change effects to time and advance adaptation measures appropriately.
parent_statement: Hiroshima City will implement an Adaptation Plan with two pillars: improving acknowledgement/understanding of climate change and improving regional comprehensive ability to deal with climate risks, and will comprehensively develop three measures under these pillars.
verbatim_text:
## 3) Building of a system to learn and evaluate the effects of climate change

In order to prepare for the estimated effects and advance 'adaptation measures' appropriately at a suitable time, the city will cooperate with the state and universities and consider a system to learn and evaluate.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to establish an evaluation/learning system with identified partners.
mechanism_description: Establishing a climate impact learning and evaluation system improves data and understanding, enabling better-timed and more effective adaptation planning and measures.

section_header: Chapter 5: Promotion of counteractions against global warming (Mitigation) > 2. Reduction target for greenhouse gas emissions
policy_statement: Hiroshima City will set greenhouse gas emissions reduction targets of 80% by FY2050, 30% by FY2030, and 5% by FY2020 compared to FY2013 (with FY2020 also equivalent to 7% compared to FY2005).
verbatim_text:
To establish the reduction target of greenhouse gas emissions, the city will set the long-term target based on the national government's target, keeping in mind the city Hiroshima aims to become.

| Timeframe            | Target Year   | Reduction Target (Total Target Amount)            |
| Long-term Target     | FY2050        | Compared to FY2013 ▲ 80%                          |
| Mid-term Target *1   | FY2030        | Compared to FY2013 ▲ 30%                          |
| Short-term Target *2 | FY2020        | Compared to FY2013 ▲ 5% (Compared to FY2005 ▲ 7%) |
extraction_rationale: Clear, quantified citywide mitigation targets with target years and base year.
mechanism_description: Setting an economy-wide GHG reduction target creates an overarching mandate/goal that guides policies and investments to reduce emissions across all sectors over time.

section_header: Chapter 5: Promotion of counteractions against global warming (Mitigation) > *2 Setting of short-term energy reduction target
policy_statement: Hiroshima City will set a short-term (FY2020) energy-use reduction target of 5% for total energy use not affected by the CO2 emission factor of electric utilities.
verbatim_text:
*2 Setting of short-term energy reduction target

Total amount of energy use that is not affected by the carbon dioxide emission factor of electric utility

- -Total amount of energy use: ▲ 5%
extraction_rationale: Explicit, quantified short-term energy reduction target.
mechanism_description: Setting an economy-wide energy-use reduction target drives actions that reduce overall energy demand, leading to lower energy-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Chapter 5: Promotion of counteractions against global warming (Mitigation) > ◆ Household > ◇ Key Performance Indicator (KPI)
policy_statement: For households, Hiroshima City sets KPIs including: newly-built ZEH share 50% by FY2020 and almost 100% by FY2030; low-carbon house share 50% by FY2020; household fuel cells total 15,000 by FY2020 and 56,000 by FY2030; and LED adoption (>80% of household lighting) 50% by FY2020 and almost 100% by FY2030.
verbatim_text:
## ◇ Key Performance Indicator (KPI)

| ... | ZEH | 4.3% (FY2015) | 50% | Almost100% |
| ... | Low-carbon house | 1.3% (FY2015) | 50% | - |
| Number of introduced household fuel cells (Total) | ... | 822 (FY2015) | 15,000 | 56,000 |
| Rate of citizens who have introduced LED lighting for more than 80%ofhousehold lighting | ... | 12.5% (FY2016) | 50% | Almost100% |
extraction_rationale: Quantified KPI targets with years provide concrete commitments for household sector implementation.
mechanism_description: Increase adoption of high-performance/low-carbon homes and efficient household technologies (e.g., LEDs, fuel cells) to reduce household energy consumption and related emissions.

section_header: Chapter 5: Promotion of counteractions against global warming (Mitigation) > ◆ Business activities > ◇ Key Performance Indicator (KPI)
policy_statement: For business activities, Hiroshima City sets KPIs including Eco Action 21/ISO14001 introductions (500 by FY2020; 1,000 by FY2030), LED lighting use in offices (88.1% by FY2020; almost 100% by FY2030), and BEMS introduction rate (13% by FY2020; 47% by FY2030).
verbatim_text:
## ◇ Key Performance Indicator (KPI)

| No. of introductions of Eco Action 21 and ISO14001 (total) | 370 (FY2015) | 500 | 1,000 |
| Rate of offices that use LED lights for lighting | 52.5% (FY2016) | 88.1% | Almost100% |
| Rate of offices that introduce Business Energy Management System (BEMS) | 2.8% (FY2016) | 13% | 47% |
extraction_rationale: Quantified KPI targets define concrete expected uptake of standards, LEDs, and energy management.
mechanism_description: Increase adoption of office energy management and efficient lighting to optimize operations and reduce commercial building energy use, lowering associated emissions.

section_header: Chapter 5: Promotion of counteractions against global warming (Mitigation) > ◆ Waste > ◇ Key Performance Indicator (KPI)
policy_statement: For waste, Hiroshima City sets KPIs including reducing waste per person per day to 826 g/person/day by FY2020 and reducing incineration amount to 295,000 ton/year by FY2020.
verbatim_text:
## ◇ Key Performance Indicator (KPI)

| Waste amount per day per person | 859 g/person/day (FY2013) | 826 g/ person/day | - |
| Incineration amount of waste | 304,000 ton/year (FY2013) | 295,000 ton/year | - |
extraction_rationale: Quantified waste prevention KPIs with baseline and target year.
mechanism_description: Setting targets to reduce per-capita waste generation and the amount of waste incinerated decreases the quantity of waste needing disposal, reducing waste-sector greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Chapter 5: Promotion of counteractions against global warming (Mitigation) > ◆ Source of forest absorption /planting trees > ◇ Key Performance Indicator (KPI)
policy_statement: Hiroshima City sets KPIs to increase introduced biomass boilers in city facilities to 3 by FY2020 and 9 by FY2030, and to increase area of park and green spaces to 1,000 ha by FY2020.
verbatim_text:
## ◇ Key Performance Indicator (KPI)

| No. of introduced biomass boilers in the city facilities (cumulative total) | 1 (FY2016) | 3 | 9 |
| Area of park and green spaces | 975.71ha (FY2015) | 1,000ha | - |
extraction_rationale: Quantified KPIs for biomass boiler deployment and greenspace expansion.
mechanism_description: Install biomass boilers in municipal facilities to replace fossil-fuel heating, reducing combustion-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Chapter 5: Promotion of counteractions against global warming (Mitigation) > ◆ Cross-sectional measures > ◇ Key Performance Indicator (KPI)
policy_statement: Hiroshima City sets KPIs for cross-sectional measures: dispersed power introductions in city facilities (1 by FY2020; 8 by FY2030) and smart communities introduced (3 by FY2020; 10 by FY2030).
verbatim_text:
## ◇ Key Performance Indicator (KPI)

| Introduction no. of dispersed power in the city facilities (cumulative total) | 0 (FY2016) | 1 | 8 |
| Introduction no. of smart communities (cumulative total) | 1 (FY2016) | 3 | 10 |
extraction_rationale: Quantified implementation KPIs for smart energy/community systems.
mechanism_description: Deploying distributed energy resources and smart community energy systems increases local clean generation/optimized energy management, thereby reducing reliance on fossil electricity and lowering emissions.

policy_statement: For government operations, Hiroshima City sets targets to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by FY2020 by 5% in the city area and 5.1% for the city office (vs FY2013), and by FY2030 by 30% for both city area and city office (vs FY2013).
verbatim_text:
## 3. Reduction target

| ... | Base Year | Target Year | City area | City office |
| Short-term target | FY2013 | FY2020 | ▲ 5% | ▲ 5.1% |
| Mid-term target | FY2013 | FY2030 | ▲ 30% | ▲ 30% |
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions reduction targets for municipal operations with specified years.
mechanism_description: Setting jurisdiction- and government-operations GHG reduction targets drives planning and actions across sectors to reduce overall emissions over time.

policy_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text:
## Net-zero Electricity Generation

## Long-term target : Net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050

At  present,  fossil  fuels,  including  natural  gas and coal, account for over 70% of Hong Kong's fuel mix for electricity generation.  Under the overall strategy of achieving carbon neutrality, we  must  progressively  increase  the  use  of zero-carbon  energy  for  electricity  generation before 2050.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy section with long-term target and multiple specific sub-actions/targets presented underneath in subsequent paragraphs.
mechanism_description: Increase the share of zero-carbon electricity generation to displace fossil fuel power, reducing power-sector greenhouse gas emissions and decarbonizing the grid.

section_header: Medium-term targets: Phasing out coal for electricity generation · Developing RE
policy_statement: By 2035, Hong Kong will cease using coal for daily electricity generation and keep coal only for backup; coal will be replaced by natural gas and zero-carbon energy (including renewables and nuclear).
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text: By  2035,  we  will cease using coal for daily electricity generation and  will  only  keep  it  for  providing  backup support. Coal will be replaced by natural gas with lower carbon emissions and zero-carbon energy (e.g. RE and nuclear energy) by then.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound fuel switching commitment under the net-zero electricity strategy; clearly a specific sub-measure.
mechanism_description: Ending coal-fired electricity generation and replacing it with lower- and zero-carbon power sources lowers the emissions intensity of grid electricity and reduces overall power-sector GHG emissions.

policy_statement: Hong Kong will increase the share of renewable energy in the electricity generation fuel mix from less than 1% to 7.5–10% by 2035, and to 15% before 2050.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text:
we are committed to increasing the share  of  RE  in  the  fuel  mix  for  electricity generation from less than 1% at present to 7.5% to 10% by 2035.

Our goal is to increase the share of RE in the fuel mix for electricity  generation  from  the  existing  less than 1% to 15% before 2050.
extraction_rationale: Quantified RE share targets with clear deadlines; presented as part of the net-zero electricity pathway.
mechanism_description: Setting a higher renewable electricity share increases renewable generation and displaces fossil power on the grid, reducing electricity-sector greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: The Government will incorporate renewable energy technologies in all capital works projects, update the Green Government Buildings circular, and increase required roof space for installing RE systems in new government buildings from 10% to 25%, while continuing RE installation in existing government buildings where possible.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text: The Government will continue to take the lead in developing RE and strive to incorporate RE technologies  in  all  capital  works  projects. We  will  update  the  Government  circular  on Green  Government  Buildings  and  raise  the requirements of applying RE technologies in new government buildings. For instance, the proportion of available roof space for installing RE systems will be increased from the current 10% to 25%. We will also continue to install RE systems in existing government buildings (including outdoor parking lots) as far as possible.
extraction_rationale: Specific government-led implementation measures with a quantified design requirement (10% to 25%).
mechanism_description: Install renewable energy systems on government buildings and facilities to generate clean electricity that replaces fossil-fueled power generation and reduces emissions.

policy_statement: WSD plans to install larger floating solar systems (5–10 MW each) at multiple reservoirs and is studying a >100 MW floating solar system at Plover Cove Reservoir, targeted to generate over 100 million kWh/year.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text:
the  Water  Supplies  Department  (WSD)  plans  to  install  larger  floating solar energy generation systems with a generating capacity of 5-10 megawatts (MW) each at  various  reservoirs,  including  Plover  Cove Reservoir,  Shek  Pik  Reservoir  and  Tai  Lam Chung Reservoir.

WSD  has embarked on a study on installing a floating solar energy generation system with a generating capacity of over 100 MW at Plover Cove Reservoir. The project is estimated to generate more than 100 million kWh of electricity a year
extraction_rationale: Concrete deployment plan with capacities and expected annual generation.
mechanism_description: Install floating solar PV systems on reservoirs to produce renewable electricity that replaces fossil-based power generation and lowers emissions.

policy_statement: The Government and power companies will enhance regional cooperation to identify and invest in zero-carbon energy sources/projects in neighbouring regions through joint investment and development.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text: The  Government,  together  with  the  power companies, will also explore ways to enhance regional  cooperation  on  zero-carbon  energy and identify sources of zero-carbon energy in neighbouring regions, including seeking joint investment and development opportunities for participating in and operating zero-carbon energy projects near Hong Kong.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (regional cooperation and joint investment) to increase zero-carbon energy supply.
mechanism_description: Joint regional investment and development of zero-carbon power projects increases clean electricity supply, which reduces reliance on fossil generation and lowers power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text:
## Energy Saving and Green Buildings

Through regulation as well as promotion and public education in energy saving, we can reduce the overall electricity consumption of  buildings,  and  hence  lower  the  cost  of adopting  new  zero-carbon  energy.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy section that introduces a package of measures and multiple explicit targets/actions in subsequent paragraphs.
mechanism_description: Strengthening building energy-performance requirements and related programs reduces building electricity consumption, lowering energy demand and associated emissions.

section_header: Medium-term targets: Reduce the electricity consumption of commercial buildings by 15% to 20%, and that of residential buildings by 10% to 15% by 2035
policy_statement: Hong Kong will reduce electricity consumption of new and existing commercial buildings by 15–20% and residential buildings by 10–15% by 2035 (baseline: 2015 operational conditions).
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text: we  have  also  set  a  medium-term target of reducing the electricity consumption of new and existing commercial buildings by 15% to 20%, and that of residential buildings by 10% to 15% by 2035, using the operational conditions  of  2015  as  the  comparison  basis.
extraction_rationale: Quantified medium-term building energy reduction targets with baseline year.
mechanism_description: Setting a target to cut electricity use in residential and commercial buildings leads to reduced building energy consumption and associated power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: The Government plans to tighten statutory OTTV standards by 2025 and complete RTTV reviews by 2023 and 2030; new government buildings will have OTTV at least 10% above statutory standards.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text: The  Government  is  conducting  a review on the OTTV, and plans to complete the  review  for  tightening  up  the  statutory standards  by  2025. The  Government  also plans to complete two reviews on the Residential  Thermal  Transfer  Value  (RTTV) by 2023 and 2030. To lead by example, the OTTV of new government buildings will be at least 10% above the statutory standards.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory tightening with explicit deadlines and a quantified government-building requirement.
mechanism_description: Strengthening building thermal performance standards lowers heat gain/loss, reducing HVAC energy demand and overall building energy use (and related emissions).

policy_statement: The Government will study adding district cooling systems (DCS) in more new development areas (including Hung Shui Kiu/Ha Tsuen and artificial islands in Central Waters).
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text: We will work on the infrastructure by integrating energy efficient facilities in development projects.  We  will  study  the  feasibility  of incorporating DCS in more new development areas, such as Hung Shui Kiu/Ha Tsuen New Development Area and the artificial islands in the  Central  Waters
extraction_rationale: Concrete planned action (feasibility study) to expand a named energy-efficiency infrastructure solution.
mechanism_description: Implementing district cooling systems in new developments reduces electricity used for space cooling, lowering upstream emissions from power generation.

policy_statement: The Government will consider more frequent energy audits and mandating implementation of identified energy management opportunities, strengthen promotion of retro-commissioning, and consider requiring large commercial buildings to regularly commission key systems.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text:
we will consider conducting energy audits more frequently and mandating the implementation of identified energy management opportunities. At the same time, we will strengthen the promotion of RCx

We will consider requiring large commercial buildings to carry out regular commissioning of air-conditioning systems and major building services installations
extraction_rationale: Regulatory/mandatory mechanisms under consideration are specific policy interventions (audits, mandated actions, commissioning requirements).
mechanism_description: Require and promote regular (retro-)commissioning and follow-through on audit-identified operational improvements in large commercial buildings to optimize systems performance and reduce energy consumption and emissions.

policy_statement: The Government will keep the Mandatory Energy Efficiency Labelling Scheme (MEELS) under review, consider setting minimum energy efficiency requirements for specified appliances before supply in Hong Kong, and consider expanding MEELS to non-domestic/commercial appliances.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text:
We will keep the scheme under review.  Apart from upgrading  the  grading  standards,  we  will  also consider  setting  a  minimum  energy  efficiency requirement  so  that  certain  appliances  must meet the minimum energy efficiency standards before they can be supplied in Hong Kong

we  will consider expanding the scope of the scheme to cover non-domestic or commercial appliances.
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy intent to introduce minimum standards and expand scope of an existing mandatory labeling scheme.
mechanism_description: Require (and label) minimum energy performance for appliances sold, leading to higher average appliance efficiency and lower electricity consumption, thereby reducing emissions from power supply.

policy_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text:
## Green Transport

## Long-term target: Zero carbon emissions from vehicles and transport sector before 2050

We expect to achieve the long-term target of zero carbon emissions from  vehicles and the transport  sector  before  2050  through  the electrification of vehicles and ferries, development of new energy transport and implementation of measures to improve traffic management.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with long-term sectoral target and multiple distinct measures described underneath.
mechanism_description: Electrifying road vehicles and ferries (and shifting to other low/zero-carbon propulsion) reduces fossil fuel use in transport, thereby lowering transport-sector greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Hong Kong targets ceasing new registration of fuel-propelled and hybrid private cars in 2035 or earlier.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text: the  Hong  Kong Roadmap on Popularisation of Electric Vehicles announced by the Government in 2021 cover various  aspects,  including  ceasing  the  new registration of fuel-propelled and hybrid private cars in 2035 or earlier
extraction_rationale: Clear regulatory/market access commitment with a specific year.
mechanism_description: Ending new registrations of fuel-powered and hybrid private cars shifts new purchases to zero-emission vehicles, cutting road transport greenhouse gas emissions over time.

section_header: Medium-term target: Set a concrete timetable for adopting new energy public transport
policy_statement: The Government will partner with sectors to test electric and new energy public transport and commercial vehicles and aims to set a more concrete way forward and timetable around 2025; it will review EV charging installation targets around 2025.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text:
the Government will play an active role  in  developing  new  energy  transport,  and will partner with different sectors in the coming years  to  actively  test  the  technological  and commercial viability of various electric and new energy public transport and commercial vehicles, with a view to setting a more concrete way  forward  and  timetable  in  around  2025.

In  around  2025,  we  will  review  this year's target for the installation of charging facilities
extraction_rationale: Time-referenced implementation commitment (tests leading to timetable by ~2025; review of charging targets by ~2025).
mechanism_description: Deploying and planning EV charging infrastructure (alongside vehicle pilots) enables greater adoption of electric public and commercial vehicles, supporting future transport emissions reductions.

policy_statement: The Government plans to collaborate with franchised bus companies and stakeholders in the next three years to test hydrogen fuel cell electric buses and heavy vehicles, supported by an interdepartmental working group on hydrogen energy.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text: Following the release of the Plan, the Government has set up an Interdepartmental  Working  Group  on  the  application  of hydrogen energy in Hong Kong. We plan to collaborate with the franchised bus companies and other stakeholders in the next three years to test out hydrogen fuel cell electric buses and heavy vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Specific planned trials with a defined timeframe and enabling governance action (working group).
mechanism_description: Pilot testing hydrogen fuel cell buses and heavy vehicles helps enable deployment of zero-emission drivetrains, reducing transport greenhouse gas emissions from diesel fuel use.

policy_statement: The Government will subsidise ferry operators to trial electric and hybrid ferries and will explore progressively replacing traditional ferries with new energy ferries before 2035, subject to trial results and technology.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text:
As  regards  ferries,  the Government  will  subsidise  ferry  operators  to conduct  trials  for  electric  and  hybrid  ferries.

the  Government will  explore  with  the  ferry  operators  the possibility of progressively replacing traditional ferries with new energy ferries before 2035.
extraction_rationale: Concrete financial mechanism (subsidies) plus a time-bound transition intention (before 2035).
mechanism_description: Subsidies and trials support switching ferry fleets from fossil-fueled vessels to electric or hybrid ferries, reducing operational fuel use and associated transport emissions.

policy_statement: The Government will roll out a Free-flow Tolling System at government tolled tunnels and press ahead with congestion charging, with preliminary proposals to be drawn up in 2022.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text:
the Free-flow Tolling System will be rolled out at government tolled tunnels and the Tsing Sha Control Area, while 'congestion charging' will be  pressed  ahead  actively

We commenced  a  traffic  survey  in  September 2021  to  collect  the  latest  data  and  draw  up preliminary proposals for 'congestion charging' in 2022.
extraction_rationale: Specific transport demand management measures with an implementation/planning milestone (proposals in 2022).
mechanism_description: Implementing congestion charging and electronic tolling reduces driving demand and congestion, leading to lower vehicle travel and reduced transport emissions.

policy_statement: Parent strategy: Waste reduction, aiming to move away from reliance on landfills for municipal waste disposal by 2035 and support carbon neutrality in waste management.
verbatim_text:
## Waste Reduction

The Government promulgated the Waste Blueprint for Hong Kong 2035 (the Waste Blueprint) early this year, advocating  the  vision  of  'Waste  Reduction · Resources Circulation · Zero Landfill' .

The  Waste  Blueprint sets out targets for per capita MSW disposal and  recovery  rate,  and  the  goal  of  developing adequate waste-to-energy facilities, with the aim to  move  away from the reliance on landfills for municipal waste disposal  b y   2 0 3 5 .
extraction_rationale: Named strategy section linked to a specific plan (Waste Blueprint 2035) with clear goal and multiple actions/targets described thereafter.
mechanism_description: Reduce and divert municipal solid waste away from landfills, thereby avoiding landfill methane emissions and lowering waste-sector climate impacts.

section_header: Medium-term target: Enhance waste reduction and recycling
policy_statement: Hong Kong aims to reduce per capita municipal solid waste disposal by 40–45% and raise the recovery rate to about 55% through MSW charging and other waste reduction and recycling initiatives.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Waste reduction, aiming to move away from reliance on landfills for municipal waste disposal by 2035 and support carbon neutrality in waste management.
verbatim_text: Through  implementing  MSW  charging  and other waste reduction and recycling initiatives, and encouraging the whole community to work together,  we  aim  to  progressively  reduce  the per capita MSW disposal by 40% to 45% and raise the recovery rate to about 55%.
extraction_rationale: Quantified waste reduction and recycling targets tied to specific instruments (MSW charging and initiatives).
mechanism_description: Implementing MSW charging and related recycling/reduction programs increases waste diversion from disposal, avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: The Government will enhance recovery of landfill gases from the three operating landfills to provide thermal energy and electricity for landfill operations and export to gas company/power grids.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Waste reduction, aiming to move away from reliance on landfills for municipal waste disposal by 2035 and support carbon neutrality in waste management.
verbatim_text: we will enhance the recovery of landfill gases from the three landfills currently in operation for providing thermal energy and electricity  to  support  landfill  operation  and export to the gas company and power grids to provide energy for the public.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure/energy recovery action stated as a future measure.
mechanism_description: Capture landfill gas and use it to generate heat and electricity, cutting methane leaks while offsetting fossil-based energy supply.

policy_statement: The Government has set a Green Energy Target to improve the energy performance of the Government by 6% by 2024-25.
verbatim_text: We have also set  a  new  Green  Energy  Target  to  further improve the energy performance of the whole Government by 6% by 2024-25.
extraction_rationale: Quantified government operational energy target with a deadline.
mechanism_description: Sets a government-wide energy performance target that drives efficiency actions to reduce overall energy consumption and associated emissions.

section_header: Combating Extreme Droughts and Safeguarding Water Supply
policy_statement: WSD targets reducing average fresh water per capita consumption by 10% in 2030 (base year 2016) and containing fresh water demand at around 1 billion m³ per annum before 2040, via water conservation, water loss management, and expanded use of seawater/recycled water for non-potable purposes.
verbatim_text: To  achieve  the  goal  of  reducing  the  average fresh water per capita consumption by 10% in 2030,  using  2016  as  the  base  year,  and containing the fresh water demand at the level of around 1 billion m³   per annum before 2040, WSD  is  taking  forward  three  major  water demand management initiatives, namely water  conservation,  water  loss  management and expansion of the use of lower grade water (viz. seawater and recycled water) for non-potable purposes.
extraction_rationale: Quantified adaptation/water management targets with timeframes and named initiative pillars.
mechanism_description: Implement water conservation, reduce distribution losses, and substitute non-potable supplies (seawater/recycled water) to lower per-capita freshwater consumption and overall freshwater demand.

policy_statement: The Tseung Kwan O Desalination Plant (first stage) is targeted to be commissioned in 2023 with capacity about 135,000 m³/day (about 5% of overall fresh water demand) and provision to expand to 270,000 m³/day.
verbatim_text:
WSD is constructing the first stage of Tseung  Kwan  O  Desalination  Plant

The plant, targeted to be commissioned in 2023... It will have a daily capacity of  about  135  000  m³ ... with provision for future expansion to a daily capacity of up to 270 000 m³ .
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure project with commissioning date and capacity figures.
mechanism_description: Build and expand desalination capacity to diversify and increase freshwater supply, improving water supply reliability and drought resilience.

section_header: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted adaptation guidance
policy_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted adaptation guidance

The Government will roll out a programme of non-statutory guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks. This could also support regulatory requirements to be developed through the National Planning Framework.
extraction_rationale: Umbrella programme explicitly introducing multiple specific guidance products listed as sub-items (3.7.1–3.7.8).
mechanism_description: Provide targeted climate-risk guidance to decision makers so they can better assess, plan for, and manage climate impacts, increasing overall adaptive capacity.

section_header: 3.7.1: Promote the use of the Aotearoa New Zealand Climate Change Projections guidance
policy_statement: Promote the use of updated Aotearoa New Zealand Climate Change Projections guidance highlighting changes from AR6 to support updated risk management plans.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.1: Promote the use of the Aotearoa New Zealand Climate Change Projections guidance

An update for Aotearoa that highlights changes from the 2021 AR6 IPCC.12 report. The update will enable stakeholders to quickly assess where they may need to adapt their risk management plans to account for the latest science.
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance deliverable under the rolling programme.
mechanism_description: Updated climate projections guidance improves access to current climate risk information, which supports stakeholders to revise risk management and adaptation plans.

section_header: 3.7.2: Produce adaptation guidance for central government policy makers
policy_statement: Produce adaptation guidance for central government policy makers, including a methodology to ensure adaptation is considered in new policy and services to avoid increased exposure or vulnerability.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.2: Produce adaptation guidance for central government policy makers

A methodology for central government to ensure it considers adaptation in new policy and services. This will avoid increased exposure or vulnerability to climate impacts.
extraction_rationale: Specific methodology commitment under the guidance programme.
mechanism_description: Developing and applying adaptation guidance and a standard methodology embeds climate-risk consideration into new policies and services, reducing maladaptive decisions and improving institutional capacity to manage climate impacts.

section_header: 3.7.3: Produce guidance for dynamic adaptive pathways planning (DAPP)
policy_statement: Produce guidance for dynamic adaptive pathways planning (DAPP) for central and local government to plan adaptation under uncertain climate futures.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.3: Produce guidance for dynamic adaptive pathways planning (DAPP)

A guide for central and local government on how to plan for adaptation in a context of uncertain climate futures.
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance product with a defined purpose.
mechanism_description: Developing DAPP guidance enables governments to plan and sequence adaptation actions under uncertain climate futures, strengthening institutional capacity to manage climate risks.

section_header: 3.7.4: Produce guidance on using different socio-economic scenarios for adaptation planning
policy_statement: Produce guidance on using different socio-economic scenarios for adaptation planning to help governments and businesses assess climate risks and plan responses.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.4: Produce guidance on using different socio-economic scenarios for adaptation planning

Assists central and local government and businesses to consider future socio-economic scenarios when assessing climate risks, and planning how to manage them.
extraction_rationale: Concrete guidance output under an identified action sub-item.
mechanism_description: Providing scenario-based guidance improves how governments and businesses assess future climate risks and plan adaptation responses, increasing institutional adaptive capacity.

section_header: 3.7.5: Regularly update adaptation guidance for local government
policy_statement: Regularly update adaptation guidance for local government to support adaptation in planning and decisions, including communicating technical information and making climate decisions.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.5: Regularly update adaptation guidance for local government

Supports local government to consider adaptation in planning and decisions. This will include guidance on communicating scientific and technical information to communities, and on making climate decisions.
extraction_rationale: Ongoing guidance update commitment for a specific user group (local government).
mechanism_description: Updating and providing adaptation guidance improves local governments’ ability to incorporate climate risks into plans, communicate technical information, and make adaptation-oriented decisions, strengthening institutional adaptive capacity.

section_header: 3.7.6: Produce guidance on integrating mātauranga Māori into adaptive planning and working with mana whenua
policy_statement: Produce guidance on integrating mātauranga Māori into adaptive planning and working with mana whenua.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.6: Produce guidance on integrating mātauranga Māori into adaptive planning and working with mana whenua

Helps central and local government effectively engage with hapū, iwi and Māori when managing risk and planning for adaptation.
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance deliverable focused on Māori knowledge and engagement.
mechanism_description: Developing guidance to integrate mātauranga Māori and work with mana whenua improves governmental engagement and decision-making processes, strengthening institutional capacity for climate adaptation planning and risk management.

section_header: 3.7.7: Produce guidance for preparing adaptation plans
policy_statement: Produce guidance for preparing adaptation plans using a standard approach under uncertain climate projections.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.7: Produce guidance for preparing adaptation plans

Enables communities and different audiences, sectors and levels of government to produce their own adaptation plans, using a standard approach under conditions of uncertainty in climate projections.
extraction_rationale: Concrete guidance product intended to standardise adaptation planning.
mechanism_description: Providing standardized guidance for adaptation planning enables consistent plan development under uncertainty, improving institutional capacity to anticipate and manage climate risks.

section_header: 3.7.8: Regularly update the guide to local climate change risk assessments
policy_statement: Regularly update the guide to local climate change risk assessments on a six-yearly cycle after each national risk assessment.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.8: Regularly update the guide to local climate change risk assessments

Sets out a step-by-step process for local risk assessments. It will be updated on a six-yearly cycle after the release of each national climate change risk assessment.
extraction_rationale: Specific commitment with a defined update cycle linked to risk assessments.
mechanism_description: Updating risk assessment guidance on a regular cycle improves the quality and consistency of local climate risk assessments, strengthening institutions’ ability to plan and implement adaptation actions.

section_header: TABLE ES-1 SUMMARY OF DRAFT 2045 CAP MEASURES AND AFFECTED RESOURCE AREAS
policy_statement: Strategy 1: Decarbonize the Energy Supply through measures including a sunset strategy for oil and gas operations, procuring zero-carbon electricity, increasing renewable energy production, increasing energy resilience, and establishing GHG requirements for new development.
verbatim_text:
TABLE ES-1 SUMMARY OF DRAFT 2045 CAP MEASURES AND AFFECTED RESOURCE AREAS

| Draft 2045 CAP Strategies and Measures                                |
| Strategy 1: Decarbonize the Energy Supply                             |
| Measure ES1: Develop a Sunset Strategy for All Oil and Gas Operations |
| Measure ES2: Procure Zero-Carbon Electricity                          |
| Measure ES3: Increase Renewable Energy Production                     |
| Measure ES4: Increase Energy Resilience                               |
| Measure ES5: Establish GHG Requirements for New Development |
extraction_rationale: The document names Strategy 1 and enumerates multiple measures under it, indicating an umbrella policy framework with sub-actions.
mechanism_description: Shift electricity supply from fossil generation to zero-carbon/renewable sources (via procurement, renewable buildout, and fossil phaseout), reducing power-sector greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TABLE ES-1 SUMMARY OF DRAFT 2045 CAP MEASURES AND AFFECTED RESOURCE AREAS
policy_statement: Measure ES1: Develop a sunset strategy for all oil and gas operations, including collaboration with jurisdictions/utilities and development of an ordinance.
parent_statement: Strategy 1: Decarbonize the Energy Supply through measures including a sunset strategy for oil and gas operations, procuring zero-carbon electricity, increasing renewable energy production, increasing energy resilience, and establishing GHG requirements for new development.
verbatim_text:
Measure ES1: Develop a Sunset Strategy for All Oil and Gas Operations
Energy: • Action ES1.1 - Collaborate with other local jurisdictions and utilities to develop a sunset strategy for all oil and gas operations that prioritizes disproportionately affected communities. Develop an ordinance.
extraction_rationale: Named measure under Strategy 1 with specific implementing actions, including an ordinance.
mechanism_description: Adopting an ordinance to sunset oil and gas operations reduces fossil fuel production-related activity and associated greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TABLE ES-1 SUMMARY OF DRAFT 2045 CAP MEASURES AND AFFECTED RESOURCE AREAS
policy_statement: Collaborate with other local jurisdictions and utilities to develop a sunset strategy for all oil and gas operations prioritizing disproportionately affected communities and develop an ordinance.

verbatim_text: Action ES1.1 - Collaborate with other local jurisdictions and utilities to develop a sunset strategy for all oil and gas operations that prioritizes disproportionately affected communities. Develop an ordinance.
extraction_rationale: Concrete intervention and regulatory mechanism (ordinance) stated as an implementing action.
mechanism_description: Adopt an ordinance to phase out oil and gas operations, reducing fossil fuel production and associated methane and CO₂ emissions, thereby lowering overall greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Measure ES2: Procure zero-carbon electricity by transitioning County facilities to 100% renewable electricity services and completing community enrollment in 100% green power/green rate options.
parent_statement: Strategy 1: Decarbonize the Energy Supply through measures including a sunset strategy for oil and gas operations, procuring zero-carbon electricity, increasing renewable energy production, increasing energy resilience, and establishing GHG requirements for new development.
verbatim_text:
Measure ES2: Procure Zero-Carbon Electricity
Energy: • Action ES2.1 - Transition all County facilities within unincorporated areas to CPA's 100% Green Power option, SCE's 100% Green Rate option, or other available 100% renewable electricity service. • Action ES2.2 - Complete enrollment of the community in CPA's 100% Green Power option or SCE's Green Rate option.
extraction_rationale: Named measure with explicit actions to transition facilities and enroll community in 100% renewable options.
mechanism_description: Shift electricity procurement/enrollment to 100% renewable supply options to displace fossil electricity generation and reduce power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Measure ES3: Increase renewable energy production through requirements for rooftop solar in new development, solar installations on existing buildings and County facilities, community-shared solar, affordable housing renewable energy requirements/incentives, and streamlined permitting for solar and storage.
parent_statement: Strategy 1: Decarbonize the Energy Supply through measures including a sunset strategy for oil and gas operations, procuring zero-carbon electricity, increasing renewable energy production, increasing energy resilience, and establishing GHG requirements for new development.
verbatim_text:
Measure ES3: Increase Renewable Energy Production
Energy: • Action ES3.1 - Require rooftop solar PV for all new development. • Action ES3.2 - Install rooftop solar PV at existing buildings. • Action ES3.3 - Identify and install solar PV systems at existing viable County facilities and properties. • Action ES3.4 - Explore the feasibility to install community-shared solar facilities on County properties where opportunities exist. • Action ES3.5 - Require and incentivize renewable energy for affordable housing developments for both new development and existing buildings. • Action ES3.6 - Streamline and prioritize permitting for solar and battery storage projects.
extraction_rationale: Named measure with multiple specific interventions including requirements and permitting changes.
mechanism_description: Require and facilitate installation of solar PV (including shared solar and public-facility systems) so renewable electricity replaces fossil-based generation, reducing power-sector emissions.

• Action ES5.1 - Identify new requirements for new development, including reach codes, ordinances, and conditions of approval...

mechanism_description: Adopt and enforce GHG requirements for new development (including codes/ordinances and offsite mitigation) so new projects are built/operated with lower greenhouse gas emissions.

mechanism_description: Adopting ordinances and green building code standards for new construction (all-electric, ZNE, CALGreen tiers) reduces operational building energy demand and avoids on-site fossil fuel use, lowering emissions.
