section_header: 2.6.2.2 Strategy 2: Increase Densities and Diversity of Land Uses near Transit
policy_statement: Strategy 2 will increase densities and diversity of land uses near transit to reduce vehicle miles traveled by coordinating land use development outcomes such as increased densities near transit, jobs-housing balance, and strategically located land uses.
verbatim_text:
## 2.6.2.2 Strategy 2: Increase Densities and Diversity of Land Uses near Transit

Strategy 2 focuses on coordinating land use development that leads to outcomes associated with reduced vehicle miles traveled, such as increased densities near transit, jobs-housing balance, and strategically located land uses that can reduce travel distances for many trip purposes.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with defined intervention focus (land use coordination to reduce VMT); serves as umbrella for listed measures T1 and T2.
mechanism_description: Increase density and mixed land uses around transit to reduce trip distances and vehicle miles traveled, lowering transportation-related emissions.

section_header: Measure T1: Increase Density near High-Quality Transit Areas.
policy_statement: Measure T1 will increase density near High-Quality Transit Areas (HQTAs) by setting minimum and maximum dwelling unit density ranges, locating most residential and employment centers within 1 mile of an HQTA, and increasing dwelling units within HQTAs.
parent_statement: Strategy 2 will increase densities and diversity of land uses near transit to reduce vehicle miles traveled by coordinating land use development outcomes such as increased densities near transit, jobs-housing balance, and strategically located land uses.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T1: Increase Density near High-Quality Transit Areas.

The performance objectives for Measure T1 are to: (1) achieve a minimum of 20 dwelling units (DUs) per acre (maximum of 30-150 DUs per acre) for HQTAs; (2) locate a majority of residential and employment centers in the unincorporated Los Angeles County within 1 mile of an HQTA; and (3) achieve a 27 percent increase in dwelling units within HQTAs.
extraction_rationale: Specific measure with quantified performance objectives for density and location, linked under Strategy 2.
mechanism_description: Increase residential and employment density near high-quality transit so more trips shift from driving to transit (and trip lengths fall), reducing transport emissions and associated land-use impacts.

section_header: Measure T2: Develop Land Use Plans Addressing Jobs-Housing Balance and Increase Mixed Use.
policy_statement: Measure T2 will develop land use plans to address jobs-housing balance and increase mixed use, including achieving job density targets for new nonresidential projects and requiring community plans to identify strategies to reduce jobs/housing imbalance below 20%.
parent_statement: Strategy 2 will increase densities and diversity of land uses near transit to reduce vehicle miles traveled by coordinating land use development outcomes such as increased densities near transit, jobs-housing balance, and strategically located land uses.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T2: Develop Land Use Plans Addressing Jobs-Housing Balance and Increase Mixed Use.

The performance objective for Measure T2 is to achieve a job density of 300 jobs per acre for all new projects with nonresidential development by 2030. For communities with an imbalance of jobs/housing (±20 percent), community plans will identify and quantify strategies for bringing that imbalance below 20 percent.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target and planning requirement stated as a measure under Strategy 2.
mechanism_description: Land use planning for mixed-use development and improved jobs–housing balance reduces commute distances and driving, lowering transport emissions via reduced vehicle miles traveled.

policy_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
verbatim_text:
## 2.6.2.3 Strategy 3: Reduce Single-Occupancy Vehicle Trips

Strategy 3 focuses on development of transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes to help reduce trips made in single-occupancy vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy functioning as umbrella for measures T3–T5.
mechanism_description: Improving active travel networks shifts trips from single-occupancy vehicles to walking and cycling, lowering vehicle miles traveled and associated greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Measure T3: Expand Bicycle and Pedestrian Network to Serve Residential, Employment, and Recreational Trips.
policy_statement: Measure T3 will expand the bicycle and pedestrian network, including increasing bikeway miles by 300% by 2035 and implementing the County Bicycle Master Plan and updating pedestrian/bicycle/active transportation plans every five years.
parent_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T3: Expand Bicycle and Pedestrian Network to Serve Residential, Employment, and Recreational Trips.

The performance objective for Measure T3 is to increase bikeway miles by 300 percent by 2035. This measure also requires implementing the County Bicycle Master Plan and completing updates to County's Pedestrian Action Plan, Bicycle Master Plan, and Active Transportation Plans every five years.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target (300% by 2035) and specific implementation requirements; listed under Strategy 3.
mechanism_description: Expanding bicycle and pedestrian networks increases active travel and shifts trips away from private vehicles, reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Measure T4: Broaden Options for Transit, Active Transportation, and Alternative Modes of Transportation.
policy_statement: Measure T4 will broaden options for transit and alternative modes by 2030 by doubling transit service hours, installing bus-only lanes and signal prioritization on major transit thoroughfares, and ensuring at least 75% of unincorporated County residents live within one-half mile of a bus or active transportation option.
parent_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T4: Broaden Options for Transit, Active Transportation, and Alternative Modes of Transportation.

The performance objectives for Measure T4 are by 2030 to: (1) double transit service hours from 560,000 to 1.12 million; (2) install bus-only lanes and signal prioritization on all major transit thoroughfares; and (3) have a minimum of 75 percent of unincorporated Los Angeles County residents live within one-half mile of a bus or active transportation option;
extraction_rationale: Contains concrete, quantified performance objectives and infrastructure actions; listed under Strategy 3.
mechanism_description: Increase transit service and prioritize buses (lanes/signals) to improve transit access and reliability, shifting trips from cars to lower-emitting modes and reducing transport emissions.

section_header: Measure T5: Limit and Remove Parking Minimums.
policy_statement: Measure T5 will limit and remove parking minimums through parking supply reductions, unbundling parking costs, and implementing parking pricing to encourage park-once behavior.
parent_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T5: Limit and Remove Parking Minimums.

The performance objectives for Measure T5 are to: (1) reduce parking stipulations to reduce parking supply and encourage transit use; (2) unbundle parking costs to reflect the cost of parking; and (3) implement parking pricing to encourage 'park-once' behavior.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention set (parking reforms) framed as performance objectives; listed under Strategy 3.
mechanism_description: Reducing and pricing parking supply (including unbundling parking costs) discourages driving and encourages park-once, transit, walking, and cycling, reducing vehicle miles traveled and transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
verbatim_text:
## 2.6.2.4 Strategy 4: Institutionalize Low-Carbon Transportation

The County also would endeavor to install EV charging stations (EVCSs) at County properties and in the public right-of-way, require new development to install EVCSs, and develop incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EVCSs.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with explicit interventions; umbrella for measures T6–T9.
mechanism_description: Expand and mandate EV charging infrastructure so more people can adopt electric vehicles, enabling reductions in transportation emissions.

section_header: Measure T6: Increase ZEV Market Share and Reduce Gasoline and Diesel Fuel Sales.
policy_statement: Measure T6 will increase zero-emission vehicle (ZEV) market share and EV charging stations, including targets for fleetwide ZEV penetration, ZEV sales shares, total new EV charging stations, and EV charging stations at County facilities by 2030/2035/2045.
parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T6: Increase ZEV Market Share and Reduce Gasoline and Diesel Fuel Sales.

The performance objectives for Measure T6 are to: (1) increase the fleetwide percentage of light-duty vehicles in the unincorporated Los Angeles County that are ZEVs to 30 percent by 2030, 50 percent by 2035, and 90 percent by 2045; (2) increase the sales of new light-duty vehicles in the unincorporated Los Angeles County that are ZEVs to 68 percent by 2030 and 100 percent by 2035; (3) install 37,000 total new public and private shared EVCSs (including EVCSs at County facilities and properties) by 2030, 74,000 by 2035, and 140,000 by 2045; and (4) install 5,000 total new EVCSs at County facilities and properties by 2030, 10,000 by 2035, and 25,000 by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Multiple quantified targets with timelines; listed as a measure under Strategy 4.
mechanism_description: Increase zero-emission vehicle adoption (supported by expanded EV charging availability) to displace gasoline and diesel driving and reduce transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Measure T7: Electrify County Fleet Vehicles.
policy_statement: Measure T7 will electrify County fleet vehicles by electrifying bus and shuttle fleets by 2035, increasing County-owned light-duty ZEV shares by 2030/2035/2045, and supporting the state's goal that new light-duty fleet purchases (with exceptions) will be ZEVs.
parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T7: Electrify County Fleet Vehicles.

The performance objectives for Measure T7 are to: (1) electrify the County bus and shuttle fleets by 2035; (2) increase the fleetwide percentage of light-duty vehicles in the Countyowned fleet that are ZEVs to 35 percent by 2030, 60 percent by 2035, and 100 percent by 2045; and (3) support the state's goal that all new light-duty vehicle fleet purchases, with certain exceptions, will be ZEVs.
extraction_rationale: Concrete electrification commitments and quantified targets; under Strategy 4.
mechanism_description: Electrifying the county vehicle fleet replaces fossil-fueled vehicles with zero-emission vehicles, reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Measure T8 will accelerate freight decarbonization with ZEV targets for medium- and heavy-duty fleets and drayage, ZEV sales targets, and requirements for EV charging stations at new and existing warehouse loading docks by 2030.
parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T8: Accelerate Freight Decarbonization.

The performance objectives for Measure T8 are to: (1) increase the fleetwide percentage of medium-duty and heavy-duty vehicles in the unincorporated Los Angeles County that are ZEVs to 40 percent by 2030, 60 percent by 2035, and 90 percent by 2045; (2) increase the fleetwide percentage of medium-duty and heavy-duty trucks in the County-owned fleet that are ZEVs to 50 percent by 2030, 70 percent by 2035, and 95 percent by 2045; (3) ensure that 100 percent of the drayage truck fleet is ZEV by 2035; (4) ensure that 100 percent of sales of medium- and heavy-duty trucks are ZEVs by 2045; (5) require that all new warehouse loading docks have EVCSs by 2030; and (6) require that all existing warehouse loading docks have EVCSs by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified ZEV targets and explicit charging infrastructure requirements; measure under Strategy 4.
mechanism_description: Mandating/targeting zero-emission medium- and heavy-duty freight vehicles (supported by required charging at warehouses) increases freight fleet electrification, reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Measure T9: Expand Use of Zero-Emission Technologies for Off-Road Vehicles and Equipment.
policy_statement: Measure T9 will expand use of zero-emission off-road vehicles and equipment by increasing ZEV shares for off-road fleets and for construction/agriculture/manufacturing equipment by 2030/2035/2045.
parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T9: Expand Use of Zero-Emission Technologies for Off-Road Vehicles and Equipment.

The performance objectives for Measure T9 are to: (1) increase the fleetwide percentage of off-road fleet and equipment in the unincorporated Los Angeles County that are ZEVs to 20 percent by 2030, 50 percent by 2035, and 95 percent by 2045; and (2) increase the fleetwide percentage of construction, agriculture, and manufacturing equipment in the unincorporated Los Angeles County that are ZEVs to 50 percent by 2030, 75 percent by 2035, and 100 percent by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Specific quantified targets for off-road equipment electrification/zero-emission transition; under Strategy 4.
mechanism_description: Increase adoption of zero-emission off-road vehicles and equipment to displace fossil fuel combustion, thereby reducing transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Measure E1: Transition Existing Buildings to All-Electric.
policy_statement: Measure E1 will transition existing buildings to all-electric by electrifying shares of existing residential and nonresidential buildings and requiring zero net energy for major renovations by 2030/2035/2045.
verbatim_text:
## Measure E1: Transition Existing Buildings to All-Electric.

The primary performance objectives for Measure E1 are to: (1) electrify 25 percent of the existing residential buildings by 2030, 40 percent by 2035, and 80 percent by 2045; (2) electrify 15 percent of the existing nonresidential buildings by 2030, 25 percent by 2035, and 60 percent by 2045; and (3) require zero net energy (ZNE) for 50 percent of all major renovations by 2030, 75 percent by 2035, and 100 percent by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Concrete measure with quantified electrification and renovation performance requirements; not shown in prior summary.
mechanism_description: Mandating electrification and zero-net-energy performance in existing-building renovations reduces on-site fossil fuel combustion and energy use, lowering building-sector greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Measure E3: Implement Other Decarbonization Actions.
policy_statement: Measure E3 will implement other building decarbonization actions by increasing biomethane share in utility gas, using low/zero/negative-carbon concrete for all new construction, and replacing high-GWP refrigerants at specified rates by 2030/2035/2045.
verbatim_text:
## Measure E3: Implement Other Decarbonization Actions.

The performance objectives of Measure E3 are to: (1) increase the proportion of biomethane in the utility natural gas mix to 20 percent by 2030, 30 percent by 2035, and 80 percent by 2045; (2) use low-carbon, carbon-neutral, or negative-carbon concrete for all new construction; and (3) replace high-global-warming-potential refrigerants with lowglobal-warming-potential refrigerants 15 percent by 2030, 25 percent by 2035, and 50 percent by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Specific interventions with quantified targets and material requirements; standalone measure text in this chunk.
canonical_mechanism: biomethane_blending_in_gas_supply → fossil_fuel_emissions_reduction; embodied_carbon_reduction → construction_emissions_reduction; refrigerant_management → fugitive_emissions_reduction
mechanism_description: Increase low-carbon gaseous fuel in the gas mix, require lower-embodied-carbon concrete in new builds, and replace high-GWP refrigerants to reduce combustion, materials lifecycle, and fugitive emissions.

section_header: Measure E4: Improve Energy Efficiency of Existing Buildings.
policy_statement: Measure E4 will improve energy efficiency of existing buildings by reducing building energy use intensity relative to 2015 by sector-specific percentages by 2030/2035/2045.
verbatim_text:
## Measure E4: Improve Energy Efficiency of Existing Buildings.

The performance objective of Measure E4 is to reduce building Energy Use Intensity (thousand British thermal units per square foot) below 2015 levels by 20 percent for residential, 15 percent for industrial, and 25 percent for commercial by 2030; 25 percent for residential and industrial and 35 percent for commercial by 2035; and 50 percent for residential, industrial, and commercial by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Quantified efficiency targets over time; clear performance standard.
mechanism_description: Improve energy efficiency in existing buildings to reduce building energy use intensity, thereby lowering overall building energy consumption and associated emissions.

section_header: Measure E5: Increase Use of Recycled Water and Gray Water Systems.
policy_statement: Measure E5 will increase use of recycled water and gray water systems by meeting specified shares of total and agricultural/industrial water demand with recycled/gray/potable reuse by 2025/2030/2045 and implementing a direct potable reuse project by 2025.
verbatim_text:
## Measure E5: Increase Use of Recycled Water and Gray Water Systems.

The performance objectives of Measure E5 are to: (1) meet 25 percent of total unincorporated Los Angeles County water demand with recycled water, gray water, or potable reuse by 2025, 50 percent by 2030, and 90 percent by 2045; (2) meet 30 percent of water demand for agricultural and industrial uses with recycled water or gray water by 2025, 50 percent by 2030, and 80 percent by 2045; and (3) implement a successful direct potable reuse project by 2025.
extraction_rationale: Water-sector measure with quantified demand shares and a dated project commitment.
mechanism_description: Expanding recycled water/graywater/direct potable reuse substitutes reclaimed water for conventional potable supplies, reducing energy use and associated GHG emissions from water sourcing, conveyance, and treatment.

section_header: Measure E6: Reduce Indoor and Outdoor Water Consumption.
policy_statement: Measure E6 will reduce indoor and outdoor water consumption by setting per-capita water use targets and reducing outdoor landscaping and municipal water consumption by specified percentages by 2030/2035/2045.
verbatim_text:
## Measure E6: Reduce Indoor and Outdoor Water Consumption.

The performance objectives of Measure E6 are to: (1) reduce total water use to less than 110 gallons per capita per day (GPCD) by 2030, 100 GPCD by 2035, and 85 GPCD by 2045; (2) reduce outdoor landscaping water use to 10 percent by 2030, 20 percent by 2035, and 50 percent by 2045; and (3) reduce municipal water consumption 10 percent by 2030, 20 percent by 2035, and 50 percent by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Quantified conservation targets with dates; concrete performance objectives.
mechanism_description: Setting per-capita and use-specific water reduction targets drives efficiency and conservation actions that reduce overall water demand.

section_header: Measure W1: Institutionalize Sustainable Waste Systems and Practices.
policy_statement: Measure W1 will institutionalize sustainable waste systems and practices by increasing waste diversion rates to 85%/90%/95% by 2030/2035/2045, reducing single-use plastics disposal, increasing construction and demolition diversion to 70%, and increasing reuse of construction and demolition debris in new projects.
verbatim_text:
## Measure W1: Institutionalize Sustainable Waste Systems and Practices.

The performance objectives of Measure W1 are to: (1) increase the total unincorporated Los Angeles County waste diversion rate to 85 percent by 2030, 90 percent by 2035, and 95 percent by 2045; (2) reduce the disposal of single-use plastics in landfills; (3) increase the Construction and Demolition Ordinance to 70 percent diversion; and (4) increase the percentage of construction and demolition debris reused in new projects (private and public).
extraction_rationale: Waste policy with quantified diversion targets and specific ordinance-related actions.
mechanism_description: Increase diversion and reuse of waste (including C&D materials and reduced single-use plastics landfilling) to send less material to landfills, thereby avoiding landfill methane emissions.

section_header: Measure A1: Conserve Forests, Woodlands, Shrublands, Grasslands, Desert, and other Carbon-Sequestering Wildlands and Working Lands.
policy_statement: Measure A1 will conserve and restore carbon-sequestering wildlands and working lands by reducing natural land conversion, conserving/restoring forest lands, and managing wildland acreage for wildfire risk reduction and carbon stock savings by 2030/2035/2045.
verbatim_text:
## Measure A1: Conserve Forests, Woodlands, Shrublands, Grasslands, Desert, and other Carbon-Sequestering Wildlands and Working Lands.

The performance objectives of Measure A1 are to: (1) reduce the average annual amount of natural land converted for urbanized uses 25 percent by 2030 (53 hectares conserved annually), 50 percent by 2035 (106 hectares conserved annually), and 75 percent by 2045 (159 hectares conserved annually); (2) conserve and restore 2,000 acres of natural forest lands by 2030, 4,000 acres by 2035, and 6,000 acres by 2045; and (3) manage 10,000 acres of wildland for wildfire risk reduction and carbon stock savings by 2030, 20,000 acres by 2035, and 50,000 acres by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Land conservation/restoration measure with quantified acreage/hectare targets and timelines.
mechanism_description: Protecting, restoring, and managing natural and working lands reduces land conversion and wildfire-related carbon loss, preserving ecosystems and maintaining or increasing stored carbon.

section_header: Measure A3: Expand Unincorporated Los Angeles County's Tree Canopy and Green Spaces.
policy_statement: Measure A3 will expand the unincorporated County's tree canopy and green spaces by planting 130,000/200,000/270,000 total new trees by 2030/2035/2045 and developing and implementing an Urban Forest Management Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Measure A3: Expand Unincorporated Los Angeles County's Tree Canopy and Green Spaces.

The performance objectives of Measure A3 are to: (1) plant 130,000 total new trees by 2030, 200,000 total new trees by 2035, and 270,000 total new trees by 2045; and (2) develop and implement an Urban Forest Management Plan.
extraction_rationale: Quantified tree-planting targets and planning requirement.
mechanism_description: Planting and managing more urban trees increases canopy cover, which sequesters carbon and cools the urban environment, reducing heat impacts.

policy_statement: Require mitigation for oak woodland loss at a minimum 1:1 in-kind replacement via a County-approved habitat mitigation plan, including monitoring for at least five years and consistency with the County Oak Woodland Conservation Management Plan and 2014 Guide (prioritizing acquisition over restoration and in-lieu fees where applicable).
verbatim_text:
Mitigation Measure 3.5-5: Proponents of projects resulting in the loss of oak woodlands shall mitigate with in-kind replacement habitat at a minimum of 1:1 mitigation ratio documented through a County-approved habitat mitigation plan... The plan shall include monitoring for a minimum of five years, with annual reports submitted to the County.

For oak woodlands impacts, project mitigation shall be consistent with recommendations in the County's Oak Woodland Conservation Management Plan and its 2014 Guide.
extraction_rationale: This is a specific compensatory mitigation requirement with a defined minimum ratio and monitoring duration, plus required consistency with named County guidance.
mechanism_description: Requiring in-kind replacement and protection of oak woodland habitat for any woodland loss maintains ecosystem area and carbon storage capacity, helping preserve carbon stocks and associated resilience benefits.

policy_statement: Draft 2045 CAP sets a 2045 target to reduce unincorporated Los Angeles County greenhouse gas emissions 83% below 2015 baseline levels.
verbatim_text:
The unincorporated County's emissions with Draft 2045 CAP implementation are compared to the following future targets:

- The Draft 2045 CAP's target of 83 percent below 2015 levels by 2045, which aligns with the statewide 2045 target of 85 percent below 1990 levels as codified in AB 1279 and included in the 2022 Scoping Plan.
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions-reduction target with a deadline (2045) stated as a CAP target.
mechanism_description: Setting an economy-wide GHG reduction target creates a pathway and accountability framework intended to drive overall emissions reductions across sectors.

section_header: ## Natural Disasters — ## Efforts for the Future
policy_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Efforts for the Future

In response to natural threats, such as floods, inland floods, storm surges, and landslides due to intensified heavy rains and typhoons, TMG will promote the utilization of state-of-the-art technologies and the development of urban facilities in both structural and non-structural aspects.

To cope with increasingly larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains in recent years, TMG will further improve our initiatives.
extraction_rationale: Introduces an umbrella set of measures with multiple structured subcomponents (structural and non-structural) detailed below.
mechanism_description: Implementing advanced technology-enabled structural and non-structural urban disaster measures reduces damages and disruption from heavier rains and stronger typhoons (e.g., flooding, storm surge, landslides).

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will develop regulating reservoirs (including extending the Wide-Area Regulating Reservoir under Loop Road No. 7 as an underground river), improve river discharge capacity in the Tama area, and implement preventive river maintenance using ICT/AI for inspections.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Measures for rivers against heavy rains

- ' Promote the development of regulating reservoirs under construction, such as the Wide-Area Regulating Reservoir under Loop Road No. 7, in parallel with that of river revetment.
- ' Proceed with the study to operationalize new regulating reservoirs and extend the Wide-Area Regulating Reservoir under Loop Road No. 7 by making it an underground river.
- ' In the Tama area damaged by Typhoon Hagibis in 2019, TMG will improve the discharge capacity of rivers through local improvements.

## Promotion of strategic maintenance of rivers

- ' Steadily implement maintenance based on a preventive maintenance plan...
- ' Ensure more efficient and sophisticated inspections through the introduction of ICT/AI...
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure and maintenance actions for rivers with clear verbs ('promote,' 'proceed,' 'will improve,' 'introduce ICT/AI').
mechanism_description: Build and maintain regulating reservoirs and improve river channel discharge capacity to increase stormwater buffering and conveyance, thereby reducing flooding risk during heavy rains.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will develop sewerage trunk lines and storage to handle 75 mm/hour rainfall in high-risk areas, verify sewerage capacity via runoff simulation for 75 mm/hour rainfall, and strengthen countermeasures in newly identified high-risk districts.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Development of sewerage facilities

- ' Develop large trunk lines and storage facilities that can handle precipitation of 75 mm/hour...
- ' By utilizing runoff analysis simulation technology, TMG will verify the capacity of sewerage facilities in case of precipitation of 75 mm/hour, add new districts at high risk of inundation, and strengthen the relevant countermeasures.
extraction_rationale: Contains a quantifiable design standard (75 mm/hour) and concrete infrastructure and analytical actions.
mechanism_description: Increase stormwater sewer trunk and storage capacity (validated with runoff simulations) to convey heavy rainfall and thereby reduce urban inundation and flood impacts.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will set non-binding rainwater runoff control targets (equivalent to 10 mm/hour rainfall) for municipalities, post progress information, consider expanding subsidized prioritized basins, conduct model projects with municipalities, and promote greening to reduce flood damage.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Promotion of measures for river basins

- ' To encourage municipalities to make voluntary and systematic efforts to control rainwater runoff equivalent to precipitation of 10 mm/hour, TMG will set non-binding targets for the amount to be controlled and post information on progress made towards achieving such targets.
- ' Consider adding prioritized basins that will be subsidized...
- ' Carry out model projects in cooperation with municipalities...
- ' Promote efforts to create and conserve greenery and reduce flood damage through a rainwater drainage function.
extraction_rationale: Specific interventions include target-setting (10 mm/hour), subsidies, model projects, and greening for drainage.
mechanism_description: Setting runoff-control targets and supporting basin projects and greening increases stormwater retention and drainage capacity, which reduces flooding and associated damage.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will use digital technology to improve disaster preparedness functions of agricultural canal reservoirs and intake gates.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Enhancement of agricultural infrastructure

- ' Utilize digital technology to improve the disaster preparedness function of reservoirs and intake gates of agricultural canals.
extraction_rationale: Concrete technology-enabled adaptation action for agricultural water infrastructure.
mechanism_description: Deploying digital controls/monitoring for reservoirs and intake gates improves agricultural water infrastructure preparedness and operational response during disasters, increasing water system resilience.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will develop storm surge protection facilities for the eastern lowlands, formulate the next Port of Tokyo Coastal Conservation Facilities Development Plan, develop coastal conservation facilities considering climate impacts, and enable remote floodgate operations and monitoring.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Measures for storm surges

- ' Promote the development of storm surge protection facilities to protect the lives and livelihoods of approximately 3 million people living in the eastern lowlands.
- ' Formulate the next Port of Tokyo Coastal Conservation Facilities Development Plan and promote the development of coastal conservation facilities taking into account the impacts of climate change.
- ' Prepare for emergencies by remotely opening and closing floodgates whenever possible and monitoring camera videos and information signals sent from facilities.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure planning and operational measures; includes an affected population figure (~3 million).
mechanism_description: Developing coastal storm-surge defenses and enabling remote floodgate operation and monitoring reduces coastal flooding impacts and associated risks to people and assets under climate change.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will construct coastal conservation facilities (e.g., detached breakwaters) on islands to protect coastal villages from high tidal waves caused by typhoons and other disasters.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Construction of coastal conservation facilities on the islands

- ' Promote the construction of coastal conservation facilities, such as detached breakwaters, to protect villages etc. along the coast from high tidal waves caused by typhoons and other natural disasters.
extraction_rationale: Concrete coastal infrastructure commitment for island protection.
mechanism_description: Constructing coastal defense facilities like breakwaters reduces exposure to typhoon-driven storm surge and high waves, lowering flood and wave damage risk for coastal communities.

section_header: ## ii Protection of functions of urban facilities
policy_statement: TMG will promote removal of utility poles and strengthen support for municipality roads under the Strategy for Accelerating the Removal of Utility Poles.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Promoting the removal of utility poles

- ' Promote the removal of utility poles to prevent their collapse during earthquakes, storms and flooding, and facilitate a smooth response to disasters.
- ' Work to strengthen support for municipality roads in addition to Tokyo metropolitan roads and efforts for urban development based on the Strategy for Accelerating the Removal of Utility Poles.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure resilience action (undergrounding/removal of poles) tied to a named strategy.
mechanism_description: Removing/undergrounding utility poles and strengthening road infrastructure reduces damage and service disruptions during extreme events, improving overall disaster resilience and response capacity.

section_header: ## ii Protection of functions of urban facilities
policy_statement: TMG will build redundancy in roads/bridges for disasters by building a highway network, constructing new bridges (including along borders with Chiba), improving access roads to the Tachikawa Wide-Area Disaster Management Base, and upgrading emergency transport routes (bridges, alternative routes, widening).
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Development of roads and bridges

- ' Build a highway network to ensure redundancy 1 in the event of a disaster.
- ' Promote the construction of new bridges along sections that border Chiba Prefecture...
- ' Improve accessibility to the Tachikawa Wide-Area Disaster Management Base...
- ' Improve the disaster preparedness of Tokyo... through the construction or replacement of bridges along emergency transport routes, the development of roads as alternative routes..., and the widening of emergency transport roads.
extraction_rationale: Multiple concrete transport infrastructure commitments to maintain evacuation/logistics during disasters.
mechanism_description: Upgrading and adding redundant roads and bridges strengthens emergency transport routes so travel and logistics can continue during disasters, improving overall resilience and response capability.

section_header: ## ii Protection of functions of urban facilities
policy_statement: TMG will diagnose and systematically renew roadside trees in areas with many fallen trees to strengthen their disaster preparedness function.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Strengthening the disaster preparedness function of roadside trees

- ' To strengthen the disaster preparedness function of roadside trees, TMG will focus on the diagnosis of roadside trees in areas with many fallen trees due to typhoons for their systematic renewal.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (diagnosis and renewal) targeting typhoon-related treefall risk.
mechanism_description: Diagnosing and renewing roadside trees maintains and strengthens urban tree cover, improving climate resilience (e.g., storm/heat buffering) while also supporting carbon sequestration.

section_header: ## ii Protection of functions of urban facilities
policy_statement: TMG will improve stability of water supply by doubling water conveyance facilities, building wide-area transmission pipe networks with a dual system, and burying pipelines that cross rivers.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## More stable water supply

- ' Double water conveyance facilities to improve the stability of water supply.
- ' Construct wide-area water transmission pipe networks and promote a dual system for water transmission to water supply stations...
- ' Bury pipelines that cross rivers, as surface pipes may be affected by river flooding...
extraction_rationale: Concrete water infrastructure resilience measures to prevent service disruption during floods.
mechanism_description: Upgrade and add redundancy to water transmission and conveyance infrastructure to reduce service disruptions and improve water supply reliability during hazards such as flooding.

section_header: ## ii Protection of functions of urban facilities
policy_statement: TMG will add and strengthen flood control measures for the Toei Subway based on newly announced expected inundation areas and examine measures for early restoration from large-scale floods.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Flood control in subways

- ' Take necessary additional measures for the Toei Subway in light of the announcement of new expected inundation areas that may assume the heaviest possible rainfall.
- ' Strengthen measures against inundation by examining more effective measures for early restoration from large-scale floods, such as overflowing of the Arakawa River.
extraction_rationale: Specific public transport flood resilience actions tied to inundation projections.
mechanism_description: Strengthening subway flood defenses and preparing early-restoration measures reduces inundation impacts and shortens transit service disruptions during large-scale floods.

section_header: ## iii Promotion of urban development with disaster preparedness
policy_statement: TMG will promote urban development with elevation in model districts to avoid catastrophic flood damage and continue coordination with the national government and wards.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Promotion of urban development with elevation

- ' Promote urban development with elevation to avoid catastrophic damage in the event of large-scale floods by continuing liaison meetings with the national government and promoting examination and operationalization in model districts in cooperation with the national government and wards.
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning and operationalization commitment for elevated urban development.
mechanism_description: Elevating urban development reduces exposure to floodwaters, thereby avoiding or reducing catastrophic flood damage during large-scale flood events.

section_header: ## iii Promotion of urban development with disaster preparedness
policy_statement: TMG will elevate Shinozaki Park to secure evacuation routes to the Edogawa Levee.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Promoting the elevation of a park

- ' Elevate Shinozaki Park and secure evacuation routes to the Edogawa Levee.
extraction_rationale: Specific named project (Shinozaki Park elevation) for evacuation resilience.
mechanism_description: Elevating park land and improving evacuation routes increases protection and access during floods, improving community flood resilience and evacuation safety.

section_header: ## iii Promotion of urban development with disaster preparedness
policy_statement: TMG will support municipalities' risk assessment efforts to prevent damage from broken residential land retaining walls.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Efforts for building land retaining walls

- ' Provide support for municipalities working on risk assessment to prevent damage caused by broken retaining walls for residential land.
extraction_rationale: Concrete support action (risk assessment support) aimed at preventing failures.
mechanism_description: Supporting municipal risk assessments for residential retaining walls helps identify and address failure risks, reducing landslide-related damage to homes and infrastructure.

policy_statement: TMG will prioritize erosion control work in landslide-prone areas with evacuation centers and where landslides have occurred, and manage slope protection facilities (e.g., ground anchors) with preventive maintenance; it will also manage forests for disaster preparedness and encourage relocation to safer areas via consolidated regional structure efforts.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Implementation of erosion control work

- ' Prioritize erosion control work in landslide prone areas where evacuation centers are located and locations where landslides have actually occurred.

## Preventive maintenance management of existing ground anchors

- ' Promote preventive maintenance management of various slope protection facilities...

## Strengthening the disaster preparedness function of forests

- ' Improve benefits... by thinning and pruning...
- ' Develop disaster-resistant forests...

## Making regional structure consolidated

- ' Promote efforts for making regional structure consolidated... TMG will encourage relocation to safer areas...
extraction_rationale: Multiple specific landslide-risk actions including erosion control prioritization, preventive maintenance, forest management, and relocation encouragement.
mechanism_description: Prioritizing erosion control and slope-protection maintenance, managing forests for slope/disaster resilience, and encouraging relocation away from high-risk zones reduces exposure to landslides and associated disaster impacts.

policy_statement: TMG will strengthen flood response by developing personal flood-disaster materials and equipment.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Strengthening disaster response in rescue activities

- ' Strengthen the flood prevention activity system by developing personal materials and equipment for flood disasters.
extraction_rationale: Specific preparedness investment/action: develop equipment and materials.
mechanism_description: Developing and equipping personal flood-disaster response materials improves emergency response capability, reducing impacts and damages from flooding events.

policy_statement: TMG will promote self-consumed renewable generation (solar, storage batteries) and fuel cells for emergency power availability, and install emergency power supplies at TMG and municipal facilities and other important bases.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Expansion and installation of emergency power supplies

- ' To ensure electricity is available... even in the event of a disaster, TMG will promote the spread of selfconsumed renewable generation, such as solar power generation and storage batteries, as well as fuel cells, for better regional disaster preparedness.
- ' Promote the installation of emergency power supplies at TMG facilities, municipalities' buildings, and other important bases.
extraction_rationale: Concrete actions to deploy distributed energy resources and emergency power supplies for resilience.
mechanism_description: Deploying on-site renewables, storage, and other emergency power systems at critical facilities maintains electricity availability during disruptions, improving power-supply resilience.

policy_statement: TMG will promote the spread of ZEVs for disaster power supply (V2H/V2B), including installing public chargers and hydrogen stations and introducing portable V2L and V2H equipment.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Promoting the spread of ZEVs

- ' Promote the spread of zero-emission vehicles (ZEVs 2 ) as "moving storage batteries"...
- ' Promote the installation of public chargers and hydrogen stations necessary for the spread, as well as the introduction of portable vehicle-to-load systems and V2H equipment necessary for the supply of power.
extraction_rationale: Specific technology and infrastructure actions supporting resilience and low-emission transport.
mechanism_description: Installing EV charging and hydrogen refueling infrastructure (and related V2X equipment) enables wider zero-emission vehicle adoption, supporting reduced transport emissions while also improving emergency power resilience.

policy_statement: TMG will improve island resilience by installing solar generation and storage on islands and considering regional renewable energy sharing.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Better resilience in regions

- ' Improve the resilience of the islands by promoting the installation of solar power generation equipment and storage batteries on the islands.
- ' Consider the sharing of regional renewable energy and aim to strengthen regional disaster preparedness.
extraction_rationale: Concrete resilience measures for islands including renewable deployment and energy sharing consideration.
mechanism_description: Deploying island solar-plus-storage and enabling regional renewable power sharing increases electricity system flexibility and reliability, supporting higher renewable penetration and more resilient power supply during disasters.

policy_statement: TMG will raise awareness of disaster preparedness through targeted communications and tools (Trial Examination, booklet, app, My Timeline), distribute flood risk maps and VR content, continue drills for foreigners with plain Japanese, and provide condominium-focused information tools.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## i Improvement of awareness of disaster preparedness

- ' Raise awareness of disaster preparedness fine-tuned to different generations... through the Tokyo Disaster Preparedness Trial Examination... and distribution of the Tokyo Disaster Preparedness App.
- ' Distribute Tokyo My Timeline... and introduce a reminder...
- ' Raise public awareness of floods by distributing a flood risk map... and a VR video...
- ' Continue disaster preparedness drills for foreigners...
- ' Promote awareness raising by providing information through the Condominium Portal Site...
extraction_rationale: Specific non-structural interventions: apps, timelines, maps/VR, drills, and information portals.

policy_statement: TMG will strengthen evacuation support systems (including guidelines considering emerging infectious diseases and vulnerable groups), consider wide-area evacuation for large floods, expand shelter options including hotels/inns, update hazard maps, enhance flood measures for underground spaces, review/designate landslide-risk areas and support municipal alert/evacuation systems, and digitize disaster preparedness (digital signage, smart poles, open APIs).
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.

- ' Promote the update of hazard maps...

extraction_rationale: Bundle of concrete system-building commitments across evacuation, mapping, underground flood control, landslides, and digitization.
mechanism_description: Upgrading evacuation systems, shelters, hazard mapping, risk-area management, and digital disaster communications improves preparedness and response, reducing exposure and harm from floods and landslides under climate-driven extreme events.

policy_statement: TMG will support remote disaster response and business continuity by identifying operations that can be handled remotely, supporting SMBs in BCP formulation and telework introduction, and verifying the Central Market BCP while supporting BCPs for market-related businesses.

mechanism_description: Support for business continuity plans and remote/telework-capable operations helps organizations maintain critical services during disasters, improving adaptive capacity and resilience to climate-related disruptions.

mechanism_description: Providing technical and financial assistance enables businesses handling chemicals to implement flood control measures, reducing flood impacts and the risk of hazardous substance releases during extreme weather.

section_header: ## 2) Response in the event of a disaster / ## i Collection, analysis, and provision of information

mechanism_description: Upgrading and integrating disaster information and communications systems improves situational awareness and coordination, enabling faster, more effective emergency response and climate resilience.

section_header: ## 2) Response in the event of a disaster / ## i Collection, analysis, and provision of information

mechanism_description: Deploying AI-enabled digital information and situational awareness tools improves disaster-response coordination and decision-making, strengthening adaptive capacity to climate-related emergencies.

section_header: ## 2) Response in the event of a disaster / ## i Collection, analysis, and provision of information

section_header: ## 2) Response in the event of a disaster / ## i Collection, analysis, and provision of information

mechanism_description: Improving inter-organizational liaison deployment and conducting iterative disaster-response training strengthens plans and coordination, increasing institutional capacity to manage storm and flood impacts.

section_header: ## 2) Response in the event of a disaster / ## ii Enhancement of the system

section_header: ## 2) Response in the event of a disaster / ## ii Enhancement of the system
policy_statement: TMG will support disaster waste planning and wide-area cooperation for proper disposal and promote systems for temporary housing and emergency repairs after disasters.

mechanism_description: Disaster waste planning, manuals, and wide-area cooperation improve preparedness and coordination so debris can be handled safely and quickly and recovery systems (temporary housing/repairs) function after disasters, reducing cascading environmental and public-health risks and speeding recovery.

policy_statement: TMG will strengthen health adaptation by implementing preventive and ex-post measures to minimize heatstroke, infectious disease risks, and air-pollution health hazards.
verbatim_text:
## Efforts for the Future

TMG will further strengthen appropriate preventive and ex-post measures to minimize adverse health effects due to temperature rise, such as patients with heat stroke or infectious diseases and health hazards caused by air pollution.
extraction_rationale: Umbrella commitment introducing multiple sub-areas (heat, infectious diseases, air pollution) detailed below.
mechanism_description: Implement preventive and response public-health measures to reduce heat-related illness, infectious disease risks, and air-pollution health hazards under climate change.

policy_statement: TMG will promote public heat countermeasures by fostering momentum for Uchimizu, creating cool spots with misting devices, constructing about 245 km of heat-blocking/water-retaining pavements by FY2030, promoting urban greening, increasing Tokyo Zero Emission Houses and supporting municipal heat-shielding/efficient appliance upgrades, and using smart poles to collect temperature/humidity data.
parent_statement: TMG will strengthen health adaptation by implementing preventive and ex-post measures to minimize heatstroke, infectious disease risks, and air-pollution health hazards.
verbatim_text:
## (1) Heat countermeasures

- ' Foster momentum for 'Uchimizu'... through the distribution of 'Uchimizu' goods...
- ' Install heat countermeasure equipment, including fine misting devices...
- ' Construct a total of approximately 245 km of heat blocking and water-retaining pavements... by FY 2030.
- ' Steadily operate the Greenery Program...
- ' Increase the number of Tokyo Zero Emission Houses...
- ' Collect temperature and humidity data through smart poles...
extraction_rationale: Contains multiple concrete interventions and a quantified pavement target (245 km by FY2030).
mechanism_description: Deploying urban cooling measures (e.g., misting/water-retaining surfaces and greening) reduces urban heat island intensity and lowers human heat exposure.

policy_statement: TMG will reduce mosquito-borne infectious disease risk by providing occurrence/prevention/source-control information, conducting mosquito surveillance, and ensuring pathogen inspection systems; it will also surveil fire ants with partners and raise awareness to reduce risks from harmful alien species.
parent_statement: TMG will strengthen health adaptation by implementing preventive and ex-post measures to minimize heatstroke, infectious disease risks, and air-pollution health hazards.
verbatim_text:
## Countermeasures for mosquito-borne infectious diseases

- ' Reduce the risk of infectious disease outbreak by providing information...
- ' Conduct surveillance of mosquitoes... and ensure an inspection system for pathogens.

## Measures against alien species which cause damage to humans

- ' ...TMG will... conducting surveillance of fire ants, and raising awareness of Tokyo residents.
extraction_rationale: Concrete surveillance, information provision, and inspection system commitments for infectious disease and alien species risks.
mechanism_description: Implementing vector/invasive-species surveillance, pathogen inspection, and public information systems improves early detection and response, reducing climate-amplified health risks and strengthening adaptive capacity.

policy_statement: TMG will reduce air pollutant emissions by encouraging factory measures, vehicle emission reduction, and voluntary business efforts; coordinate with the Nine Local Governments Coalition to analyze mechanisms and take measures; and publish air quality data as open data for advanced-tech-enabled air pollution measures.
parent_statement: TMG will strengthen health adaptation by implementing preventive and ex-post measures to minimize heatstroke, infectious disease risks, and air-pollution health hazards.
verbatim_text:
## Reduction of air pollutant emissions

- ' To reduce the concentrations of PM2.5 and photochemical oxidants, TMG will reduce the emissions... at factories, vehicle emission reduction measures, and voluntary efforts by businesses.
- ' Work with other members of the Nine Local Governments Coalition to analyze the mechanism and take measures accordingly...
- ' Make air quality data available as open data...
extraction_rationale: Specific regulatory/coordination/data actions aimed at reducing PM2.5 and photochemical oxidants.
mechanism_description: Publishing air quality data and coordinating analysis improves monitoring and institutional capacity, enabling better-targeted pollution control measures and planning.

section_header: ## Major Efforts through DX / ## ¢ Tokyo-Style Smart Agriculture Project
policy_statement: TMG will promote Tokyo-Style Smart Agriculture to maintain stable agricultural production under climate change and realize high profits on a small scale using IoT, AI, and other advanced technologies, including R&D across three fields.
verbatim_text:
## ¢ Tokyo-Style Smart Agriculture Project

To maintain stable agricultural production even under climate change, including large typhoons and extreme heat... TMG will promote Tokyo-Style Smart Agriculture... by making use of IoT, AI, and other advanced technologies.

...move ahead with research and development in the following three fields:
extraction_rationale: Named project with structured subcomponents (three R&D fields) described below.
mechanism_description: Deploying IoT/AI-enabled smart agriculture practices improves farm monitoring and management, helping agricultural production remain stable under extreme weather and heat driven by climate change.

section_header: ## (1) Tokyo Future Agrisystem (small solar-powered plant factory)
policy_statement: TMG will develop/verify the Tokyo Future Agrisystem (small solar-powered plant factory) and consider verification tests to expand coverage to strawberries and reduce facility/equipment costs using small computers.
parent_statement: TMG will promote Tokyo-Style Smart Agriculture to maintain stable agricultural production under climate change and realize high profits on a small scale using IoT, AI, and other advanced technologies, including R&D across three fields.
verbatim_text:
## (1) Tokyo Future Agrisystem (small solar-powered plant factory)

TMG will consider a verification test of expanding coverage to strawberries based on the knowledge of tomatoes and cucumbers we have developed and additional cost savings in facilities and equipment by taking advantage of small computers.
extraction_rationale: Concrete planned action: verification testing and expansion to specific crop (strawberries).
mechanism_description: Pilot and scale solar-powered plant factories to grow crops with lower fossil energy use, reducing emissions associated with agricultural production energy demand.

section_header: ## (2) Technical verification of new agricultural systems built on IoT, AI, and other advanced technologies
policy_statement: TMG will conduct verification tests of new agricultural systems using IoT/AI and advanced technologies (e.g., farm stand support, environmentally controlled fruit cultivation, lightweight flexible solar cells).
parent_statement: TMG will promote Tokyo-Style Smart Agriculture to maintain stable agricultural production under climate change and realize high profits on a small scale using IoT, AI, and other advanced technologies, including R&D across three fields.
verbatim_text:
## (2) Technical verification of new agricultural systems built on IoT, AI, and other advanced technologies

TMG will conduct verification tests of new elements of agriculture, such as a farm stand support system, an environmentally controlled cultivation system for fruit tree root restriction cultivation, and systems using lightweight flexible solar cells.
extraction_rationale: Specific R&D verification actions with examples of systems/technologies.
mechanism_description: Test and deploy advanced precision/controlled-environment agricultural systems (including solar-enabled tech) to improve farm resource efficiency and climate resilience, supporting lower-impact land management and carbon/ecosystem benefits.

section_header: ## (3) Development of new agricultural technologies based on local 5G
policy_statement: TMG will aim to implement new agricultural technologies using local 5G, including ultra-high resolution cameras, smart glasses, and autonomous driving robots, in collaboration with private businesses.
parent_statement: TMG will promote Tokyo-Style Smart Agriculture to maintain stable agricultural production under climate change and realize high profits on a small scale using IoT, AI, and other advanced technologies, including R&D across three fields.
verbatim_text:
## (3) Development of new agricultural technologies based on local 5G

In collaboration with private businesses, TMG aims to implement new agricultural technologies using local 5G, which incorporate advanced technologies, such as ultra-high resolution cameras, smart glasses, and autonomous driving robots.
extraction_rationale: Concrete implementation aim with specific enabling technology (local 5G) and examples.
mechanism_description: Deploying 5G-enabled precision agriculture and automation improves farm operational efficiency, reducing fertilizer/fuel/energy inputs and thereby lowering associated greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: ## Water resources and water environment — ## Efforts for the Future
policy_statement: TMG will ensure stable supply of high-quality tap water by reducing drought and raw water quality risks, improving sewer systems and treatment facilities, and continuing monitoring for water quality improvements.
verbatim_text:
## Efforts for the Future

TMG will reduce the risks posed by severe droughts and deterioration of raw water quality as much as possible to ensure stable supply of high-quality tap water.

We will create a comfortable water environment through improvements in water quality by enhancing the combined sewer system and developing advanced treatment facilities... In addition, we will continue monitoring.
extraction_rationale: Umbrella commitment for multiple water supply and water quality measures detailed in subsections.
mechanism_description: Upgrading sewer systems and water/wastewater treatment and maintaining water-quality monitoring reduces contamination and drought-related water-quality risks, improving the reliability and safety of tap water supply.

section_header: ## (1) Stable supply of high-quality tap water
policy_statement: TMG will improve water supply stability by doubling water conveyance facilities.
parent_statement: TMG will ensure stable supply of high-quality tap water by reducing drought and raw water quality risks, improving sewer systems and treatment facilities, and continuing monitoring for water quality improvements.
verbatim_text:
## (1) Stable supply of high-quality tap water

## More stable water supply

- ' Double water conveyance facilities to improve the stability of water supply.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure action clearly stated.
mechanism_description: Expanding water conveyance capacity increases system redundancy and delivery capability, improving the reliability and stability of water supply under disruptions and climate stressors.

section_header: ## (1) Stable supply of high-quality tap water

- ' Purchase devastated privately owned forests and cooperate with local governments to restore such forests.

extraction_rationale: Concrete forest management and acquisition/restoration actions linked to water security.
mechanism_description: Actively restoring and managing degraded forests (and monitoring conditions) improves forest health and resilience, helping preserve ecosystem functions and maintain or protect carbon storage.

section_header: ## (1) Stable supply of high-quality tap water
policy_statement: TMG will improve purification plant operations by considering new purification technologies, reducing environmental load, and introducing ICT and other latest technologies.
parent_statement: TMG will ensure stable supply of high-quality tap water by reducing drought and raw water quality risks, improving sewer systems and treatment facilities, and continuing monitoring for water quality improvements.

extraction_rationale: Specific operational and technology adoption commitment.
mechanism_description: Deploying improved purification technologies and ICT to optimize treatment plant operations increases the reliability and effectiveness of safe water treatment under changing raw water conditions while reducing operational environmental impacts.

section_header: ## (2) Measures for preservation of water quality of public water bodies
policy_statement: TMG will reduce pollution loads by continuously improving combined sewer systems (including capturing initial stormwater and introducing separate sewer systems for some facilities/development districts).
parent_statement: TMG will ensure stable supply of high-quality tap water by reducing drought and raw water quality risks, improving sewer systems and treatment facilities, and continuing monitoring for water quality improvements.
verbatim_text:
## (2) Measures for preservation of water quality of public water bodies Improvement of the combined sewer system

- ' Strive to reduce pollution load discharged to rivers or the sea through the continuous improvement of the combined sewer system, such as the development of facilities to capture initial stormwater mixed with sanitary sewage... and the introduction of a separate sewer system for some public facilities and development districts.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure and system changes to reduce discharges.
mechanism_description: Upgrading combined sewer infrastructure (e.g., first-flush capture and sewer separation) reduces combined sewer overflows and pollutant loads discharged to receiving waters, improving water quality.

section_header: ## (2) Measures for preservation of water quality of public water bodies
policy_statement: TMG will introduce advanced and semi-advanced treatment facilities at water reclamation centers to improve treated sewage quality.

verbatim_text:
## Improvement of quality of treated water

- ' Introduce advanced and semi-advanced treatment facilities at water reclamation centers to further improve the quality of treated sewage.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure upgrade commitment.
mechanism_description: Upgrading wastewater treatment at reclamation facilities improves the quality of treated effluent, reducing water pollution and strengthening sanitation and water-system resilience.

section_header: ## (2) Measures for preservation of water quality of public water bodies
policy_statement: TMG will systematically dredge sludge from tidal rivers and canals (including the Sumida River) to improve water quality and prevent bad odors.
parent_statement: TMG will ensure stable supply of high-quality tap water by reducing drought and raw water quality risks, improving sewer systems and treatment facilities, and continuing monitoring for water quality improvements.
verbatim_text:
## Dredging sludge from rivers and canals systematically

- ' Systematically dredge sludge from tidal rivers and canals, including Sumida River, to improve water quality and prevent bad odors.
extraction_rationale: Concrete maintenance activity with specified water bodies.
mechanism_description: A systematic dredging program removes accumulated sludge from rivers and canals, reducing stagnant/anaerobic conditions and thereby improving water quality and preventing foul odors.

section_header: ## (2) Measures for preservation of water quality of public water bodies
policy_statement: TMG will continue monitoring water quality and researching aquatic organisms to verify initiative effects and inform future improvements.
parent_statement: TMG will ensure stable supply of high-quality tap water by reducing drought and raw water quality risks, improving sewer systems and treatment facilities, and continuing monitoring for water quality improvements.
verbatim_text:
## Monitoring of water quality as well as research and study of aquatic organisms

- ' Continue monitoring of water quality as well as research and study of aquatic organisms to verify the effects of initiatives and identify the improvements in the waterfront environment for better initiatives in the future.
extraction_rationale: Ongoing monitoring and research commitment with a clear purpose.
mechanism_description: Monitoring water quality and aquatic ecosystems generates data that helps evaluate current measures and improve future waterfront/environmental adaptation planning.

policy_statement: ES.1: Supply 100% renewable electricity to residents and businesses.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
## ENERGY SUPPLY (ES)
- ES 1 Supply 100% renewable electricity to residents and businesses.

ES.1
## Supporting Actions
- ES.1-1 Provide 100% renewable electricity at affordable rates.
- ES.1-2 Promote early adoption of 100% renewable electricity products to all San Franciscans, with a preference for City programs.
- ES.1-3 Ensure 100% renewable electricity is the only option for San Francisco residents and businesses by 2025, by supporting state or local regulatory requirements and/or acquiring PG&E's grid assets serving San Francisco.

extraction_rationale: Procurement guidance action with deadline.
mechanism_description: Procurement guidance steers departments toward lower-GWP refrigerants, reducing refrigerant leaks and associated high-impact fugitive greenhouse gas emissions over time.

policy_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- TLU 1 Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.

TLU.1
Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
TLU.1-1 Fund and implement the recommendations of the ConnectSF Transit Corridors Study and Muni Forward Plan...
TLU.1-2 ... fund State of Good Repair with at least $300 million annually.
TLU.1-6 By 2025, implement 50 miles of Muni Forward transit priority improvements...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple detailed supporting actions and quantified funding/mileage commitments.
mechanism_description: Improve transit speed, reliability, and priority infrastructure to increase transit ridership and shift trips from private cars to lower-emission public transport, reducing transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Fund and implement recommendations of the ConnectSF Transit Corridors Study and Muni Forward Plan, including frequent corridors (5-minute service), transit priority measures, signal retiming, stop spacing optimization, and major transit capital projects (TLU.1-1).
parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text:
TLU.1-1 Fund and implement the recommendations of the ConnectSF Transit Corridors Study and Muni Forward Plan, including taking steps to:
- a) Identify and implement key transit corridors for service every 5 minutes or better all day long.

b) Ensure transit on frequent corridors is not delayed by recurring congestion by investing in transit-only lanes, signal management, queue-jump lanes and other transit priority treatments.

- c) Retime traffic lights to minimize signal delay for frequent lines.
- d) Optimize stop spacing on frequent lines to maximize transit ridership.
- e) Advance major transit capital projects, including a new Westside Subway along 19th Avenue and Geary, the Caltrain Downtown Extension, Central Subway extension, and the Link21 new transbay tube.
extraction_rationale: Concrete package of transit service and capital project actions.
mechanism_description: Improve and expand public transit speed, reliability, and capacity to increase ridership and shift travel away from private cars, reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: Improve transit reliability by bringing infrastructure into a state of good repair and fund State of Good Repair with at least $300 million annually (TLU.1-2).
parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text: TLU.1-2 Improve transit reliability by bringing infrastructure into a state of good repair. Adequately fund State of Good Repair with at least $300 million annually.
extraction_rationale: Specific funding commitment with quantified annual amount.
mechanism_description: Investing in transit state-of-good-repair upgrades improves reliability, which can shift trips from private vehicles to transit and reduce transport-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Greatly improve rider comfort, safety, and experience on transit across demographics and ability, including data collection/reporting, sensitivity training of fare inspectors, and expanding the Muni Transit Assistance Program (TLU.1-3).
parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text: TLU.1-3 Greatly improve rider comfort, safety, and experience on transit across age, gender, race, and ability to encourage more people to ride transit. Example activities include data collection, reporting, sensitivity training of fare inspectors, and expanding the Muni Transit Assistance Program.
extraction_rationale: Specified intervention set to improve transit experience and equity.
mechanism_description: Improve transit comfort, safety, and accessibility to increase transit ridership and shift trips from private vehicles to transit, reducing transport-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Implement Phase One of SFMTA's Racial Equity Action Plan and develop Phase Two in 2021 and implement Phase Two in 2022 (TLU.1-4).
