parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text: - TLU.1-4 Implement Phase One of SFMTA's Racial Equity Action Plan to improve working conditions and initiate the development of Phase Two in 2021 and then implement Phase Two in 2022 to improve safety, access, and opportunities for the public.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound implementation steps for a named equity plan.
mechanism_description: Implementing a racial equity action plan strengthens institutional processes and equitable service delivery, indirectly supporting the ability to design and implement climate-related actions.

policy_statement: By 2025, implement 50 miles of Muni Forward transit priority improvements including 30 miles of new transit-only lanes (TLU.1-6).
parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text: - TLU.1-6 By 2025, implement 50 miles of Muni Forward transit priority improvements, including 30 miles of new transit-only lanes. to increase reliability, frequency and safety for riders.
extraction_rationale: Quantified infrastructure delivery commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Implementing transit-priority improvements (e.g., dedicated transit lanes) increases transit performance and ridership, shifting travel away from cars and lowering transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: By 2022, study the role of Muni fare programs on equity, climate, and mobility goals and adopt recommendations (TLU.1-7).
parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text: - TLU.1-7 By 2022, study the role of Muni fare programs on equity, climate, and mobility goals and adopt recommendations.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound study with commitment to adopt recommendations.
mechanism_description: Adjusting public transit fare programs to improve affordability increases transit ridership and shifts trips from private vehicles to transit, reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: TLU.2: Create a complete and connected active transportation network that shifts trips from automobiles to walking, biking, and other active transportation modes.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- TLU 2 Create a complete and connected active transportation network that shifts trips from automobiles to walking, biking, and other active transportation modes.

TLU.2
Create a complete and connected active transportation network that shifts trips from automobiles to walking, biking, and other active transportation modes.
TLU.2-4 Expand the protected bikeway network by at least 20 miles by 2025.
TLU.2-6 Update San Francisco's Bike Plan by 2023...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with supporting actions and explicit mileage and deadlines.
mechanism_description: Expanding connected walking and bicycling infrastructure shifts trips from cars to active modes, reducing vehicle travel and transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Expand active-mobility corridors and connect Slow Streets, car-free park roads, and protected bikeways to neighborhoods (TLU.2-1).
parent_statement: TLU.2: Create a complete and connected active transportation network that shifts trips from automobiles to walking, biking, and other active transportation modes.
verbatim_text: TLU.2-1 Continue to expand programs that provide corridors that are attractive to all demographics for walking, biking, and using scooters, wheelchairs, and other small mobility devices. Connect the Slow Streets network, car-free roads in parks, and the protected bikeway network to neighborhoods in San Francisco.
extraction_rationale: Specific program/infrastructure connectivity action.
mechanism_description: Build and connect active-mobility networks to induce a shift from car trips to walking/biking/micromobility, thereby lowering transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Expand community programs to make biking more accessible, including subsidies for electric bikes for low-income residents (TLU.2-2).
parent_statement: TLU.2: Create a complete and connected active transportation network that shifts trips from automobiles to walking, biking, and other active transportation modes.
verbatim_text: TLU.2-2 Expand community programs and partnerships to make biking more accessible, via safety and maintenance classes, community parking, and subsidies for electric bikes for low-income residents.
extraction_rationale: Specific equity-linked subsidy intervention.
mechanism_description: Improve access to biking (including e-bikes) so more trips shift from cars to bicycles, lowering transport fuel use and emissions.

policy_statement: By 2022, establish a modal planning framework prioritizing transit and active modes to guide right-of-way design and use decisions (TLU.2-3).
parent_statement: TLU.2: Create a complete and connected active transportation network that shifts trips from automobiles to walking, biking, and other active transportation modes.
verbatim_text: TLU.2-3 By 2022, establish a modal planning framework, placing transit and active modes at the forefront, that will guide decisions about design and utilization of the City's rights-of-way.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound planning framework commitment.
mechanism_description: Adopting a right-of-way planning framework that prioritizes transit and active travel supports shifting trips away from private cars, lowering transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Expand the protected bikeway network by at least 20 miles by 2025 (TLU.2-4).
parent_statement: TLU.2: Create a complete and connected active transportation network that shifts trips from automobiles to walking, biking, and other active transportation modes.
verbatim_text: TLU.2-4 Expand the protected bikeway network by at least 20 miles by 2025.
extraction_rationale: Quantified infrastructure expansion with deadline.
mechanism_description: Building more protected bike lanes increases cycling and shifts trips away from driving, reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Update San Francisco's Bike Plan by 2023 with robust community input (TLU.2-6).
parent_statement: TLU.2: Create a complete and connected active transportation network that shifts trips from automobiles to walking, biking, and other active transportation modes.
verbatim_text: TLU.2-6 Update San Francisco's Bike Plan by 2023 to improve and expand the active transportation network with robust community input.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound plan update commitment.
mechanism_description: Updating and expanding the biking/active transportation network increases cycling uptake and shifts trips away from cars, reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: TLU.3: Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflect carbon cost/efficiency and correct inequities of past investments.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- TLU 3 Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflects the carbon cost and efficiency of different modes and projects, and correct for inequities of past investments and priorities.

TLU.3
Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflect the carbon cost and efficiency of different modes and projects and correct for inequities of past investments and priorities.
TLU.3-1 By 2022, develop recommendations...
TLU.3-4 Develop and take all necessary steps to implement an integrated system of tolling...
TLU.3-5 Implement the Treasure Island Mobility Management Program...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple time-bound pricing actions (congestion pricing, fees, tolling).
mechanism_description: Implementing carbon-reflective mobility pricing and financing shifts travel and investment toward more efficient, lower-emitting modes, reducing transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: By 2022, develop recommendations for equitable pricing programs (discounts/exemptions), reduce vehicle traffic, and increase transit service downtown; by 2026, study and implement appropriate pricing policies (TLU.3-1).
parent_statement: TLU.3: Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflect carbon cost/efficiency and correct inequities of past investments.
verbatim_text: TLU.3-1 By 2022, develop recommendations for programs and policies that will advance equity (e.g., provide discounts and exemptions for low-income individuals), reduce vehicle traffic, and increase transit service to downtown. For example, complete the Downtown San Francisco Congestion Pricing Study recommendations, and by 2026, study and implement the appropriate pricing policies.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound congestion pricing pathway with explicit equity features and implementation timeline.
mechanism_description: Implementing congestion (downtown) pricing with equity provisions discourages private vehicle trips and shifts travel behavior, reducing vehicle traffic and associated transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: By 2022, identify local pricing mechanisms (e.g., vehicle license fee) and establish priorities to advocate for regional/state/federal legislation (e.g., increased gas tax, road user charges) to increase fees to drive (TLU.3-2).
parent_statement: TLU.3: Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflect carbon cost/efficiency and correct inequities of past investments.
verbatim_text:
TLU.3-2 Advance local, regional, state, and federal opportunities to transition away from fossil fuels by increasing fees to drive.

- a)  By 2022, identify and consider pricing mechanisms that can be implemented locally (e.g. vehicle license fee).
- b)  By 2022, establish priorities to advocate for regional, state and federal legislation (e.g. increase gas tax, application of road user charges).
extraction_rationale: Concrete fee/tax policy and advocacy actions with deadlines.
mechanism_description: Increase fees and taxes associated with driving to reduce vehicle travel and fossil fuel consumption, leading to lower transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: By 2023, introduce new tools to manage short-term curb uses such as flexible regulations and pricing (TLU.3-3).
parent_statement: TLU.3: Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflect carbon cost/efficiency and correct inequities of past investments.
verbatim_text: TLU.3-3 By 2023, introduce new tools to manage shortterm curb uses, such as flexible regulations and pricing.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound regulatory/pricing management action.
mechanism_description: Implementing curb management tools (e.g., flexible regulations and pricing) reduces inefficient vehicle activity like circling and idling, cutting fuel consumption and transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Implement an integrated tolling system for bridges/freeways and on Treasure Island to prioritize transit and higher-occupancy vehicles (TLU.3-4).
parent_statement: TLU.3: Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflect carbon cost/efficiency and correct inequities of past investments.
verbatim_text: TLU.3-4 Develop and take all necessary steps to implement an integrated system of tolling for bridges and freeways and on Treasure Island to prioritize transit and higher occupancy vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Specific pricing mechanism (integrated tolling) with stated prioritization objective.
mechanism_description: Road tolling with priority for transit and higher-occupancy vehicles shifts travel away from single-occupancy driving toward more efficient modes, lowering transport fuel use and emissions.

policy_statement: Implement the Treasure Island Mobility Management Program including new ferry service, East Bay bus service, and island tolling (TLU.3-5).
parent_statement: TLU.3: Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflect carbon cost/efficiency and correct inequities of past investments.
verbatim_text: TLU.3-5 Implement the Treasure Island Mobility Management Program including new ferry service, East Bay bus service, and island tolling.
extraction_rationale: Named program implementation with specified components.
mechanism_description: Increase transit/ferry service and apply road pricing to shift trips away from private vehicles, reducing transportation fuel use and emissions.

policy_statement: Ensure pricing program revenues are invested in transportation improvements, applying tools to reduce impacts on low-income and historically marginalized communities (TLU.3-6).
parent_statement: TLU.3: Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflect carbon cost/efficiency and correct inequities of past investments.
verbatim_text: TLU.3-6 Apply policy tools to reduce impacts on low-income and historically marginalized communities and ensure that money generated from pricing programs is invested in transportation improvements, especially for those communities.
extraction_rationale: Explicit equity and reinvestment requirement.
mechanism_description: Use transportation pricing programs and reinvest the revenues into transportation improvements (with equity mitigations) to reduce vehicle travel demand and associated transportation emissions.

policy_statement: TLU.4: Manage parking resources more efficiently.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- TLU 4 Manage parking resources more efficiently.

TLU.4
## Supporting Actions
TLU.4-2 Expand paid parking citywide...
TLU.4-3 Steadily reduce the City's overall parking supply...
TLU.4-6 Implement a program to prioritize access and parking for people-with-disability parking placards.
extraction_rationale: Parking management strategy with multiple concrete supporting actions.
mechanism_description: Manage and price parking while reducing parking supply to discourage car use, shifting trips to lower-carbon modes and reducing vehicle miles traveled and transport emissions.

policy_statement: Prioritize enforcement of parking and curb regulations that impact street safety and efficiency (TLU.4-1).
parent_statement: TLU.4: Manage parking resources more efficiently.
verbatim_text: TLU.4-1 Prioritize enforcement of parking and curb regulations that impact street safety and efficiency
extraction_rationale: Explicit enforcement prioritization action.
mechanism_description: Stronger enforcement of parking and curb rules improves traffic flow and reduces idling/congestion, lowering transportation-related emissions.

policy_statement: Expand paid parking citywide where appropriate, set prices to reduce demand, reinvent/expand Residential Parking Permit program, expand paid parking to Sundays/evenings where appropriate, and expand demand-responsive meter/garage pricing (TLU.4-2).
parent_statement: TLU.4: Manage parking resources more efficiently.
verbatim_text:
TLU.4-2 Expand paid parking citywide, where appropriate Set prices at a level that reduces demand for parking so that drivers can always find a parking space near their destination.

- a)  Reinvent and expand the Residential Parking Permit program.
- b)  Expand paid hourly parking to Sundays and evenings, where appropriate.
- c)  Expand demand-responsive parking meter and garage pricing.
extraction_rationale: Detailed pricing program changes under parking strategy.
mechanism_description: Expanding and dynamically pricing paid parking reduces parking demand and discourages car trips, leading to lower driving activity and reduced transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Steadily reduce the City's overall parking supply and convert underutilized parking lots/spaces/garages to productive uses such as housing and car-free park roads (TLU.4-3).
parent_statement: TLU.4: Manage parking resources more efficiently.
verbatim_text: TLU.4-3 Steadily reduce the City's overall parking supply in keeping with traffic reduction and emissions reduction goals, and convert underutilized public and private parking lots, parking spaces, and garages to more productive uses, such as housing and car-free roads in parks.
extraction_rationale: Clear land use/parking supply policy action.
mechanism_description: Reducing parking supply and converting parking areas to housing and car-free spaces reduces driving demand and enables a shift away from car travel, cutting transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Reinvent and expand the private parking tax to reduce congestion, air pollution, and emissions (TLU.4-4).
parent_statement: TLU.4: Manage parking resources more efficiently.
verbatim_text: TLU.4-4 Reinvent and expand the parking tax on private parking to reduce congestion, air pollution and emissions.
extraction_rationale: Tax policy lever explicitly stated.
mechanism_description: Increasing taxes on private parking raises the cost of car use, reducing driving and encouraging alternative modes, which lowers congestion and transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Develop programs to reduce impacts of parking pricing on low-income, auto-dependent people and ensure net benefit to low-income individuals (TLU.4-5).
parent_statement: TLU.4: Manage parking resources more efficiently.
verbatim_text: TLU.4-5 While using pricing to balance parking supply and demand, develop programs to reduce impact on low-income, auto-dependent people and ensure net benefit to low-income individuals.
extraction_rationale: Equity safeguard linked to pricing policies.
mechanism_description: Implement parking pricing (with mitigating programs for low-income users) to manage parking demand and reduce vehicle travel, leading to lower transport emissions.

policy_statement: Implement a program to prioritize access and parking for people with disability parking placards (TLU.4-6).
parent_statement: TLU.4: Manage parking resources more efficiently.
verbatim_text: TLU.4-6 Implement a program to prioritize access and parking for people-with-disability parking placards.
extraction_rationale: Specific accessibility-focused program commitment.
mechanism_description: Prioritizing parking and access for people with disability placards improves mobility accessibility but does not directly describe a greenhouse-gas mitigation or adaptation impact pathway.

policy_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- TLU 6 Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.

TLU.6
Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
TLU.6-1 ...Create a working group...
TLU.6-2 Examine rezoning...
TLU.6-6 Update the Transportation Element of the City's General Plan.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple governance/zoning/planning supporting actions.
mechanism_description: Increase density and mix land uses to improve location efficiency, reducing driving demand and related greenhouse gas emissions through more compact, transit-supportive development.

policy_statement: Create a working group of City agencies and residents to plan and design neighborhoods where people can walk/roll to daily needs (TLU.6-1).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-1 Facilitate the development of neighborhoods where people live within an easy walk or roll of their daily needs. Create a working group of City agencies and residents to plan and design for such neighborhoods.
extraction_rationale: Specific governance/planning intervention.
mechanism_description: Planning and designing walkable, mixed-use neighborhoods reduces reliance on car trips by shortening distances to daily needs, lowering vehicle miles traveled and associated transport emissions.

policy_statement: Examine rezoning to allow multi-family housing throughout San Francisco (TLU.6-2).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-2 Examine rezoning to allow for multi-family housing throughout San Francisco.
extraction_rationale: Explicit zoning policy exploration/commitment.
mechanism_description: Change zoning to enable denser multi-family housing, which supports compact development and reduces car-dependent travel and sprawl-related emissions.

policy_statement: By 2023, increase the types of home-based businesses allowed in residential districts (TLU.6-3).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-3 By 2023, increase the types of home-based businesses allowed in residential districts.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound land use regulatory change.
mechanism_description: Expanding permitted home-based businesses enables more people to work locally or from home, reducing commuting travel and associated transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Identify and reimagine under-utilized publicly owned land and roadways for transformation/repurposing (TLU.6-4).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-4 Identify and reimagine under-utilized publicly owned land and roadways that could be transformed or repurposed.
extraction_rationale: Specific action to repurpose public assets.
mechanism_description: Repurposing underused public land and roadway space supports more efficient land use and reallocates space toward lower-carbon travel and development patterns, reducing transport-related emissions and land-use impacts.

policy_statement: Co-develop plans/projects with BIPOC community members to design public space and the transportation system to advance racial and social equity (TLU.6-5).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-5 Design public space and the transportation system (including roadways) to advance racial and social equity by co-developing plans and projects with BIPOC community members and understanding their needs before designing the space.
extraction_rationale: Explicit participatory design requirement tied to equity outcomes.
mechanism_description: Co-developing transportation and public-space plans with BIPOC community members improves inclusive governance and equity-informed decision-making, which can shape downstream projects but is not itself a direct emissions-reduction measure.

policy_statement: Update the Transportation Element of the City's General Plan (TLU.6-6).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-6 Update the Transportation Element of the City's General Plan.
extraction_rationale: Formal planning document update action.
mechanism_description: Updating the transportation element of the general plan strengthens planning and policy frameworks that enable future transportation measures that reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Co-develop plans/projects with the disability community to design public space and the transportation system to advance disability justice (TLU.6-7).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-7 Design public space and the transportation system to advance disability justice by codeveloping plans and projects with diverse elements of the disability community and understanding their needs before designs are complete.
extraction_rationale: Explicit accessibility/disability justice co-development requirement.
mechanism_description: Co-developing accessible transportation and public space designs can reduce barriers to using transit/walking/rolling, enabling a shift away from private car travel and thereby lowering transport emissions.

policy_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- TLU 7 Where motor vehicle use or travel is necessary, accelerate the adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEV's) and other electric mobility options.

Where motor vehicle use or travel is necessary, accelerate the adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
TLU.7-2 Expand publicly available EV charging...
TLU.7-6 By 2030, create incentives... to reduce emissions from construction equipment at least 50% from 2020 levels.
extraction_rationale: Named ZEV strategy with multiple supporting actions including charging targets and construction equipment incentives.
mechanism_description: Speeding adoption of zero-emissions (electric) vehicles replaces internal combustion vehicle miles, reducing tailpipe fuel use and associated transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: By 2023, launch a community-tailored public awareness campaign on benefits of transit, active transportation, and electric vehicles (TLU.7-1).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: TLU.7-1 By 2023, launch a public awareness campaign, including messaging tailored to specific communities, with the goal of educating residents about the health, economic, and environmental benefits of transit, active transportation, and electric vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound outreach/education intervention.
mechanism_description: A targeted public awareness campaign encourages residents to choose lower-emission travel options (transit and active transportation, and potentially EVs), reducing transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Expand publicly available EV charging that is financially and geographically accessible to low-income households and renters, including: 2022 evaluation framework for curbside pilots; by 2023 charging in 10% of municipal lot spaces and 10% of private large commercial garage spaces and creation of three fast-charging hubs (one in a disadvantaged community); by 2025 charging in 10% of SFO-owned parking stalls (TLU.7-2).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text:
TLU.7-2 Expand publicly available EV charging across the city that is financially and geographically accessible to low-income households and renters.

a) By 2022, complete an evaluation framework to develop curbside charging pilots b) By 2023, expand charging to 10% of spaces in municipally owned parking lots

c) By 2023, expand charging to 10% of spaces within privately owned large commercial garages d) By 2023, create three 'fast-charging hubs' with one serving a disadvantaged community within San Francisco.

e) By 2025, install charging to 10% of SFOowned parking stalls supported by load management software.
extraction_rationale: Quantified charging deployment targets with multiple deadlines and equity condition.
mechanism_description: Installing and expanding publicly accessible EV charging increases charging availability and convenience, enabling higher electric vehicle adoption and use and thereby supporting reductions in transportation-related emissions.

policy_statement: By 2024, develop a plan to help the City's non-revenue fleet and small/local businesses build infrastructure for zero-emission delivery, drayage, and longer-haul trucks (TLU.7-3).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: TLU.7-3 By 2024, develop a plan to help the City's nonrevenue fleet and small and locally owned businesses build infrastructure that allows for zero emission delivery, drayage, and longer haul trucks.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound infrastructure planning for fleets and small businesses.
mechanism_description: Building zero-emission truck enabling infrastructure (e.g., charging/fueling) supports adoption and use of zero-emission freight vehicles, which enables subsequent reductions in transportation emissions.

policy_statement: By 2023, establish a pathway to incentivize ZEVs for passenger service vehicles operating at the airport (TLU.7-4).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: TLU.7-4 By 2023, establish a pathway to incentivize ZEVs for passenger service vehicles operating at the airport.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound incentive pathway for airport ground transport.
mechanism_description: Incentives accelerate deployment of zero-emission passenger service vehicles, reducing combustion fuel use and associated transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: By 2024, launch a pilot to advance ZEVs, e-bikes, and other low-carbon modes for door-to-door goods and meal delivery services (TLU.7-5).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: - TLU.7-5 By 2024, launch a pilot to advance the use of ZEVs, e-bikes, and other low-carbon modes for door-to-door goods and meal delivery services.
extraction_rationale: Pilot program commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: A pilot program promotes ZEVs, e-bikes, and other low-carbon delivery modes, replacing higher-emitting delivery vehicles and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, create incentives for renewable diesel and emerging zero-emission technologies to cut emissions from construction equipment at least 50% from 2020 levels (TLU.7-6).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: - TLU.7-6 By 2030, create incentives for the use of renewable diesel and emerging zero-emission technologies to reduce emissions from construction equipment at least 50% from 2020 levels.
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions reduction target for construction equipment with deadline and policy tool (incentives).
mechanism_description: Provide incentives to shift construction equipment to renewable diesel and zero-emission technologies, lowering off-road equipment fuel combustion emissions.

policy_statement: Design by 2023 and launch by 2024 a pilot to test accessible bicycles, e-bikes, and e-scooters for commuting and recreation (TLU.7-7).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: - TLU.7-7 Design by 2023 and launch by 2024 a pilot project to test the use of accessible bicycles, e-bicycles and e-scooters for commuting, as well as recreation.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound accessibility-focused mobility pilot.
mechanism_description: A micromobility pilot increases access to bikes/e-bikes/e-scooters, encouraging mode shift from car trips to lower-carbon travel and thereby reducing transportation emissions.

policy_statement: H.1: Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
## HOUSING (H)
- H 1 Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.

H.1
## Supporting Actions
H.1-1 Leverage every housing action and investment...
H.1-2 Prioritize affordable housing in cultural districts...
H.1-3 Expand tenant services...
H.1-4 Initiate steps to increase housing production... in higher opportunity neighborhoods...
extraction_rationale: Named housing equity strategy with multiple supporting actions.
mechanism_description: Housing and tenant stabilization/affordable housing programs reduce displacement and improve community stability, strengthening overall social resilience and equity in the face of climate impacts.

policy_statement: Leverage every housing action and investment to reverse historic dispossession and enable housing security for affected communities (H.1-1).
parent_statement: H.1: Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.
verbatim_text: H.1-1 Leverage every housing action and investment to help reverse historic dispossession based on race, ethnicity, disability, or socio-economic status, and enable housing security for affected communities.
extraction_rationale: Explicit directive for housing investments/actions tied to equity outcomes.
mechanism_description: Direct housing actions and investments toward historically dispossessed communities to improve housing security, strengthening overall community resilience and adaptive capacity.

policy_statement: Prioritize affordable housing in cultural districts and areas with historically marginalized racial/ethnic communities to encourage stabilization and return (H.1-2).
parent_statement: H.1: Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.
verbatim_text: - H.1-2 Prioritize affordable housing in cultural districts and areas with historically marginalized racial or ethnic communities to encourage their stabilization and return.
extraction_rationale: Targeting policy for affordable housing siting/prioritization.
mechanism_description: Prioritizing affordable housing in historically marginalized and cultural areas helps stabilize communities and support residents’ ability to remain in or return to their neighborhoods, improving equitable resilience outcomes.

policy_statement: Initiate steps to increase housing production—particularly affordable and accessible housing—in higher-opportunity neighborhoods historically racially/economically exclusive (H.1-4).
parent_statement: H.1: Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.
verbatim_text: - H.1-4 Initiate steps to increase housing production, particularly affordable and accessible housing, in higher opportunity neighborhoods that historically have been racially and economically exclusive.
extraction_rationale: Explicit housing production commitment with equity siting focus.
mechanism_description: Increase affordable housing production in high-opportunity areas to promote infill growth and reduce sprawl-driven travel, thereby lowering transportation-related emissions.

policy_statement: H.2: Support vulnerable populations and underserved communities through preservation/rehabilitation of existing housing and new housing development serving their needs.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- H 2 Support vulnerable populations and underserved communities through both the preservation and rehabilitation of existing housing and new housing development that serves their needs.

H.2
Support vulnerable populations and underserved communities...
## Supporting Actions
- H.2-1 Provide funding and resources...
- H.2-3 ... acquisition and preservation... goal of at least 400 units annually.
- H.2-4 Secure ... resources for accessibility, energy efficiency, decarbonization, and resilience upgrades...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple supporting actions including an annual unit goal.
mechanism_description: Preserving and rehabilitating housing with energy-efficiency and decarbonization upgrades reduces residential building energy use and associated greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Provide funding and resources to help unhoused or unstably housed people become and stay safely housed (H.2-1).
parent_statement: H.2: Support vulnerable populations and underserved communities through preservation/rehabilitation of existing housing and new housing development serving their needs.
verbatim_text: - H.2-1 Provide funding and resources to help people who are unhoused or without stable housing become and stay safely housed.
extraction_rationale: Direct resource allocation commitment for housing stability.
mechanism_description: Providing funding and services that move unhoused people into stable housing reduces their exposure and vulnerability to climate hazards such as extreme heat, cold, and poor air quality.

policy_statement: Subsidize and incentivize housing for vulnerable populations in high-resource areas, especially along transit-rich commercial and social-service corridors (H.2-2).
parent_statement: H.2: Support vulnerable populations and underserved communities through preservation/rehabilitation of existing housing and new housing development serving their needs.
verbatim_text: - H.2-2 Subsidize and develop incentives for building housing targeted towards vulnerable populations in high resource areas, especially along transit-rich, commercial, and social service corridors.
extraction_rationale: Explicit subsidy/incentive commitment with siting priorities.
mechanism_description: Subsidizing housing development in transit-rich corridors concentrates residents near transit and services, reducing car dependence and vehicle travel, which lowers transportation-related emissions and supports more efficient land use.

policy_statement: Initiate steps to fund acquisition and preservation of existing affordable multifamily housing with a goal of at least 400 units annually (H.2-3).
parent_statement: H.2: Support vulnerable populations and underserved communities through preservation/rehabilitation of existing housing and new housing development serving their needs.
verbatim_text: - H.2-3 Initiate steps to fund the acquisition and preservation of existing, affordable, multifamily housing, with a goal of at least 400 units annually.
extraction_rationale: Quantified annual preservation goal and funding action.
mechanism_description: Preserving existing affordable multifamily housing helps maintain compact, transit-supportive development patterns and avoid displacement-related sprawl, reducing vehicle travel and associated emissions.

policy_statement: Secure federal, state, and local resources for accessibility, energy efficiency, decarbonization, and resilience upgrades in existing and new housing (H.2-4).
parent_statement: H.2: Support vulnerable populations and underserved communities through preservation/rehabilitation of existing housing and new housing development serving their needs.
verbatim_text: - H.2-4 Secure federal, state, and local resources for accessibility, energy efficiency, decarbonization, and resilience upgrades in existing and new housing.
extraction_rationale: Resource mobilization commitment for specified upgrade types.
mechanism_description: Securing public resources enables energy-efficiency and decarbonization upgrades in housing, which lowers building energy use and related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: H.3: Advance zoning and implementation improvements that support new housing production sufficient to meet goals, especially sustainable small/mid-sized/family/workforce housing in lower-density neighborhoods.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- H 3 Advance zoning and implementation improvements that support new housing production sufficient to meet goals, especially sustainable, small, mid-sized, family, and workforce housing in lower density neighborhoods.

H.3
## Supporting Actions
- H.3-1 Study changes...
- H.3-4 By 2025 establish codes and regulations...
- H.3-5 Expand green construction training...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple supporting actions including a 2025 code/regulation deadline.
mechanism_description: Reforming zoning and related regulations enables more (sustainable) housing development patterns that reduce sprawl and car dependence, lowering per-capita emissions.

policy_statement: Study changes to increase multi-family housing in higher-resource neighborhoods and near transit/jobs/services/parks/schools/amenities (H.3-1).
parent_statement: H.3: Advance zoning and implementation improvements that support new housing production sufficient to meet goals, especially sustainable small/mid-sized/family/workforce housing in lower-density neighborhoods.
verbatim_text: - H.3-1 Study changes to increase multi-family housing in higher-resource neighborhoods and near transit, jobs, services, parks, high quality schools, and other amenities.
extraction_rationale: Concrete study commitment with defined locational focus.
mechanism_description: Enable more multi-family housing near transit and key destinations to reduce car dependence and vehicle travel, cutting transport emissions and shifting land use toward compact development.

policy_statement: Develop additional approval and permit streamlining for new housing that exceeds inclusionary and sustainability requirements (H.3-2).
parent_statement: H.3: Advance zoning and implementation improvements that support new housing production sufficient to meet goals, especially sustainable small/mid-sized/family/workforce housing in lower-density neighborhoods.
verbatim_text: - H.3-2 Develop additional approval and permit streamlining for new housing that exceeds inclusionary and sustainability requirements.
extraction_rationale: Specified regulatory streamlining action.
mechanism_description: Streamlining permits for housing that exceeds sustainability standards accelerates delivery of higher-performance buildings, reducing operational energy use and associated emissions compared with typical new construction.

policy_statement: Address barriers for lower-income small property owners to add housing (e.g., ADUs) and rehabilitate healthy, resource-efficient existing units (H.3-3).
parent_statement: H.3: Advance zoning and implementation improvements that support new housing production sufficient to meet goals, especially sustainable small/mid-sized/family/workforce housing in lower-density neighborhoods.
verbatim_text: - H.3-3 Address financial and educational barriers for lower income small property owners to add housing (such as Accessory Dwelling Units) and rehabilitate existing units that are healthy and resource efficient.
extraction_rationale: Specific interventions targeting ADUs and rehab capacity.
mechanism_description: Reducing financial and knowledge barriers enables residential unit rehabilitation/efficiency retrofits, which lowers ongoing building energy use and associated emissions.

policy_statement: By 2025, establish codes and regulations to enable new materials (e.g., cross-laminated timber) and technologies (e.g., modular housing) to lower costs and increase construction resource efficiency (H.3-4).
parent_statement: H.3: Advance zoning and implementation improvements that support new housing production sufficient to meet goals, especially sustainable small/mid-sized/family/workforce housing in lower-density neighborhoods.
verbatim_text: - H.3-4 By 2025 establish codes and regulations that facilitate use of new materials (e.g. crosslaminated-timber) and new technology (e.g. modular housing) to lower costs and increase resource efficiency of construction.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound code/regulatory change with specific examples.
mechanism_description: Updating codes and regulations to permit innovative materials and construction technologies increases adoption of resource-efficient, lower-embodied-carbon building practices, reducing emissions from construction.

policy_statement: Expand green construction training and apprenticeship programs to grow skilled labor and reduce construction costs (H.3-5).
parent_statement: H.3: Advance zoning and implementation improvements that support new housing production sufficient to meet goals, especially sustainable small/mid-sized/family/workforce housing in lower-density neighborhoods.
verbatim_text: - H.3-5 Expand green construction training and apprenticeship programs to grow the local pool of skilled labor and reduce construction costs.
extraction_rationale: Workforce program expansion linked to housing delivery.
mechanism_description: Expanding green construction training increases the skilled workforce and reduces project costs, enabling more low-carbon/efficient building construction and retrofits that lower associated emissions.

policy_statement: H.4: Expand subsidized housing production and availability for low-, moderate-, and middle-income households.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- H 4 Expand subsidized housing production and availability for low-, moderate-, and middle-income households.

H.4
## Supporting Actions
- H.4-1 Meet RHNA targets...
- H.4-2 By 2025 renew and increase funding...
- H.4-3 Advocate for increased ... funding...
extraction_rationale: Named subsidized housing strategy with multiple supporting actions including 2025 funding action.
mechanism_description: Expanding subsidized housing increases housing availability (often supporting more compact/infill living), which can reduce average commute distances and vehicle miles traveled, lowering transportation-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Meet RHNA targets and affirmatively further fair housing by increasing affordable housing production (especially for families) in higher-resource neighborhoods and historically lower-income communities of color (H.4-1).
parent_statement: H.4: Expand subsidized housing production and availability for low-, moderate-, and middle-income households.
verbatim_text: - H.4-1 Meet Regional Housing Needs Allocation (RHNA) targets and requirements to affirmatively further fair housing by increasing production of affordable housing, especially for families with children, in both higher resource neighborhoods and Priority Geographies that have historically been home to lower income communities of color.
extraction_rationale: Explicit compliance target (RHNA) and fair housing action.
mechanism_description: Increasing affordable housing production in higher-resource and priority geographies supports more infill/accessible living patterns, which can reduce car travel demand and associated greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Advocate for increased regional, state, and federal funding for affordable and green housing (H.4-3).
parent_statement: H.4: Expand subsidized housing production and availability for low-, moderate-, and middle-income households.
verbatim_text: - H.4-3 Advocate for increased regional, state, and federal funding for affordable and green housing.
extraction_rationale: Explicit advocacy action linked to funding.
mechanism_description: Advocating for increased public funding enables more affordable green housing development or upgrades, which lowers building energy use and related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Identify cost-cutting measures to make San Francisco affordable housing developments more competitive for external funding (H.4-4).
parent_statement: H.4: Expand subsidized housing production and availability for low-, moderate-, and middle-income households.
verbatim_text: - H.4-4 Identify cost cutting measures to make affordable housing developments in San Francisco more competitive for regional, state, and federal funding.
extraction_rationale: Specific action to improve competitiveness for funding.
mechanism_description: Plan and identify ways to reduce affordable housing development costs to improve eligibility and competitiveness for external funding awards.

policy_statement: Prioritize surplus City/enterprise agency/public land for affordable housing based on timing and feasibility (H.4-5).
parent_statement: H.4: Expand subsidized housing production and availability for low-, moderate-, and middle-income households.
verbatim_text: - H.4-5 Continue to prioritize surplus City, enterprise agency, and other public land for affordable housing based on timing and financial feasibility.
extraction_rationale: Public land disposition priority for affordable housing.
mechanism_description: Using surplus public land to build affordable housing promotes infill/compact development, which can reduce sprawl and associated transportation emissions while lowering land conversion pressures.

policy_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
## RESPONSIBLE PRODUCTION & CONSUMPTION (RPC)
- RPC 1 Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.

RPC.1
## Supporting Actions
RPC.1-1 Between 2024-2026, phase in policies...
RPC.1-3 By 2025, establish a maximum allowance for embodied carbon...
RPC.1-4 By 2025, amend existing policies to require deconstruction...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy focused on embodied carbon with multiple time-bound supporting actions.
mechanism_description: Reduce embodied carbon from building and infrastructure construction (e.g., through material limits and deconstruction/reuse practices) to lower lifecycle construction-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Between 2024-2026, phase in policies to reduce embodied carbon more than 10% per project by addressing at least three product categories or building assembly types (RPC.1-1).
parent_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text: RPC.1-1 Between 2024-2026, phase in policies to reduce embodied carbon more than 10% per project by addressing at least three product categories or building assembly types.
extraction_rationale: Quantified embodied carbon reduction requirement with timeframe and scope.
mechanism_description: Implementing policies that reduce embodied carbon in building materials and assemblies lowers lifecycle construction-related greenhouse gas emissions from material production and use.

policy_statement: By 2025, develop incentives/policies/guidelines for adaptive reuse of existing buildings and for design/procurement of low-carbon structural materials for new construction (RPC.1-2).
parent_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text: RPC.1-2 By 2025, develop a suite of incentives, policies, and/or guidelines for adaptive reuse of existing buildings, as well as the design and procurement of low-carbon structural materials for new construction.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound development of policy/incentive tools for embodied carbon reduction.
mechanism_description: Incentives and procurement/design guidelines increase use of reused structures and low-carbon structural materials, which lowers embodied emissions from building construction.

policy_statement: By 2025, establish a maximum allowance for embodied carbon of buildings, adjusted at regular intervals (RPC.1-3).
parent_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text: RPC.1-3 By 2025, establish a maximum allowance for embodied carbon of buildings, to be adjusted at regular intervals.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory standard commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Imposing a maximum embodied-carbon allowance for buildings forces lower-carbon material and design choices, reducing upfront construction-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: By 2025, amend policies to require deconstruction of buildings and increase source separation of specific materials (RPC.1-4).
parent_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text: RPC.1-4 By 2025, amend existing policies to require deconstruction of buildings and increase the source separation of specific materials.
extraction_rationale: Policy amendment requirement with deadline.
mechanism_description: Mandating deconstruction and source separation increases diversion and recovery of construction materials, reducing landfilled waste and associated methane emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, advance 'Design for Disassembly' and 'Buildings As Material Banks' best practices and pilot at least one municipal project (RPC.1-7).
parent_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text: RPC.1-7 By 2030, advance best practices for 'Design for Disassembly' and 'Buildings As Material Banks' by creating implementation resources in partnership with global cities, and pilot at least one municipal project to maximize the value of carbon already invested in buildings.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound best-practice adoption and municipal pilot commitment.
mechanism_description: Implementing design-for-disassembly and material-banking practices increases reuse of building components, lowering demand for new construction materials and thereby reducing embodied-carbon emissions from construction.

policy_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- RPC 2 Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.

RPC.2
Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system...
## Supporting Actions
RPC.2-1 By 2030, reduce food waste by 50%...
RPC.2-3 By 2024, adopt a Food Waste Prevention and Edible Food Recovery policy...
RPC.2-6 By 2025, ... hospitals meet a 20% reduction in carbon and water footprints...
extraction_rationale: Named food system strategy with multiple quantified and time-bound actions.
mechanism_description: Preventing food waste and recovering edible surplus reduces emissions from producing, transporting, and disposing of food, including avoiding methane from landfilled organics.

policy_statement: By 2030, reduce food waste by 50% in alignment with the Pacific Coast Collaborative voluntary commitment by implementing guidelines and recommendations with food retailers/distributors/manufacturers (RPC.2-1).
parent_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
verbatim_text: RPC.2-1 By 2030, reduce food waste by 50% in alignment with the City's voluntary commitment to the Pacific Coast Collaborative initiative by implementing food waste reduction guidelines and recommendations in partnership with food retail, distributors, and manufacturers.
extraction_rationale: Quantified food waste reduction target with deadline and implementation approach.
mechanism_description: Food waste prevention programs with supply-chain partners reduce the amount of organic waste generated, which lowers landfill disposal and avoids methane emissions.

policy_statement: By 2022, continue and scale the Kitchen Zero SF pilot program to track over-purchasing and redirect wasted food to communities in need, including recovered fresh produce for communities with limited access (RPC.2-2).
parent_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
verbatim_text: RPC.2-2 By 2022, continue implementing and scale the Kitchen Zero SF pilot program, which reduces food waste by tracking over-purchasing by food generators, and redirects otherwise wasted food to communities in need, including providing recovered fresh produce to communities with limited access.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound scale-up of named pilot with equity intent.
mechanism_description: Tracking over-purchasing and redirecting edible surplus food reduces organic waste sent to landfills, thereby avoiding methane emissions from decomposition.

policy_statement: By 2024, adopt a Food Waste Prevention and Edible Food Recovery policy and develop compliance/monitoring program and incentives aligned with California SB 1383 (RPC.2-3).
parent_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
verbatim_text: RPC.2-3 By 2024, adopt a Food Waste Prevention and Edible Food Recovery policy and develop a program and incentives structure for compliance and monitoring in alignment with California's State Bill 1383 food recovery regulations.
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy adoption with deadline and compliance mechanism.
mechanism_description: Implementing food waste prevention and edible food recovery programs diverts organic material from landfills, reducing methane emissions from anaerobic decomposition.

policy_statement: By 2024, develop guidance for City food procurement aligned with Good Food Purchasing Program core values (including environmental sustainability and regenerative agriculture) (RPC.2-5).
parent_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
verbatim_text: RPC.2-5 By 2024, develop guidance in partnership with other municipal agencies to implement city procurement of food in alignment with the five core values put forth by the Good Food Purchasing Program (GFPP): developing local economies, improving health, valuing the workforce, considering animal welfare, and environmental sustainability, including regenerative agriculture.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound procurement guidance tied to named program values.
mechanism_description: Developing and applying sustainable/regenerative-aligned food procurement guidance shifts purchased food toward lower-emissions production practices, reducing supply-chain emissions and supporting soil carbon and ecosystem benefits.

policy_statement: By 2025, SFDPH will ensure Zuckerberg San Francisco General and Laguna Honda Hospitals achieve a 20% reduction in carbon and water footprints via sustainable food purchasing standards aligned with GFPP values (RPC.2-6).
parent_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
verbatim_text: RPC.2-6 By 2025, San Francisco Department of Public Health will ensure the Zuckerberg San Francisco General and Laguna Honda Hospitals meet a 20% reduction in carbon and water footprints by implementing sustainable food purchasing standards that ensure food procurement aligns with the core values of the GFPP.
extraction_rationale: Quantified institutional target with deadline and implementation mechanism.
mechanism_description: Implementing sustainable food procurement standards shifts institutional food purchasing toward lower-carbon and lower-water-intensity options, reducing lifecycle food-system emissions and water footprint.

policy_statement: By 2030, SF Unified School District will build on its GFPP resolution to procure food locally and from minority-owned businesses/farms, switch entrees to lower-emissions alternatives, reduce over-purchasing, and donate meals to communities in need (RPC.2-7).
parent_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
verbatim_text: RPC.2-7 By 2030, San Francisco Unified School District will continue to build upon its adopted resolution to participate in the GFPP, aiming to procure food locally and from minority owned businesses and farms, switch entrees to lower-emissions alternatives, reduce over-purchasing of food, and donate meals to communities in need.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound school district procurement/diet/waste actions with equity components.
mechanism_description: Shift institutional food purchasing toward lower-emissions and local suppliers while preventing food waste (including donation), reducing lifecycle food-system greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: RPC.3: Promote reduction, reuse, repair, and recovery of goods and materials.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- RPC 3 Promote reduction, reuse, repair, and recovery of goods and materials.

RPC.3
## Supporting Actions
RPC.3-1 By 2023, reduce use of non-reusable foodware...
RPC.3-2 By 2023, ... take-back and resale of used clothing...
RPC.3-6 By 2025, ... maximize material recovery.
extraction_rationale: Named materials circularity strategy with several time-bound supporting actions.
mechanism_description: Increase reduction/reuse/repair and material recovery so less waste is landfilled, reducing methane emissions from landfill decomposition.

policy_statement: By 2023, reduce use of non-reusable foodware by requiring/incentivizing/supporting/promoting reusables for on- and off-site dining (to-go/delivery) (RPC.3-1).
parent_statement: RPC.3: Promote reduction, reuse, repair, and recovery of goods and materials.
verbatim_text: RPC.3-1 By 2023, reduce use of non-reusable foodware by requiring, incentivizing, supporting and/or promoting reusables for on and off-site dining (to-go or delivery).
extraction_rationale: Time-bound policy/program action with specified scope.
mechanism_description: Require and/or incentivize reusable foodware to replace single-use items, reducing material throughput and associated manufacturing and disposal emissions.

policy_statement: By 2023, require take-back and resale of used clothing and promote donation and longevity of apparel/textiles (RPC.3-2).
parent_statement: RPC.3: Promote reduction, reuse, repair, and recovery of goods and materials.
verbatim_text: RPC.3-2 By 2023, reduce, reuse, and repair, by requiring take-back and resale of used clothing, and promoting donation and longevity of used apparel and textiles.
extraction_rationale: Explicit requirement and promotional actions with deadline.
mechanism_description: Require and encourage textile take-back, resale, donation, and repair to keep clothing in use longer, reducing textile waste disposal and avoiding associated landfill/incineration and upstream production emissions.

policy_statement: By 2024, encourage/facilitate inclusive neighborhood-scale lending libraries, repair clinics, and reuse exchanges for tools/electronics/furniture and other goods (RPC.3-3).
parent_statement: RPC.3: Promote reduction, reuse, repair, and recovery of goods and materials.
verbatim_text: RPC.3-3 By 2024, encourage or facilitate inclusive and networked neighborhood-scale projects such as lending libraries, repair clinics, and reuse exchanges for tools, equipment, electronics, furniture and other goods that reduce production and consumption of goods.
extraction_rationale: Concrete community infrastructure actions with deadline.
mechanism_description: Expanding lending, repair, and reuse exchanges reduces demand for new products, thereby cutting emissions from manufacturing and associated supply chains.

policy_statement: Increase compliance with mandatory construction and demolition debris recovery (Environment Code Chapter 14) and mandatory recycling/composting (Environment Code Chapter 19) to increase recovery and reduce disposal (RPC.3-5).
parent_statement: RPC.3: Promote reduction, reuse, repair, and recovery of goods and materials.
verbatim_text: RPC.3-5   Increase compliance with mandatory construction and demolition debris recovery (newly amended Environment Code Chapter 14) and mandatory recycling and composting (Environment Code Chapter 19) to increase recovery and reduce disposal while providing economic and social benefits such as local jobs and reduced illegal dumping.
extraction_rationale: Explicit compliance/enforcement action referencing legal requirements.
mechanism_description: Enforcing recycling/composting and C&D recovery increases waste diversion from landfill, which reduces methane-generating disposal and lowers GHG emissions.

policy_statement: RPC.4: Lead the aviation sector by reducing emissions across the airline passenger journey.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- RPC 4 Lead the aviation sector by reducing emissions across the airline passenger journey.

RPC.4
## Supporting Actions
RPC.4-1 SFO will encourage and incentivize...
RPC.4-2 ... replace up to 50% of its fuel supply with Sustainable Aviation Fuels by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Named aviation strategy with SFO-specific supporting actions and quantified SAF target.
mechanism_description: Increase use of sustainable aviation fuels in place of fossil jet fuel to reduce lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions from aviation.

policy_statement: SFO will encourage and incentivize switching aviation sector fuel to low-carbon sources for air and ground fleets where viable (RPC.4-1).
parent_statement: RPC.4: Lead the aviation sector by reducing emissions across the airline passenger journey.
verbatim_text: RPC.4-1 SFO will encourage and incentivize, where viable, switching aviation sector fuel to low carbon sources for both air and ground fleets.
extraction_rationale: Explicit incentive action by SFO.
mechanism_description: Incentivizing a switch from conventional aviation and ground fleet fuels to low-carbon fuels reduces greenhouse gas emissions from transportation energy use.

policy_statement: SFO will work with airlines to replace up to 50% of its fuel supply with Sustainable Aviation Fuels by 2050 (RPC.4-2).
parent_statement: RPC.4: Lead the aviation sector by reducing emissions across the airline passenger journey.
verbatim_text: RPC.4-2 SFO will continue its leadership and partnership with airlines to work to replace up to 50% of its fuel supply with Sustainable Aviation Fuels by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Quantified long-term SAF target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Replacing fossil jet fuel with sustainable aviation fuels reduces the lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions from air travel.

policy_statement: HE.1: Advance citywide collaboration to refine nature-based climate solutions that sequester carbon, restore ecosystems, and conserve biodiversity.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
## HEALTHY ECOSYSTEMS (HE)
- HE 1 Advance citywide collaboration to continually refine nature-based climate solutions that sequester carbon, restore ecosystems and conserve biodiversity.

HE.1
## Supporting Actions
HE.1-1 By 2022, complete the Alameda watershed carbon case study...
HE.1-5 By 2025, incorporate findings into a Carbon Sequestration and Ecosystem Restoration Strategy...
extraction_rationale: Named nature-based solutions strategy with multiple time-bound planning actions.
mechanism_description: Developing and improving nature-based solutions increases ecosystem restoration and protected/healthy natural systems, which enhances carbon sequestration and supports biodiversity.

policy_statement: By 2022, complete the Alameda watershed carbon case study and quantify carbon storage value (HE.1-1).
parent_statement: HE.1: Advance citywide collaboration to refine nature-based climate solutions that sequester carbon, restore ecosystems, and conserve biodiversity.
verbatim_text: HE.1-1 By 2022, complete the Alameda watershed carbon case study and quantify the value of carbon storage provided by protecting this natural area.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound study deliverable.
mechanism_description: Conducting a carbon storage valuation case study generates evidence that can guide protection of natural areas, supporting preservation of ecosystem carbon stocks.

policy_statement: By 2023, City departments will develop policies/procedures to assess carbon sequestration opportunities in capital projects, prioritize biodiversity/green infrastructure, and maximize native plants, working through the Biodiversity Interagency Working Group (HE.1-3).
parent_statement: HE.1: Advance citywide collaboration to refine nature-based climate solutions that sequester carbon, restore ecosystems, and conserve biodiversity.
verbatim_text: HE.1-3 By 2023, City departments should develop their own policies and procedures to assess carbon sequestration opportunities for capital projects, prioritize biodiversity and green infrastructure, and maximize local native plants. Departments should work together in the Biodiversity Interagency Working Group to create shared policies and procedures where possible.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound policy/procedure development commitment across departments.
mechanism_description: Require city capital projects to prioritize green infrastructure and native plants, increasing ecosystem carbon sequestration and improving ecological resilience.

policy_statement: By 2025, incorporate sequestration and biodiversity findings into a Carbon Sequestration and Ecosystem Restoration Strategy for City land and watershed management (HE.1-5).
parent_statement: HE.1: Advance citywide collaboration to refine nature-based climate solutions that sequester carbon, restore ecosystems, and conserve biodiversity.
verbatim_text: HE.1-5 By 2025, incorporate carbon sequestration and biodiversity conservation findings into a Carbon Sequestration and Ecosystem Restoration Strategy for City land and watershed management, consistent with agencies' existing plans and policies.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound strategy development deliverable.
mechanism_description: Developing and applying an ecosystem restoration and sequestration strategy for land and watershed management increases protection and restoration of natural systems, preserving and enhancing carbon storage while supporting biodiversity.

policy_statement: HE.2: Increase equitable community participation in nature-based climate solutions, prioritizing Indigenous science and Traditional Ecological Knowledge.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- HE 2 Increase equitable community participation and perspectives in nature-based climate solutions, including meaningful efforts to prioritize Indigenous science and Traditional Ecological Knowledge.

## Supporting Actions
HE.2-1 The City will engage American Indian tribes...
HE.2-2 By 2022, establish an inter-jurisdictional working group...
HE.2-3 The City will honor Indigenous knowledge... Ramaytush Ohlone...
extraction_rationale: Named equity/participation strategy with listed supporting actions.
mechanism_description: Increase equitable and Indigenous-led participation in nature-based climate solution planning so decisions incorporate Traditional Ecological Knowledge, improving governance quality and capacity to implement effective climate actions.

policy_statement: Engage American Indian tribes and other stakeholders in planning/implementation of greening projects, including for local hiring and workforce development (HE.2-1).
parent_statement: HE.2: Increase equitable community participation in nature-based climate solutions, prioritizing Indigenous science and Traditional Ecological Knowledge.
verbatim_text: HE.2-1 The City will engage American Indian tribes, cultural bearers, neighborhood organizations, local businesses, the San Francisco Unified School District, and non-profit organizations during the planning and implementation of greening projects, including for the purpose of local hiring and workforce development.
extraction_rationale: Stakeholder engagement commitment with workforce component.
mechanism_description: Engaging tribes and other stakeholders and developing the local workforce strengthens governance and delivery capacity, improving the ability to plan and implement greening projects that support climate resilience.

policy_statement: By 2022, establish an inter-jurisdictional working group including American Indian representation and agencies/institutions focused on nature-based solutions for resilience and biodiversity (HE.2-2).
parent_statement: HE.2: Increase equitable community participation in nature-based climate solutions, prioritizing Indigenous science and Traditional Ecological Knowledge.
verbatim_text: HE.2-2  By 2022, establish an inter-jurisdictional working group of American Indian representation, federal and state parks agencies, cultural districts, local non-profits, and educational and research institutions, dedicated to nature-based solutions, focused on resilience and biodiversity conservation.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound governance structure creation.
mechanism_description: Establishing a cross-agency working group improves coordination and planning capacity to develop and implement nature-based resilience and biodiversity conservation actions.

policy_statement: Create partnerships with the Ramaytush Ohlone and American Indian community for stewardship of lands managed by San Francisco (HE.2-3).
parent_statement: HE.2: Increase equitable community participation in nature-based climate solutions, prioritizing Indigenous science and Traditional Ecological Knowledge.
verbatim_text: HE.2-3   The City will honor Indigenous knowledge from the original stewards of these lands (Yelamu) and create strong partnerships through meaningful engagement with the Ramaytush Ohlone and the American Indian community to participate in stewardship of lands managed by San Francisco.
extraction_rationale: Explicit partnership and stewardship participation commitment.
mechanism_description: Partnering with Indigenous communities for stewardship of publicly managed lands supports improved land management practices that protect ecosystems and help preserve stored carbon while strengthening climate resilience.

policy_statement: HE.3: Restore and enhance parks, natural lands, and large open spaces.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- HE 3 Restore and enhance parks, natural lands and large open spaces.

## Supporting Actions
HE.3-3  By 2025, create a 3-acre horizontal levee at Heron's Head Park.
HE.3-4  By 2030, restore and create 173 acres... on Yerba Buena and Treasure Islands...
HE.3-5  By 2030, restore 100+ acres... at the San Bruno Jail and SFO West of Bayshore Properties.
extraction_rationale: Named restoration strategy with quantified acreage and specific project actions.
mechanism_description: Restoring and enhancing parks and natural/open lands improves ecosystem condition and protects/expands carbon storage, supporting both resilience and emissions mitigation through preserved and restored natural carbon sinks.

policy_statement: By 2025, create a 3-acre horizontal levee at Heron's Head Park (HE.3-3).
parent_statement: HE.3: Restore and enhance parks, natural lands, and large open spaces.
verbatim_text: - HE.3-3  By 2025, create a 3-acre horizontal levee at Heron's Head Park.
extraction_rationale: Specific nature-based infrastructure project with deadline and size.
mechanism_description: Building a horizontal levee provides a coastal buffer that reduces flooding and shoreline impact risks from sea level rise and storm events.

policy_statement: By 2030, restore and create 173 acres of natural ecological parkland on Yerba Buena and Treasure Islands (HE.3-4).
parent_statement: HE.3: Restore and enhance parks, natural lands, and large open spaces.
verbatim_text: - HE.3-4  By 2030, restore and create 173 acres of natural ecological parkland on Yerba Buena and Treasure Islands, including implementing the Yerba Buena Island Habitat Management Plan.
extraction_rationale: Quantified acreage restoration/creation commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Restoring and creating natural parkland increases protected/restored ecosystems, preserving and enhancing carbon storage while improving local climate resilience.

policy_statement: By 2030, restore 100+ acres of upland and wetland habitats at the San Bruno Jail and SFO West of Bayshore Properties (HE.3-5).
parent_statement: HE.3: Restore and enhance parks, natural lands, and large open spaces.
verbatim_text: - HE.3-5  By 2030, restore 100+ acres of upland and wetland habitats at the San Bruno Jail and SFO West of Bayshore Properties.
extraction_rationale: Quantified habitat restoration commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Restoring upland and wetland habitats improves ecosystem condition, increasing carbon storage/sequestration and strengthening climate resilience.

policy_statement: HE.5: Maximize trees throughout the public realm.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- HE 5 Maximize trees throughout the public realm.

## Supporting Actions
- HE.5-1 By 2040, plant 30,000 street trees...
- HE.5-3  By 2023, create a City-managed and -dedicated street tree nursery.
- HE.5-4  By 2023, create a policy to require preservation of mature trees...
extraction_rationale: Named urban forestry strategy with quantified tree planting and policy actions.
mechanism_description: Expanding and preserving trees in public spaces increases urban canopy, which cools the city and sequesters carbon, reducing climate impacts.

policy_statement: By 2040, plant 30,000 street trees in sidewalk tree wells (about a 25% increase) to complete the street tree network (HE.5-1).
parent_statement: HE.5: Maximize trees throughout the public realm.
verbatim_text: - HE.5-1 By 2040, plant 30,000 street trees in the sidewalk tree wells, approximately a 25% increase, to complete the street tree network.,
extraction_rationale: Quantified tree planting target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Planting more street trees expands urban canopy, which cools the urban environment and increases carbon storage in vegetation.

policy_statement: By 2023, create a City-managed and dedicated street tree nursery (HE.5-3).
parent_statement: HE.5: Maximize trees throughout the public realm.
verbatim_text: - HE.5-3  By 2023, create a City-managed and -dedicated street tree nursery.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound creation of municipal nursery infrastructure.
mechanism_description: A city-run street tree nursery increases tree availability for planting, expanding urban canopy to reduce heat impacts and store carbon.

policy_statement: By 2023, create a policy requiring preservation of mature trees during development/infrastructure modifications and replacement planting of basal area equivalent when removal is unavoidable (HE.5-4).
parent_statement: HE.5: Maximize trees throughout the public realm.
verbatim_text: - HE.5-4  By 2023, create a policy to require preservation of mature trees during development or infrastructure modifications and for planting of basal area equivalent of mature trees whose removal is unavoidable.
extraction_rationale: Explicit regulatory policy with deadline and replacement standard.
mechanism_description: Require mature tree preservation and equivalent replacement planting so urban canopy is maintained/expanded, increasing carbon storage and reducing urban heat impacts.

policy_statement: HE.7: Conduct carbon sequestration farming pilot projects and research.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- HE 7 Conduct carbon sequestration farming pilot projects and research.

## Supporting Actions
HE.7-1 By 2024, apply approximately 500 wet tons of biosolids per year...
HE.7-4 By 2025, SFO will expand its carbon mitigation and offset program...
extraction_rationale: Named carbon farming/sequestration pilot strategy with quantified supporting actions.
