mechanism_description: Pilot and research soil-building farming practices (e.g., organic amendments) to increase soil carbon sequestration, thereby storing more carbon and improving ecosystem function.

policy_statement: By 2024, apply approximately 500 wet tons of biosolids per year as a soil amendment to sequester carbon on identified sites such as mine reclamation projects in Northern California (HE.7-1).
parent_statement: HE.7: Conduct carbon sequestration farming pilot projects and research.
verbatim_text: HE.7-1 By 2024, apply approximately 500 wet tons of biosolids per year as a soil amendment and to sequester carbon on newly identified sites such as mine reclamation projects in Northern California.
extraction_rationale: Quantified annual application commitment with deadline and purpose.
mechanism_description: Applying biosolids to land as a soil amendment increases soil organic matter, leading to greater carbon storage in soils (often alongside improved reclamation outcomes).

policy_statement: Improve compliance with mandatory composting (Environment Code Chapter 19 and SB 1383) and optimize organics processing to increase compost quantity/quality for soil carbon sequestration (HE.7-2).
parent_statement: HE.7: Conduct carbon sequestration farming pilot projects and research.
verbatim_text: HE.7-2 Improve compliance with Mandatory Composting (Environment Code Chapter 19 and SB 1383) and optimize organics processing to increase the quantity and quality of compost produced to support soil carbon sequestration activities.
extraction_rationale: Compliance and process optimization action tied to sequestration.
mechanism_description: Increase organics diversion and improve compost processing so organic waste is composted instead of landfilled, reducing methane emissions and producing compost that supports soil carbon sequestration.

policy_statement: By 2025, SFO will expand its carbon mitigation and offset program to include soil carbon sequestration projects where viable (HE.7-4).
parent_statement: HE.7: Conduct carbon sequestration farming pilot projects and research.
verbatim_text: HE.7-4  By 2025, SFO will expand its carbon mitigation and offset program to include soil carbon sequestration projects, where viable.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound expansion of an existing offset program to include soil carbon projects.
mechanism_description: Expand an offset program to support soil carbon sequestration projects, increasing carbon stored in soils and thereby reducing net greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: THE PATH TO REACH NET-ZERO BY 2040 / EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS
policy_statement: Reduce San Francisco sector-based greenhouse gas emissions by at least 61% below 1990 levels by 2030 and achieve net-zero sector-based emissions by 2040 (at least a 90% reduction below 1990), sequestering any residual emissions through nature-based solutions.
verbatim_text:
By integrating these four climate justice commitments, the CAP proposes two ambitious and achievable climate emission reduction targets:
- An interim target of cutting sector-based emissions 61% below 1990 levels by 2030; and
- Net-zero sector-based emissions by 2040, a 90% reduction from 1990 levels

TABLE 5: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION TARGETS
By 2040, achieve net-zero emissions by reducing emissions at least 90%compared to 1990 levels and sequester any residual emissions through nature- based solutions
extraction_rationale: Citywide quantified emissions targets with deadlines and defined net-zero mechanism (90% reduction plus sequestration).
mechanism_description: Set an economy-wide/net-zero greenhouse gas target to drive actions across sectors that reduce emissions, with any remaining emissions balanced through nature-based sequestration.

section_header: THE PATH TO REACH NET-ZERO BY 2040 / EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS
policy_statement: Reduce consumption-based emissions by 40% below 1990 levels by 2030 and by 80% below 1990 levels by 2050 (expressed as less than 30 mtCO2e per household by 2030 and less than 10 mtCO2e per household by 2050).
verbatim_text:
San Francisco is determined to reduce the impacts of these outsourced emissions and has set two targets:
- A 40% reduction in consumption-based emissions by 2030
- An 80% reduction in consumption-based emissions by 2050

TABLE 5: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION TARGETS
By 2030, reduce consumption-based emissions to less than 30 mtCO2e per household, equivalent to a 40% reduction compared to 1990 levels
By 2050, reduce consumption-based emissions to less than 10 mtCO2e per household, equivalent to an 80% reduction compared to 1990 levels
extraction_rationale: Quantified consumption-based emissions reduction targets with deadlines and per-household metrics.
mechanism_description: Setting a jurisdiction-wide consumption-based emissions reduction target drives planning and actions across sectors to reduce total lifecycle emissions associated with household and community consumption.

policy_statement: Supply 100% renewable electricity in San Francisco by 2025 and supply 100% renewable energy (no more fossil fuels) by 2040.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
ENERGY  By 2025, supplying 100% renewable electricity, and by 2040, supplying 100% renewable energy (no more fossil fuels).
extraction_rationale: Clear, dated renewable energy goals stated in the CAP goals table.
mechanism_description: Setting a 100% renewable energy supply target drives deployment of renewable generation to replace fossil energy, reducing emissions from electricity/energy supply.

section_header: EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS / BUILDING OPERATIONS GOALS
policy_statement: Require zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all new buildings by 2021; require zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all large existing commercial buildings by 2035; and achieve zero emissions for all buildings by 2040.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
BUILDINGS  By 2021, requiring zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all new buildings, and by 2035, requiring zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all large existing commercial buildings.

## BUILDING OPERATIONS
## GOALS:
1) Zero emisisons new construction by 2021
2) All large commerical buildings are zero emissions by 2035
3) All buildings zero emissions by 2040
extraction_rationale: Mandate-style requirements with deadlines for new buildings, large commercial buildings, and all buildings.
mechanism_description: Mandating zero onsite fossil-fuel emissions in buildings drives replacement of fossil-fueled equipment with non-combustion alternatives (typically electric), thereby cutting building operational greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS / TRANSPORTATION & LAND USE GOALS
policy_statement: By 2030, achieve at least 80% of trips by low-carbon modes; electrify at least 25% of registered private vehicles by 2030 and 100% by 2040.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
TRANSPORTATION  By 2030, an increase in low-carbon trips to at least 80%of all trips measured and an increase in the level of electrification of vehicles to at least 25%of all private vehicles registered, and by 2040, an increase in the level of electrification of vehicles to 100% of all private vehicles registered.

## TRANSPORTATION & LAND USE
## GOALS:
1) By 2030, 80% of trips taken by low-carbon modes
2) By 2030, at least 25% of all  vehicles registered in SF are electric, reaching 100% by 2040
extraction_rationale: Quantified transport mode-share and EV adoption targets with explicit deadlines.
mechanism_description: Increase the share of trips made by low-carbon modes and electrify private vehicles to lower per-trip and per-mile greenhouse gas emissions from transportation.

section_header: EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS / RESPONSIBLE PRODUCTION & CONSUMPTION GOALS
policy_statement: By 2030, reduce solid waste generation at least 15% below 2015 levels and reduce waste disposed by incineration or landfill at least 50% below 2015 levels.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
ZEROWASTE  By 2030, a reduction in the generation of solid waste of at least 15% below 2015 levels and a reduction in the amount of solid waste disposed of by incineration or deposited in landfill of at least 50%below 2015 levels.

RESPONSIBLE PRODUCTION & CONSUMPTION
## GOALS:
By 2030:
1) Reduce solid waste generation 15% below 2015 levels
2) Reduce disposal to landfill 50% below 2015 levels
extraction_rationale: Quantified waste reduction and landfill disposal targets with deadline.
mechanism_description: Reducing waste generation and diverting waste away from landfill/incineration lowers the amount of waste landfilled, thereby avoiding methane emissions from landfill decomposition.

policy_statement: To meet San Francisco's goal of zero emissions by 2040, all existing buildings must be renovated or replaced by 2040.
verbatim_text: As buildings are retrofitted and renovated, the stock of existing buildings decreases in this model. To meet San Francisco's goal of zero emissions by 2040, 100% of existing buildings must be renovated or replaced by 2040.
extraction_rationale: This is a quantified, deadline-based requirement explicitly linked to achieving the City's 2040 zero-emissions goal; it functions as a policy target/condition.
mechanism_description: Requiring existing buildings to be renovated or replaced drives building retrofits that lower operational energy consumption and thereby reduce associated greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: 3.3 Commitments in the application of the social pillar for updating and implementing the NDC
policy_statement: Contribution in Just Transition and Sustainable Development N°1-2-3 (SP1-2-3).
verbatim_text:
“## 3.3 Commitments in the application of the social pillar for updating and implementing the NDC

## Contribution in Just Transition and Sustainable Development N°1-2-3 (SP1-2-3)

## CONTRIBUTION

SP1) Ensure the application of the criteria mentioned in Section 3.2 in the processes of update, elaboration and implementation of the NDC.

SP2) Establish a mechanism to measure, report and verify the application of the criteria mentioned in section 3.2.

SP3) Develop by 2021 a 'Strategy for Just Transition' that protects the rights of the most vulnerable in the process of decarbonizing the energy matrix, ensuring active participation of citizens in its design and implementation.”
extraction_rationale: Named set of commitments (SP1-3) presented as a grouped contribution under the Social Pillar.
mechanism_description: Establishing just-transition criteria, monitoring/reporting/verification, and a participatory just-transition strategy strengthens governance and social safeguards to enable equitable implementation of energy decarbonization policies.

section_header: 3.3 Commitments in the application of the social pillar for updating and implementing the NDC

extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to apply specified criteria across NDC processes.

mechanism_description: Create a just transition strategy with citizen participation to protect vulnerable groups and strengthen social/institutional conditions that enable energy matrix decarbonization.

policy_statement: Contribución en Mitigación N°1 (M1).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribución en Mitigación N°1 (M1)

## CONTRIBUTION

M1) Chile commits to a GHG emission budget not exceeding 1,100 MtCO2eq between 2020 and 2030, with a GHG emissions maximum (peak) by 2025, and a GHG emissions level of 95 MtCO2eq by 2030.

... Therefore, at the national level, in 2020 we will establish a public-private dialogue roundtable to define specific policy for the use of markets, taking into account clear guidelines that preserve environmental integrity, avoid double-counting and promote sustainable development.”
extraction_rationale: Contains quantified, time-bound economy-wide mitigation targets plus an implementation action (market policy dialogue).
mechanism_description: Setting an economy-wide GHG emissions budget/peak target guides and constrains actions across sectors to reduce overall greenhouse gas emissions over time.

policy_statement: Chile will keep its GHG emissions budget to no more than 1,100 MtCO2eq for 2020–2030, with emissions peaking by 2025 and reaching 95 MtCO2eq by 2030 (M1).
parent_statement: Contribución en Mitigación N°1 (M1).
verbatim_text: “M1) Chile commits to a GHG emission budget not exceeding 1,100 MtCO2eq between 2020 and 2030, with a GHG emissions maximum (peak) by 2025, and a GHG emissions level of 95 MtCO2eq by 2030.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified mitigation commitment with timeframe and milestone year.
mechanism_description: Setting an economy-wide emissions budget and targets drives policies and actions across sectors to reduce total greenhouse gas emissions over time.

policy_statement: In 2020, Chile will establish a public-private dialogue roundtable to define policy for the use of markets under Article 6, with guidelines to preserve environmental integrity, avoid double-counting, and promote sustainable development (M1).
parent_statement: Contribución en Mitigación N°1 (M1).
verbatim_text: “Therefore, at the national level, in 2020 we will establish a public-private dialogue roundtable to define specific policy for the use of markets, taking into account clear guidelines that preserve environmental integrity, avoid double-counting and promote sustainable development.”
extraction_rationale: Specific institutional mechanism with a deadline and stated safeguards.
mechanism_description: Establishes a public-private process to set rules for participating in carbon markets that ensure integrity (e.g., no double counting), enabling credible mitigation actions and associated emissions reductions.

policy_statement: Contribution to Mitigation Nº2 (M2).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Mitigation Nº2 (M2)

## CONTRIBUTION

M2) Reduce total black carbon emissions by at least 25% by 2030, with respect to 2016 levels. This commitment will be implemented primarily through national policies focused on air quality. In addition, it will be monitored through permanent and periodic work to improve information available in the black carbon inventory.”
extraction_rationale: Clear quantified black carbon reduction target with year and monitoring approach.
mechanism_description: Set and implement a black carbon emissions reduction target (via air-quality policies), resulting in lower black carbon releases and reduced near-term climate warming impacts.

section_header: 5.2.1 Climate change policies, strategies and plans
policy_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2)

## CONTRIBUTION

A2) The coordination of climate action on adaptation at national level will be strengthened through the national adaptation plan, and the adaptation plans for 11 priority sectors, incorporating lessons learned from the implementation of earlier plans. Those documents will be used as inputs for the Adaptation Communication that will be sent to the UNFCCC in 2022 at the latest.

## The plans will be developed according to the following schedule:

a) 2022: The National Adaptation Plan shall be updated and in the first stages of implementation.

b) 2022: The first Adaptation Plans on water resources, coastal areas and the mining sector shall be developed and in the first stages of implementation.

c) 2021-2028: The Adaptation Plans for sectors in Agriculture and Forestry ... shall be updated and implemented.”
extraction_rationale: Umbrella adaptation planning commitment with a detailed schedule of sub-actions.
mechanism_description: Developing, updating, and implementing national and sectoral adaptation plans improves coordination and planning, increasing institutional capacity to manage climate risks.

section_header: 5.2.1 Climate change policies, strategies and plans
policy_statement: By 2021, estimate the costs of inaction on climate change; by 2025, estimate costs associated with historic losses and damage (A4b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “b) By 2021, an estimation of the costs of inaction on climate change, and by 2025 an estimation of costs associated with historic by losses and damage as part of this.”
extraction_rationale: Two time-bound analytical estimates specified as climate actions.
mechanism_description: Developing formal estimates of the costs of climate inaction and loss-and-damage improves the evidence base for planning and prioritizing climate action and resilience investments.

section_header: 5.2.1 Climate change policies, strategies and plans
policy_statement: By 2025, assess nationwide climate change risk to vulnerable groups, focusing on indigenous peoples, poverty, and gender (A4c).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “c) By 2025, assessments of climate change risk to vulnerable groups nation-wide, with a special focus on indigenous peoples, poverty and gender.”
extraction_rationale: Dated assessment task with defined focus.
mechanism_description: Conducting climate risk assessments for vulnerable groups generates evidence that supports better-targeted adaptation planning and resource allocation to reduce climate impacts.

section_header: 5.2.1 Climate change policies, strategies and plans
policy_statement: By 2026, strengthen the monitoring and evaluation system with progress and impact indicators for all climate change adaptation instruments (A5).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “A5) By 2026, the current monitoring and evaluation system will have been strengthened, with indicators on progress and impact for all climate change adaptation instruments...”
extraction_rationale: Specific system strengthening with a deadline and defined scope.
mechanism_description: Strengthening monitoring and evaluation with progress and impact indicators improves data and feedback on adaptation actions, enabling better-informed planning and adaptive management.

section_header: 5.2.1 Climate change policies, strategies and plans
policy_statement: Strengthen inclusion of non-governmental actors in planning and implementing adaptation measures during the NDC implementation period (A6).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “A6) During the implementation period of this NDC, the inclusion of non-governmental actors in the planning and implementation of adaptation measures will be strengthened.”
extraction_rationale: Explicit governance/participation strengthening commitment.
mechanism_description: Increasing non-governmental actor inclusion in adaptation planning and delivery improves coordination, legitimacy, and implementation capacity, strengthening overall adaptive capacity.

section_header: 5.2.1 Climate change policies, strategies and plans
policy_statement: By 2025, implement and keep updated a record of adaptation actions by non-governmental actors (A6a).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “a) By 2025, an implemented and updated record of actions on adaptation by non-governmental actors.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete registry deliverable with deadline.
mechanism_description: Create and maintain an information system that records non-governmental adaptation actions to improve visibility and coordination and thereby support adaptation planning and implementation.

section_header: 5.2.1 Climate change policies, strategies and plans
policy_statement: By 2030, establish public-private cooperation mechanisms to execute adaptation actions at national and local scale (A6b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “b) By 2030, public-private cooperation mechanisms for the execution of adaptation actions, at national and local scale.”
extraction_rationale: Specific cooperation mechanism commitment with a deadline.
mechanism_description: Establishing public–private cooperation mechanisms improves coordination and implementation capacity, enabling more effective execution of adaptation measures and strengthening overall adaptive capacity.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: Enhance information mechanisms to manage climate change impacts on water resources to improve resilience (A7).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “A7) Information mechanisms for managing the impacts of climate change on water resources will be enhanced in order to improve resilience.”
extraction_rationale: Clear intervention on information mechanisms tied to water resilience.
mechanism_description: Enhancing water-related climate information systems improves decision-making and planning to better manage climate impacts on water resources and increase resilience.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2025, establish a national indicator for hydrological watersheds to track water shortages and risk (A7a).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “a) By 2025, a national indicator for hydrological watersheds will be established to track water shortages and risk, helping to promote water security nationwide.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified time-bound creation of a national indicator.
mechanism_description: Establishing a watershed indicator improves monitoring of water shortages and risks, enabling better planning and management actions that strengthen water security and climate resilience.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2025, create a system to certify water consumption management levels at an organizational level through the HuellaChile Program (A7b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “b) By 2025, a system to certify levels of water consumption management at an organizational level will be created, through the HuellaChile Program.”
extraction_rationale: Specific instrument creation with deadline and named program.
mechanism_description: Establishing a certification system for organizational water management drives improved water-use practices and accountability, resulting in reduced water consumption.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2030, establish Watershed Strategic Plans for Water Resources Management in Chile’s 101 hydrological basins, incorporating climate change adaptation (A7c).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “c) By 2030, Watershed Strategic Plans for the Management of Water Resources will be established, taking into account climate change adaptation in the country's 101 hydrological basins.”
extraction_rationale: Nationwide quantified basin planning commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Developing climate-informed watershed management plans strengthens governance and decision-making for water allocation and risk management, improving resilience to climate impacts such as droughts and floods.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2030, inspect and register 95% of Rural Health Systems to ensure rural drinking water quality standards (A7d).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “d) By 2030, 95% of Rural Health Systems will be inspected and registered, ensuring the quality standards of rural drinking water.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified service coverage target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Inspecting and registering rural water systems increases compliance and corrective action, which improves rural drinking water quality and health resilience.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2030, ensure 100% of companies in the health sector implement disaster risk management plans that consider climate-change-related risks (A7e).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “e) By 2030, 100% of companies in the health sector will have implemented disaster risk management plans, including consideration of risks resulting from climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Universal coverage target for risk management planning with climate consideration.
mechanism_description: Mandating climate-informed disaster risk management plans increases organizational preparedness and response capability, reducing vulnerability to climate-related shocks.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2030, implement 100% of the 2030 Agenda goals for the health sector (A7f).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “f) By 2030, 100% of the 2030 Agenda Goals for the health sector will be implemented.”
extraction_rationale: Time-bound implementation commitment (though broad) explicitly stated with 100% target.
mechanism_description: Implementing the health-sector agenda goals strengthens health-system institutions and capacity, which can indirectly improve preparedness and resilience to climate-related health risks.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2030, require all water-related public infrastructure projects to evaluate protection of population/land (e.g., river works) and prioritize urban/rural human water consumption demands in their areas of influence (A7g).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “g) By 2030, all water-related public infrastructure projects will consider in their evaluation, the ability to protect the population and land (through river works) and/or support as a priority the demands of urban and/or rural human water consumption in their areas of influence.”
extraction_rationale: Applies a defined evaluation/priority requirement to a class of public projects by a deadline.
mechanism_description: Requiring water infrastructure projects to prioritize river protection measures and human water supply needs improves resilience to flooding and water scarcity impacts.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2030, reduce non-treated sewage by at least 25% (A7h).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “h) By  2030, non-treated sewage will be reduced by at least 25%.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Upgrading wastewater collection and treatment reduces the amount of sewage discharged untreated, improving sanitation and water quality outcomes.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: Strengthen nationwide capacity to adapt to climate-related risks and manage adverse effects of socio-natural disasters (A8).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “A8) The capacity to adapt to climate-related risks and the capacity to manage the adverse effects of socio-natural disasters will be strenghtened throughout the country.”
extraction_rationale: Explicit strengthening commitment in DRR/adaptation.
mechanism_description: Build nationwide adaptive and disaster-management capacity to reduce the impacts of climate-related risks and socio-natural disasters.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2021, develop guidelines on climate change effects on human mobility in Chile aligned with international instruments (A8a).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “a) By 2021, guidelines on the effect of climate change in the phenomenon of human mobility in Chile will be developed, in line with related international instruments.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete guidance deliverable with deadline.
mechanism_description: Developing and aligning guidelines on climate change–related human mobility improves institutional readiness and coordination to manage climate-driven displacement and migration risks.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2022, develop a specific national plan for heat-wave-associated risk variables via cross-sectoral work of the Meteorological Risk Variables Working Group (A8b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “b) By 2022, a specific national plan for risk variables associated with heat wave will be developed, through the cross-sectoral work of the Meteorological Risk Variables Working Group.”
extraction_rationale: Specific national plan with a deadline and responsible working group.
mechanism_description: Developing a cross-sectoral national plan for heat-wave risk variables strengthens institutional capacity to anticipate, monitor, and respond to heat risks.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2025, all regions will incorporate climate change adaptation actions into Regional Disaster Risk Reduction Plans in coordination with CORECC (A8c).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “c) By 2025, all Regions in Chile will incorporate climate change adaptation actions in the Regional Plans for Disaster Risk Reduction, in coordination with the Regional Committees for Climate Change (CORECC).”
extraction_rationale: Nationwide coverage requirement with deadline and coordination mechanism.
mechanism_description: Integrating climate adaptation actions into disaster risk reduction plans improves coordinated preparedness and institutional capacity to manage climate-related risks.

section_header: 5.2.2 Areas of greatest urgency in climate action to adaptation
policy_statement: By 2030, fully implement the National Policy for Disaster Risk Reduction 2019–2030, harmonize it with Sendai Framework, Paris Agreement and 2030 Agenda, and review it by 2023 (A8d).
parent_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text: “d) By 2030, the National Policy for Disaster Risk Reduction 2019-2030 will be fully implemented, and its content will be harmonized with the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction, the Paris Agreement and the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development. This policy instrument will also be reviewed by 2023.”
extraction_rationale: Specific policy implementation/harmonization commitment with deadlines.
mechanism_description: Implementing and harmonizing a national disaster risk reduction policy with international frameworks improves institutional planning and coordination, increasing overall adaptive capacity to climate-related risks.

policy_statement: Contribution to Integration - Circular Economy N°2 (I2).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Integration - Circular Economy N°2 (I2)

## CONTRIBUTION

I2) In 2020, develop a National Organic Waste Strategy, aimed at increasing the recovery of organic waste generated at municipal level by recovering nutrients, organic materials or substrates into the productive process and, thereby, contributing to climate change adaptation and mitigation.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete strategy development with timing and objective.
mechanism_description: A national organics strategy increases diversion and recovery of municipal organic waste into compost or other productive uses, reducing methane from landfills while recovering nutrients and organic matter.

policy_statement: In 2020, develop a nationally agreed Circular Economy Roadmap (2020–2040) setting short-, medium- and long-term measures to 2040 (I1).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Circular Economy N°2 (I2).
verbatim_text: “I1) In 2020, develop a Circular Economy Roadmap for the years 2020 to 2040, agreed at a national level...”
extraction_rationale: Specific policy instrument with clear timeframe.
mechanism_description: Developing a circular economy roadmap sets coordinated measures that drive waste prevention and increased reuse/recycling, reducing emissions from material production and waste management.

policy_statement: In 2020, develop a National Organic Waste Strategy to increase municipal organic waste recovery by returning nutrients/materials/substrates into production, contributing to adaptation and mitigation (I2).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Circular Economy N°2 (I2).
verbatim_text: “I2) In 2020, develop a National Organic Waste Strategy, aimed at increasing the recovery of organic waste generated at municipal level...”
extraction_rationale: Specific strategy instrument with stated mechanism and climate link.
mechanism_description: Increase municipal organics recovery by diverting organic waste to composting/processing so it avoids landfill methane emissions while recovering nutrients/materials for reuse.

policy_statement: By 2022, establish and implement circularity metrics and indicators to monitor progress and identify contributions to mitigation and adaptation (I3).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Circular Economy N°2 (I2).
verbatim_text: “I3) By 2022, establish and implement, metrics and indicators on circularity...”
extraction_rationale: Specific monitoring system commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Establishing circularity metrics and indicators improves measurement and decision-making, enabling better planning and management actions that support climate mitigation and adaptation.

policy_statement: Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4)

## CONTRIBUTION

I4) Chile commits to the sustainable management and recovery of 200,000 hectares of native forests, representing GHG captures of around 0.9 to 1.2 MtCO2eq annually by 2030.

The management plan to be implemented shall consider the following sustainability criteria:

a) Planning of long-term activities to ensure forest conservation (permanence).

b) Sustainable yield parameters guaranteeing that extractions do not exceed forest growth will be applied.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified hectares and sequestration target with implementation criteria listed.
mechanism_description: Implementing sustainable management and recovery of native forests increases and preserves forest carbon stocks, resulting in greater long-term CO2 removal and avoided emissions from forest degradation.

policy_statement: Sustainably manage and recover 200,000 hectares of native forests, delivering around 0.9–1.2 MtCO2eq annual GHG captures by 2030 (I4).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4).
verbatim_text: “I4) Chile commits to the sustainable management and recovery of 200,000 hectares of native forests, representing GHG captures of around 0.9 to 1.2 MtCO2eq annually by 2030.”
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified LULUCF commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Sustainably managing and restoring native forests increases and preserves forest carbon stocks, resulting in greater long-term CO2 sequestration and avoided ecosystem carbon loss.

policy_statement: Ensure I4 forest management planning includes long-term activities to ensure permanence of forest conservation (I4a).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4).
verbatim_text: “a) Planning of long-term activities to ensure forest conservation (permanence).”
extraction_rationale: Listed sustainability criterion under an implementation plan.
mechanism_description: Long-term forest management planning supports permanent forest conservation, helping preserve forest ecosystems and maintain stored carbon over time.

policy_statement: Apply sustainable yield parameters so extractions do not exceed forest growth under I4 (I4b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4).
verbatim_text: “b) Sustainable yield parameters guaranteeing that extractions do not exceed forest growth will be applied.”
extraction_rationale: Specific management rule stated as a criterion to be applied.
mechanism_description: Applying sustainable yield rules caps forest extraction at or below regrowth, preserving forest carbon stocks and avoiding emissions from overharvesting.

policy_statement: Afforest 200,000 hectares, including at least 100,000 hectares of permanent forest cover and at least 70,000 hectares of native species, delivering 3.0–3.4 MtCO2eq annual captures by 2030 (I5).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4).
verbatim_text: “I5) Chile commits to afforest 200,000 hectares, of which at least 100,000 hectares will comprise permanent forest cover, with at least 70,000 hectares of native species... will represent captures of between 3.0 and 3.4 MtCO2eq annually by 2030.”
extraction_rationale: Direct quantified land-based mitigation commitment.
mechanism_description: Planting and maintaining new forest cover increases carbon stored in biomass and soils, resulting in ongoing atmospheric CO2 removal.

policy_statement: By 2030, reduce forestry-sector emissions from degradation and deforestation of native forest by 25% relative to average 2001–2013 emissions (I6).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4).
verbatim_text: “I6) By 2030, reduce emissions in the forestry sector associated with degradation and deforestation of the native forest by 25%, with respect to average emissions in the period 2001-2013.”
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified reduction commitment.
mechanism_description: Protecting native forests by reducing deforestation and degradation preserves existing carbon stocks and avoids land-use change emissions.

policy_statement: By 2025, identify peatland areas and other wetlands in a national inventory (I7).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4).
verbatim_text: “I7) By 2025, peatland areas and any other types of wetland will be identified under a national inventory.”
extraction_rationale: Specific inventory deliverable with deadline.
mechanism_description: Developing a national inventory of peatlands and wetlands improves environmental data and mapping, enabling better planning and management actions that support climate mitigation and adaptation.

policy_statement: By 2030, develop standardized metrics to evaluate wetlands’ (especially peatlands) adaptation/mitigation capacity and implement actions to enhance co-benefits in five pilot sites in protected areas (I8).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4).
verbatim_text: “I8) By 2030, standardized metrics will be developed to evaluate the capacity of wetlands (especially peatlands) for climate change adaptation or mitigation, implementing actions to enhance these co-benefits in five pilot sites...”
extraction_rationale: Explicit metric development and pilot implementation commitment with numeric scope.
mechanism_description: Developing assessment metrics and piloting management actions improves protection/restoration of wetlands (especially peatlands), preserving/enhancing carbon storage and ecosystem resilience for mitigation and adaptation co-benefits.

policy_statement: Contribution to Integration -Crosscutting to ecosystems N°9 (I9).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Integration -Crosscutting to ecosystems N°9 (I9)

## CONTRIBUTION

I9) By 2021, a National Plan for the Restoration of Landscapes will be developed, which will consider restoration of 1,000,000 hectares of ecosystems, prioritizing those facing greatest social, economic and environmental vulnerabilities.”
extraction_rationale: Plan development with deadline and quantified restoration scale.
mechanism_description: Create and implement a landscape restoration plan that restores degraded ecosystems, enhancing ecosystem function and rebuilding carbon stocks to improve climate mitigation and resilience.

policy_statement: By 2021, develop a National Plan for the Restoration of Landscapes considering restoration of 1,000,000 hectares, prioritizing the most vulnerable ecosystems (I9).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration -Crosscutting to ecosystems N°9 (I9).
verbatim_text: “I9) By 2021, a National Plan for the Restoration of Landscapes will be developed, which will consider restoration of 1,000,000 hectares of ecosystems...”
extraction_rationale: Quantified hectares and deadline make it a concrete commitment.
mechanism_description: Developing and implementing a national landscape restoration plan enables restoration of vulnerable ecosystems, increasing ecosystem health and carbon sequestration while improving climate resilience.

policy_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Integration - Ocean N ° 11 (I11)

## CONTRIBUTION

I11) All marine protected areas of Chile created up to 2020 will have a management or administration plan under implementation, taking into account actions for adaptation to climate change.

The following climate actions will be specifically developed:

a) By 2025: 100% of marine protected areas created up to 2020 will hold management or administration plans including actions for adaptation to climate change.

... e) By 2030: Implementation of a methodology for the evaluation of management effectiveness of 100% management or administration plans...”
extraction_rationale: Umbrella commitment with multiple quantified sub-actions and deadlines for MPA management plans.
mechanism_description: Implementing climate-adaptation management plans for marine protected areas strengthens ecosystem management and adaptive capacity, improving resilience to climate impacts.

policy_statement: Establish new protected areas in under-represented marine ecoregions and in coastal wetland ecosystems to supplement the marine network (I10).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “I10) New protected areas will be established in under-represented marine ecoregions... In addition, protected areas will be established in coastal ecosystems for wetlands...”
extraction_rationale: Concrete establishment of protected areas as a policy action.
mechanism_description: Establishing marine and coastal wetland protected areas conserves ecosystems, preventing habitat conversion/degradation and thereby preserving carbon stocks and related ecosystem functions.

policy_statement: By 2030, protect at least 10% of under-represented marine ecoregions (Humboldt, Central Chile, Araucanía and Chiloe) via participatory, science-based marine spatial planning including climate-change criteria (I10a).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “a) By 2030, protect at least 10% of under-represented marine eco-regions (Humboldt, Central Chile, Araucanía and Chiloe), in the framework of a participatory marine spatial planning, based on science and holding criteria to deal with the effects of climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified protection target with deadline and implementation approach.
mechanism_description: Implementing marine spatial planning to designate and protect marine areas preserves marine ecosystems (and associated carbon stocks) and improves resilience to climate change impacts.

policy_statement: By 2025, protect at least 20 coastal wetlands as new protected areas (I10b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “b) By 2025, protect at least 20 coastal wetlands as new protected areas.”
extraction_rationale: Clear numeric target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Designating coastal wetlands as protected areas prevents degradation and conversion, preserving ecosystem carbon and climate-regulating services.

policy_statement: By 2030, protect at least 10 additional coastal wetlands as protected areas (I10c).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “c) By 2030, protect at least 10 additional coastal wetlands as protected areas.”
extraction_rationale: Clear numeric target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Designating coastal wetlands as protected areas conserves these ecosystems, helping preserve stored carbon and prevent degradation-related emissions while maintaining ecological resilience.

policy_statement: Ensure all marine protected areas created up to 2020 have management/administration plans under implementation that include climate change adaptation actions (I11).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “I11) All marine protected areas of Chile created up to 2020 will have a management or administration plan under implementation, taking into account actions for adaptation to climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete requirement for MPA plans and implementation.
mechanism_description: Implementing climate-adaptation actions within marine protected area management plans strengthens protected-area governance and preparedness, increasing institutional capacity to manage climate risks to ecosystems.

policy_statement: By 2030, ensure 100% of MPAs created between 2020 and 2025 have management/administration plans including climate change adaptation actions (I11c).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “c) By 2030: 100% of marine protected areas created between 2020 and 2025 will have management or administration plans including actions for adaptation to climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified planning target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Require climate-adaptation actions in marine protected area management plans to improve ecosystem preparedness and resilience to climate impacts.

policy_statement: By 2025, implement management/administration plans for at least 40% of MPAs created before 2020 through monitoring, control, community involvement, and threat control programs (I11b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “b) By 2025: Management or administration plans of at least 40% of the marine protected areas created before 2020 will be implemented at least through monitoring, control, community involvement and threat control programs.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified implementation share with specified program elements.
mechanism_description: Implementing and enforcing marine protected area management plans reduces ecological threats and improves habitat protection, preserving marine ecosystems and associated carbon storage while supporting climate resilience.

policy_statement: By 2030, implement a methodology to evaluate management effectiveness for 100% of MPA management/administration plans to track mitigation and adaptation targets (I11e).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “e) By 2030: Implementation of a methodology for the evaluation of management effectiveness of 100% management or administration plans of marine protected areas in order to know the progress of targets proposed in said plans for mitigation and adaptation to climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Specific methodological implementation with quantified scope and deadline.
mechanism_description: Implementing a standardized evaluation methodology for MPA management plans improves monitoring and tracking of climate targets, enabling better-informed mitigation and adaptation planning and management actions.

policy_statement: Assess co-benefits of ecosystems in marine protected areas for mitigation and adaptation and implement actions to strengthen these co-benefits (I12).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “I12) The co-benefits of different ecosystems in marine protected areas will be assessed with respect to climate change mitigation and adaptation. Actions will also be implemented to strengthen these co-benefits.”
extraction_rationale: Specific assessment and action commitment.
mechanism_description: Assessing and strengthening ecosystem co-benefits in marine protected areas supports conservation and improved management that preserves or enhances carbon storage and ecosystem resilience for mitigation and adaptation.

policy_statement: By 2025, develop standardized metrics in three MPAs to evaluate their adaptation/mitigation capacities (I12a).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “a) By 2025: Three marine protected areas of Chile will have standardized metrics for the evaluation of their capacities for adaptation or mitigation to climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Developing standardized evaluation metrics for marine protected areas improves climate data and assessment, enabling better adaptation and mitigation planning and management.

policy_statement: By 2030, apply monitoring and verification metrics in at least five MPAs and strengthen co-benefits in their management plans (I12b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “b) By 2030: metrics for monitoring and verification of adaptation or mitigation capacities will be applied in at least five marine protected areas, while strengthening co-benefits in their management plans.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified application commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Implementing monitoring and verification metrics in marine protected areas improves information and accountability, enabling stronger management decisions that support adaptation and mitigation co-benefits.

section_header: 7.3 Contribution in capacity building and strengthening
policy_statement: Contribución en Medios de Implementación Creación y fortalecimiento de capacidades N°1 (IM1).
verbatim_text:
“## 7.3 Contribution in capacity building and strengthening

Contribución en Medios de Implementación Creación y fortalecimiento de capacidades N°1 (IM1)

## CONTRIBUTION

IM1) In 2020, Chile will develop 'Strategy for 'Capacity Development and Climate Empowerment' and will begin its implementation in 2021, with the aim of strengthening the sectoral, national and subnational capacities...”
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with development and implementation start dates and defined objective.
mechanism_description: Create and implement a capacity-development strategy to strengthen institutional and sectoral capabilities, enabling more effective climate planning and action over time.

section_header: 7.3 Contribution in capacity building and strengthening
policy_statement: Develop in 2020 a Capacity Development and Climate Empowerment Strategy and begin implementation in 2021 to strengthen sectoral, national and subnational capacities to meet mitigation and adaptation targets (IM1).
parent_statement: Contribución en Medios de Implementación Creación y fortalecimiento de capacidades N°1 (IM1).
verbatim_text: “IM1) In 2020, Chile will develop 'Strategy for 'Capacity Development and Climate Empowerment' and will begin its implementation in 2021, with the aim of strengthening the sectoral, national and subnational capacities... to achieve national mitigation and adaptation targets.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete strategy and start date for implementation.
mechanism_description: Create and implement a capacity development and climate empowerment strategy to build institutional and sectoral capabilities, improving the ability to plan and execute mitigation and adaptation actions.

section_header: 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer
policy_statement: Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2).
verbatim_text:
“## 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer

Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2)

## CONTRIBUTION

IM2) In 2020, Chile will present its 'Climate Change Technology Transfer and Development Strategy' (EDTTCC) and it will initiate the implementation of this strategy...”
extraction_rationale: Named technology strategy with timing and multiple dated sub-actions.
mechanism_description: Developing and implementing a climate technology development and transfer strategy enables wider adoption of low-carbon and resilience technologies, supporting downstream emissions reductions and adaptation outcomes.

section_header: 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer
policy_statement: In 2020, present and initiate implementation of the Climate Change Technology Transfer and Development Strategy (EDTTCC) to support sustainable, resilient and carbon neutral development by 2050 (IM2).
parent_statement: Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2).
verbatim_text: “IM2) In 2020, Chile will present its 'Climate Change Technology Transfer and Development Strategy' (EDTTCC) and it will initiate the implementation of this strategy... necessary to achieve sustainable, resilient and carbon neutral development by 2050.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete presentation and implementation initiation commitment with year.

section_header: CHILE'S NATIONALLY DETERMINED CONTRIBUTION (NDC) UPDATE 2020 (Foreword message by the President of Chile)
policy_statement: Chile will close all coal-fired power generation plants nationwide no later than 2040, with closures continuing through 2024 and beyond.
verbatim_text: “Their commitment is materializing itself in specific results, such as the closure of coal-fired power generation plants. The process has already started in 3 plants, will continue with 7 more by 2024, and will conclude no later than 2040 with the closure of all coal plants nationwide.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete phase-out action with a clear deadline (2040) and interim milestone (by 2024).
mechanism_description: Requiring coal plants to retire reduces coal electricity generation, leading to lower grid emissions as remaining demand is met by cleaner sources.

policy_statement: Japan has legally positioned net-zero (carbon neutrality) by 2050 as a basic principle through the Act Partially Amending the Act on Promotion of Global Warming Countermeasures (Act No. 54 of 2021).
verbatim_text: By the Act Partially Amending the Act on Promotion of Global Warming Countermeasures (Act No. 54 of 2021) which was enacted in 204th session of the Diet, net-zero by 2050 became a Basic Principle of the Act.
extraction_rationale: States a binding mechanism: a legislative amendment that enshrines net-zero by 2050 as a basic principle.
mechanism_description: Setting a legally grounded net-zero target creates an overarching mandate that steers economy-wide policies and investments toward reducing greenhouse gas emissions to net zero.

section_header: (g) The intention to use voluntary cooperation under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement, if applicable.
policy_statement: Japan will establish and implement the Joint Crediting Mechanism (JCM) to quantitatively evaluate and use international emission reductions/removals toward its NDC, aiming to secure approximately 100 million t-CO2 of accumulated emission reductions and removals by fiscal year 2030 through public-private collaborations, and will appropriately count acquired credits while ensuring environmental integrity and avoiding double-counting.
verbatim_text: Japan will establish and implement the Joint Crediting Mechanism (JCM) in order to quantitatively evaluate contributions of Japan to greenhouse gas emission reductions and removals... and in order to use such contributions to achieve Japan's NDC. By doing so, through public-private collaborations, Japan aims to secure accumulated emission reductions and removals at the level of approximately 100 million t-CO2 by fiscal year 2030. Japan will appropriately count the acquired credits to achieve its NDC. With regards to the JCM which Japan has initiated to establish, Japan secures environmental integrity and the avoidance of double-counting in line with the international rules including the Paris Agreement.
extraction_rationale: Named mechanism with a quantified cumulative target (100 million t-CO2 by FY2030), plus explicit commitments to establish/implement, count credits, and ensure integrity/avoid double counting.
mechanism_description: Create and operate an international crediting framework that funds and verifies overseas emission reductions/removals and counts the resulting credits toward national climate targets under rules that prevent double counting.

policy_statement: Lebanon commits to unconditionally generate 18% of electricity (power demand) and 11% of building-sector heat demand from renewable energy sources by 2030.

extraction_rationale: Quantified renewable energy shares with a deadline (2030).

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas

| Main objectives: • Reducing biomass fuel • Reducing the speed at which fires spread • Increasing the forest's resilience to fire • Ensuring the safety of people and property
extraction_rationale: A named line of action with an objective and multiple concrete measures listed beneath it, fitting a parent policy.
mechanism_description: Implementing fuel-reduction and other structural measures in rural agricultural and forest landscapes reduces wildfire intensity/spread and associated damages, improving ecosystem and community resilience.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Introduce discontinuity bands/patches to prevent rural fires, including (re)afforestation with fire-resistant species, controlling excessive natural regeneration after fire, creating agricultural mosaics, and creating grazing areas (including use of goats) to control spontaneous vegetation.
parent_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text:
• Introduction of discontinuity bands or patches by:
- (re)afforestation with other forest species resistant to the risk of fire
- control of excessive densities of natural regeneration after fire
- introduction of agricultural mosaic
- creation of areas for grazing and use of goats to control spontaneous vegetation
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention measures listed under the named Line of Action #1.
mechanism_description: Implementing firebreaks, fire-resistant planting, thinning, agricultural mosaics, and targeted grazing reduces fuel loads and continuity, decreasing wildfire occurrence/severity and related climate impacts.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Economically valorise biomass by installing intermunicipal systems for collecting and interim storage of biomass residues from forestry and agricultural activities in rural areas.
parent_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text: • Economic valorisation of biomass through the installation of Intermunicipal Systems for the collection and interim storage of biomass left over from forestry and agricultural activities in rural areas
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure/programmatic action under Line of Action #1.
mechanism_description: Creating systems to collect and store forestry and agricultural residues enables their productive use (e.g., energy/materials), reducing emissions from unmanaged residue decay/burning and displacing fossil fuels.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Convert and reconfigure electricity transmission/distribution structures that cross forest areas (e.g., burying cables or changing routes) to reduce rural fire risk.
parent_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.

extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (grid infrastructure changes) under the Line of Action #1 list of measures.
mechanism_description: Hardening, undergrounding, or rerouting power lines in forested corridors reduces utility-related ignition sources, lowering wildfire risk and improving climate resilience.

extraction_rationale: Concrete intervention listed as a measure under Line of Action #1.
