verbatim_text: Three main policies have been established to realize the targets, as follows: - Mandates: This refers to renewable energy mandates for new buildings.
extraction_rationale: Explicit mandate requirement tied to new buildings under NREAP.
mechanism_description: Mandating renewable energy in new buildings increases renewable energy supply for building energy needs, displacing fossil energy and lowering GHG emissions.

policy_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text:
## Adaptation interventions

Two strategic interventions were considered to ensure a stable equilibrium between future water supply and demand under climate change.
extraction_rationale: Umbrella intervention package explicitly introducing multiple specific sub-policies described in subsequent bullets.
mechanism_description: Reduce per-capita water use and system leakage to lower overall demand and losses, helping maintain a stable water supply-demand balance under climate change.

policy_statement: Reduce per capita municipal water demand by 33% from 183 to 122 m3/person/year by 2035, with a policy start year of 2016, using demand-side management policies (awareness, strengthened block tariffs, and new legislation).
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: The first intervention aims to achieve a 33% reduction in per capita municipal water demand, from the current level of  183 to 122 m 3 per person per year by 2035, with a policy start year of  2016. To achieve this target, three complementary  demand  side  management  policies would be advanced, as outlined in the bullets below.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target, timeframe, and implementation approach clearly specified as a planned intervention.
mechanism_description: Demand-side water management measures (pricing, regulations, and awareness) reduce per-capita municipal water use.

policy_statement: Implement awareness raising campaigns and government incentives to promote municipal water efficiency and conservation.
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: § Awareness raising : This would involve social campaigns and government incentives to emphasize the importance of water efficiency and conservation among the general population.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention mechanism (social campaigns and incentives) presented as part of the demand-side package.
mechanism_description: Awareness campaigns and incentives motivate residents and organizations to adopt water-saving behaviors and measures, reducing overall per-capita water consumption.

policy_statement: Strengthen block water tariffs for non-household users under EWA's tariff schedule effective 1 March 2016.
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: § Strengthened  block  tariffs: Up  until  recently  water tariffs were heavily subsidized and did not recover  minimum  production  and  distribution costs.  Effective  1  March  2016,  water  tariffs  for commercial,  industrial,  touristic,  and  other  nonhousehold users are being gradually strengthened in accordance with the new tariff schedule established by EWA.
extraction_rationale: Explicit tariff reform with an effective date and defined target groups; constitutes a policy lever.
mechanism_description: Increasing block water tariffs for commercial and industrial users raises the cost of high consumption, which incentivizes conservation and reduces overall water demand (and the energy/emissions tied to water supply operations).

policy_statement: Develop new legislation to promote water use efficiency and conservation by strengthening building codes and promoting water-saving devices (with retrofits for existing buildings).
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: § New legislation: To promote water use efficiency and conservation, new legislation would be needed to strengthen existing building codes, promote water saving  devices  such  as  low-flow  shower  heads, faucet  aerators,  automatic  shut-off   nozzles  and others for new buildings while existing buildings would  be  retrofitted.
extraction_rationale: Concrete proposed legislative action plus enumerated device standards/retrofit scope.
mechanism_description: Strengthening codes and retrofitting/installing water-saving devices reduces water use in buildings, lowering overall per-capita water demand (and related emissions from water treatment and heating).

policy_statement: Reduce leakage in the municipal water distribution network to achieve a 22% reduction in per capita municipal water demand from 183 to 142 m3/person/year by 2035, with a policy start year of 2016 (implemented starting 2020 per phase-in schedule).
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: The  second  intervention  aims  to  reduce  leakage  in the  water  distribution  network. ... Through investments in detection and repair, this second intervention aims to achieve a 22% reduction in per capita municipal water demand, from the current level of 183 to 142 m 3 per person per year  by  2035,  with  a  policy  start  year  of 2016.
extraction_rationale: Quantified leakage-related demand reduction target and deadline; framed as an intervention with investments.
mechanism_description: Investing in leak detection and repair reduces distribution-system water losses, which lowers overall municipal water demand per person.

policy_statement: Strengthen EWA's leak detection programme to conduct more frequent surveys and expand the use of leak detection technologies.
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: § Leak detection: This would involve the strengthening of  the  leak  detection  programme  within  EWA's Leakage Detection and Control Group to perform more frequent leak detection surveys and expand the  use  of   leak  detection  technologies  (e.g.,  leak noise correlators, thermography free-floating inline  leak  detectors,  etc).
extraction_rationale: Specific programmatic action to strengthen leak detection with examples of technologies.
mechanism_description: More frequent leak surveys and better detection technology find and fix leaks sooner, reducing water losses and the energy/emissions tied to producing and pumping wasted water.

policy_statement: Implement leak control measures including Active Leakage Control, Pressure Management, Network Rehabilitation, and improved speed and quality of repair.
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: § Leak control: This  would  involve  several  available currently  technologies  including  Active  Leakage Control, Pressure Management, Networks Rehabilitations  and  Speed  &  Quality  of   Repair (Lambert, 2003).
extraction_rationale: Clearly specified set of operational measures to control leakage; part of the leakage reduction intervention.
mechanism_description: Implementing leak detection, pressure management, network rehabilitation, and improved repairs reduces leakage in water distribution systems, lowering overall water losses.

policy_statement: The Government of Armenia approved the Nationally Determined Contributions (2021-2030) with an unconditional target to reduce emissions by 40% below 1990 levels by 2030.
verbatim_text: On  April  22,  2021  the  Government  of Armenia  approved  Armenia's  Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) for a tenyear  implementation  period  (2021-2030), setting  the  new  unconditional  mitigation target of 40% reduction below 1990 emissions levels, to be achieved in 2030.
extraction_rationale: Contains a quantified national mitigation target with a deadline (40% below 1990 by 2030) approved by government.
mechanism_description: Adopting an economy-wide emissions reduction target creates a policy signal and framework that guides actions across sectors to reduce greenhouse gas emissions over time.

policy_statement: Armenia plans to construct about 1000 MW of solar power plants (including autonomous ones) by 2030 to support reaching solar generation targets.
verbatim_text: According  to  'The  Republic  of  Armenia Energy Sector Development Strategic Program (till 2040)", 2021, it is planned to increase the share of solar energy generation  in  total  to  at  least  15%  or  1.8 billion  kWh  by  2030.  To  this  end,  solar power  plants  with  the  capacity  of  about 1000 MW, including autonomous ones, are planned to be constructed.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure plan with quantified capacity (~1000 MW) and timeline (by 2030).
mechanism_description: Build new solar PV power plants to increase renewable electricity supply and displace fossil-fuel generation, lowering power-sector greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Armenia plans to commission the Masrik-1 utility-scale solar PV plant (55 MW peak) by 2022 and hold tenders for additional solar PV plants totaling about 520 MW (including two 200 MW plants).
verbatim_text: It is planned by 2022 to commission Masrik-1 utility scale solar PV with a peak capacity  of  55  MW.  Further,  new  tenders will be held for the construction of another 7 solar PVs with a total installed capacity of about 520 MW, whereby the capacity of two of these plants will be 200 MW each.
extraction_rationale: Specific planned actions with clear capacities and a commissioning date plus procurement/tenders.
mechanism_description: Build and procure utility-scale solar PV so renewable electricity replaces fossil generation on the grid, cutting emissions.

policy_statement: Armenia aims to reduce electricity distribution losses to 6.4% in 2027 under Electric Networks of Armenia's 2016-2027 investment program.
verbatim_text: Reduction of distribution losses to 6.4% in 2027 (ENA 2016-2027 Investment program).
extraction_rationale: Quantified performance target (losses to 6.4%) with a deadline under an investment program.
mechanism_description: Utility investments that cut distribution losses mean less electricity needs to be generated for the same delivered consumption, reducing power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Armenia plans to reach total small hydropower plant (SHPP) capacity of 430 MW by 2023, supported by the Hydro Energy Development Concept and 15-year Power Purchase Agreements under the Energy Law.
verbatim_text:
In 2016 the GoA approved the Hydro Energy Development Concept of the RA, which envisages commissioning of new SHPPs. The Energy Law supports the development of SHPPs through the Power Purchasing Agreement for the 15- year period.

Quantitative goal: To reach the total capacity of SHPPs of 430 MWby 2023.
extraction_rationale: Clear policy support instrument (15-year PPA) and quantified capacity target with timeline.
mechanism_description: Developing small hydropower capacity through supportive agreements increases renewable electricity supply, displacing fossil fuel generation and lowering grid emissions.

policy_statement: Under the Strategy and Action Plan, Armenia will construct medium utility-scale solar PV power plants totaling 175 MW by end-2024, including Masrik-1 (55 MW) in 2022 and additional 120 MW by end-2024.
verbatim_text: According to the Strategy and Action Plan, medium utility-scale solar power plants with total capa- city of 175 MWwill be constructed by the end of 2024. It includes: • PV 'Masrik-1', with a peak capa- city of 55 MW(about 110 GWh of annually produced electricity) in 2022; • Additional solar PVs with total capacity of 120 MWwill be cons- tructed by the end of 2024 (about 192 GWh of annually produced electricity).
extraction_rationale: Specific construction commitment with quantified capacities and deadlines tied to named Strategy/Action Plan.
mechanism_description: Build utility-scale solar PV plants to generate renewable electricity that replaces fossil electricity generation, lowering greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Under the Strategy and Action Plan, Armenia envisages construction of two 200 MW solar PV plants (400 MW total) in 2023 and 2024, including the 200 MW 'Ayg-1' plant.
verbatim_text:
According to the Strategy and Action Plan construction of 2 Solar PVs with the capacity of 200MW each is envisaged in 2023 and 2024.

Request for Qualification was announced in 2020 seeking private developers for a pro- ject to design, finance, build, own, and operate the first of considered 2 plants, which will be grid-connected 200MW solar PV power plant called 'Ayg-1'.
extraction_rationale: Planned utility-scale build-out with quantified capacities, years, and a named project with procurement step.
mechanism_description: Deploying grid-connected solar PV adds renewable generation that substitutes for fossil electricity generation, lowering associated emissions.

policy_statement: Armenia will support installation of solar PV plants up to 5 MW through tariff policy and power purchase agreements, targeting 315 MW of such PV capacity by 2030.
verbatim_text:
According to the Strategy and Action Plan, the construction of solar PV power plants with an installed capacity of up to5MW (inclusively) will be supported through tariff policy and power purchase agreement.

The quantitative goal is to support the installation of the total of 315 MWof solar PV power plants with an installed capacity of up to5MW (inclusively) by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Explicit support mechanism (tariff policy and PPA) plus a quantified capacity goal with deadline.
mechanism_description: Support for installing solar PV increases renewable electricity generation, which displaces fossil electricity generation and lowers associated greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: AFOLU-related policies and measures supporting REDD+ and forest governance include a peatland license moratorium, mandatory sustainable forest management certification, strategic environmental assessments (SEAs) via landscape approach, environmental economic instruments, and establishment of the Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH).
verbatim_text: This successful endeavour has been supported by a number of PaMs including: (i) Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019 on termination of new license issuance on peatland, (ii) implementation of mandatory certification for sustainable forest management, (iii) Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on strategic environmental assessments (SEAs) through landscape approach aiming at securing food, water, and energy security based on sound ecosystem management, (iv) Government Regulation No. 46/2017 on Environmental Economic Instrument, and (v) Presidential Regulation No. 77/2018 on the establishment of Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH).
extraction_rationale: Introduces a list of specific named policies/measures; functions as an umbrella set of PaMs.
mechanism_description: Strengthening forest/peatland governance and limiting new land-use licenses reduces deforestation and peatland degradation, thereby avoiding land-use emissions and preserving ecosystem carbon stocks.

policy_statement: Terminate issuance of new licenses on peatland under Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019.
parent_statement: AFOLU-related policies and measures supporting REDD+ and forest governance include a peatland license moratorium, mandatory sustainable forest management certification, strategic environmental assessments (SEAs) via landscape approach, environmental economic instruments, and establishment of the Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH).
verbatim_text: This successful endeavour has been supported by a number of PaMs including: (i) Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019 on termination of new license issuance on peatland.
extraction_rationale: Specific binding government instruction described as a policy measure.
mechanism_description: Stopping new peatland licenses prevents peatland conversion and drainage, preserving peat carbon stocks and avoiding associated land-use emissions.

policy_statement: Implement mandatory certification for sustainable forest management.
parent_statement: AFOLU-related policies and measures supporting REDD+ and forest governance include a peatland license moratorium, mandatory sustainable forest management certification, strategic environmental assessments (SEAs) via landscape approach, environmental economic instruments, and establishment of the Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH).
verbatim_text: This successful endeavour has been supported by a number of PaMs including: (ii) implementation of mandatory certification for sustainable forest management.
extraction_rationale: Explicit regulatory/standards intervention.
mechanism_description: A mandatory sustainable forest management certification requirement improves forest practices, reducing forest carbon losses and preserving carbon stored in forests.

policy_statement: Apply Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on Strategic Environmental Assessments (SEAs) using a landscape approach to secure food, water, and energy security based on sound ecosystem management.
parent_statement: AFOLU-related policies and measures supporting REDD+ and forest governance include a peatland license moratorium, mandatory sustainable forest management certification, strategic environmental assessments (SEAs) via landscape approach, environmental economic instruments, and establishment of the Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH).
verbatim_text: This successful endeavour has been supported by a number of PaMs including: (iii) Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on strategic environmental assessments (SEAs) through landscape approach aiming at securing food, water, and energy security based on sound ecosystem management.
extraction_rationale: Named regulation with defined purpose and mechanism (SEA/landscape approach).
mechanism_description: Implementing strategic environmental assessments with a landscape approach integrates ecosystem-based considerations into planning, improving decision-making to protect food, water, and energy security under climate stress.

policy_statement: Implement Government Regulation No. 46/2017 on Environmental Economic Instruments.
parent_statement: AFOLU-related policies and measures supporting REDD+ and forest governance include a peatland license moratorium, mandatory sustainable forest management certification, strategic environmental assessments (SEAs) via landscape approach, environmental economic instruments, and establishment of the Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH).
verbatim_text: This successful endeavour has been supported by a number of PaMs including: (iv) Government Regulation No. 46/2017 on Environmental Economic Instrument.
extraction_rationale: Named regulation establishing economic instruments for environmental management.
mechanism_description: Implementing environmental economic instruments (e.g., taxes/fees/incentives) shifts economy-wide decisions toward lower-emission activities, reducing overall greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Establish the Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH) via Presidential Regulation No. 77/2018.
parent_statement: AFOLU-related policies and measures supporting REDD+ and forest governance include a peatland license moratorium, mandatory sustainable forest management certification, strategic environmental assessments (SEAs) via landscape approach, environmental economic instruments, and establishment of the Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH).
verbatim_text: This successful endeavour has been supported by a number of PaMs including: (v) Presidential Regulation No. 77/2018 on the establishment of Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH).
extraction_rationale: Creates an institution to manage environmental/climate-related funds.
mechanism_description: Establishing a dedicated environmental fund management agency mobilizes and administers climate-related finance, enabling and scaling implementation of mitigation and adaptation measures.

policy_statement: In forestry and other land uses, Indonesia identifies actions required to reduce emissions and achieve net sink (2050 under CPOS; 2030 under LCCP), including reducing deforestation/peat emissions, increasing sequestration via reduced degradation and enhanced regeneration, peatland restoration, forest restoration/sink enhancement, sustainable forest management, and maximizing use of unproductive lands.
verbatim_text: Efforts to significantly reduce the emission of this sector and turn it into net sink by 2050 under CPOS and by 2030 under LCCP depends primarily on the success of the following actions: (i) reducing emission from deforestation and peatland (peat decomposition and peat fire); (ii) increasing the capacity of natural forest in sequestering carbon (by reducing degradation and enhancing the regeneration); (iii) restoring peatland, (iv) implementing forest restoration (enrichment planting/sink enhancement), (v) adopting sustainable forest management practices; and (vi) maximizing the use of unproductive lands (idle lands) for the establishment of forest and agriculture plantations.
extraction_rationale: Clear umbrella list of required actions with CPOS/LCCP timelines.
mechanism_description: Protecting and restoring forests and peatlands reduces land-use change emissions and increases carbon sequestration by preserving and enhancing ecosystem carbon stocks.

policy_statement: Reduce emissions from deforestation and peatland (peat decomposition and peat fire).
parent_statement: In forestry and other land uses, Indonesia identifies actions required to reduce emissions and achieve net sink (2050 under CPOS; 2030 under LCCP), including reducing deforestation/peat emissions, increasing sequestration via reduced degradation and enhanced regeneration, peatland restoration, forest restoration/sink enhancement, sustainable forest management, and maximizing use of unproductive lands.
verbatim_text: Efforts to significantly reduce the emission of this sector and turn it into net sink by 2050 under CPOS and by 2030 under LCCP depends primarily on the success of the following actions: (i) reducing emission from deforestation and peatland (peat decomposition and peat fire).
extraction_rationale: Specific mitigation action item.
mechanism_description: Protecting forests and peatlands reduces land-use change and peat oxidation/fire, thereby avoiding large releases of stored carbon and helping maintain or increase the landscape carbon sink.

policy_statement: Increase natural forest carbon sequestration capacity by reducing degradation and enhancing regeneration.
parent_statement: In forestry and other land uses, Indonesia identifies actions required to reduce emissions and achieve net sink (2050 under CPOS; 2030 under LCCP), including reducing deforestation/peat emissions, increasing sequestration via reduced degradation and enhanced regeneration, peatland restoration, forest restoration/sink enhancement, sustainable forest management, and maximizing use of unproductive lands.
verbatim_text: Efforts to significantly reduce the emission of this sector and turn it into net sink by 2050 under CPOS and by 2030 under LCCP depends primarily on the success of the following actions: (ii) increasing the capacity of natural forest in sequestering carbon (by reducing degradation and enhancing the regeneration).
extraction_rationale: Specific mitigation action item.
mechanism_description: Protecting and regenerating natural forests increases carbon stored in biomass and soils, reducing net greenhouse gas emissions by strengthening the land carbon sink.

policy_statement: Restore peatland.
parent_statement: In forestry and other land uses, Indonesia identifies actions required to reduce emissions and achieve net sink (2050 under CPOS; 2030 under LCCP), including reducing deforestation/peat emissions, increasing sequestration via reduced degradation and enhanced regeneration, peatland restoration, forest restoration/sink enhancement, sustainable forest management, and maximizing use of unproductive lands.
verbatim_text: Efforts to significantly reduce the emission of this sector and turn it into net sink by 2050 under CPOS and by 2030 under LCCP depends primarily on the success of the following actions: (iii) restoring peatland.
extraction_rationale: Specific mitigation action item.
mechanism_description: Restoring peatlands re-wets and rehabilitates degraded peat soils, avoiding large CO2 emissions from oxidation/fires and preserving or increasing long-term carbon storage.

policy_statement: Implement forest restoration (enrichment planting/sink enhancement).
parent_statement: In forestry and other land uses, Indonesia identifies actions required to reduce emissions and achieve net sink (2050 under CPOS; 2030 under LCCP), including reducing deforestation/peat emissions, increasing sequestration via reduced degradation and enhanced regeneration, peatland restoration, forest restoration/sink enhancement, sustainable forest management, and maximizing use of unproductive lands.
verbatim_text: Efforts to significantly reduce the emission of this sector and turn it into net sink by 2050 under CPOS and by 2030 under LCCP depends primarily on the success of the following actions: (iv) implementing forest restoration (enrichment planting/sink enhancement).
extraction_rationale: Specific mitigation action item.
mechanism_description: Restoring forests through enrichment planting increases ecosystem carbon uptake and storage, enhancing net sequestration and reducing net emissions from the land-use sector.

policy_statement: Adopt sustainable forest management practices.
parent_statement: In forestry and other land uses, Indonesia identifies actions required to reduce emissions and achieve net sink (2050 under CPOS; 2030 under LCCP), including reducing deforestation/peat emissions, increasing sequestration via reduced degradation and enhanced regeneration, peatland restoration, forest restoration/sink enhancement, sustainable forest management, and maximizing use of unproductive lands.
verbatim_text: Efforts to significantly reduce the emission of this sector and turn it into net sink by 2050 under CPOS and by 2030 under LCCP depends primarily on the success of the following actions: (v) adopting sustainable forest management practices.
extraction_rationale: Specific mitigation action item.
mechanism_description: Implementing sustainable forest management increases maintained and growing forest carbon stocks while reducing emissions from degradation and unsustainable harvesting, improving net sequestration.

policy_statement: Maximize the use of unproductive (idle) lands for establishing forest and agriculture plantations.
parent_statement: In forestry and other land uses, Indonesia identifies actions required to reduce emissions and achieve net sink (2050 under CPOS; 2030 under LCCP), including reducing deforestation/peat emissions, increasing sequestration via reduced degradation and enhanced regeneration, peatland restoration, forest restoration/sink enhancement, sustainable forest management, and maximizing use of unproductive lands.
verbatim_text: Efforts to significantly reduce the emission of this sector and turn it into net sink by 2050 under CPOS and by 2030 under LCCP depends primarily on the success of the following actions: (vi) maximizing the use of unproductive lands (idle lands) for the establishment of forest and agriculture plantations.
extraction_rationale: Specific mitigation action item.
mechanism_description: Convert idle land to forest/agricultural plantations to increase biomass and soil carbon storage, resulting in net carbon sequestration.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – a. Avoiding Deforestation
policy_statement: To avoid further conversion of forest, Indonesia establishes key regulations including Government Regulation No. 104/2015, Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019, and Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on SEAs.
verbatim_text:
To avoid further conversion of forest, Indonesia has issued a number of regulations. Some of key regulations are:

1. Government Regulation No.104/2015 on the changes of forest land use and function...
2. Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019 on Termination of New Permit and Improvement of Primary Natural Forest and Peatland Governance; and
3. Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on strategic environmental assessments (SEAs)...
extraction_rationale: Umbrella list of ‘key regulations’ explicitly issued to avoid forest conversion.
mechanism_description: Restricting permits and regulating forest land-use changes prevents forest and peatland conversion, thereby avoiding deforestation-related emissions and preserving ecosystem carbon stocks.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – a. Avoiding Deforestation
policy_statement: Ban conversion of forested land (productive production forest) in forest areas for APL, except where non-forested lands in production forest are not available, under Government Regulation No. 104/2015.
parent_statement: To avoid further conversion of forest, Indonesia establishes key regulations including Government Regulation No. 104/2015, Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019, and Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on SEAs.
verbatim_text: Government Regulation No.104/2015 on the changes of forest land use and function, which ban the conversion of forested land (productive production forest) in forest area for APL, except in the province where the non-forested lands in the production forest are not available;
extraction_rationale: Explicit regulatory ban with conditional exception.
mechanism_description: Banning conversion of forested land prevents deforestation, preserving ecosystem functions and stored carbon and thereby avoiding emissions from land-use change.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – a. Avoiding Deforestation
policy_statement: Terminate new permits and improve primary natural forest and peatland governance under Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019.
parent_statement: To avoid further conversion of forest, Indonesia establishes key regulations including Government Regulation No. 104/2015, Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019, and Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on SEAs.
verbatim_text: Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019 on Termination of New Permit and Improvement of Primary Natural Forest and Peatland Governance; and
extraction_rationale: Named presidential instruction with clear governance/permit objective.
mechanism_description: Ending new permits and improving governance for primary forests and peatlands reduces conversion and degradation, preserving carbon stocks and avoiding land-use change emissions.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – a. Avoiding Deforestation
policy_statement: Use Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on SEAs to guide integrated, comprehensive, spatially explicit land-use planning aimed at food, water, and energy security based on ecosystem management.
parent_statement: To avoid further conversion of forest, Indonesia establishes key regulations including Government Regulation No. 104/2015, Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019, and Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on SEAs.
verbatim_text: Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on strategic environmental assessments (SEAs), which provide guidance on the integrated, comprehensive, spatially explicit land use planning at national and subnational level aiming at food, water, and energy security based on sound ecosystem management.
extraction_rationale: Explicit planning guidance and purpose.
mechanism_description: Mandating SEA-guided, ecosystem-based spatial planning improves decision-making and safeguards ecosystems, strengthening long-term resilience and integrated resource security.

policy_statement: Indonesia commits to an updated First NDC target of 29% (unconditional) and up to 41% (conditional) GHG reduction versus BAU by 2030.
verbatim_text: In the First NDC, Indonesia has unconditional target of 29% and conditional target up to 41% compared to business as usual in 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified national mitigation targets with a 2030 timeline.
mechanism_description: Sets an economy-wide emissions reduction target that drives policies and investments across sectors to reduce greenhouse gas emissions over time.

policy_statement: Indonesia sets an adaptation pathways goal to reduce climate change impacts on national GDP loss by 3.45% in 2050 by increasing resilience in food, water, energy, and environmental health across economic, social/livelihood, and ecosystem/landscape resilience areas.
verbatim_text: The LTS-LCCR 2050 sets the goal of adaptation pathways to reduce the impact of climate change on national GDP loss by 3.45% in 2050, through increasing resilience in four basic necessities (food, water, energy, and environmental health), with three target areas of resilience (economy, social and livelihood, ecosystem and landscape).
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified adaptation objective with timeframe and defined resilience domains.
mechanism_description: Establishing an adaptation pathways framework guides coordinated resilience actions across key sectors, strengthening institutional capacity to reduce climate impact risks and associated economic losses.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – a. Avoiding Deforestation
policy_statement: In RPJMN 2020–2024, Indonesia expands forest areas that need to be protected based on ecosystem services from 51.8 Mha to 65.3 Mha (inside and outside forest areas).
verbatim_text: In the National Medium-Term Development Plan (RPJMN) 2020-2024, forest area that need to be protected based on the value of ecosystem services of the forest, are expanded from 51.8 Mha to 65.3 Mha, which are located inside and outside forest area.
extraction_rationale: Quantified protection-area expansion target embedded in a national plan.
mechanism_description: Protecting a larger area of forests and other natural lands preserves existing carbon stocks and ecosystem services by preventing land conversion and degradation.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – c. Protection and Restoration of Peatland
policy_statement: Indonesia sets peatland restoration targets: CPOS 1.03 Mha by 2030 and 1.7 Mha by 2050; LCCP 2.7 Mha by 2030 and 4.2 Mha by 2050.
verbatim_text: For peatland restoration, CPOS figures the area of degraded peatland being restored should reach 1.03 Mha by 2030 and 1.7 Mha by 2050. While under LCCP, the target will be increased to 2.7 Mha by 2030 and 4.2 Mha by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Quantified restoration targets with timelines under two scenarios.
mechanism_description: Restoring degraded peatlands re-wets and rehabilitates peat ecosystems, reducing peat decomposition and fire-related emissions while preserving and rebuilding carbon stocks.

section_header: 4.2.1.3. Achieving the Goals – b. Integrated Farming System
policy_statement: Develop integrated oil palm–livestock farming with targets: CPOS 11.70% (1.68 Mha) by 2030 and 22.72% (3.25 Mha) by 2050; LCCP 21.11% (3.02 Mha) by 2030 and 34.33% (4.91 Mha) by 2050.
verbatim_text: Under CPOS, integrated farming of oil palm-livestock is targeted to reach 11.70% (1.68 Mha) in 2030 and 22.72% (3.25 Mha) in 2050. Under LCCP, the target is increased to 21.11% (3.02 Mha) in 2030 and to 34.33% (  4.91 Mha) in 2050.
extraction_rationale: Quantified adoption targets for a specific integrated farming mitigation approach.
mechanism_description: Expand integrated oil palm–livestock systems to increase productivity and nutrient cycling on existing land, reducing the emissions intensity of agricultural production and pressure for land conversion.

section_header: 3.3. Role of Ocean and Integrated Terrestrial and Marine Ecosystem Management and Conservation
policy_statement: Implement COVID-19 recovery coastal/marine actions including 15,000 hectares of mangrove planting and 50 hectares of coral reef restoration in Bali in 2020.
verbatim_text: Coastal and marine based activities have also been part of COVID-19 recovery programme, including 15 thousands hectare mangrove planting and 50 hectare coral reef restoration in marine tourism area in Bali in 2020.
extraction_rationale: Specific implemented actions with quantified areas and date, framed as part of a government recovery programme.
mechanism_description: Restore mangroves and coral reefs to increase coastal carbon sequestration/storage while enhancing protection from storms and erosion.

policy_statement: Italy implements programmes and initiatives in agriculture and forestry to increase and better manage forest areas/woodlands, reclaim abandoned territories, and protect areas at instability or desertification risk to increase carbon sinks.

section_header: AGREEMENT PROTOCOL OF THE CONFERENCE OF THE PRESIDENTS OF REGIONAL GOVERNMENTS AND OF AUTONOMOUS PROVINCES FOR THE COORDINATION OF POLICIES AIMED AT REDUCING EMISSIONS OF GREENHOUSE GASES INTO THE ATMOSPHERE
policy_statement: Agreement Protocol (Turin, 5 June 2001) by the Conference of Presidents of Regional Governments and Autonomous Provinces coordinates policies aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions, including preparing an Environmental Energy Plan by end-2002.
verbatim_text:
AGREEMENT PROTOCOL OF THE CONFERENCE OF THE PRESIDENTS OF REGIONAL GOVERNMENTS AND OF AUTONOMOUS PROVINCES FOR THE COORDINATION OF POLICIES AIMED AT REDUCING EMISSIONS OF GREENHOUSE GASES INTO THE ATMOSPHERE
...
undertake to guarantee:
...
that an Environmental Energy Plan shall be drawn up by the end of the year 2002, based on the individual energy balances...
extraction_rationale: Named protocol containing multiple explicit commitments ('undertake to guarantee') including a dated deliverable and several listed action areas.
mechanism_description: Creates a coordinated environmental/energy planning framework across regional governments to guide and enable future actions that reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: AGREEMENT PROTOCOL OF THE CONFERENCE OF THE PRESIDENTS OF REGIONAL GOVERNMENTS AND OF AUTONOMOUS PROVINCES FOR THE COORDINATION OF POLICIES AIMED AT REDUCING EMISSIONS OF GREENHOUSE GASES INTO THE ATMOSPHERE
policy_statement: Regions and Autonomous Provinces commit to orient their policies as far as possible toward reducing greenhouse gases and to coordinate central-state and local initiatives/financing with sustainability as a priority objective.
parent_statement: Agreement Protocol (Turin, 5 June 2001) by the Conference of Presidents of Regional Governments and Autonomous Provinces coordinates policies aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions, including preparing an Environmental Energy Plan by end-2002.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment language by subnational governments to align policies and coordinate financing toward GHG reduction/sustainability.
mechanism_description: Coordinate and prioritize government policies and funding around sustainability goals to drive economy-wide greenhouse-gas emissions reductions.

section_header: AGREEMENT PROTOCOL OF THE CONFERENCE OF THE PRESIDENTS OF REGIONAL GOVERNMENTS AND OF AUTONOMOUS PROVINCES FOR THE COORDINATION OF POLICIES AIMED AT REDUCING EMISSIONS OF GREENHOUSE GASES INTO THE ATMOSPHERE
policy_statement: Regions and Autonomous Provinces commit to formulate optimal GHG reduction strategies within Plans for the Safeguarding and Reclamation of Air Quality.
parent_statement: Agreement Protocol (Turin, 5 June 2001) by the Conference of Presidents of Regional Governments and Autonomous Provinces coordinates policies aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions, including preparing an Environmental Energy Plan by end-2002.
verbatim_text:
undertake to guarantee:

that optimal strategies for the reduction of greenhouse gases shall be formulated as part of the Plans for the Safeguarding and Reclamation of Air Quality;
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning commitment to embed GHG strategies in a specific planning instrument (air quality plans).
mechanism_description: Integrating greenhouse-gas reduction strategies into formal air-quality plans strengthens institutional planning and coordination capacity to implement emissions-reduction actions.

section_header: AGREEMENT PROTOCOL OF THE CONFERENCE OF THE PRESIDENTS OF REGIONAL GOVERNMENTS AND OF AUTONOMOUS PROVINCES FOR THE COORDINATION OF POLICIES AIMED AT REDUCING EMISSIONS OF GREENHOUSE GASES INTO THE ATMOSPHERE
policy_statement: An Environmental Energy Plan will be drawn up by end of 2002, based on energy balances and favoring renewable sources and technological innovation.

that an Environmental Energy Plan shall be drawn up by the end of the year 2002, based on the individual energy balances and favouring:

renewable sources and technological innovation;
extraction_rationale: Dated plan commitment with specified priority content (renewables, innovation).
mechanism_description: Develop an energy plan based on energy balance data to prioritize renewable energy and innovation, thereby shifting the energy mix away from fossil generation and reducing emissions.

section_header: AGREEMENT PROTOCOL OF THE CONFERENCE OF THE PRESIDENTS OF REGIONAL GOVERNMENTS AND OF AUTONOMOUS PROVINCES FOR THE COORDINATION OF POLICIES AIMED AT REDUCING EMISSIONS OF GREENHOUSE GASES INTO THE ATMOSPHERE
policy_statement: The Environmental Energy Plan will include rationalisation of electricity production and rationalisation of energy consumption, including potential introduction of energy certification for the civil sector.

extraction_rationale: Specifies concrete elements to be included in the plan; includes an explicit mechanism (energy certification) though framed as possible ('eventually').
mechanism_description: Implementing energy certification for buildings provides performance information that encourages efficiency upgrades and reduced building energy use, lowering associated emissions.

section_header: AGREEMENT PROTOCOL OF THE CONFERENCE OF THE PRESIDENTS OF REGIONAL GOVERNMENTS AND OF AUTONOMOUS PROVINCES FOR THE COORDINATION OF POLICIES AIMED AT REDUCING EMISSIONS OF GREENHOUSE GASES INTO THE ATMOSPHERE
policy_statement: The Environmental Energy Plan will link planning sectors for sustainability and promote eco-efficiency in the production sector, technological innovation support policies, and macroeconomic tools (taxation, fees, incentives), including international cooperation.
parent_statement: Agreement Protocol (Turin, 5 June 2001) by the Conference of Presidents of Regional Governments and Autonomous Provinces coordinates policies aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions, including preparing an Environmental Energy Plan by end-2002.
extraction_rationale: List of specified plan priorities and instruments (innovation support, fiscal tools, eco-efficiency, international cooperation).
mechanism_description: Create an integrated energy/environment plan and apply innovation support and tax/fee/incentive tools to improve production eco-efficiency, leading to reduced economy-wide greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: 4.2.1 Measures implemented in the electric sector, including renewable sources
policy_statement: Enel voluntary agreement aims to reduce emissions by 22 MtCO2 by 2006 compared to 1990 through power plant efficiency/CC conversion, renewables obligation, distribution leak reductions, and DSM measures.
verbatim_text:
The voluntary agreement with Enel has the objective of reducing emissions of 22 MtCO2 by 2006, compared to the emissions of 1990, by:
- increasing the efficiency of existing plants and converting 15.000 MW to combined-cycle plants...
- increasing the use of renewable sources (2% obligation)...
- reducing leaks from distribution networks...
- DSM measures: (Demand Side Management)...
extraction_rationale: Named agreement with quantified target and multiple explicit sub-actions listed.
mechanism_description: A voluntary utility agreement drives a mix of generation efficiency upgrades, renewable energy deployment, grid loss reduction, and demand-side management, resulting in lower fossil electricity generation and reduced power-sector emissions.

section_header: 4.2.1 Measures implemented in the electric sector, including renewable sources
policy_statement: Increase efficiency of existing plants and convert 15,000 MW to combined-cycle plants under the Enel voluntary agreement to cut CO2 emissions.
parent_statement: Enel voluntary agreement aims to reduce emissions by 22 MtCO2 by 2006 compared to 1990 through power plant efficiency/CC conversion, renewables obligation, distribution leak reductions, and DSM measures.
verbatim_text: - increasing the efficiency of existing plants and converting 15.000 MW to combined-cycle plants; of the reduced 12 MtCO2, only 10 MtCO2 are to be attributed to the measures implemented after the Kyoto Protocol;
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (15,000 MW conversion) explicitly listed as part of the agreement.
mechanism_description: Improve and repower existing electricity generation units (e.g., to combined-cycle) to reduce fuel use per unit of electricity and cut power-sector CO2 emissions.

section_header: 4.2.1 Measures implemented in the electric sector, including renewable sources
policy_statement: Increase the use of renewable sources via a 2% obligation under the Enel voluntary agreement to reduce emissions (stated as 2 MtCO2 in the agreement).
parent_statement: Enel voluntary agreement aims to reduce emissions by 22 MtCO2 by 2006 compared to 1990 through power plant efficiency/CC conversion, renewables obligation, distribution leak reductions, and DSM measures.
verbatim_text: - increasing the use of renewable sources (2% obligation) capable of reducing emissions by 2 MtCO2 ;
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions impact tied to a specific mechanism (2% obligation) within the agreement.
mechanism_description: Setting an obligation to increase renewable energy use increases renewable generation share, displacing fossil electricity and lowering power-sector emissions.

section_header: 4.2.1 Measures implemented in the electric sector, including renewable sources
policy_statement: Reduce leaks from electricity distribution networks by 0.1 MtCO2 under the Enel voluntary agreement.
parent_statement: Enel voluntary agreement aims to reduce emissions by 22 MtCO2 by 2006 compared to 1990 through power plant efficiency/CC conversion, renewables obligation, distribution leak reductions, and DSM measures.
verbatim_text: - reducing leaks from distribution networks by 0.1 MtCO2;
extraction_rationale: Specific quantified measure (0.1 MtCO2) listed under the agreement.
mechanism_description: Reduce fugitive greenhouse gas leaks from electricity distribution equipment, directly lowering emissions from the power network.

section_header: 4.2.1 Measures implemented in the electric sector, including renewable sources
policy_statement: Implement DSM measures to spread more efficient electric technologies with a net reduction effect of 0.4 MtCO2 (stated) under the Enel voluntary agreement.
parent_statement: Enel voluntary agreement aims to reduce emissions by 22 MtCO2 by 2006 compared to 1990 through power plant efficiency/CC conversion, renewables obligation, distribution leak reductions, and DSM measures.
verbatim_text: - DSM measures: (Demand Side Management) by quantifying only the effects of the spread of most efficient electric technologies, it is possible to have a net reduction effect of 0.4 MtCO2...
extraction_rationale: Concrete demand-side intervention with quantified CO2 impact under the agreement.
mechanism_description: A DSM program accelerates uptake of more efficient electric technologies, lowering end-use electricity demand and thereby reducing associated emissions.

verbatim_text: Law 388 ... Contains several measures and initiatives involving sustainable development. ... The - tion of the income of small and medium- size enterprises earmarked for environ - mental investments is not counted towards determining taxable income... Resources available 2001-2002: 307.7 billion Lire. art. 21 'Measures on exemptions from excise tax for bio-diesel fuel'... Art. 29 'Rules and regulations on geother - mal energy': users who connect a remote-heating network that operates geothermal energy or is fuelled by - masses receive a subsidy (tax credit) of 40,000 Lire for each kW of power used. ... Art. 110 Fund for the reduction of - sions in the atmosphere and for - motion of energy efficiency and - able sources of energy.
extraction_rationale: Umbrella law explicitly listing several distinct measures across taxation, energy, and emissions reduction; appropriate to structure as a parent with article-level sub-policies.
mechanism_description: Provide tax incentives and dedicated funding for renewable energy and energy-efficiency investments, leading to reduced fossil energy consumption and lower greenhouse-gas/air-pollutant emissions.

section_header: 2.5.4.5 Indicators for the generation of electricity
policy_statement: ENEL signed a voluntary agreement (20 July 2000) with relevant ministries committing to reduce its CO2 emissions by 20% by 2006.
verbatim_text: The most important step has been the signing of a voluntary agreement on 20 July 2000 by the Ministry of the Environment, the Ministry of Production Activities and ENEL, with the latter, Italy's leading producer of electricity, voluntarily  committing  itself  to  reduce  its  emissions  of CO2 (by  -20%  within  2006).
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified emissions reduction commitment with deadline under a voluntary agreement.
mechanism_description: A voluntary agreement commits the electricity producer to cut its CO2 emissions, driving actions that reduce emissions from electricity generation.

section_header: 2.5.4.5 Indicators for the generation of electricity
policy_statement: Legislative Decree No. 79 of 16 March 1999 requires that starting in 2002 producers and importers supply at least 2% of prior-year thermoelectricity as electricity from new renewable plants, with compliance possible via purchasing GRTN-issued green certificates.
verbatim_text: Article 11 of Legislative Decree no. 79 of 16 March 1999 on the liberalisation  of  the  electricity  industry  stipulates that,  starting  in  2002,  producers  and  importers  are required  to  supply  to  the  national  electricity  system quantities of energy produced by new plants operated with renewable sources and equal to at least 2% of the thermoelectricity produced or imported in the course of the previous year. This requirement... can  also  be  met  by  purchasing from  other  operators  the  'green  certificates' issued by the Manager of the National Transmission  Network  (GRTN)  to  certify  production from renewable sources.
extraction_rationale: Binding renewable portfolio requirement with start date and compliance mechanism (green certificates).
mechanism_description: A mandated renewable electricity share (optionally met via tradable green certificates) increases renewable generation and displaces fossil-based power, lowering grid emissions.

policy_statement: Italy will implement additional expansion of renewable sources requiring an increase of 0.3% each year of the mandatory 2% renewable electricity obligation from 2005 to 2012.
verbatim_text: This additional expansion requires an increase of 0.3% each year of the mandatory 2% obligation, from 2005 to 2012..
extraction_rationale: Clear quantified policy change (annual percentage increase) with timeframe.
mechanism_description: A mandated increase in renewable electricity share drives more renewable generation on the grid, displacing fossil generation and lowering electricity-sector emissions.

section_header: 4.3.3.2 Increase in the efficiency of the transformers stock
policy_statement: From 2008, Italy plans to ban the sale of transformers whose losses exceed the standard in CEI 14.13 to increase average efficiency of the transformer stock.
verbatim_text: it is expected that, as from 2008, all transformers with leaks exceeding the standard indicated in the rules CEI 14.13. will not be allowed to be sold.
extraction_rationale: Clear regulatory prohibition with an effective date and referenced technical standard.
mechanism_description: Banning the sale of high-loss transformers forces adoption of more efficient transformers, cutting grid/distribution losses and reducing the amount of electricity that must be generated, thereby lowering emissions.

section_header: 4.4.4 Additional initiatives planned for the transport sector
policy_statement: Italy plans biodiesel blending in diesel up to 5%, with blending forecast to begin in 2006 at 1–1.5% and reach 4–5% by 2010, supported by a voluntary agreement and rules on diesel formulation (and possible tax reductions).
verbatim_text:
- -Bio-fuels. There are plans for the blending of diesel fuel used in engines with bio-diesel... up to a level of 5%...
...it can be forecast that blending of the commercial product will begin in 2006 at levels of 1÷1.5%, reaching rates of 4÷5% in the year 2010.
Plans call for a voluntary agreement... together with rules and regulations on the formulation of diesel oils sold to the public. There is the added possibility of a partial or total tax reduction for the bio-diesel fuel utilised.
extraction_rationale: Quantified blending targets with timeline and stated implementation instruments.
mechanism_description: Require/encourage biodiesel blending in diesel fuel so a share of fossil diesel is displaced by biofuel, reducing lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions from transport fuel use.

section_header: 4.5.1 Measures implemented in the residential and tertiary sector
policy_statement: Italy will implement a National Program for Introducing Solar Energy in Italian Penitentiaries, installing 3,000 m² of solar collectors within 5 years.
verbatim_text:
June 2001 Inter-Ministerial Agreement Ministries of the Environment and Justice
National Program for Introducing Solar Energy in Italian Penitentiaries: shall result in the installation of 3,000 m2 of collectors in 5 years.
extraction_rationale: Concrete program with quantified deployment target and timeframe.
mechanism_description: Deploying solar energy systems on prison facilities generates renewable energy (or solar heat) that reduces reliance on fossil energy, thereby lowering greenhouse gas emissions from conventional generation.

policy_statement: Italy plans to reduce SF6 leakage from electrical equipment by 2010 through an agreement between manufacturers and the Ministry for the Environment (including users for disposal), under assumptions including reducing installed-equipment leakage rate to 0.5% and end-of-life leakage rate to 1% by 2010.
verbatim_text: Electrical equipment... assumptions have been made: 1) stabilisation of manifacturing emissions at 2000 levels; 2) reduction of the leakage rate from installed equipment up to 0,5% by 2010; 3) reduction of the leakage rate from end-of-life equiment up to 1% by 2010. This will require the negotiation of an agreement between manifacturers and the Ministry for the Environment (also users shall be included as concerns the disposal of the systems).
extraction_rationale: Concrete target rates and deadline; specifies governance mechanism (negotiated agreement).
mechanism_description: A negotiated agreement reduces SF6 leakage during equipment operation and disposal, lowering fugitive high-global-warming-potential emissions.

policy_statement: Italy plans reforestation in hydrogeologically unstable areas under Law No. 183/1989 at about 6,000 hectares per year for 2003–2012 (total 60,000 hectares).

policy_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Accordingly, the NAP process is divided into six strategies. The strategies discuss each sector's principal vulnerabilities and management from a climate change perspective and present current guidelines for implementing adaptation measures to increase climate resilience.

## 5.2. Proposed sectors adaptation strategies
extraction_rationale: Introduces a structured set of sector strategies with multiple detailed actions underneath, functioning as an umbrella (parent) for the sector-specific sub-policies extracted next.
mechanism_description: Establishing and executing a national adaptation planning framework coordinates sectoral adaptation actions and guidelines, strengthening institutional capacity to manage climate risks and improve resilience.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: strengthen Ministry of Agriculture capacity; improve integrated pest/disease/weed management and resistant varieties; assess crop vulnerability/suitability by agro-ecological zones; and enhance climate-proof agro-infrastructure to raise farmer productivity.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Strengthen the capacity of the Ministry of Agriculture, including training of experts, logistics, and use of technology for the management of the sector.
• Improve the effectiveness of pest, disease, and weed management practices through the broader use of integrated pest and pathogen management...
• Assess crops vulnerability and suitability (cropping pattern) for different Agroecological zones.
• Enhance climate-proof agro-infrastructural systems that strengthen the capacity of local farmers to increase productivity.
extraction_rationale: Bullet-listed concrete interventions under the agriculture adaptation strategy table; clearly actionable capacity/infrastructure and management measures.
mechanism_description: Build agricultural institutional capacity and deploy climate-resilient agronomy and infrastructure (IPM, resistant varieties, suitability assessments, climate-proofed systems) to reduce climate-related crop losses and improve farmer productivity.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: support communities via indigenous knowledge dissemination, insurance schemes, early warning/early action systems, vaccination campaigns and disease control to cope with climate variability.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Support communities in livestock and crop sectors by inventorying and disseminating indigenous knowledge, establishing and/or strengthening insurance schemes, early warning, early action system, vaccination campaign, disease control, etc., to cope with the stress based on climate variability.
extraction_rationale: Specifies multiple concrete support mechanisms (insurance, EWS/EAS, vaccination, disease control) for adaptation.
mechanism_description: Provides climate risk information, financial risk transfer, and livestock/crop health interventions to help agricultural communities anticipate, withstand, and recover from climate variability impacts.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: develop and introduce integrated soil fertility management, water harvesting, and conservation techniques to improve soil fertility and cope with aridity, waterlogging and floods.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Develop and introduce a diverse range of integrated Soil fertility management (SFM), water harvesting, and conservation techniques to farmers as a sustainable means of improving soil fertility, intensifying agricultural production, and coping with extreme conditions (aridity, waterlogging, flood, etc.)
extraction_rationale: Concrete practice/technology package to be developed and introduced to farmers.
mechanism_description: Implementing integrated soil fertility, water harvesting, and conservation practices improves soil and water management, helping farms sustain production and withstand drought, flooding, and waterlogging impacts.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: strengthen CARI capacity for climate-smart agriculture, including establishing seed banks and improving soil management, crop diversification, and livestock breeds; and develop climate-resilient crops including drought-resistant and flood-tolerant varieties and gene banks.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Strengthen the Central Agricultural Research Institute (CARI) capacity for research development of climate smart agriculture initiates in Liberia, including the setting up of seed banks and soil management, crop diversification... improved livestock breeds, etc.
• Develop climate-resilient crops, promote drought - resistant, flood-tolerant, and establishing gene bank of climate-resilient varieties of indigenous food crops (NDC, 2015)
extraction_rationale: Institutional strengthening plus specific deliverables (seed banks/gene banks; resilient varieties) are clear adaptation actions.
mechanism_description: Investing in climate-smart agriculture research, seed/gene banks, and resilient crop/livestock and soil practices increases the ability of agriculture to withstand droughts, floods, and other climate impacts.

policy_statement: Coastal adaptation strategy: develop and implement coastal zone policy/design/management plan; develop an integrated coastal management plan and early warning system with training; promote disaster risk management and preparedness; rehabilitate and protect wetlands and mangroves; and climate-proof new coastal infrastructure investments.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 8: Adaptation strategies of the coastal sector

• develop and implement coastal zone policy, design, and management plan(NDC, 2015)
• Develop an integrated management plan for coastal zone management as well as an early warning system that includes training and capacity development for coastal management and monitoring
• Promote and implement disaster risk management in general (especially disaster preparedness).
• Support the rehabilitation and protection of wetlands and mangroves...
• Develop and implement a program for climate-proofing new investments in infrastructure (roads, sewers, water supplies, and other infrastructure) in coastal settlements and rural areas...
extraction_rationale: A suite of concrete coastal adaptation measures, including planning instruments, EWS, DRM, ecosystem protection, and infrastructure climate-proofing.
mechanism_description: Implementing integrated coastal management (including early warning and preparedness), protecting/restoring wetlands and mangroves, and climate-proofing coastal infrastructure reduces exposure and vulnerability to coastal hazards such as storm surge, flooding, and erosion.

policy_statement: Coastal adaptation strategy: design and implement a strategic communication action plan to inform and educate the public on climate change-related coastal changes, challenges, and adaptation options.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 8: Adaptation strategies of the coastal sector

• Design and implement a strategic communication action plan to inform and educate people about changes and challenges associated with coastal areas related to climate change and how they can adapt to cope with these changes and challenges.
extraction_rationale: Specific planned intervention (communication action plan) with clear purpose and scope.
mechanism_description: A strategic public communication and education program increases awareness of coastal climate risks and available adaptation measures, enabling better preparedness and supporting effective adaptation planning and actions.

policy_statement: Energy adaptation strategy: establish and promote a national solar energy program and other energy-efficient lighting technologies, including provision of energy-efficient technologies for schools, public institutions, and households.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 9: Adaptation strategies of the energy sector

• Establish and promote a robust national program on solar energy (e.g., hybrid systems, installation of solar panels, promotion of solar street lighting, etc.) and other energy-efficient lighting technologies.
• Support the provision of energy-efficient technologies such as energy-efficient bulbs to provide power and lighting for schools and other public institutions and households...
extraction_rationale: Concrete energy adaptation/transition actions (solar program, efficiency tech deployment) explicitly proposed as strategy measures.
mechanism_description: Deploying solar systems and energy-efficient lighting reduces fossil-fuel electricity generation by replacing some grid supply with solar power and cutting electricity demand for lighting.

policy_statement: Energy adaptation strategy: promote energy plantations to reduce pressure on natural forests, regulate sustainable biomass energy use, support community-based off-grid/mini-grids, conserve/protect water catchments for hydropower and municipal supply, and prevent sedimentation affecting hydropower generation.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 9: Adaptation strategies of the energy sector

• Support the promotion and implementation of energy plantation schemes to minimize natural forest pressure and reduce energy stress.
• Develop a system to regulate the sustainable use of biomass energy.
• Promote and support the development and utilization of community-based off- grid/mini-grids.
• Conservation and protection of water catchments, including around hydro- power and municipal water supply sources.
• Prevent sedimentation that could hinder the production of energy in hydroelectric facilities
extraction_rationale: Multiple specific interventions for resilience and sustainability in the energy sector are explicitly listed.
mechanism_description: Protecting and managing water catchments (including reducing sedimentation) maintains inflows and operational reliability for hydropower, improving electricity supply resilience under climate variability.

policy_statement: Fisheries adaptation strategy: strengthen capacity of fisheries authorities for research/monitoring/enforcement; support artisanal fishing communities with training, gear and alternative livelihoods; establish monitoring/reporting/verification and adaptive management; support research on climate pressures; identify/map/protect valuable fisheries areas including marine protected areas; reduce external stressors via regulatory measures; integrate fisheries into national adaptation and food security policies; establish early warning and information/communication networks; and regulate fishing to prevent overexploitation.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 10: Adaptation strategies of the fisheries sector

• Strengthen the capacity of the Bureau of National Fisheries... for research monitoring and enforcement.
• Invest in and support artisanal fishing communities, including training, fishing gear, and alternative livelihoods.
• Set up robust monitoring, reporting, and verification system...
• Identify, map, and protect areas valuable for fisheries... including the setting up of marine protected areas.
• ...instituting actions and regulatory measures to reduce land-based sources of pollution... and destructive fishing practices...
• Integrate fisheries fully into climate change adaptation and food security policies at the national level...
• Support the establishment of early warning systems...
• ...Regulate fishing practices to prevent overexploitation and fishing in restricted areas (NAPA, 2008)
extraction_rationale: Comprehensive list of concrete governance, capacity, protection, and regulatory measures presented as sector adaptation strategies.
mechanism_description: Protecting and regulating fisheries areas (e.g., marine protected areas) and reducing pollution/overfishing pressures improves marine ecosystem condition, helping fisheries and coastal ecosystems remain resilient to climate impacts.

policy_statement: Forestry adaptation strategy: strengthen FDA capacity and logistics; implement sustainable/alternative livelihoods for forest-dependent communities; promote community forest activities beyond timber extraction; establish comprehensive forest (including NTFPs) monitoring; implement reforestation/afforestation in degraded areas; map and manage forest water catchments; consolidate protected area networks with landscape connectivity; strengthen coordination with other agencies; enforce anti-poaching regulations and a wildlife code of ethics; and implement communication strategies to raise awareness on climate impacts and adaptation.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 31: Adaptation strategies of the forestry sector

• Strengthen the capacity of the FDA, including training of experts and logistics for forest management
• Implement sustainable and, where applicable alternative livelihood initiatives for forest-dependent communities...
• Promote community forest activities beyond timber extraction...
• Establish a comprehensive monitoring system for forest resources...
• Implement reforestation and afforestation activities in degraded areas...
• Identify and map for proper management of water catchment areas in forests...
• Promote the consolidation of protected area network...
• Enforce regulations related to illegal hunting...
• Develop and implement a communication strategy to increase the awareness...
extraction_rationale: Explicit sector strategy with multiple specific interventions including capacity, monitoring, restoration, protected areas, enforcement, and outreach.
mechanism_description: Restore and expand forest cover (and protect connected habitats) to enhance ecosystem resilience and maintain/increase terrestrial carbon storage while reducing land degradation impacts.

policy_statement: Waste management adaptation/mitigation strategy: strengthen community and institutional capacity for integrated waste management; develop an integrated waste management strategy prioritizing waste prevention and low-GHG technologies compatible with methane capture for electricity; promote PPP financing for waste infrastructure; implement composting to convert urban waste into inputs for agriculture; and develop landfills for major cities including methane recovery and power generation using CDM and NAMAs.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 42: Adaptation strategies of the waste management sector

• Strengthen capacity at the community and institutional level for integrated waste management.
• Develop an integrated waste management strategy and system for all types of waste, assigning priority to prevent waste generation with nationally appropriate low greenhouse gas emission technologies... compatible with methane capture and use for electricity generation.
• Promote private-public partnership (PPP)...
• Design and implement a system to run urban waste into input for agricultural production through composting...
• Develop landfills for all major cities and use the Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) and Nationally Appropriate Mitigation Actions (NAMAs)to develop methane recovery and power generation projects in landfills.
extraction_rationale: Lists concrete policy/program actions with specific mechanisms (PPP, composting, landfills, methane capture, CDM/NAMAs).
mechanism_description: Build/upgrade landfill and waste infrastructure to capture methane and use it to generate electricity, reducing methane emissions and displacing fossil-based power generation.

policy_statement: Reduce California greenhouse gas emissions to 1990 levels by 2020 as required by AB 32.
verbatim_text: By requiring in law a reduction of greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions to 1990 levels by 2020, California set the stage for its transition to a sustainable, clean energy future.
extraction_rationale: Legally binding statewide emissions target with a clear deadline (2020) anchored in AB 32.
mechanism_description: Setting a legally binding economy-wide GHG target creates a mandate that drives policies and actions across sectors to reduce overall emissions.

section_header: A. The Role of State Government:  Setting an Example
policy_statement: Set a California State government operations target to reduce GHG emissions by at least 30% below estimated business-as-usual emissions by 2020 (about 15% below current levels).
verbatim_text: Therefore, California State government has established a target of reducing its greenhouse gas emissions by a minimum of 30 percent below its estimated business-as-usual emissions by 2020 - approximately a 15 percent reduction from current levels.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with a deadline for state government operations.
mechanism_description: Establishes a government-operations-wide GHG reduction target that drives planning and implementation of measures to reduce emissions over time.

policy_statement: Develop and adopt a Low Carbon Fuel Standard to reduce the carbon intensity of California transportation fuels by at least 10% by 2020, with regulation for Board consideration in March 2009.
verbatim_text: ARB is developing a Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS), which would reduce the carbon intensity of California's transportation fuels by at least ten percent by 2020... ARB identified the LCFS as a Discrete Early Action item, and is developing a regulation for Board consideration in March 2009.
extraction_rationale: Specific performance standard target (10% by 2020) and near-term regulatory milestone.
mechanism_description: A regulatory low-carbon fuel standard reduces the lifecycle carbon intensity of transportation fuels, leading to lower greenhouse gas emissions from the transport fuel supply.

policy_statement: Implement the Solar Hot Water and Efficiency Act incentive program with a goal of installing 200,000 solar water heating systems by 2017.
verbatim_text: The Solar Hot Water and Efficiency Act of 2007 (SHWEA) authorized a ten year, $250-million incentive program for solar water heaters with a goal of promoting the installation of 200,000 systems in California by 2017.
extraction_rationale: Funded incentive program with quantified deployment goal and date.
mechanism_description: Incentives drive adoption of solar hot water systems, which displace conventional fossil-fueled water heating and reduce associated greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Set a target of an additional 4,000 MW of installed combined heat and power (CHP) capacity by 2020 (displacing about 30,000 GWh).
verbatim_text: This measure sets a target of an additional 4,000 MW of installed CHP capacity by 2020, enough to displace approximately 30,000 GWh of demand from other power generation sources.
extraction_rationale: Quantified capacity target with a 2020 deadline for CHP deployment.
mechanism_description: Deploying additional combined heat and power capacity improves energy efficiency and provides local generation that displaces higher-emitting conventional power generation, reducing overall emissions.

policy_statement: Achieve a statewide Renewables Portfolio Standard of 33% renewable electricity by 2020.
verbatim_text: Based on Governor Schwarzenegger's call for a statewide 33 percent RPS, the Plan anticipates that California will have 33 percent of its electricity provided by renewable resources by 2020
extraction_rationale: Clear quantified renewables share target with date.
